Name: Pointcloud Inc
Description: Pointcloud is a technology company developing high performance 3D imaging solutions.

Name: Laboratory of Nano and Quantum Engineering
Description:
Leibniz Research Centre LNQE

We are an interdisciplinary Leibniz Research Centre of the Leibniz University Hannover in the field of nanotechnology. Our goals are both excellent basic research and application-oriented engineering in the nanoscale accompanied by appropriate interdisciplinary training. Currently, more than 30 working groups from physics, chemistry and engineering are involved. To achieve our goals, we operate a shared research building in Hanover.

Name: BrightComSol
Description: At BrightComSol, our mission is to transform global access to safe and effective radiotherapy. This is possible by unlocking the potential of FLASH radiotherapy, a revolutionary treatment approach where cancer patients receive their entire radiation dose in less than one second, instead of multiple sessions over several weeks. Major radiotherapy machine producers are betting on FLASH technology, but how do they know their multi-million dollar radiation machine is actually delivering the exact prescribed dose during the treatment? Our product, BrightEye, is an imaging-based real-time dosimetry solution designed to solve this problem by monitoring the radiation beam during the treatment to ensure the radiotherapy machine delivers the exact radiation dose to the cancer patient to avoid over- or under-dose delivery. At BrightComSol, we are making FLASH radiotherapy a clinical reality by working closely with radiotherapy machine producers in a clinical environment.

Name: Bio Graphene Solutions
Description: Bio Graphene Solutions (BGS) is a manufacturer and supplier of graphene from non-graphite based, organic renewable resources. Graphene’s applications are wide-ranging with the potential to disrupt numerous verticals; however, traditional graphite-based production methods are hugely cost prohibitive and environmentally taxing. This is where we come in. BGS has a unique and proprietary approach in providing a real nanotechnology solution (green graphene) with a focus on improving the environmental impact in the concrete and asphalt markets. As a performance additive, our biographene product provides real measurable performance results across the multiple applications that we support. Our graphene production technology is easily scalable, ensuring we are able to provide a consistent and high-quality graphene product while being able to support large markets that require commodity volumes – minimalizing the environmental impact for industrial applications while ensuring that our value proposition of our product is cost-conscious in the markets we serve.

Name: NewSpec Pty Ltd
Description:
NewSpec was established in December 2004 as an Australian owned company, as the exclusive Australasian distributor for Newport Corporation. We specialise in the marketing, sales, importation, installation and service of scientific research equipment, primarily in the fields of lasers, optics and surface metrology. 

Based in Adelaide, with offices in Melbourne, Brisbane and Sydney, NewSpec continues to grow, offering an expanding range of Laser Systems, AFM's, Surface Profilers, and related optical equipment to the scientific research, aerospace & defence, microelectronics and life science markets throughout Australia and New Zealand.

Name: Brnoregion Microscopy
Description:
We are a community of companies, research and educational institutions from the Brno region, dealing in electron microscopy, with support from the City of Brno and the South Moravian Region.

Our activities are dedicated to:
- Community building
- PR and promotion
- Supply chain development
- Promoting commercialisation

Name: Cluster Nanotechnologie
Description:
We cross-link nanotechnology competences from the economy, science, politics and education. The focus is on the networking with small and medium-sized enterprises with each other and with universities, universities of applied sciences and research institutions, on an efficient transfer of R&D&I results into marketable products and the promotion of cooperations aiming at concrete projects, i.e. in the field of nanocarbon materials, nanoinks, nanosilver or nanoanalytics.

Core competencies are the initiation, support and coordination of projects and networks up to the complete project management, the support of project proposals and the organisation of customer-specific innovation workshops and seminars.

Name: Raith BV
Description:
Raith BV develops and manufactures electron-beam lithography equipment based on Gaussian Beam technology and provides cutting edge technology solutions for a wide range of existing and emerging semiconductor and nanotechnology applications. Our electron-beam systems (the EBPG series) are world-wide accepted in advanced research laboratories, universities and industrial applications. 
Raith offers not only proven equipment for applied research and universities, but also customized tool adaptions, which are in-line with customer’s specific process technology.

Name: NanoSpek
Description: Şirketimiz NanoSpek araştırma-geliştirme, malzeme karakterizasyonu, kalite-kontrol ve proses-kontrol uygulamalarında gelişen teknoloji ile artan müşteri taleplerini karşılamak için Türkiye Sanayi, Üniversite ve Araştırma Kurumlarına kaliteli, yenilikçi analitik cihazlar sunmak ve satış sonrasında güvenilir teknik destek vermek amacıyla 2005 yılında kurulmuştur. Konusunda dünya lideri olan firmaların malzeme karakterizasyon ürün gruplarının Türkiye yetkili temsilcisi olan şirketimiz, proses kontrol çözümleri için de danışmanlık hizmeti vermektedir.

Name: NANOS
Description: NANOS is a company that aims to deliver technological solutions so that research institutes, technology centers and companies that produce or manipulate nanomaterials develop optimized, more sustainable and safe processes, thus, proactively positioning itself to the regulations of the area, and also minimizing harmful effects on the environment and people. Our focus is on caring for the environment and people - from whom it produces to those who will make use of nanotechnology - enabling the responsible development of more competitive nanoproducts.

Name: TERA-print
Description:
TERA-print commercializes desktop nanofabrication tools and technologies designed to accelerate R&D and discovery at the nanoscale. The 'TERA-Fab' allows researchers to rapidly prototype electronic devices, construct nanoscale and microscale patterned substrates, regardless of substrate material or ink; study the chemical consequences of materials miniaturization, prototype microfluidic devices and sensors, and control cell surface interactions at the sub-cellular level. 

By combining unique hardware, software/UI, and surface engineering techniques, we have redefined how nanoscale structures and devices are designed, engineered, and made. With our tools, researchers can explore nanoscale phenomena and discover new materials faster and more efficiently than ever before.

Name: HARDTEN Precision Technologies
Description:
Welcome to Hardten Precision Technologies Pvt Ltd, the global specialist in manufacturing high-precision CVD diamond tools across diverse industries and sectors. Our tools are distinctly developed to achieve ultra-precise finishing on non-ferrous and various other materials, setting new industry standards. 
 
𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝗳𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝘀 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝘂𝗽𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗲 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲𝘀:
 
𝗣𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻-𝗖𝗿𝗮𝗳𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗦𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀: Delivering ultra-high precision, fine-quality diamond tools with waviness ranging from Uncontrolled (2000-900nm) to Ultra controlled (<50nm) for super-finishing and finishing applications. 
 
𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗻𝘂𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Achieving dimensional stability and error-proof solutions through advanced R&D and design.  

𝗔𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝘂𝗳𝗮𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴: Leveraging global-standard technologies for precise micro and nano requirements. 
 
𝗘𝗺𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗚𝗼𝗮𝗹𝘀: Catering to different specific demands from various industries like 
 
Aerospace | Automotive | Optics | Medical | Printing & Packaging and many more with a wide network of world-class tool delivery. 
 
Envisioning a future where our precision technologies shape industries, we continually push boundaries, empowering businesses worldwide with innovative solutions. 
 
Connect us on: 

Info@hardtenprecision.com
technical@hardtenprecision.com
career@hardtenprecision.com
sales@hardtenprecision.com

Name: HT Materials Science
Description:
HT Materials Science (HTMS) is the global leader in smart heat transfer fluids for commercial and industrial heating and cooling systems. We help any business with a high heating or cooling demand to make significant energy and carbon savings – from commercial offices and data centres through to manufacturers, pharmaceutical & healthcare facilities and more. 

Our patented Maxwell product is a drop-in nanofluid additive that’s proven to enhance the heat transfer of a base fluid by more than 15%. It delivers immediate results (with no costly downtime needed), making HVAC systems instantly more efficient and sustainable. 

Maxwell can reduce a company’s energy costs by 12-15% or more and can also increase a system’s capacity. This means businesses can get more out of their existing equipment for longer, as their heating or cooling demands increase. Our technology is already being successfully used by a host of major companies across the US, EMEA and Asia, bringing them enhanced efficiencies, cost savings and lower carbon footprints as a result. 

At HTMS we proudly work with some of the best science and engineering minds in the world, with offices in Dublin, Lecce (Italy) and New York. R&D is at the heart of everything we do, and we’re constantly developing future technologies to help businesses reduce energy consumption and their environmental impact.

Name: Institut Galien Paris-Saclay (UMR CNRS 8612)
Description: L'Institut Galien Paris-Saclay (UMR CNRS 8612) développe des micro et nanotechnologies appliquées au médicament et au diagnostic dans le domaine de la santé. La particularité de l’unité est de regrouper des chercheurs et enseignants-chercheurs issus de disciplines différentes: chimie, physico-chimie, galénique, chimie-analytique et biologie.

Name: Ekidna
Description:
If you’re a large producer with your own lab, centralized testing means production bottlenecks and idle resources. For smaller producers who have to mail samples to a lab service, delays to market and lower quality products can be the result. 

Ekidna’s cannabis testing kit accurately provides the cannabinoid information you need to fine-tune your production methods anywhere in your process or on your facility.

No matter the size of your operation, Ekidna can help you streamline testing, get products to market faster, and maximize operational efficiency.

Name: Universal Photonics, Inc.
Description:
Name the Application - We Provide the Solution. With more than 9,000 products for optics fabrication, we meet the surfacing requirements of a variety of industries including Precision Optics, Ophthalmic, Flat Glass & Mirror, Semi-Conductor & Electronics.

Universal Photonics is a world-leader in critical surface preparation materials. Our dedication to the advancement of surfacing and polishing technology in all phases of fabrication spans nearly a century, and is supported with state-of-art laboratory, manufacturing and distribution facilities throughout the United States, Europe, and the Far East.

Our memberships and affiliations include The International Society for Optics and Photonics (SPIE), American Precision Optics Manufacturers Association and New York Photonics Rochester.

Our goal as a manufacturer and global distributor is to provide you with the most advanced surfacing and production products available. To assure this, we are committed to research and product development, maintaining in-depth knowledge of the latest advancements across a variety of surfacing technologies, and ongoing, comprehensive field research.

More than offer an extensive line of quality products, UPI applies the Science of Surfacing to all applications. Contact our Account Services team or arrange to consult with a Field Applications Engineer. As we have for nearly a century, UPI stands ready to meet your specific application needs quickly and thoroughly, while always providing fast, dependable service and accurate, technical consultations.

Name: Copprint
Description:
The future of printed electronics is copper. 

Copprint is the leader in conductive Nano copper inks for additive copper printing. We are ushering in a new era of printed electronics, empowering ubiquitous conductivity. 

Our rapid sintering, oxidation-free Nano Copper Inks enable very-low cost, high conductivity, environmentally friendly printed electronics embedded everywhere.

Copprint enables to fabricate compostable RFID antennas on paper which are cheaper than standard RFID (nondegradable and non-recyclable Aluminum on Plastic).

Copprint breakthrough Nano Copper inks outperform silver inks and etched Copper or Aluminum, bringing the advantages of additive manufacturing to printed electronics. 

Copprint’s product portfolio offers full substrate freedom for really simple printed electronics fabrication on paper, PET, HJT PV, and many other substrates. 
Copprint is pioneering a world of conductivity for high volume additive manufacturing applications, including RFID, PV cells, flexible electronics and PCB’s. Muhlbauer Automated Antenna Printing Solution (APS) developed for Copprint Inks is available now. 
Copprint strategic investors include Henkel, HP and Tatsuta.

Name: Onnes Technologies
Description:
Onnes Technologies is a leading provider of advanced cryogenic nano-positioners and cryogenic vibration isolation for scientific research and industrial applications. The company is dedicated to developing innovative products that enable scientists and researchers to explore the frontiers of quantum computing, nanoelectronics, and materials science.
With a focus on precision engineering and cutting-edge CRYO-WALKING technology, Onnes Technologies offers a range of products, including cryogenic nanopositioners arQtika, vibration isolation systems cVIS, and Quantum Scanning Probe Microscopy qSPM systems. Our products are designed to operate at ultra-low temperatures down to 10 mK, allowing for precise and accurate positioning preserving milli-Kelvin temperatures and pm/√Hz vibration levels.
The company's team has extensive expertise in cryogenic technology, nanopositioning, and scanning probe microscopy techniques. We are committed to providing exceptional service and support to customers around the world. Onnes Technologies also collaborates with leading research institutions and universities to advance the state of the art in cryogenic technology and its applications.
Founded in 2018, Onnes Technologies is headquartered in Leiden, the Netherlands and has a global presence, serving customers. The company is dedicated to making a positive impact on the world through the development of advanced technology solutions that help drive scientific discovery and innovation.

Name: Durham Magneto Optics Ltd
Description:
Welcome to Durham Magneto Optics Ltd. We specialize in advanced scientific instrumentation for nanotechnology research and development. Our product range includes a family of magneto-optical Kerr effect magnetometers (NanoMOKE3) and a family of direct-write laser lithography machines (MicroWriter ML).

Durham Magneto Optics Ltd was founded in 2002 as a spin-off company from Professor Russell Cowburn’s nanomagnetism research group in Durham University Physics Department. During a decade of successful trading since then, DMO has deployed machines world wide. We have a global network of sales and support staff provided by the well known scientific instrumentation companies Quantum Design and LOT-Oriel.

Name: piezosystem jena
Description:
PSJ (piezosystem jena) is a worldwide developer and manufacturer of high precision piezo equipment. 

PSJ is a privately owned cooperation, with a strong focus to create customized solutions for industrial and research application. Through its more than 25 years of existence, PSJ has worked together with leading OEMs in the fields of semiconductor, photonics, metrology, aerospace and the automotive industry.  

Together with our customers, we have developed systems that can achieve extreme levels of precision, high force generation, or stable high-frequency movements.

PSJ's team of experts knows everything about motion and we share this passion with our customers. Reliability and excellent performance are the key factors of PSJ's success. PSJ has a sales network covering 25 countries from Australia to the US.

PSJ has two major lines of products:
Low Power Stages and Actuators for positioning and frequency generation, used in microscopy, spectroscopy, laser positioning, 3D scanning, probes and wafer handling, and autofocus systems.

High Power Actuators for pulse generation, fatigue testing, shaker systems, and vibration testing.

Name: °Catalytic Instruments
Description:
Catalytic Instruments GmbH & Co. KG develops, manufactures, and markets innovative systems for aerosol conditioning and processing. 
Our catalytic stripper technology is designed to remove volatile compounds from aerosols, preventing renucleation effects through an oxidizing catalyst. The particle generator enables nanoparticle production for aerosol calibration, as well as the synthesis of nanoparticles and catalysts. The core technologies were developed at the University of Minneapolis and University of Cambridge with a focus on environmental aerosols, catalysis and nanotechnology, today a growing interest is the investigation of combustion generated particles. The company was founded in 2013 by Dr. Hans-Joachim Schulz, Dr. Jacob Swanson and Dr. Adam Boies.

The technology has a broad range of applications, which have only been investigated in a narrow research and application area. Customized OEM solutions, integrated into particle measurement equipment or as stand alone solutions, we provide tailored units to meet specific customer requirements and research needs.

Vision

“To be a global leader in creating innovative solutions for catalysis, conditioning emissions and accurately measuring particle concentration to driving excellence in research and positively impacting the world through our pioneering technology.”

Mission

“Our mission is to develop, manufacture, and market cutting-edge products that condition emissions and enable precise particle measurements with a creative technology.”

Statement

“We are dedicated to promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion in all aspects of our company, recognizing that the collaboration of diverse perspectives is essential for our collective success.”

Get in touch with our expert team to learn more!

info@catalytic-instruments.com
Phone: +49-171-621-6947
Catalytic Instruments GmbH & Co.KG
Zellerhornstrasse 7
83026 Rosenheim, Germany

Name: Optitune
Description:
Optitune's chemists and engineers are a determined team, intent on pushing the boundaries of possibility in terms of light transmission, surface management, and durability.

Transparent functional surfaces have become ubiquitous in our lives; whether it is a window to protect us, a touch screen to inform us, or a solar panel to power our lives; the clarity, feel and durability of these surfaces has become mission-critical.

Our product portfolio includes:
- flexible plastic hard coats
- easy-to-clean (anti-fingerprint) (anti-scratch)metal coatings
- light management materials (anti-reflection, optical cladding)
- optoelectronics (photo-patternable dielectric overcoats)

If you can Imagine a window without reflection, or a surface that never scratches then you are starting to think as we do.

Name: Technolutions Ltd.
Description:
Technolutions is build by people, experienced, young, sometimes crazy, but most of all full of enthusiasm for the new challenges we face dealing with people like you. We work in the field of coating, surface modification, light microscopy and profilometry, mechanical testing including hardness, scratch and tribology. Together with you, we learn about the new possible application of the systems we already know. Every inquiry is different, unique and important. 
Test us!

Name: NanoMEGAS
Description:
Nanomegas was established in 2004 and specializes in advanced electron diffraction techniques for TEM. It was the first to develop and commercialize  Precession Electron Diffraction (PED) device (“spinning star”) for structure solution, being able to retrofit various TEM electron microscopes. 
Today, our advanced solutions (hardware & software)  include 4D–SPED (scanning precession electron diffraction)  Phase & Orientation Mapping  (ASTAR) down to 1 nm resolution, Strain mapping with a sensitivity of 0.02% down to 2-3 nm resolution, 3D Diffraction Tomography (ADT-3D) for crystal structure determination  of  nm size crystals and Electron Pair Distribution Function (e-PDF) for amorphous materials analysis. 

Our hardware product range is compatible with most commercial TEM electron microscopes (including Cs corrected TEMs) and latest technology CCD cameras / Direct  Detection detectors. Actually, PED & electron crystallography related applications are present in every major electron microscopy/ X-Ray crystallography Congress worldwide ; there are more than 800 scientific publications (period 2004-2020) where Nanomegas  related instrumentation has been used  from more than 200 TEM  laboratories worldwide.

Name: Accelerated Materials
Description:
Accelerated Materials provides businesses with end-to-end solutions for nanomaterial production. We develop and supply next generation hardware, software and services for materials innovation and manufacturing. We are located in the United Kingdom and Singapore.

It often takes decades to industrialize new material technologies. AM uses a fundamentally different R&D methodology, incorporating intensified microreactor technology and machine learning to accelerate scale-up. We reduce the time and cost to scale high precision materials by up to 90%.

AM was founded to commercialize inventions and know-how developed in various projects at the University of Cambridge and Cambridge Centre for Advanced Research and Education in Singapore (CARES Ltd) since 2015.

Name: Aceso Optics
Description: Aceso Optics is the industry leader in omniphobic, oleophobic, and superhydrophobic coating applications.  We partner with ophthalmic and precision optical laboratories to provide one of the most innovative products on the market in our Super Oleo line of evaporate chemicals.

Name: ENWAIR
Description: Enwair is a startup which aims to develop Li-ion batteries' capacity and life time. Enwair established in 2015 and have funded by the governmental organizations such as TUBITAK and KOSGEB. Till now, we developed an anode which tripled the capacity of commercial anode materials. Now, Enwair team works on the cathode active materials which will meet this anode capacity. Our aim is to develop a battery which has 3 times more capacity than normal batteries.

Name: Lund Nano Lab
Description: Lund Nano Lab (LNL) – the nanofabrication facility #Myfab Lund – is a cornerstone of nanoscience and nanotechnology at #NanoLund, providing advanced micro- and nanofabrication capabilities to researchers across strategically important fields. LNL supports Swedish researchers, entrepreneurs, and industry with leading-edge tools and expertise, and maintains strong ties with the Nordic Nanolab Network (NNN) and other European partners. The cleanroom facility is continuously upgraded and supported by a dedicated team of skilled staff. LNL is a flagship core facility at Lund University, actively contributing to deep-tech innovation, sustainable operations, and education in semiconductor processing and fabrication. Through ongoing improvement initiatives, startup support, and strategic engagement in the design of a new cleanroom, LNL continues to shape an outstanding user environment and strengthen international collaborations with MAX IV, ESS, and beyond.

Name: Veloxint
Description:
Extraordinary Metals, Rapid Net-Shape Manufacturing.

Veloxint provides high performance products & parts enabled by our novel nanocrystalline metal alloys based on fundamental science developed at MIT.

Name: Smart Nano NI
Description:
Smart Nano NI is a Northern Ireland consortium collaborating to develop game-changing advanced prototyping and smart manufacturing methods to deliver new technologies. The consortium shares a niche capability around nano manufacturing and world-leading knowledge in photonics. The consortium has been awarded £42.4m UK Research and Innovation’s Strength in Places Fund. The total project value including leveraged funding from other sources is £63.9m. Our collaboration is aimed at driving self-sustaining local industry and economic growth through research and innovation. 

The consortium has been awarded seedcorn funding of £50,000 to develop their bid and compete for a share of £236m from the UK Research and Innovation’s Strength in Places Fund. Our collaboration is aimed at driving self-sustaining local industry and economic growth through research and innovation.

Name: Electronic Coast
Description:
EC norges ledende klynge av virksomheter som driver innovasjon, utvikling og produksjon av muliggjørende teknologi, spesielt mikroelektronikk og sensorteknologi. 

Teknologiklyngen har som formål å fremme og forene ideer og kunnskap for å løse viktige samfunnsutfordringer nasjonalt og internasjonalt.

Dette skal gjøres ved aktivt å oppfordre til og legge til rette for utveksling av kunnskap, erfaring og problemløsning. 

EC skal være et nettverk for medlemmer å søke råd, innspill og veiledning. Det være seg å bli satt i forbindelse med andre som kan støtte og bidra, eller tilrettelegge for fagutvikling og kompetansedeling. 

EC skal jobbe aktivt for å fremme næringens rammevilkår både nasjonalt og internasjonalt, og sørge for informasjonsflyt som er relevant for bransjen.

Name: geCKo Materials
Description: “The specific public benefit purpose of the Corporation is to economically scale an energy efficient adhesive, improving environmental sustainability in commercial applications and simplifying attachment and adhesion for consumer applications.” -geCKo Materials, PBC

Name: GBneuhaus GmbH
Description: GBneuhaus is one of the leading suppliers for individual, custom-tailored coating solutions based upon state-of-the-art nanotechnology. Our company is certified according to ISO 9001:2008 and we comply with the standards under the terms of ISO TS 16949. Our internal environmental management refers to ISO 14001 according to which we define our objectives regarding environmental protection. We work as a team and achieve our targets together. The business success of our company encourages us for “working in times of continuous change”.

Name: Advanced Nanotechnologies
Description:
Who we are

Advanced Nanotechnologies S.L. is a spin-off company issued from the University of Barcelona (UB) which participates to its social capital.

The company was founded by a team composed by scientists with wide experience in applied physics and materials engineering and professionals highly experienced in different industrial business.


Our Mission

Advanced Nanotechnologies S.L. is devoted to materials and surface applications addressed to the consumers and business market. It supports R&D projects by developing specific processes and equipments for each application.

We offer innovative solutions based on nanotechnology adapted to specific developments of our costumers.


Capabilities' Fields


- Encapsulation (Carbon Encapsulated Iron Nanoparticles)

- Energy (Supercapacitors and Solar Cells)

- Graphene (Production technology and characterisation)

- Manufacturing (Plasma Deposition Reactors, CVD and PECVD, Sputtering and Thermal treatments)

- Nanocoatings (Thin Films, nanostructured surfaces and Surface Treatments)

- Nanomaterials (Thin Films, nanoparticles, Core@shell, Carbon Nanotubes, Graphene, membranes)

- Nanophotonics (Colloidal crystals, bidimensional Photonic Crystals)

- Nanoparticles (Si based nanoparticles, Fe@C nanoparticles)

- Nanotubes (CNTs and vertically aligned CNTs)

Name: Boeckeler Instruments, Inc.
Description: Boeckeler Instruments – The Ultramicrotome Company – develops, manufactures and distributes sample preparation equipment for nanoscale research, under the RMC Boeckeler brand. Our solutions are employed in the advancement of a broad range of areas including materials science and cell biology, with special emphasis in 3D sample preparation. We’re a small, passionate team, based in Tucson, Arizona, dedicated to the science of nanotechnology as well as providing outstanding customer service.

Name: Nanode Battery Technologies
Description:
We need high performance batteries for electric vehicles, portable electronics, and to provide energy storage for intermittent renewable energy resources. Graphite as conventional anode material can’t satisfy those needs. At Nanode, we are focused on the design and production of high performance anodes for lithium and sodium ion batteries that can meet those needs and beyond.
We produce metal alloy anode which has higher energy storage capacity, simpler one-step preparation method, and shorter manufacturing time than traditional anodes.

Name: Polaris Electro-Optics, Inc.
Description: At Polaris Electro-Optics, Inc., we believe that overcoming society’s greatest challenges requires mastery of the creation, movement, and use of information. We are mastering the movement of information by leveraging patented material and design innovations to offer the highest-speed, most energy efficient integrated photonics products to the communications and computation industries. Our goal is to define the standard for moving information.  We work hard in a collaborative, cross-functional team environment, and value mutual trust and respect built on a foundation of integrity, transparency, consistency, and humility.  Additional information about Polaris is available at www.polariseo.com.

Name: Flexiramics
Description:
Flexiramics is a spin-off from the renowned MESA+ Institute for Nanotechnology at the University of Twente, the Netherlands. Its activities are focused on its core invention: Flexiramics, a pure ceramic microfiber material that is light and flexible like paper but retains the main physiochemical properties of traditional ceramics. The most important properties of ceramics are heat resistance, electrical insulation and resistance to corrosion.

All Flexiramics products have the unique and novel characteristics of being pure ceramic and being flexible at the same time. The level of flexibility, density, the ceramic composition and the geometry of the material are just some of the parameters that can be customized based on our client’s needs. Flexiramics material shows strong potential as a versatile substrate reinforcement in electronics and semiconductors, while also offering effective
filtration media functionalities for separation and purification. It is suitable for catalytic support and structural reinforcement, enhancing strength and
performance in various industrial applications.
Furthermore, the material can serve as an efficient battery separator in energy storage and as a thermal interface material for both insulation and
heat management solutions.

Name: Volt14 Solutions
Description:
Volt14 is an advanced battery materials startup developing ultra-high
capacity electrode materials for lithium-ion batteries.

By developing turnkey technologies for battery cell producers globally, we enable breakthrough advancements by leveraging the scale and maturity of existing production processes.

Name: Nano Catalytics
Description:
Nano Catalytics changes how we cure, polymerize, especially in molding and large objects delivering on-demand curing that simplified and eliminates equipment, accelerates the mold and molding process from weeks and months to hours and days and delivers parts in moments without the complexity, slow speeds of 3D printing.

Developing and commercializing highly unique platform surface, bulk curing and bulk reactive chemistry technologies revolutionizing wide ranging industries from medicinal chemistry delivery to an injection molding revolution to high speed, ultra low energy composite and 3D printing cures.

Actively developing investment, government, university, supply and commercial partnerships that support rapid deployment and impact.

Name: Nano Brasil
Description: The perception of the necessity to align the availability of nanotechnology already under study and use worldwide, to brazillian companies, inspired this group on focusing their efforts on identifying the best viable opportunities for immediate application and, thus, created this company, which aims to be a nanotechnological hub in Brazil.

Name: Plasmagear Inc
Description: PlasmaGear is a nanofiber roll material manufacturer.  Our patented technologies allow us to make scalable surface engineered nanofiber membranes.  Our company sell or process nonwoven membranes or develop formulations through contract manufacturing for different organizations.  We're proud to offer products that contribute to both individual well-being and environmental responsibility.

Name: Ceylon Graphene Technologies (Pvt) Ltd
Description:
Ceylon Graphene Technologies (CGT), a joint venture between the LOLC Group and the Sri Lanka Institute of Nanotechnology (SLINTEC), is Sri Lanka's first graphene and advanced material company and a national laboratory. 
CGT is the most competitive graphene manufacturer, product developer and researcher in the world due to the complete product life cycle

Name: MTS sa
Description: Active à La Chaux-de-Fonds depuis 2007, notre entreprise participe à la valorisation des surfaces de l’habillage horloger. Notre procédé exclusif INORYS permettant d'obtenir un acier durci, s’inscrit dans une chaîne logistique orientée vers la conservation durable de la valeur esthétique et des propriétés physiques du produit. Nos compétences, nos moyens et notre expérience permettent de mettre en place des concepts d’industrialisation personnalisés et de maîtriser les étapes de sous-traitance.

Name: ICS - Innovative Coating Solutions
Description: ICS is a spin-off of University of Namur, located in Wallonia, in the center of Belgium, with unique expertise in nanotechnology, plasma techniques and numerical modelling. We proudly put our 30-years academic and industrial experience surface treatment to the service of our clients, providing consulting and services in Physical Vapor Deposition as well as surface characterization and machine design. Our Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) proprietary processes include a variety of vacuum deposition methodes used for producing coatings answering very specific needs and providing unique optical, electronic and chemical features. We help our clients developing and producing high tech applications with our green and sustainable technology. We are flexible, and we put high emphasis on customer involvement/collaboration.

Name: 3photon
Description:
3photon (three-photon) stands in front of optical coatings and polishing facilities by design and commerce of laser & non-linear crystals and optical components.

Both facilities are located in Vilnius, Lithuania and successfully employed to work side by side, this partnership allows to process various hardness and sensitive crystals with high laser damage threshold like LBO, BBO, DKDP, AgGaS2, Cr:ZnSe, Cr: CdSe, ZGP, Nd:YAG, Nd:YLF and others.

Optical coatings such as mirrors, beamsplitters or more complex like polarizers and variable reflection coatings can be deposited as well.

Highly experienced team merges technical competencies and capabilities of key stages of laser optical component production: starting from optical glass & crystal polishing moving to optical coating process employing optimal coating technology.

Name: DSI-Mandela Nanomedicine Platform
Description:
The DSI-NMU Nanomedicine platform focuses on the synthesis of nanoparticles to enhance the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of communicable and non-communicable diseases. We are located at the Nelson Mandela University Medical School on the Missionvale campus. Our research focus areas are the synthesis and manufacture of nanotechnology-based pharmaceuticals, blood transfusion products, sensors, and air purification technologies.

MISSION STATEMENTS

To lead in the emerging field of nanomedicine by developing smart nanotechnologies that improve health and quality of life.

To work with postgraduate students, researchers, and clinicians in an interdisciplinary research environment to develop cutting-edge nanomedicine research that make a positive impact on society and help address Sustainable Development Goals

To train the next generation of leaders in nanomedicine, and support diversity and gender equality.

To raise public awareness and inform the scientific community, the public and policy makers about our research findings and the potential of nanomedicine.

Name: Neuralink Group
Description:
Mission: Link Civilisations. 

Neuralink Group is a subsidiary of Khamsa Group of Businesses (KGB) based in Phnom Penh Capital of The Kingdom of Cambodia.

Name: Veillenanos
Description:
AVICENN, association d'intérêt général, exerce une veille citoyenne et propose sur le site http://veillenanos.fr une information transversale et indépendante sur les enjeux sociétaux soulevés par les nanos. 
Elle agit pour davantage de transparence et de vigilance dans le domaine nano.

Name: magtec coating solutions
Description:
Magnetron Technology – sputtering specialist for highend PVD coatings

MagTec GmbH located in Ellerstadt near Ludwigshafen, Germany, is based on fifteen years of unique experience in the field of custom PVD-coating by magnetron sputtering technologies under clean room conditions.

Our specialists offer a broad coating knowhow in various fields of science and technology in the areas of optics and semiconductor industry, space technology and neutron physics.

Our techniques include DC-, RF- and reactive magnetron sputtering of a large variety of materials on different substrates including glass, metal (-foils) and silicon wafers surfaces.

We are experts in magnetic field simulations and design, construct and manufacture customized magnetron sputter sources for complex geometries.

Coating analysis technologies like XRD, XRR, GID and transmission/reflectivity measurements are used in our lab.

Name: Technoorg Linda Co. Ltd.
Description:
Technoorg Linda specializes in designing and manufacturing advanced sample preparation equipment for electron microscopy, serving industries ranging from semiconductors, materials science, and nanotechnology to aerospace, automotive, and beyond.

With over 30 years of expertise, our brand is synonymous with precision, reliability, and innovation. Recognized globally by the scientific community, we continually push the boundaries of ion beam and mechanical preparation technologies to meet the ever-evolving demands of researchers.

Driven by a spirit of collaboration, we are committed to empowering scientists worldwide with tools and expertise that inspire breakthroughs.

Name: Hacettepe University Nanotechnology and Nanomedicine Division
Description: The graduate program in Nanotechnology and Nanomedicine is a joint interdisciplinary program. The program is open to the students of undergraduate degree from physical and/or life sciences, engineering, medicine and pharmacy departments.

Name: Microsaic Systems
Description: We serve Human & Environmental Health with online, at-line and front-line workflows for therapeutics, diagnostics, manufacturing, & environmental epidemiology

Name: nanoFAB
Description: An open access centre for researchers and industry develop new technologies through building and seeing at the smallest scales. The nanoFAB focuses on providing training, fee for service and collaboration opportunities in micro and nano scale fabrication and characterization. With over $100M in fabrication and characterization capabilities, the nanoFAB supports over 700 unique users from 120 academic research groups and 50 companies annually in their research, development, prototyping and manufacturing needs.

Name: IONTOF
Description:
IONTOF is a manufacturer of innovative instruments for surface analysis with different product lines for time-of-flight secondary ion mass spectrometry (TOF-SIMS) and high-sensitivity low-energy ion scattering (LEIS).
Founded as a classical spin-off of the University of Münster, IONTOF has meanwhile become the technological leader in the field of TOF-SIMS and LEIS instrumentation.
The company was founded by Prof. Alfred Benninghoven, Dr. Ewald Niehuis and Mr. Thomas Heller in 1989 to commercialise the original research carried out by Prof. Benninghoven and his team at the University of Muenster in Germany from the early 80's. Since the technique became commercially viable IONTOF has made many product improvements and more than 400 instruments are in operation in industrial and academic laboratories worldwide.
Today, the IONTOF group consists of four different companies. The IONTOF GmbH, which sells, produces and services all IONTOF analytical instruments. The IONTOF Technologies GmbH which is in charge of all R&D projects. The subsidiaries IONTOF USA and IONTOF Japan which were founded for sales and after sales service in the United States (founded 2000) and Japan (founded 2022).

IONTOF GmbH
Heisenbergstr.15
48149 Münster
Germany
www.iontof.com

Amtsgericht Münster, HRB 10680

Dr. Ewald Niehuis

Name: Physical Electronics GmbH
Description:
Physical Electronics GmbH is an owner-run company supporting its customers since 1994. We offer innovative analysis tools from the world's leading companies, analytical services and customer support.

Our Products:

- AES, XPS and TOF-SIMS from Physical Electronics; USA
- 3D micro and nano X-Ray CT from RX Solutions; France 
- 3D X-Ray CT Benchtop Systems from Neoscan; Belgium
- Low-Voltage Electron Microscopes (LVEM) from Delong Instruments; Czech Republic
- Recycling Preparative HPLC and Curie point Pyrolyzer from JAI; Japan
- Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy (LIBS) from Lightigo; Czech Republic
- Raman Spectroscopy from Timegate Instruments; Finland
- In Situ Atomic Force Microscopy from NenoVision; Czech Republic
- Atomic Force Microscopy Stand-alone Systems from ICSPI; Canada
- Product Introduction with NanoX-Perience Seminar Series 

Our Analytical Services:

- Fast and easy access to our surface science instruments 
- Support of an analytical expert
- Reliable outsourcing of your analytical questions
- Education with NanoX-Pert Seminar Series

Our Customer Support:

- Certified engineers
- Fast response time
- Spare and loaner stock in Munich

Summary:
We believe the most efficient way is a personal discussion. Feel free to contact us. 
We are here to support you and we are passionate to work with our customers.

Name: Cambrios Technologies Corporation
Description: Cambrios is the leader in silver nanowire solutions to enable the development of electronic devices with transparent conductors.  Our proprietary nanostructured materials can be deposited using existing production equipment to achieve enhanced performance of display devices and components at lower manufacturing cost.  ClearOhm, our first product, is a directly patternable, wet-processable transparent conductive film made from silver nanowires that is poised to replace the industry standard sputtered indium tin oxide (ITO).  Subsequent products will leverage this technology to produce other functional films for display and thin film applications for multiple consumer electronic device markets.

Name: My Nano Academy
Description:
My Nano Academy is the digital platform that links professionals and organizations focused on the research and development of nanomaterials in Latin America. Through marketing and business intelligence tools, we seek to publicize innovative projects in professional networks to boost their launches and development. 

Here you will find interviews and articles related to the worldwide development of the nanotechnology. Moreover, we will add information about the importance of the implementation of this science in productive processes.

Name: Nano Center Indonesia
Description: NCI is a start-up venture on non-digital science-tech based business incubation. In the last 5 years, NCI has established several tech start-up with different foci : nanotechnology, herbal, advanced materials, energy&environment, etc (details in center.nano.or.id).

Name: Sunchem
Description:
We provide Nano Leach & Nano Filters for the Precision Separation of Critical Metals from waste and wastewater.

Sunchem is a nanotechnology organization based out of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Our mission is to develop leaching formulations & Nano Filters for precision separation of critical metals from complex water mixtures to replace inadequate separation technologies in high-value markets such as water purification, energy, and critical metal extraction. Water contamination resulting from extractive operations and manufacturing systems is a worldwide problem, but Sunchem’s Nano Filter technology can provide industries with a cost-effective and energy-efficient approach to improving water quality while also capturing value from their waste streams. This technology has the potential to revolutionize how critical metals are sourced and purified while it protects downstream communities from toxic water pollution.

Name: Nectar Chemical & Bio Fertilizers
Description: Nectar Chemical & Bio Fertilizers ( Nectar Groups Of Companies) is India’s one of the fast growing group aiming to give quality products to Indian farmers. The Company is into Import & Export , Manufacturing & Marketing of Insecticides, Bio pesticides, Fungicide, fertilizers, Micro-nutrients, all kinds of special PGRs, specialty agro Chemicals and 100% water soluble fertilizers,

Name: Everix Optical Filters
Description:
WHAT WE DO
Everix produces the world's thinnest optical filters. Customers use our filters for applications where traditional filters are a too bulky, too rigid, or too expensive to produce at scale.

OUR COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE
Protected by 34 patents, our unique process produces thin-film interference filters that are 20x thinner (0.05 - 0.5 mm) and 60x lighter than other filters. Customers can use them with glass or plastic for to achieve the precise flexibility or rigidity they need. And our manufacturing process can scale to deliver millions of filters at competitive prices.  

CUSTOMERS
We serve companies of all sizes, including 10% of the Fortune 50.

USE CASES
Customers use Everix for applications where traditional filters are too bulky, too rigid, or too expensive to produce at scale. Our initial areas of focus include:

> Portable and wearable healthcare devices and biosensors
> AR/VR/MR
> LiDAR and similar IoT sensors for smart devices, robots, drones, and more

Name: Blue Nano
Description: Blue Nano is a nanomaterials manufacturer that develops high quality, cost effective and reliable nano-focused industrial solutions in the highest volumes available anywhere. We serve universities, independent research labs and OEM manufacturers in a wide variety of sectors ranging from automotive to energy to healthcare.

Name: Cello Therapeutics, Inc.
Description:
Cello Therapeutics, Inc. (Cello) was founded in April 2016 and specializes in the development of nanoparticles for cancer treatment. Our nanoparticles are composed of FDA-approved cancer drugs loaded within a biocompatible polymer core. These nanoparticles are then coated with natural red blood cell membrane, a recent breakthrough technology in the field of nanomedicine. Appearing as red blood cells to the body, these membrane-coated nanoparticles are able to avoid evoking an immune response while circulating in the bloodstream for an extended period of time for improved drug delivery to tumor sites. With this novel approach to cancer treatment, our products offer unique performance advantages for oncological applications. Currently, the company is in the drug development and testing stage.

Cello Therapeutics, Inc. licenses technology and laboratory protocols for nanoparticle development from its parent company, Arytha Biosciences, LLC. Cello also benefits from the efforts of its sibling company, Cellics Therapeutics, Inc., which specializes in developing nanoparticles for biodetoxification and the treatment of infectious diseases, and provides engineering expertise in membrane-coating technology.

Name: REWIRE-IKC
Description:
REWIRE is an Innovation and Knowledge Centre (IKC) funded by the Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC) and Innovate UK, both part of UK Research and Innovation, looking to develop pioneering semiconductor technologies and new electronic devices to accelerate the UK’s net zero ambitions. 
Our mission is to become the go-to centre of excellence nationally and internationally for next-generation power electronics by combining research excellence with technology translation and innovation, driven by industrial engagement and collaboration. 
We are aiming to transform the next generation of high voltage electronic wide and ultra-wide band gap compound semiconductor devices into the technologies of tomorrow, delivering new products to the market and economic growth for the UK semiconductor sector. The IKC will foster a collaborative, inclusive and innovative environment to nurture the next generation of semiconductor materials scientists and engineers.
The REWIRE program is led by world-leading academic teams at the University of Bristol, the University of Cambridge and the University of Warwick, and has been co-created with industry partners across the breadth of the semiconductor supply chain. By combining academic expertise and capability with industry insights, we aim to reduce risks and accelerate the development of semiconductor technologies.

Name: MINT Lab - Université d'Angers
Description: The MINT lab' (UMR INSERM U1066 and CNRS 6021) consists of 40 researchers and students. Our research: the design of nano and micro-scaled vectors for the delivery of drugs.

Name: School of Nano Science and Technology (SNST), IIT Kharagpur
Description:
The School of Nano Science and Technology stands at the forefront of interdisciplinary research, unlocking the transformative potential of nanoscale phenomena. By integrating principles from physics, chemistry, biology, and engineering, the school fosters a dynamic environment where fundamental science meets real-world application. Our mission is to explore and manipulate matter at the atomic and molecular levels to address pressing global challenges in energy, healthcare, electronics, and materials science.

Equipped with cutting-edge infrastructure, our laboratories feature high-resolution electron and atomic force microscopes, nanofabrication units, and advanced spectroscopic and imaging tools. These facilities enable precise synthesis, characterization, and functionalization of nanomaterials, supporting research in areas such as drug delivery, regenerative medicine, nanoelectronics, and sustainable technologies.

We invite students, researchers, and industry collaborators to engage in pioneering projects that push the boundaries of innovation. Whether you're interested in designing smart biomaterials, developing nanosensors, or engineering quantum devices, the School provides a fertile ground for exploration and discovery. Through rigorous academic programs and hands-on research, we aim to cultivate the next generation of scientists and technologists who will shape the future of nanoscience.

Name: STS-Elionix
Description: STS-Elionix is a partnership that delivers state-of-the-art nanotechnology products throughout the North America and Europe.  Since 1975, Elionix has been manufacturing, installing, and servicing Electron Beam Lithography (EBL) systems. During that time, Elionix has sold over 400 EBL systems to research institutes and industrial customers. The Elionix electron beam lithography tools are known for ultra-high precision to fabricate small nano-structures, excellent reliability, small footprint, and low cost of ownership.

Name: Optogan Group
Description:
Optogan Group is one of technology leaders developing and producing High Brightness Light Emitting Diodes (HB LEDs) for global applications including Solid State Lighting. Optogan’s geography includes R&D operation in Helsinki, Finland and Dortmund, Germany and 2 production facilities: Landshut, Germany and St. Petersburg, Russia. 

Today Optogan is ramping up high-volume manufacturing fab in St.Petersburg, Russia after acquiring a facility from Elcoteq SE in mid 2010. The operation will be launched in several phases starting end of 2010. 

Since 2009 Optogan is a group of vertically integrated companies acting on all levels of LED luminaire production. Optogan develops and produces chips, high brightness LEDs, LED lamps and luminaries office, residential, industrial and outdoor lighting applications.

The Optogan group was dissolved in 2015. The rights to the Optogan brand have now been acquired by a Spanish America investment group, which focuses on the development and research of new lighting technologies.

info@optogan.com

Name: NANOSMAT
Description: The NANOSMAT Society (TNS) has been set up to serve the needs of the NANO community. TNS is designed to provide an effective and stimulating platform for international people to foster, develop and promote communication, education, networking, dissemination of knowledge, research and innovations in all aspects of nanoscience and nanotechnology.

Name: HONGWU INTERNATIONAL GROUP LTD
Description:
HONGWU INTERNATIONAL GROUP LTD is a collectivize technology company, which is professional manufacturer and supplier for nano-sized powders, nano dispersions, nanowires, nanoparticles, nano oxide powders and nano compound materials in small quantity for researchers and in bulk order for industry groups. Business involved all kinds of aspects: manufacturing, research, development, processing, supplying and after-sales service.

We provide their customers with:

High quality nanoparticles, nanopowders, and nanofibers
Volume pricing
Reliable service and technical assistance

Our website:
https://www.hwnanomaterial.com

Name: Imagia
Description: Imagia designs and manufactures high-performance optical lenses based on metamaterial principles with an emphasis on performance and scalability. We provide solutions for advanced display systems like AR/VR and lightfield displays. Imagia lenses enable new possibilities in optical design in an ultra-thin and completely flat form factor. We are at the cutting edge of commercializing metamaterial technology.

Name: METİSTEC
EVŌQ Nano
Description:
EVŌQ Nano is a nanoscience company that engineers novel nanoparticles for the life, materials, and textile science industries. The company’s multi-patented, high-volume laser nanofabrication process creates uniform, sub-10 nm nanoparticles with distinct surface chemistry. These properties represent a significant advancement in nanoscience with the potential for a wide range of applications.  
 
EVŌQ Nano is currently focused on developing nanoparticles for 3 core industries:   
 
EVŌQ Bio has developed an inhaled therapeutic using EVQ-218 for the treatment of pulmonary bacterial infections in patients with cystic fibrosis. The company recently completed a successful Pre-Investigational New Drug (pre-IND) meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and is confidently advancing toward Phase I clinical trials. 
 
EVŌQ MedTech integrates antimicrobial capabilities into medical devices to defend against a broad spectrum of contaminants like bacteria and fungi. The team is collaborating with leading catheter manufacturers with the aim of curbing healthcare-associated infections (HAIs), impacting more than 1 million hospital patients annually. 
 
FUZE Technologies serves the textile, hospitality, fitness, and workspace industries. Its EPA-approved antimicrobial technology is free of chemicals, toxicity, and environmental waste. FUZE is working with some of the world’s most notable brands, including Nike, Adidas, the New England Patriots, Marriott, and Hilton.

Name: INTERCIENTÍFICA
Description:
A INTERCIENTIFICA é uma empresa nacional que, que há mais de quinze anos se dedica ao desenvolvimento e aplicação de um novo conceito na realização de ensaios laboratoriais relacionados à área diagnóstica e pesquisa em saúde, sempre implementando sua tecnologia em estudos comparativos aplicados em rotina.

Disponibilizando em sua linha de produtos NeoMAP, NeoLISA, a INTERCIENTIFICA utiliza a tecnologia xMAP, que tem como principal característica a realização de múltiplas análises, detectando e quantificando até 100 ensaios simultâneos em uma mesma amostra.

Visualizando a necessidade única de cada cliente, a INTERCIENTIFICA disponibiliza uma equipe de profissionais renomados no Brasil e no exterior a desenvolver um painel multiplex pronto a atender as mais exigentes pesquisas trazendo soluções de precisão inigualáveis, reduzindo custos e tempo com a mais alta qualidade.

Name: Irradiant Technologies
Description: We create novel optical/photonic components made  uniquely possible by our 3D nano fabrication platform to create solutions for sensing, imaging, telecommunications, computation, and display.

Name: TEKNO TIP
Description:
2003 yılından beri , Nanoteknolojik Ar-Ge çalışmaları ve Laboratuvar cihazları alanında tecrübemiz ve bilgi birikimimizle Türkiye pazarında hizmet vermekteyiz.


Dünya çapında , alanında en iyi ürünlere sahip temsilciliklerimizle , çağdaş bir laboratuvarın sahip olması gereken kalitede ileri teknoloji ürünlerini müşterilerimize sunmaktayız.


Müşterilerimize ,en kaliteli ürünleri en uygun fiyatlarla sunmak ve satış sonrası gerekli desteği sağlamak olarak belirlediğimiz rotada, daima en kaliteli ürünleri içeren ürün portföyü oluşturmaya çalıştık ve çalışıyoruz.


Kurulduğu günden bu yana giderek büyüyen firmamız bugün Türkiye’nin birçok kurumunda , gelişmiş araştırma laboratuvarları için hizmet vermektedir.


TEKNOTIP ANALİTİK SİSTEMLER LTD.ŞTİ. zengin ve kaliteli ürün portföyü, lojistik ve teknik desteği, uzman kadroları, proje çözümleri ile sektörel pazarında yıllardır liderliğini korumuştur.

Name: CN Technical Services Ltd
Description:
Trusted Distributors of Industry Leading Scientific Instrumentation.

CN Tech specialises in Metrology Instrumentation, Mechanical and Tribological Testing, Electron Microscopy, Surface Analysis, Environmental Isolation, Gloveboxes, Sample Preparation, and Laboratory Equipment and Consumables.

Founded in 2009, CN Tech’s focused and experienced team is dedicated to finding the right solution for customers in UK and beyond. With many years of experience in the fields of metrology and nanoscience, our technical specialists can help provide imaging and measurement solutions for a wide range of academic and industrial needs.

We pride ourselves in providing a complete package of analysis and testing solutions. Whatever the application, our skilled engineers can offer new insight in imaging and metrology.

Name: Functional Materials and Energy Devices Research Group
Description: The Functional Materials and Energy Devices group (FMED) led by Dr Mojtaba Abdi-Jalebi researches the discovery of novel energy materials and advanced energy devices based on low-temperature processable molecular semiconductors and hybrid organic-inorganic heterostructures between such organic semiconductors and inorganic semiconductors, such as metal halide perovskites, metal oxides and nanoparticles. These functional materials are of growing interest for a range of emerging technological applications, including low-cost, large area photovoltaics, energy-efficient light-emitters, electrochemical devices and other energy applications and flexible electronics. Our research aims to discover and incorporate new inexpensive functional materials in energy devices to alter the energy landscape by reducing the cost of energy production, consumption, and storage.

Name: CARBOMEX
Description: CARBOMEX es una empresa mexicana dedicada al diseño, fabricación y caracterización de nanomateriales, para su aplicación en diversos productos industriales. Estamos enfocados en incrementar la propuesta de valor de nuestros clientes con investigación, desarrollo, innovación, capacitación y consultoría, con el propósito de llevar la Nanotecnología a la vida diaria en México y en el mundo.

Custom Vacuum Systems, Retrofits, Upgrades and Process Engineering for the Thin Film Vacuum Coating Industry

GP Plasma solves complex functional surface material problems from design to manufacture. Let our years of trials and tribulations in the thin film coatings industry guide you through the pitfalls. 
Using a holistic approach to application problems, we’ll arrive at the best solution for your manufacturing. 

Through our network of specialists, we offer speedy development and agility.

At the heart of innovation is collaboration, we’ll work together with you to find a solid path to success.

Name: Mapsi Photonics
Description:
Mapsi Photonics is a nanotechnology based spin-off company from Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya. We are speciallized in mid infrared photonics for gas and biochemical sensing using an unique patented silicon platform. 

Our goal is to provide competitive mid infrared silicon photonic devices for industry and research institutions.

Name: NanoKorp
Description:
Somos una empresa dedicada a la comercialización y consultoría especializada en maquinaria y equipo de producción y caracterización de nanomateriales; fundada por profesionales expertos en el área de las nanociencias tanto en investigación como en producción.  Nuestro principal objetivo es brindar equipos de la más alta calidad, así como todo el acompañamiento desde su selección hasta el mantenimiento a través de proyectos integrales abriendo las puertas a la transferencia tecnológica a nuevos productos.

To be part of NINE means to share all these values and to be ready to explore new applicative frontiers, towards a real and positive impact on society.

Visit our site to collaborate with us!

Name: Alphaion
Description:
Alphaion is your trusted partner in innovating and delivering tailored printed electrochemical sensor-based technologies, either by supplying standard products or by executing confined research and development on electrochemical sensors, biosensors, and printed electronics on a customized basis for range of  electrochemical sensing application.

Key Offerings: SPE's for electrochemical detection, Modified SPE's, 2D & 3D electrodes, Array sensors for multi-substance analysis, Disposable sensors for medical diagnostics, Printed electronics, Circuits, Wearable sensors, Patches and Customized R&D alongside standard products.

Welcome to GraphenePioneer, where innovation meets sustainability. As a leading company in the field of graphene technology, we specialize in integrating this extraordinary material into the build environment, revolutionizing how we think about construction, energy efficiency, and environmental sustainability.

At GraphenePioneer, our core focus is developing and implementing graphene-based solutions. Our groundbreaking product, QuantumPlaster, exemplifies our commitment to sustainable building practices. By enhancing concrete with graphene, we create a stronger, more energy-efficient, and environmentally friendly construction material.

Name: VitroTEM
Description: Vitrotem offers an easy-to-use automated system for the fabrication of graphene liquid cells (GLCs). Fabricated on a standard TEM grid, GLCs allow a researcher to record transmission electron microscopy on liquid samples in a conventional TEM system - without the need for vitrification or the use of special sample holders. GLCs enable dynamic TEM imaging of whole biological cells as well as single molecules and nanoparticles in liquid at unprecedented resolution.

Name: Proto9 Materials
Description:
Proto9 Materials is an innovation driven enterprise of the SAMTA Group. The SAMTA group is working towards creating equilibrium between humanity and environment. Our companies are already making a visible impact in the fields of renewable energy (#Samta Energy), sustainable agriculture (#Gencrest), responsible mining (Samta Mines), blockchain solutions (#Infinichains), and equitable nurturing of human minds (#Samta Foundation). 

Proto9 is committed to create disruptive materials that improve energy utilization, conserve natural resources, and preserve human health. We develop industrial product lines that combine a high sustainability quotient with superior functional performance.

Our researchers utilize the insights from nanosciences and advanced materials to create innovative industrial feedstock for textiles, agriculture, energy storage, and specialty chemicals.

Name: Nadir Plasma & Polymers
Description:
Nadir is a SME established in 2008 by a group of researchers that worked for several years as scientific consultants in the technological transfer field between academia and industries.

Nadir  business is focused in the development of an innovative atmospheric plasma technology and in the development of advanced polymer composites.

The company has applied for an international patent related to the new plasma jet device now emerging in the manufacturing field and in the biomedical field for adhesion promotion surface treatments and functional coatings deposition.

Nadir’s Plasma Lab is equipped with different plasma power supplies and devices, ranging from KHz to MHz generators, and with all the electrical characterization equipment needed for the related developments.

Nadir’s Polymer Lab is equipped with a double twin screw extruder for the study of innovative melt compounding processes, where new generations of active fillers are used for the advanced polymer composites production.

Regional, national and European founds have supported Nadir projects. 

The company is now involved in the H2020 European project FAST  ("Functionally Graded Additive Manufacturing Scaffolds by Hybrid Manufacturing" NMP07, GA n. 685825,  http://project-fast.eu/en/home ). A new Hybrid 3D printing technology for implants manufacturing will be developed by integrating both Nadir’s technologies: a new active  polymer nanocomposite for 3D printers, and an innovative plasma module for surface functionalization of the printed polymer.

Description:
European School On Nanosciences & Nanotechnologies, Grenoble, France.
ESONN is a two-week course aimed at providing training for graduate students, postdoctoral and junior scientists in the field of nanosciences and nanotechnologies. The academic and practical courses cover topics such as the elaboration, characterization and functionalities of nano-objects.

Description: Potomac Photonics, a leader microfabrication, has an extensive history of helping its clients develop miniature products and bring them to market. Name: Microsol

Name: EIPBN
Description: The International Conference on Electron, Ion and Photon Beam Technology and Nanofabrication (EIPBN), affectionately known as "3-Beams," is the premier gathering of scientists and engineers who are dedicated to electron, ion and photon lithography, imaging, and analysis; atomically precise fabrication; nanofabrication process technologies and related emerging technologies; and their applications in a broad spectrum of fields. In its 65th meeting, researchers from academia, government laboratories, and industry from around the world meet to present and discuss recent and future trends in these technologies.

Name: IEEE NANO Conferences
Description: IEEE NANO is the flagship conference series of the IEEE Nanotechnology Council (NTC). It promotes advanced research in nanoscience and nanotechnology.

Name: Invisible-Light Labs GmbH
Description: At Invisible-Light Labs, we strive to make a positive impact on the world we live in by enabling ground breaking scientific discoveries. We do so by providing radically new IR sensors based on nanomechanical sensing with incomparable sensitivity at room temperature, from NIR to THz. Applications include improved disease understanding, better drugs, increased safety and more efficient protection of the environment.

Name: Nanobiosensors and Bioanalytical Applications group (NanoB2A)
Description:
WHO WE ARE…
The NanoB2A group is a multidisciplinary research team led by Prof. Laura M. Lechuga. We are physicists, engineers, chemists, and biologists working in the broad field of Nanobiotechnology. Our motivation is to develop new and cutting-edge biosensor nanotechnologies using photonics, one of the key technologies of the 21st century, to promote and boost healthcare and well-being for our society.
20220923 Fotografi╠üas Corporativas Icn2 55 Lq
WHAT WE DO…
Our research focuses on the development of photonic point-of-care biosensors and their employment in clinical, biomedical, and environmental areas. We mainly exploit Nanoplasmonics and Silicon Nanophotonics technologies to accomplish integrated biosensing systems for the label-free, real-time, and multiplexed analysis at the point of care. Our activity encompasses the entire process of generating new biosensor devices, including fundamental research and theoretical design, nanofabrication, surface biofunctionalization, lab-on-a-chip integration, and their final validation in real scenarios.

Name: 2DSPIN-TECH
Description:
2DSPIN-TECH is an Horizon Europe funded project, that aims to significantly reduce energy consumption, promote sustainability, and improve the overall performance of computer memory. It is vitally important for the future use of information technology.
2DSPIN-TECH will develop a new energy-efficient spintronic memory device platform based on emerging atomically-thin two-dimensional (2D) quantum materials for the next generation of memory technologies. The 2D topological spin-orbit materials can generate a giant current-induced spin polarization, whereas room temperature 2D magnets provide the prospective of electric control of magnetism.
To achieve this game changer for computer memory, 2DSPIN-TECH bring together pioneering and world-leading experimental and theoretical researchers and a company in the field of spintronics and 2D materials in Europe. It is a consortium of seven partners from five different countries: Chalmers University of Technology, Chalmers Industriteknik, Universität Regensburg, University of Jena, Budapest University of Technology and Economics, The University of Manchester and HQ Graphene.
2D SPIN-TECH is involved in the GrapheneEU CSA project, under the Graphene Flagship Initiative. Watch us create a new generation of memories.

Name: Resysten International Ltd
Description:
Resysten is a photocatalytic coating system based on a uniquely developed chemical solution. 

The system uses light as a catalyst to achieve titanium-dioxides’ hygienic effects. The coating system - thanks to our recent researches - integrates into the surface on a molecular level therefore it cannot be removed by conventional cleaning methods and remains active continuously for up to 1 year. 

These unique properties of our system result in a surface, which keeps its long-term continuous effect, protecting the users and the environment from contaminations.

Name: NBD Nanotechnologies
Description: We functionalize and create new molecular structures to enhance surface properties for a variety of materials. Description:

Name: POLIMA - Center for Polariton-driven Light-Matter Interactions
Description:
Enabled by concerted efforts from fundamental theory, nano- and quantum-optics experiments, low-dimensional material synthesis, advanced nanofabrication, and atomic-scale material characterization, Center for Polariton-driven Light–Matter Interactions—POLIMA—embraces a curiosity-driven exploration with new paradigms intersecting quantum optics and polaritonic matter. 

The center is lead by professor N. Asger Mortensen and with participation of several key investigators, including professors Joel D. Cox, Christian Wolff, and Christos Tserkezis.

POLIMA is a DNRF Center of Excellence at the University of Southern Denmark (SDU), being funded by a grant from the Danish National Research Foundation (8 M€, Project No. DNRF165).

Additional funding is provided by a number of personal grants, including the VILLUM Investigator program of Mortensen (5.3 M€, Grant No. 16498) from the VILLUM Foundation, and the Sapere Aude research leader grant of Cox (800 k€, Grant no. 0165-00051B) from the Independent Research Fund Denmark.

POLIMA has access to ~500 m2 office and lab space, including state-of-the-art fascilities for near and far-field optical spectroscopy, scanning near-field optical microscopy (Neaspec SNOM), cathodoluminescense spectroscopy (Delmic CL), and low-temperature characterization of quantum emitters.

Name: Poradigm Technologies
Description:
Porous liquids are a new class of liquid materials that contain microscopic cavities (pores), each the size of a single molecule. They contain up to 10 000 times the number of cavities that are found in conventional liquids, and up to around 20% of the liquid is actually empty space.

Thanks to these cavities, porous liquids can absorb large amounts of gas and they can be tuned to selectively absorb one gas over another — as for porous solids, which are widely applied in industry. However, a critical advantage over porous solids is that porous liquids can also flow through pipes, meaning that they can be applied in a host of different ways.

We will help to solve your company’s problems and realise new opportunities using our team’s expertise in porous liquids and related materials combined with our industry knowledge and commercial experience. We provide solution-focused consulting, research and development, and technology licensing.

Name: Synano - advanced liquid cooling
Description:
How industrial cooling has changed. 

As computer chips, batteries, and other energy systems get more powerful, overheating slows performance, increases energy costs, and wears out equipment faster. At Synano, we engineer nano-particle suspensions for cooling fluids and dielectric oils, improving heat-transfer efficiency by up to another 10-20% creating a "red-bull effect" for liquid-cooled systems.

In liquid-cooled data centers, it keeps racks cooler, prevents hotspots, and allows for generating more revenue per rack. In batteries and EVs, nano-enhanced battery coolants reduce overheating, extend battery life, and improve charging efficiency. In CHP and industrial cooling nano-enhanced engine coolants recover more heat and reduce wear on equipment. In solar PVT nano heat-transfer fluids draw out more warmth, raise outlet temperatures, and increase both electricity and heat yields from existing panels.

If your operations rely on water glycol coolants or cooling oils and would like to decrease your energy costs and improve equipment performance within single-phase specialty heat-transfer heating and cooling systems with one stone, contact us to see how much value you can unlock by upgrading your loops with safe and non-toxic nanotechnology-enhanced cooling chemicals for industrial fluids, within your direct liquid cooling or immersion cooling setup.

Get more info:
https://synano.eu/

Name: Industrial Sonomechanics, LLC
Description:
ISM is a research & development, equipment design and process consulting firm, specializing in scalable high-intensity ultrasonic technology. Our main focus is on the production of nanoemulsions, nanocrystals, and liposomes for the pharmaceutical, cosmetic, medical cannabis, and food industries. Other target applications include viscous liquid degassing, cell disruption, particle deagglomeration/dispersing, and plant oil extraction. Our mission is to help businesses optimize these ultrasound-assisted processes and implement them in commercial-scale production 

One of the most common application of our high-amplitude ultrasonic processors is the production of food- and pharmaceutical-grade cannabinoid nanoemulsions on laboratory, bench and industrial scales. See how in this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S9xg8_gxvLY

Benefits of the ultrasonic production of cannabis oil nanoemulsions include:
- Make water-soluble CBD and THC nanoemulsions with enhanced bioavailability and rapid onset of action.
- Ensure precise and reproducible therapeutic dosing.
- Infuse water with a strong dose of cannabinoids while retaining optical clarity.
- Achieve cannabinoid content of up to 50 mg/ml.

High ultrasonic amplitudes able to generate intense acoustic cavitation are required for most processes. Unlike conventional ultrasonic devices, ISM's patented BHUT makes it possible to produce extremely high ultrasonic amplitudes at any scale, from laboratory to industrial, guaranteeing reproducible results after scale-up. Our experienced consultants will be glad to discuss your process and implement it in your production environment.

Name: Deutsche Vakuum-Gesellschaft e.V.
Description:
Vakuum, insbesondere aber die Vakuumtechnik, ist für verschiedenste Industrien und für weite Teile der Forschung und Entwicklung unverzichtbar. Aber auch  in vielen Bereichen des täglichen Lebens spielt Vakuumtechnik eine zentrale Rolle. So würde es ohne die Vakuumtechnik viele Selbstverständlichkeiten unseres modernen Lebens schlicht nicht geben. Die vakuumgestützten Wissenschaften und Technologien, deren Förderung wir uns zur Aufgabe gemacht haben, sind also keine Nische, sondern haben eine wahrhaft umfassende Bedeutung für unsere Gesellschaft. Das Bewusstsein dafür im Interesse der Weiterentwicklung der vakuumgestützten Wissenschaften und Technologien zu fördern, ist unsere Kernaufgabe. 
Als eingetragener, gemeinnütziger Verein können wir unabhängig von anderen Interessen agieren und arbeiten daran, der Vakuumtechnik auf gesellschaftlicher und politischer Ebene mehr Gehör zu verschaffen. 
Um dies zu erreichen, haben wir uns mit anderen nationalen Vakuumgesellschaften in der „International Union of Vacuum Science and Technology“ (IUVSTA) organisiert und können unsere Mitglieder auch auf internationaler Ebene vertreten. Zusätzlich zur IUVSTA pflegen wir enge und direkte Kontakte zur Deutschen Physikalischen Gesellschaft (DPG).
