#Sustainability #Innovation #Nanotechnology #EnvironmentalProtection #PrivateLabel

Name: Cordouan Technologies SAS
Description:
Cordouan Technologies SAS is specialized in the design, fabrication & industrialization of innovative instruments for physico-chemical characterization (size, charge, shape) of nanoparticles and nanomaterials for academic research & industrial application (process monitoring, quality control, R&D). 

We offer to our customers measurement solutions for granulometry, zetametry, electronic microscopy and preparation accessories. We use among other techniques : the Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS) method for size measurement, the Laser Doppler Electrophoresis (LDE) technique for Zeta potential analysis and the Laser Induced Breakdown Detection (LIBD) technique for nanoparticle traces detection in water. Our unique product portfolio is issued from patented and innovative technologies transferred from prestigious research institutes: Institut Français du Pétrole (IFPEN), Karlsruhe Institute of Technologie (KIT), Institut Charles Sadron (ICS).

Landa Labs is the science innovation and incubation center of the Landa Group, developing technologies to the proof-of-concept phase and then spinning them out as independent businesses. We have spun out three businesses to-date: Landa Digital Printing, Nano-Metallography and Lusix.

Landa Labs has a staff of about 150 researchers, including chemists, physicists, materials scientists and engineers, most with advanced degrees. Collaborating with market-leading companies in the fields in which it operates, Landa Labs is entirely self-funded.

Name: Future Energy and Innovation Lab
Description: At the Future Energy and Innovation Lab, we take pride in having trained over 70 postdoctoral alumni and awarded 25 PhD titles. More than 25 of our alumni now hold leading positions in academia worldwide. Under the leadership of Prof. Martin Pumera, a globally recognized expert in 2D materials and advanced functional nanorobots, the lab has published over 900 papers, garnering more than 70,000 citations and an h-index of 126. Prof. Pumera is a five-time Clarivate Analytics "Highly Cited Researcher" and has received numerous prestigious awards, including ERC Starting Grant, two Marie-Curie Fellowships (EU), JSPS fellowship (Japan), and Humboldt Fellowship (Germany). His experience spans international research environments in USA, Spain, Japan, Singapore and Czechia. Martin ensures robust financial support for the lab, securing ongoing grants of 7,4 M EUR value for next 5 years. Pumera lab, housed within CEITEC at Brno University of Technology, comprises 20+ postdocs and 10+ students from various international backgrounds. The research environment is enhanced by a spacious 2000 m2 clean room fabrication and characterization facility accessible to all group members. We prioritize a supportive and dynamic research environment, reflected in our core values of Integrity, Clarity, and Energy. Prof. Pumera ensures rigorous monitoring of research progress through weekly seminars and bi-weekly individual discussions, alongside yearly career growth evaluations. These sessions focus on career development, skill enhancement, and participation in global conferences and events. We are dedicated to enhancing lives and tackling the world’s toughest challenges through collaborative research and innovative education. By uniting talented individuals globally, we aim to drive significant scientific advancements for humanity. We believe that it's not about money. It's about the people you have, how you're led, and how much you get it. What starts here changes the World!

Name: CORNERSTONE
Description:
CORNERSTONE is an open source, license free Silicon Photonics rapid prototyping foundry operating from the Universities of Southampton & Glasgow in the UK. 

We established CORNERSTONE as we had a vision for a “researcher’s” foundry, where new ideas could be tested away from rigid process flows and PDKs hidden in IP black boxes. From the outset, we were adamant that everything would be open-source to facilitate innovation and advance the field in a more collaborative and transparent manner.  We base our fabrication capability around deep-UV lithography to simplify the scale-up process. Our initial goal was to serve the UK academic community, but our vision quickly gained global resonance. Now, we proudly support collaborators in over 20 countries.

Description: ELDICO Scientific AG (The Electron Diffraction Company) is a Swiss technology and service company founded in 2019 and based next to the Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI), a world-leading research institute for natural sciences and engineering. ELDICO develops, manufactures and commercializes solutions based on electron diffractometers, i.e. novel analytical instruments for electron-based crystallography that make it possible to investigate nano-crystalline samples. ELDICO recently launched its ED-1, the world’s first dedicated electron diffractometer. Among universities and research institutes ELDICO’s clients are researchers in large, fast-growing industries such as the pharmaceutical industry, electromobility, advanced inorganic materials and the evolving MOF/COF/SOF sector. Name: Izon Science
Description:
Izon Science enables nanoparticle researchers across a range of disciplines to isolate and characterise particles with precision. With the field of extracellular vesicles (EVs) now pushing towards clinical applications, Izon is meeting the need for scalable EV isolation solutions for EV-based diagnostics and EV-based therapeutics, as well as fundamental EV research. 
This is achieved through its evolving range of size exclusion chromatography-based qEV columns, associated instrumentation for automating particle isolation, and customised bioprocessing development services. With such diverse customers, the qEV platform accommodates a broad range of sample types and volumes.  
Meanwhile, Izon brings high-resolution insights to the physical characterisation of EVs, lipid nanoparticles, viruses and beyond, through the Exoid and its Tunable Resistive Pulse Sensing (TRPS) technology. Through its unique particle-by-particle approach, the Exoid allows researchers across fundamental research and nanomedicine development to measure particle size, concentration, and zeta potential.
Izon’s network is global, with offices located in Christchurch (New Zealand – Headquarters), Lyon (France), Boston and Texas (US).

Name: Synopsys QuantumATK
Description:
The QuantumATK team at Synopsys develops the QuantumATK simulation tool for fast and reliable atomic-scale modeling of nanostructure. QuantumATK is fully supported and delivered in an easy-to-use interface, tailored from state-of-the-art methods, and developed by experts to the specifications of our customers.

Who we are

The QuantumATK team are former employees of QuantumWise which was acquired by Synopsys Inc. in 2017. At present, the QuantumATK team are part of Synopsys TCAD and has more than 30 employees, working with developing the QuantumATK code and supporting the users. Most of the employees have a Ph.D. in physics or chemistry, and/or are seasoned programmers with long experience in numerical modeling and GUI development.

Our aim and philosophy

We position ourselves as a facilitator by interacting with both the research community to extract the latest state-of-the-art methods and techniques, and with researchers in companies and universities that seek to utilize these tools. We strive to deliver these advanced numerical tools in an easy-to-use package. Our primary aim is to make the latest developments within atomic-scale modeling available to enable technologists to solve real life problems within the materials and semiconductor industry. 
Unlike most other commercial companies offering software for atomic-scale modeling, we develop our own code from the bottom up, and we have the in-house expertise to develop novel functionality all the way down to the quantum-mechanical level. Our software is a constant work in progress, and our development is test-driven and guided by customer requests and requirements. We spend a lot of energy on ensuring the quality and reliability of the software.

Can we help you solve a problem?
If you have any questions related to our products or company, please get in touch with us (quantumatk@synopsys.com), as we would love to hear from you! Requests and feedback from our customers is what helps us evolve our products.

Name: Nanomedicine Lab
Description:
The Nanomedicine Lab is a multidisciplinary research group based at the Catalan Institute of Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (ICN2) and affiliated with the Institute of Neurosciences at the Faculty of Medicine of the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona (UAB) all located in Barcelona, Spain. The Nanomedicine Lab is also an affiliate member of the Centre for Nanotechnology in Medicine at the Faculty of Biology, Medicine and Health of the University of Manchester in the United Kingdom which is fostering expertise and knowledge in innovative nanotechnologies for a broad range of medical applications.

The Nanomedicine Lab research portfolio includes the discovery and preclinical development of nanomedicine constructs based on novel, advanced nanomaterials of synthetic and biological nature. Great emphasis is placed at the interplay between in vitro and in vivo studies and how rationally designed and engineered nanoscale platforms can be safely translated into clinically effective therapeutics and diagnostics, with a particular interest in oncology and neurology.

Name: E-magy
Description:
With climate change a key issue in the world today, electrification of mobility is rising fast. Restricted range, charging speed and cost are standing in the way of wide scale EV adoption. Today's batteries use graphite as an active anode material, whereas silicon has up to ten times more capacity. Until now, the transition to silicon was hindered because silicon can swell and break when charged.

At E-magy, we supply nano-porous silicon for high-energy batteries. Our unique specialty silicon overcomes the swelling challenge by containing the expansion within the nanopores themselves, as validated by ZSW, a leading institute for applied battery research. We make high-energy silicon-dominant anodes a reality, compatible with existing production lines and are ready to scale up. 

Since 2013, we have been developing our solution in our cutting edge production facility near Amsterdam, The Netherlands. The team consists of driven, advanced materials specialists with over 20 years of silicon crystallisation experience. Cooperating with more than a dozen leading automotive and battery manufacturers, we bring our clients winning battery performance. We are part of multiple leading European battery associations and consortia to bring the industry forward. As ultimately, our dream is for everyone to enjoy the benefits of clean, electrically powered mobility.

E-magy. On with silicon

Name: Li-S Energy
Description:
With climate change driving the move to renewable energy and electric vehicles the demand for batteries is forecast to increase 10x by 2030. To drive this growth, there needs to be a better battery and Li-S Energy is pioneering that change.

Our new lithium sulfur and lithium metal technology will power the world’s future by delivering lighter batteries with more than twice the energy density and — with no heavy metals — offer a cleaner and greener alternative. Our unique technology is ideal for low-weight applications such as aviation, defence and heavy EVs and is set to revolutionise the industry.

Name: Medical Nanotechnologies, Inc.
Description:
Medical Nanotechnologies, Inc. (MedNanoTech) is revolutionizing the way cancer is treated by developing nanoparticle-based thermal therapies and drug delivery systems.

Our core platform is based on nanoparticles which can be delivered to the tumor, engulfed by targeted cancer cells, and irradiated by near-infrared (NIR) light. Upon NIR irradiation, the nanoparticles rapidly heat up. Depending on the amount of heat generated, which can be controlled, this rapid, localized heat enhances delivery of therapeutic agents such as DNA, proteins, and chemotherapies and, at higher temperature, directly kills the targeted tumor cells.

We also provide contract research services for Research Use Only applications that require nanoparticle purification, analysis, modification, and conjugation.

Name: Ryf AG - Microscopy & Metrology Services
Description:
Seit 1965 verschreiben wir uns mit Leib und Seele der Mikroskopie. Der Familienbetrieb ist in dieser Zeit kontinuierlich gewachsen und hat sich zum marktführenden Unternehmen in den Bereichen Mikroskopie und optische Messsysteme entwickelt.  

Nach dem Motto «Die beste Lösung für Sie» denken und handeln wir  
unabhängig, lösungsorientiert und vor allem langfristig. – Rund dreissig Personen beschäftigen sich vor und hinter den Kulissen mit den Wünschen und Vorstellungen unserer Kundschaft aus den Hauptbereichen Industrie, Biologie, Forschung, Medizin und Bildung. 

Mit Eigenentwicklungen, bekannten Markenvertretungen und einer umfassenden Produktpalette sind wir auch für Sonderlösungen und komplexe kundenspezifische Anforderungen Ihr kompetenter Ansprechpartner. Zehn Servicetechniker können auf ein Lager mit 20‘000 Artikeln zurückgreifen und sorgen dafür, dass Sie auch nach dem Kauf in jeder Hinsicht optimal betreut werden.

Name: PVD Products
Description:
PVD Products, Inc. is an Engineer to Order company that provides unique thin film deposition systems and components for material scientists and process engineers. Our equipment is used in both R&D and production environments.  We strive to form long-term lasting working relationships with our customers by insuring up-front that we can meet their specific requirements and provide strong technical support years after delivery.   At times this requires us to help guide customers through the specification process for their specific application if they are not that well versed in thin film deposition technology.   The success of our efforts to team with our customers is demonstrated by the large number of repeat customers we have.  

In addition to thin film equipment we provide  deposition services on an as-needed basis for R&D and prototype production applications.

PVD Products provides  equipment and services to customers around the world, including universities, national labs, start-ups, and Fortune 500 companies.  Our offerings include:

Name: Ad-Nano Technologies Private Limited
Description:
We founded in the year 2013 and based in Shimoga, Karnataka, India. where we manufacture nanomaterials. AD-NANO Technologies is a manufacturers and sales representation company dedicated to providing innovative systems into technological field & also in Research & Development programmes by introducing nano technology in it.
We have bridged the gap between inspiration and innovation by connecting our customers with the nano technology in their lives which is required to solve their engineering & technical problems, also to upgrade the technology to the next generation.
We also manufacture conductive inks like Carbon nanotubes conductive ink, Graphene conductive ink, Graphite conductive ink, which are widely used in industrial sectors and also in the area of research and development programmes.

Name: nanotechnology
Description: Nanotechnology

Name: FEMTIKA
Description:
Femtika is a spin-off business from Vilnius University's Laser Research Center that was founded in 2013 by a group of professionals with years of experience in 3D laser precision micro processing.

Company specializes in hybrid microfabrication technologies: universal tool using femtosecond laser - Laser Nanofactory workstation for multiphoton polymerization, laser ablation, selective laser etching technologies. Additionally, Femtika provides this field's research services. 

The company's goal is to meet the rising need for available tools and technologies on a global scale, enabling genuine 3D laser fabrication of custom design components in micro- and sub-micro scale. Microstructures provided by Femtika are used in development of new future products in semiconductors, photonics, medical, automotive, space industries and etc.

Our workstations „Laser Nanofactory“ is a user-oriented product, with simple-to-use interface which also allows a high-end user to exploit all the capabilities provided by the femtosecond laser processing. All the parameters of the system are controlled by the provided proprietary software 3DPoli. As a result, changing from one fabrication mode to another is a matter of setting parameters in the software and changing the focusing optics. Also, system is highly tunable, with the possibility to add additional functionalities. This allows us to satisfy even the most demanding customers. Alternatively, system can be oriented for maximal throughput allowing industrial scale use.

Name: FEMTOPRINT SA
Description:
FEMTOPRINT is a Swiss high-tech Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) specialized in high-precision 3D microfabrication in glass. 
With the ground-breaking FEMTOPRINT microfabrication platform we serve leading industrial Customers with feasibility, rapid prototyping, and pilot- and industrial series manufacturing at the wafer-level.

APPLICATIONS
Microfluidics, Micro-optics, Photonics, Microelectronics, Micromechanics, MEMS, PIC, Packaging, Mastering  

INDUSTRIES
Life Sciences & Diagnostics, Medical, Watchmaking, Aerospace, Automotive, Industrial Machinery, VR & AR, Photonics, Energy, Quantum

The FEMTOPRINT micro-fabrication platform relies on ultrafast, laser-based manufacturing processes, creating novel glass microdevices in 3D, with integrated waveguides, welded and laser diced. The company is certified ISO 13485:2016 and ISO 9001:2015 
Different glass types can be used as raw materials to structure components, such as, but not limited to, fused silica, borosilicate, or alkali-free glass. 
The minimum feature size range is a few microns, while the maximum working area is currently 300mm in diameter and 30mm in thickness.

MISSION
We provide technology-driven industries with advanced laser micro-fabrication services in glass, in order to solve manufacturing challenges and enable product outperformance, thus enabling our Customers to innovate and advance the well-being of people in areas like health, consumer goods, energy, transportation and communication. 

VISION
By means of our proprietary machines and technology platform, we aim to become a leading global provider of glass micro-manufacturing services, from proof-of-concept to prototyping, up to pilot and volume manufacturing. With our multi-disciplinary team, we are recognized as world-class experts in delivering superior quality and innovative solutions for our Customers.

We want to be part of your success, contact us at info@femtoprint.ch.

Name: Clúster de Materials Avançats de Catalunya
Description: El Clúster de Materials Avançats de Catalunya (Clúster MAV) és una associació sense finalitat de lucre que té com a objectiu principal promoure i contribuir a la competitivitat de les empreses i entitats de la cadena de valor del sector dels materials avançats a Catalunya.

Name: Central Metallurgical Research & Development Institute
Description:
Central Metallurgical Research and Development Institute - CMRDI 

CMRDI is a research center affiliated to the State Ministry of Scientific Research. The mission of the institute is to contribute to the national economy and the welfare of the society by improving the Egyptian industry through technological development and technology transfer.

CMRDI offers services to industry covering research projects, consultancy, technical services, training, testing and certification.

Over almost three decades of collaboration with Egyptian industry, these services have been focused on the actual demands of the clients and needs of the companies; giving the institute a profile of needs pull rather than technology push.

CMRDI   has a longstanding and successful collaboration with many national and overseas R&D institutions, which guarantees a sound scientific basis.

CMRDI has also largely benefited from cooperation with international agencies such as JICA (Japan), TNO (Netherlands) and SIDA (Sweden). Thanks to this support, CMRDI was able to play an effective role in transferring many technologies to the Egyptian industries as well as to African and Middle-East countries.

Name: Gelatex
Description:
Materials technology startup unlocking the potential of nanofibers with #halospinning for cultured meat, tissue engineering, wound care, filtration, and more.

Our proprietary high-throughput nanofiber production technology HaloSpin is the fastest solution-spinning technology. We are especially focused on the production of bio-based nanofibers, but can work with variety of different polymers. Compared to electrospinning, our technology enables over 90% price reduction and is the ideal solution for applications such as tissue engineering, cultured meat, drug discovery, wound care, energy storage, filtration, and more.

We are providing nanofiber manufacturing as well as development services for companies to develop the materials for a specific application. We have developed our own edible 100% vegan scaffolds for cultured meat and are continuously developing new products also on our own.

Name: Photio
Description:
Creamos una herramienta para que personas, empresas e instituciones públicas puedan formar parte de la solución al problema de la contaminación atmosférica, democratizando el acceso al aire puro! 

Photio es una Cleantech que utiliza como base de sus desarrollos la nanomimética con el objetivo de replicar procesos que ocurren en la naturaleza, como la fotosíntesis, en materiales de uso masivo, tales como: pinturas, telas, plásticos, asfalto, hormigón e incluso cuero sintético.

¿Quieres saber que es la Photiosíntesis? Visita nuestra página web

Name: TECNAN
Description:
TECNAN produces at industrial level simple and complex nano-oxides as well as other high performance nanoproducts for multisectoral applications, establishing TECNAN as one of the most competitive suppliers of these new materials nationwide as well as at international level.

PRODUCTS:
-Nano-powders: TECNAN produces at industrial scale nano-oxides of more than 60 elements of the Periodic System in a clean and environmentally friendly way, obtaining products with high quality and purity, very small size (7-25 nm) and with multisectoral applications.

-Dispersions: All the nano-powders produced by TECNAN are available dispersed in different media such as water, alcohols and other solvents, both in standard concentrations and in ad-hoc conditions according to the client’s demands.
TECNAN has developed a range of exclusive references based in advanced nanoparticle dispersions ready to be applied directly on different substrates (concrete and gypsum, leather, glass, wood and plastic among others), according to their porosity, achieving superficial effects such as superhydrophobicity in an complete and lasting way due to the strong adherence of the product to the surface and its excellent stability.

-Masterbatches: TECNAN is highly skilled regarding incorporation of diverse nanofillers into polymeric matrices in order to obtain optimised properties for a broad spectrum of plastic materials such as polyethylene, both high and low density, PP, EVA, rubber, etc. TECNAN produces pre-concentrates with loads between 25% and 60% in weight of nanoparticles customised according to the client’s specifications. These masterbatches for nano-composite production represent an innovative way of developing new functionalised plastic materials or a new manner of improving them.

Contact person: German Medina
german.medina@tecnan-nanomat.es
tecnan@tecnan-nanomat.es
marketing@tecnan-nanomat.es

Name: Merkoçi Research Group
Description: The research at Merkoçi Group focuses on discovering and technological development of the cutting edge nanotechnology and nanomaterials based simple biosensing platforms by employing and controlling their architectures at both nano and macro scales with the objective to link together nano and macro worlds.

Name: Superhard Research Centre
Description:
Our planet needs a new future. One that breathes better air, has better water and benefits from a progress whose benefits are accessible to all. That's what we are striving towards. By devoting our todays to this future.
A super future.
The Superhard future...
--------
Superhard Research Centre is a global R&D centre of excellence, focusing on a better quality of life for all and a better, more breathable and habitable planet. Through the sheer power and perseverance of science and technology.
Our latest invention - the Nuovo Diamond, is a wonder element that is not torn from the earth, displacing mega tonnes of habitable, fertile soil. But 'grown' in a micro specs guided, nano science controlled environment. 
Our S.T.A.R. (Science Technology and Advanced Research) Lab, across our 100,000 sq ft plus facility, is focused on disrupting multiple industries, sectors and practices. From rough blocks, machine tools & high performance machining to scientific & industrial applications across a range of optical, thermal and mechanical stability to enhanced, efficient hydro technology with supercharged active water to quantum computing involving ultra high levels of data and computing power.

Name: Pixelligent Technologies
Description:
Pixelligent specializes in high refractive index (HRI) nanocrystal composite formulations and nanomaterial dispersions, including our PixNIL, PixMicro, PixJet, PixCor, and PixClear product families. Each material is engineered for exceptional optical clarity, tunable refractive indices, and broad process compatibility. Our proprietary PixClearProcess technology ensures precise control over nanocrystal size, dispersibility, and stability, enabling consistent and scalable production.

We help power next generation technologies across Extended Reality (AR, MR, VR), OLED and MicroLED displays, optical sensors, and OLED lighting. Our materials deliver outstanding optical performance with high transmittance, low haze, and enhanced reliability, resulting in improved efficiency, brightness uniformity, and contrast in waveguided displays.

From expanding field of view in XR systems to boosting light capture in sensors and doubling light extraction in OLED lighting, Pixelligent’s nanocomposite materials drive higher performance across a wide range of optical applications.

Name: Nfinite Nanotech
Description:
Nfinite Nanotech is an advanced material company that makes smart nanocoatings for sustainable packaging. We are accelerating the world’s goal to eliminate plastic waste.

Nfinite's superior barrier coatings can make biodegradable packaging more robust and existing packaging more recyclable to extend the shelf life of products and reduce plastic waste.

Name: MackGraphe
Description: Established in 2013, in the city of São Paulo, Brazil, MackGraphe is a research, development and innovation institute specialized in graphene and  two-dimensional materials. Their researchers and students, mainly from Mackenzie Presbyterian University, develop studies focused on nanomaterials, nanotechnology, characterization and standardization of materials, business and market. The mission of MackGraphe is to generate specialized knowledge, to build the capacity of high skilled workers, and to develop RD&I projects with the commercial sector.

Name: SparkNano - Spatial Atomic Layer Deposition
Description:
Advanced Manufacturing for a clean world 

We are the leading OEM of spatial Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD) systems and the expert technology partner. Our innovative patented spatial ALD systems are and expert team are key to enable high-precision deposition of nano-scale thin film layers on large surfaces at industrial speed. From lab to fab, together with our partners, we help our customers to scale manufacturing of next-gen products, shaping a clean world for life.  

SparkNano spatial ALD systems
With our SparkNano Labline, SparkNano Vellum Sheet2Sheet and SparkNano Omega Roll2Roll devices we support customers looking to research and scale manufacturing of products in Energy and storage, as well as Electronics. With our expert team and trusted partners, such as Air Liquide, we support our customers with expert advise during the whole life cycle, as well as the initial sampling tests to support their purchase. 

Learn more about our innovative technology and systems which are now supporting customers on global scale while researching and producing next gen products in Batteries, Solar and optoelectronics through SparkNano spatial ALD systems.  

We'd love to connect and learn how we can help you further in your quest to advance manufacturing shaping a clean world for life together! 

Visit us at www.spark-nano.com or contact us at +31 6 1485 2921

Name: Nanores Lab
Description: Nanores Lab to wiodące laboratorium badawczo-analityczne, które specjalizuje się w innowacyjnych rozwiązaniach w dziedzinie nanotechnologii. Nasza gama usług badawczo-analitycznych obejmuje analizę przekrojów próbek, jak również ich rekonstrukcję 3D. Opracowujemy innowacyjne rozwiązania w dziedzinie nanotechnologii. Projektujemy i tworzymy struktury takie jak kryształy fotoniczne czy układy elektromechaniczne – wszystko w skali mikro oraz nano.

Name: Dotz Nano Ltd.
Description:
Dotz Nano Limited (ASX: DTZ) is a global leader in nanotechnology, specializing in advanced materials that solve critical industrial and environmental challenges. We harness cutting-edge carbon-based nanotechnologies to develop innovative solutions that drive sustainability and industrial decarbonization.

Our core focus areas:

Carbon Capture Technology: 
We pioneer next-generation nano-porous solid sorbents that offer unparalleled efficiency in industrial decarbonization, setting new benchmarks for sustainability.

In-product Tagging Solutions: 
We provide advanced anticounterfeiting and monitoring solutions tailored for the oil, gas, and chemicals sectors.

Dotz is committed to creating impactful technologies that enable a cleaner, more sustainable future.

Name: NanoSpin
Description:
NanoSpin is a leading designer and manufacturer of top-tier tailor-made electrospinning machines and accessories, specializing in serving the nanotechnology research and engineering community. Our cutting-edge machines are meticulously crafted to meet the unique needs of scientists and engineers in this dynamic field.
At NanoSpin, our foremost commitment is to provide our clients with the most suitable and effective solutions for their research and manufacturing requirements. To achieve this, we offer customizable electrospinning machines that can be tailored to the specific needs of each client. Our product range includes single nozzle, dual nozzle, and Electroblowing systems, providing versatility and flexibility for diverse applications.
We take a customer-centric approach, offering a diverse range of options to suit various requirements. Our product lineup includes Lab-Scale, Pilot, and Industrial machines, along with High Voltage Power supplies, syringe pumps, Collector drums, and Filter tests.

Name: Moore Nanotechnology Systems
Description:
Moore Nanotechnology Systems was established in October 1997 as a stand alone subsidiary of the Moore Tool Company.  Our initial vision remains in the continual development of state-of-the-art ultra-precision manufacturing systems and processes for the production of advanced optics.  We develop, design and manufacture ultra-precision machining systems for single point diamond turning, deterministic grinding, micro-milling and glass press molding.  Our machines are primarily used in the consumer electronics, defense, aerospace, lighting, medical and automotive sectors.  We maintain an installed base of hundreds of Nanotech ultra-precision machining systems in many countries around the world and we continue to grow.

Above all, we attribute our success to our highly skilled and diversified workforce that shares a passion and common vision for excellence.  We remain focused on the continuous development of new products and value-added services to meet the requirements of our current and future customers.  We strive to achieve 100% customer satisfaction in all that we do.  We welcome the opportunity to work with you to satisfy your requirements for advanced ultra-precision manufacturing systems.

Name: FLUIDINOVA, S.A.
Description:
FLUIDINOVA was founded in 2005 in Portugal as a specialized manufacturer of synthetic nano-hydroxyapatite and tricalcium phosphate materials, which are commercialized worldwide for different applications, under the brand name nanoXIM.

𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗫𝗜𝗠•𝗢𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗲: The key hydroxyapatite ingredients for oral care applications, to reduce dental hypersensitivity, enhance enamel remineralization and restore natural whiteness. 
Available in two different forms: water suspension (nano) and powder (micro).

𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗫𝗜𝗠•𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹: Synthetic highly pure hydroxyapatite (HAp) and tricalcium phosphate (TCP) materials for bone regeneration medical devices. 
Available in three different forms: pastes, powders and granules.

𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗫𝗜𝗠•𝗙𝗼𝗼𝗱: The key hydroxyapatite ingredients for functional food and food supplements, to promote bone strength, reduce bone loss and prevent dental hypersensitivy. 
Available in two different forms: water suspension (nano) and powder (micro).

𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗫𝗜𝗠•𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗰𝗵: Looking for a calcium phosphate for your research? nanoXIM products are also available for several research fields including bio-separation, catalysis, pharma, biotechnology and functionalized textiles.

Name: Molecular Plasma Group
Description:
MPG offers a disruptive Key Enabling Technology (KET) that enables the creation of a wide range of advanced surface functionalities. Examples of what can be achieved using our Molecular Plasma Technology include: adhesion promotion, one-step immobilisation of bio-molecules, virucidal coatings, silicone-free release functionalities, tune-able super-hydrophobicity or hydrophilicity, microfluidic channeling and much more. 

We achieve this by activating a surface and covalently binding the precursor molecules onto the surface using our proprietary Molecular Plasma Process. The outcome is a permanently bonded, custom-designed, nano-metric layer, which features the surface functionality needed by the customer. Our unique technology allows us to work with a wide variety of precursor molecules on basically any kind of surface.

As a full-service provider, we offer application development as well as design, construction and servicing of equipment ranging from lab systems for R&D centers and universities up to industrial in-line systems for continuous production. Our customers are active in a wide range of industries such as fundamental and applied research, aerospace, aeronautics, automotive, packaging, biotechnology, biomedical engineering, micro-electronics, electronics, etc.

Name: NanoAndMore
Description:
Welcome to NanoAndMore your number one source for the major brands of AFM probes such as NanoWorld, NANOSENSORS, BudgetSensors, MikroMasch and nanotools, calibration standards and other products for nanotechnology

Impressum: https://www.nanoandmore.com/impressum

Name: IFFCO-Nanoventions Private Limited
Description: IFFCO-Nanoventions works with an innate urge of discovering and innovating something novel from untapped atoms and amazing bio systems. The ultimate goal is to find solutions to problems in agriculture, health, energy and water sectors.

Name: Vorbeck
Description:
Vorbeck Materials is a U.S.-based defense- and first responder-focused materials science company pioneering next-generation technologies built around graphene and advanced chemical systems. With a robust portfolio of over 100 patents, Vorbeck develops and scales mission-ready solutions — from PFAS-free firefighting foams to conformal antennas, RF sensors, and electromagnetic shielding. Founded in 2006, Vorbeck operates out of Columbia, Maryland and a 47,000 sq ft advanced manufacturing center in Grand Forks, North Dakota.

Our technologies are deployed across U.S. Department of Defense programs and are engineered to outperform traditional materials in size, weight, power, and cost (SWaP-C) constrained environments. Vorbeck’s proprietary graphene, Vor-x, enables ultra-light, ultra-rugged systems for warfighters, unmanned platforms, and secure communications. At the same time, our fluorine-free firefighting foams are reshaping the regulatory and operational landscape for environmental safety and fire suppression — delivering performance that rivals legacy AFFF without the tradeoffs.

We don’t just invent. We deliver — scaling lab breakthroughs into real-world defense and first responder solutions with speed, precision, and purpose.

Find out more about our innovative solutions by visiting our website:
www.vorbeck.com

Name: The Advanced Materials Show (USA)
Description:
The Advanced Materials Show USA will showcase the very latest in high performance materials technology for applications including Aerospace, Automotive, Electronics, Energy and Medical Technology.

Leaders in R&D, materials science and engineering will use the show to source the latest advanced materials; along with the scientific instruments and processing equipment necessary for the development, manufacture and processing of advanced materials.

Name: Nanospan
Description:
Nanospan is a cutting edge technology company specializing in Graphene and Graphene-nanocomposites for the construction and energy storage sectors.

With headquarters in Silicon Valley, California, and a significant presence in India, Nanospan leverages its unique intellectual property and sustainable manufacturing capabilities to produce game changing, high-quality, cost-effective Graphene and Nanocomposites at scale. 

In Construction, Nanospan's portfolio features state-of-the-art Graphene-admixtures: Spanocrete, SpanOil which substantially enhance Concrete Durability, saves on water and materials, brings down the construction time by 60%, and offer substantial cost savings. As of Dec 2023, 25000 Cubic meters of Concrete and 2 Million AAC blocks in construction projects have deployed the above products.

In the Energy storage sector, the company delivers innovative graphene-based anodes and supercapacitors, aiming for higher energy densities and eco-friendly solutions. 
Targeting a combined market size of > $100billion, Nanospan seeks to serve industries:Real-Estate, Infrastructure, Data centers, Precast Panel Manufacturing, and Renewable Energy in the Construction realm, along with Electric Vehicles and Power Utilities in Energy storage. 

What sets Nanospan apart is its commitment to marrying unparalleled durability and sustainability, enabled by its multi-functional, graphene nanocomposite products.

Name: Theway Membranes
Description:
Theway Membranes has been involved in Membrane research, development and manufacture for the last decade. Theway Membranes is the first company to have developed commercial hollow fiber membrane ultrafiltration technology in India. The Chennai based company received the 'Make in India' award in 2016.

The company continues to work on novel membrane development for Membrane Distillation and Forward Osmosis. Theway Membranes are known for their quality, performance and attractive prices.

Name: SuperQ Technologies India Pvt Ltd
Description: SuperQ works towards developing and commercializing world-class superconductor-based products. With AI and Quantum enabled simulations and experimentation, SuperQ aims to become one of the leading superconducting product innovation labs in India by providing technology platforms to enable the discovery of new materials and products at ambient temperature and pressure.

Name: LABSOFT
Description:
You Achieve - we support.  Osiągasz Ty - my wspieramy. To nasze motto. Przedstawicielstwo handlowe i autoryzowany serwisow w Polsce specjalistycznych narzędzi dla rynku nanotechnologii - mikroskopia elektronowa - Thermo Fisher Scientific (FEI Company), mikroanaliza - Gatan (EDAX), elipsometria i narzędzia procesowe - SENTECH, AFM, profilometria, tribologia - Bruker Nano Surface Metrology, XRF, XRD - Bruker AXS, MBE, MOCVD - Veeco, akcesoria - SPI Supplies i inne.
Działamy na rynku od 1992 roku. Obecnie zespół 30 osób z siedzibą w Warszawie przy ul. Puławskiej 469, 02-844 Warszawa (budynek PCBC). Doskonała obsługa posprzedażowa i wsparcie aplikacyjne.

Name: Nanobiosym
Description: Nanobiosym (NBS) is an innovation engine dedicated to creating a new science that emerges from the holistic integration of physics, biomedicine, and nanotechnology. NBS focuses on incubating transformational technologies that have the potential for game-changing impact and commercializing and scaling up these technologies for deployment in developed and developing world markets. NBS leverages science and technology to address our planet’s greatest unmet needs in global health, energy and the environment. Nanobiosym Diagnostics (NBSDx) focuses on the commercialization of its proprietary technology platform, that can rapidly and accurately detect genetic fingerprints from any biological organism, empowering people worldwide with rapid, affordable, and portable diagnostic information about their own health. To maximize global humanitarian impact, we have spearheaded the Nanobiosym Global Initiative, which is building innovative public-private partnerships with governments, NGO’s, academic, industrial and global thought leaders to help bring emerging technologies to address some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

Name: SOL instruments GmbH
Description:
SOL instruments GmbH. is an innovation-focused manufacturer of technologically advanced instruments for light measuring, elemental analysis and nano-scale microscopy.

For two decades we have devoted our knowledge, innovations and experience to spectroscopy, microscopy, lasers and create robust tools for scientific and industrial applications in three business segments:

 -  Analysis
 -  Spectroscopy
 -  Lasers

We are proud to be a part of the world's most advanced scientific explorations and bring our innovations to the market of high performance equipment.

We are striving to be a reliable supplier of high-tech products and innovative solutions for customers' advanced needs and to offer top-level technical service and support all over the world.

Name: ULTRANANOTECH PVT. LTD.
Description:
Ultrananotech is the leading supplier of nanomaterials in India. We have partnership with renowned global giants in the field of nanotechnology. We can supply any nanomaterials you name upon. Your search for nanomaterials comes to end when you will reach us. We can provide carbon based nano (various graphene, graphene oxide, nanotubes, graphite), CVD graphene on various substrate, silicon wafers,metal nano powders, metal carbides, metal nitride, oxide nanopowders, nanowires, quantum dot,  etc to name few. 
Various layered structured single crystal, powders and solutions of Hexagonal boron nitride (hBN), Molybdenum disulfide (MoS2), Molybdenum diselenide (MoSe2),Tungsten disulfide (WS2), Tungsten diselenide (WSe2), Tungsten ditelluride (WTe2), Gallium Selenide (GaSe), Lead Tin Sulfide (PbSnS2). Please visit us at: www.ultrananotec.com

Name: Teledyne MEMS
Description: Teledyne offers unmatched MEMS capability, from design to prototyping on 150mm wafers to volume 200mm production.  Teledyne DALSA and Teledyne Micralyne combine to collaborate with customers offering decades of experience across a vast process portfolio.  Our deep expertise in materials and processes, combined with state-of-the-art facilities and collaborative R&D resources drive continuous improvement, learning, and innovation.  Teledyne MEMS delivers a scalable suite of MEMS and microfabrication capabilities that allow customers to ramp up to meet their business development goals.

Name: Touchlab Limited
Description: Touchlab manufactures e-skin thinner than human skin which can be wrapped around hard or soft surfaces to sense pressure and location in real-time. Applications include on-land, underwater, and in space robotics & machines.

Name: Nanomade
Description:
We bring surfaces to life.

Nanomade Technologies develops ultra-sensitive, flexible sensor solutions that transform everyday surfaces into smart, interactive interfaces.

Our patented technology enables advanced sensing capabilities across three key areas:

 Capaforce – High-resolution pressure and force sensing
 Pulse – Real-time physiological monitoring (e.g. heart rate, respiration)
 Structure Deformation – Detection of shape changes and mechanical stress

These core technologies can be applied across virtually any industry from robotics, aerospace, and automotive, to healthcare, wearables, and industrial design.

Lightweight, flexible, and scalable, our sensors unlock new possibilities for Human-Machine Interfaces (HMI), remote monitoring, and embedded intelligence in connected systems.

Based in France and collaborating globally, we help our partners bring new products to life — faster, smarter, and more intuitively.

 Follow us to explore the future of touch, sensing, and interaction.

 www.nanomade.com |  contact@nanomade.com

Name: Versarien plc
Description: The Versarien Plc group is an advanced engineering materials business developing valuable new materials through the commercialisation of innovative manufacturing processes. The company is a pioneer in the supply of graphene and on course to disrupt a wide variety of industry sectors by producing many proprietary grades of graphene and 2D nanomaterials on a commercial basis. The Group has been at the forefront of developing best-in-class graphene powders and inks, and is the world’s first company to receive full quality assurance certification by the Graphene Council. It has also significantly expanded its graphene manufacturing capabilities around the world to enhance its ability to sustainably produce graphene-based materials to match rising global demand.

Name: XRnanotech
Description: XRnanotech is an award-winning Swiss deep tech company. We offer innovative nanostructured X-ray optics with outstanding precision for high-tech applications. Our optics allow to focus ever higher-energy beams onto the smallest possible samples. This is crucial for drug discovery, medical imaging, microchip inspection or investigation of advanced materials. In this niche, we provide unique solutions for several hundred large-scale R&D beamlines and high-tech companies around the world. Our vision is to enable them to analyze yet unmeasurable nano-systems.

Name: HoloOr
Description:
HOLO/OR is an international high-tech company, dedicated to the design, manufacturing and commercialization of new products and systems based on Diffractive Optical Elements (DOEs).

Since 1989, HOLO/OR developed and manufactured a variety of diffractive optical elements for high power lasers that accomplish difficult tasks, conventional optics fail to address effectively. Using our in house software and algorithms our designs reach top quality in means of efficiency, uniformity and other required parameters. 
HOLO/OR now holds an extensive multifunctional design database, enabling us to give fast solutions for different applications and customer needs.

The company employs highly skilled personnel, and occupies a production facility at the Kiryat Weizmann High-Tech Industrial Park, Rehovot, Israel. Holo-Or has a full capability of developing and manufacturing diffractive optical elements in clean room facilities. The company holds key patents on its method of manufacturing Diffractive Elements. Name: MIT QP&AI Group
Description:
The world of quantum mechanics holds enormous potential to address unsolved problems in communications, computation, and precision measurements. Efforts are underway across the globe to develop such technologies in various physical systems, including atoms, superconductors, and topological states of matter. The MIT Quantum Photonics Laboratory pursues experimental and theoretical research towards quantum technologies using photons and semiconductor spins, combining techniques from atomic physics, optoelectronics, and modern nanofabrication. Our research follows four directions:

Modern silicon photonics opens new possibilities for high-performance quantum information processing, such as quantum simulation and high-speed quantum cryptography.

Solid state quantum memories based on electronic and nuclear spins are leading technologies for quantum repeater networks and distributed quantum computing.

2D materials, such as graphene, provide new capabilities in communications, sensing, imaging, nonlinear optics, and quantum information devices.

Quantum sensors enable precision measurements of time, fields, and forces for applications in the physical and life sciences.

Name: Kalpana Systems
Description:
What if you could accelerate the energy transition thanks to a new perspective on atomic layer deposition for thin films?   

Imagine batteries with next-level performance, unrivalled range and fast charging. Imagine affordable flexible solar panels and OLED screens for unrestricted designs. Imagine fully recyclable packaging, thanks to ultra-thin barrier coatings. We found a way to solve the challenges of applying ALD technology to thin films. We like to call it: Superspatial ALD.   

Inspired by the helical design found in nature, our technology provides an elegant solution to break from industry standards. Superspatial ALD enables the deposition of complex multi-layers in just one production step. The unique helical web transport in our thin film machine enables the roll-to-roll production of coating layers at high throughput.   

Superspatial ALD speeds up the ALD process by 1000 times, reducing cost by a factor of 10, using precious materials with the precision of a single atom. All with one machine, adaptable to fit different substrate types and widths, with a small footprint that seamlessly integrates into your production line.   

The wait is over. Superspatial ALD is finally here.

Name: RAB-Microfluidics
Description:
RAB-Microfluidics is a Research & Development company developing cutting-edge microfluidic technology to solve oil analysis problems.

For too long, stationary laboratories have been the way to test and analyse oil samples, however, oil analysis does not have to be static and one dimensional. We believe this process should be dynamic like everything else around us, that’s why we are revolutionising oil testing and analysis services by making conventional laboratory procedures mobile, rapid and routine. Our innovation is driven by the needs of our customers in oil & gas, aerospace & defence, processing & manufacturing, power generation, transport and maritime industries.

Our microfluidic technology invention is the product of a decade-long research from world recognised college of Physical Science at the University of Aberdeen. RAB-Microfluidics is strategically located at the heart of this citadel of technological expertise, being the pioneering developer of microfluidic technology for oil applications in Europe. We have unparalleled world class expertise in development of microfluidic technology at out behest working with award winning product development partners in the United Kingdom. Our advisory team has over 60 years’ worth of experience and expertise in knowledge underpinning our technology, business development and commercialisation.

Name: Smallmatek - Small Materials and Technologies, Lda
Description:
Smallmatek is an innovation-driven company that offers integrated solutions for protection of metal structures against corrosion and biofouling failure, combining the development and production of cost-effective and eco-efficient “smart” additives with expert consulting services. These additives are based on (nano)technology that promotes the controlled release of active substances from smart micro-/ nanomaterials in response to very specific stimuli. 

Smallmatek is equally committed with environmentally sustainable practices, including assessing the safety, toxicity and environmental impact of all micro- and nanomaterials produced by us or our customers. 

We have an extensive experience in services supply in the areas of monitoring and characterization of corrosion and biofouling phenomena, as well as in the toxicity assessment of chemical compounds, environmental impact assessment and environmental monitoring of anthropogenic activities (covering  physical-chemical analytical determinations and a wide-range of biological aspects, including sampling and identification of plankton, invertebrates or ichthyofauna). Recently, we started to quantify and characterize the presence of microplastics in the waters, sediments and residues of WWTPs.

Headquartered in Aveiro (Portugal) and with a close relationship with the University of Aveiro, one of the most innovative and dynamic Portuguese academic institutions, Smallmatek is intended to be an effective provider of know-how to national and international industrial players to generate high-value products, thereby fostering the growth of economical activity in specific branches of the market (corrosion, fouling and coating technology).

Name: NanoLab, Inc.
Description:
NanoLab is a product development and contract research organization with a specialization in nanotechnology-enabled products.  We develop sensors, composites, coatings, nanoscale devices, and the processes to manufacture them.  In addition, we are a leading domestic producer of high quality carbon nanotubes in bulk and in aligned array forms, supplying commercial customers and the DOD. 
We have demonstrated nanotube based tweezers, antennas, and other devices based on aligned CNT.. NanoLab also employs a group of application engineers, focused on product development. In 2001, we commercialized the first carbon nanotube buckypaper.  We also lead the development of carbon nanotube reinforced ceramic composites for armor applications, starting in 2002.  We then developed in-situ growth processes for the synthesis of CNT on carbon fiber networks. Recent work has given us experience with functionalization and dispersion of nanotubes into paints, primers, two component polyurethane elastomers, epoxies and synthetic rubbers. We have also formulated sprayable and printable nanotube coatings. Please contact us for your material or project needs.

Name: Iran Nano Product - محصولات فناوری نانو ایران
Description:
در سال ۱۳۸۲، با شناسایی فناورىنانو به عنوان یک فناوری دارای اولویت ملى، ستاد ویژة توسعة فناورى نانو به منظور پیگیری توسعة این فناوری در کشور تشکیل شد. دیدگاه ستاد برای توسعة فناوری نانو، تدوین چارچوب فعالیت بلندمدت کشور در این حوزه بود و در این مسیر، برنامة راهبردى دهسالة فناورى نانو تهیه و در مرداد ماه ۱۳۸۴ به تصویب هیئت دولت رسید. این سند با عنوان «سند راهبرد آینده» قرار گرفتن در میان ۱۵ کشور برتر جهان در حوزة فناورىنانو و تلاش براى ارتقاء مداوم اینجایگاه به منظور تولید ثروت و بهبود کیفیت زندگی مردم را هدفگیرى کرده است. هم اکنون نیز ستاد ویژه توسعه فناوری نانو با هدف حمایت از محصولات تولید داخل در حوزه نانوفناوری و همچنین تجاری سازی فناوریهای داخلی از شرکتهای دارای محصولات نانویی در صورت دریافت تاییدیه نانو از این ستاد حمایتهای معنوی و مادی مینماید.
واحد بازرسی و بررسی مواد و محصولات با هدف ارتقای کیفی محصولات، افزایش اعتماد مصرفکنندگان و ایجاد شفافیت بازار محصولات نانو در سال ۱۳۸۶ با حمایتهای ستاد ویژه توسعه فناوری نانو شکل گرفت. مأموریت اصلی این واحد، ارزیابی محصولات، نظارت بر ثبات مقیاس و خواص و اعطای گواهینامه نانومقیاس میباشد. مدیریت این واحد در سال ۱۳۸۹ به موسسه خدمات توسعه فناوری تا بازار ایرانیان واگذار گردید.
با توسعه حمایتهای معاونت علمی فناوری ریاست جمهوری از شرکتهای دانشبنیان، واحد بازرسی و بررسی مواد و محصولات علاوه بر فعالیتهای سابق، ارزیابی شرکتهای سایر حوزههای فناوری را نیز در دستور کار خود قرار داده است. این مجموعه اکنون با نام واحد ارزیابی محصولات دانشبنیان به فعالیت خود ادامه میدهد.
به محصولاتی که در حوزه فناوری نانو قرار میگیرند، گواهینامه نانومقیاس اعطا میگردد.بر همین مبنا موسسه خدمات فناوری تا بازار ستاد نانو (کریدور) بمنظور ایجاد مرجعی برای معرفی محصولات دارای گواهینامه نانومقیاس در بازار داخل و بینالملل، سامانه اطلاع رسانی محصولات نانوفناوری ایران را به آدرس www. nanoproduct. ir راهاندازی نموده است.

Name: ANTACON GmbH
Description:
Wir bieten neuartige, hoch verschleißfeste Kohlenstoffbeschichtungen (DLC bzw. ta-C) mit herausragenden Eigenschaften an.

Mit unserem Verfahren sind sehr hohe Schicht-Eindringhärten von bis zu 70 GPa (vgl. Diamant: H=100 GPa) realisierbar, wodurch die Beschichtungen äußerst widerstandsfähig gegen verschiedene Verschleißmechanismen sind.

Aufgrund der geringen Oberflächenrauheit und des dadurch niedrigen Reibungskoeffizienten sind diese Schichten auch für Anwendungen prädestiniert, bei denen eine möglichst geringe Reibung von entscheidender Bedeutung ist.

Die niedrigen Temperaturen während der Schichterzeugung (< 90 °C) ermöglichen es neben unterschiedlichen Metallen und Legierungen auch temperaturempfindliche Materialien wie z.B. Kunststoffe zu beschichten.

Neben dem Einsatz zum Verschleißschutz bieten unsere ta-C Schichten aufgrund ihrer chemischen Resistenz, Biokompatibilität, Strukturier- und Dotierbarkeit eine breite Palette an weiteren Anwendungsmöglichkeiten, bspw. in der Medizintechnik, Lebensmittelindustrie oder Sensorik.

Wir verfügen bereits über die notwendige Anlagentechnik um Kleinserien, Muster und Prototypen zu erzeugen und sind momentan auf der Suche nach interessierten Kunden, Kooperationspartnern und Investoren.

Ihre Vorteile:
	
    extreme Härte » hohe Verschleißresistenz
    beliebige Schichtdicke » erhöhte Standzeiten
    niedriger Reibkoeffizient » gesteigerte Energieeffizienz
    hohe chemische Resistenz » widerstandsfähig gegen agressive Medien
    Biokompatibilität » geeignet für medizinische und lebensmitteltechnische  Anwendungen
    Dotierbarkeit » elektrische oder bio-chemische Eigenschaften einstellbar
    sehr gute Haftung auf unterschiedlichsten Materialien » breitbandig einsetzbar
    stufenlos einstellbare Härteverläufe (Gradienten) » anwendungsspezifische Schichtdesigns

Unser Angebot:

Beratung, Lohnbeschichtung, Bemusterungen, Prototypenentwicklung, Schichtdesign, Analysen, F&E

Name: nanoSkunkWorkX
Description:
nanoSkunkWorkX | nSWX is The Interface Company.

We design nanostructured Scalable Devices (nSDs) — embedded interface devices that remove performance limits inside real systems.

As advanced technologies scale, constraints shift from the core to the interface layer: where heat, current, reactions, and signals bottleneck system performance. nSWX engineers those interfaces.

Our nSD devices embed directly into existing architectures and manufacturing flows. They are licensed as infrastructure and designed to scale with partner systems. We don’t replace partner technology. We make it work better.

Current focus areas include:
	•	advanced chip packaging (interposers and RDLs)
	•	electrochemical interfaces for hydrogen systems
	•	sensing interfaces for field-deployed diagnostics

We work with industry, system integrators, and public-sector partners to pilot, validate, and deploy interface devices that unlock step-changes in performance, reliability, and cost.

Name: European Conference on Integrated Optics (ECIO)
Description:
The annual European Conference on Integrated Optics will take place at Cardiff University, United Kingdom in 2025

The conference focuses on leading edge research on integrated optics, optoelectronics and nano-photonics and gathers experts from academia and industry to show their latest technical results, and showcase their products and services. The application scope is broad and it ranges from tele/datacom communications, optical interconnects, and (bio) optical sensing applications to more disruptive areas as quantum computing and mid-IR photonics.
  
Conference topics cover waveguide technology and platforms, devices and materials, integrated circuits and applications of photonics integrated circuits (PICs). The most recent advances in photonic integration technologies, such as silicon photonics and III-V optoelectronics, is one of the main features of the conference. Assembly, packaging and hybrid integration techniques are also present. In addition, the conference aims to discuss novel concepts on materials with unique properties such as graphene, 2D materials or phase change materials.

EVOQ is currently focused on developing metamaterials for three core industries:   
 
EVOQ Bio has developed an inhaled therapeutic using EVQ-218 for the treatment of pulmonary bacterial infections in patients with cystic fibrosis. Name: Research Institute for Electronic Science (RIES), Hokkaido University
Description:
The Research Institute for Electronic Science (RIES) at Hokkaido University was originally established as the Ultra-Shortwave Research Institute in 1943. It was later renamed the Institute of Applied Electricity in 1946 and reorganized into its current form in 1992. With a history spanning over 80 years, our institute has continuously evolved with the changing times, advancing cutting-edge research that spans optical science, materials science, life sciences, and mathematical sciences under our mission to pioneer new interdisciplinary fields. 

Our research extends beyond pure academic inquiry. In pursuit of a sustainable society, we are dedicated to developing materials and devices that enhance energy efficiency and reduce environmental impact. In 1973, we established the affiliated Electronic Measurement Development Facility (now the Green Nanotechnology Research Center), focusing on creating next-generation environmentally harmonized technologies. Additionally, in 2015, we launched the Social Creativity Mathematics Research Center, integrating mathematical sciences to explore new research frontiers. 

Advancing fundamental research into large-scale interdisciplinary domains requires robust collaboration. Since 2009, we have formed strategic alliances with research institutions such as the Institute of Multidisciplinary Research for Advanced Materials at Tohoku University, the Chemical and Life Science Research Institute at Tokyo University of Science, the Institute of Scientific and Industrial Research at Osaka University, and the Institute for Materials Chemistry and Engineering at Kyushu University. Through these partnerships, we conduct over 400 joint research projects annually, generating numerous groundbreaking scientific achievements. Furthermore, we actively foster international research networks and cultivate young researchers through academic agreements with universities in Europe, the Americas, and Asia.

Name: Collaborative Research Centre SFB 1277 University of Regensburg
Description:
Today’s electronics is mostly based on transport and optical properties of conventional semiconductors and metallic systems. However, in the past few years, novel classes of materials have been identified that might enable a paradigm shift for future electronics. Many of these materials have in common that their itinerant electrons exhibit (pseudo-)relativistic behaviour: in graphene, electrons behave as massless Dirac particles, enabling studies of relativistic phenomena “in a pencil trace”. In so-called topological insulators, the electron spin is locked to the electron momentum due to relativistic spin-orbit coupling. Moreover, relativistic spin-orbit coupling at interfaces, surfaces and in nanostructures gives rise to spin-orbit fields that strongly influence electrical transport and optics and enable novel topological phenomena.

In SFB 1277 we investigate the fundamental properties of these special electronic systems and the emergent relativistic effects they entail. Moreover, our common aim is to explore if and how the Dirac-like band structures and strong spin-orbit coupling in novel material classes and nanostructures can be exploited for future electronic concepts and lead to new, as yet unforeseen functionalities. Our common objective is to uncover electronic, transport, magnetic and optical properties of a variety of such materials and systems. These include molecules, carbon nanotubes and nanowires, two-dimensional crystals, topological insulators and superconductors. We put emphasis not only on steady-state phenomena but in particular also on novel (pseudo-)relativistic effects in the time and frequency domain.

Name: RIANA - project
Description:
RIANA, funded by Horizon Europe, is pioneering the future of nanoscience and nanotechnology through a dual approach: fostering long-term, curiosity-driven research and enabling targeted, challenge-driven projects for immediate to mid-term impact. Central to RIANA is the ARIE network, connecting seven European networks of top-level Research Infrastructures (RIs) to offer comprehensive access to advanced techniques in nanofabrication, processing, characterization, analysis, and simulation. This collaboration provides customized access to 69 infrastructures through a single entry point, supported by a robust scientific and innovation service led by a mix of senior and junior scientists.

RIANA is designed to empower research and innovation, from academia to industry, propelling ideas to higher Technology Readiness Levels (TRLs). Flexible and adaptive, it addresses emergent scientific needs alongside the Nano-community, offering additional infrastructures access and specialized services. With four years of experience, RIANA is crafting a roadmap for the European nanoscience and technology future, emphasizing collaboration, innovation, and sustainability.

Our offerings encapsulate the full nanoscience and technology chain, from simulation to synthesis and analysis, supported by a centralized user office and a dedicated Science Service team. This ensures optimum technique selection, experiment operation, data analysis, and innovation support, particularly for SMEs, aiming to upscale their technologies and processes.

Join RIANA in shaping the future of nanoscience and nanotechnology, driving forward European research and innovation.

Description:

Name: Functional Coating and Surface Engineering Laboratory (FCSEL)
Description:
The FCSEL (Functional Coating and Surface Engineering Laboratory) is an academic research laboratory that focuses on the development of innovative vacuum- and plasma-based processes and materials for coatings and thin film systems with tailored functional and multifunctional characteristics. With a long history of industrial partnerships spanning more than three decades, the FCSEL has developed a unique global (or holistic) approach to technological challenges and surface engineering solutions based on a simultaneous action, within the same laboratory, of several key elements including an in-depth understanding of the application problem and the availability of the appropriate metrology tools (testing methods) to quantitatively assess the coating system’s main functional characteristics as well as stability and durability in a working environment. Only then can an appropriate solution be sought in terms of nanostructured coating materials and their integration into specific coating architectures, while applying suitable fabrication processes, modeling approaches, and life cycle analysis.

Targeted functional coating characteristics include optical, optoelectronic, micro- and nano-mechanical, tribological, and protective properties for their application in various areas, such as optics, photonics, optoelectronics, aerospace and space exploration, biomedicine, automotive industry, security, energy saving, manufacturing and many others.

This access to the most advanced fabrication and characterization equipment under the same roof renders the FCSEL an exclusive highly dynamic and stimulating environment for training the next generation of engineers and scientists who will tackle the world’s present and future challenges. It also offers a stimulating environment for university-industry collaborations and partnerships, as well as punctual services.

Name: DropLab Inc.
Description:
At DropLab, we provide a broad range of innovative solutions to our clients in medicine, research, and pharmacology through the comprehensive design, prototyping, testing, and optimization of microfluidic devices and systems. 

Our solutions enable clients to operate at greater speed, precision, control, and productivity at a lower cost.

Name: BioTitan Nanotechnology
Description: BioTitan Nanotechnology is an Italian company leading in the development of innovative nanotechnological products for surfaces aimed at the transformation and long term improvement of everyday materials. Our products are the results of a careful research and development that boasts collaborations with spin off of the University of Bergamo and other certified bodies.

Name: Cima NanoTech
Description:
Welcome to Cima NanoTech.  

Cima NanoTech develops and manufactures high performance, next-generation transparent conductive film solutions based on our proprietary SANTE self-assembling nanoparticle technology. The rapid commercialization of this unique technology with key partners is enabling new products for a multitude of markets and applications, including large projected capacitive touch screens, transparent antennas for the Internet of Things (IoT) and other applications, transparent EMI shielding and transparent heating. 

Cima Touch is a subsidiary of Cima NanoTech, formed through a joint-venture with Foxconn, that focuses on selling, marketing, and supporting touch solutions for large format projected capacitive touch screens with screen sizes from 40” to over 100”.

Cima NanoTech was awarded "Best New Touch Technology" at the Digital Signage Expo (DSE) 2015, was a Finalist in the "2014 Red Herring North America" award, and won the SID 'Best in Show' award in 2013. The company was also presented with the "World Economic Forum Technology Pioneer" award in 2008.  

Thanks for your interest in our company. Please visit www.cimananotech.com to find out more about our rapidly growing business.

Name: MetaSeismic, Inc.
Description:
"If you want to find the secrets of the Universe, think in terms of Energy, Frequency, and Vibration." Nikola Tesla.  

Inspired by these principles, MetaSeismic is pioneering the creation of acoustic metamaterials and innovative solutions that harness the fundamental power of Energy, Frequency, and Vibration to protect people, goods, and our precious Earth.

Name: Quantum Collaborative
Description:
The Quantum Collaborative applies global expertise in advanced science & technology to transform how work gets done. We envision a world where people have the training on emerging technologies to enable economic mobility for all. 

As a network of researchers, educators, technologists, and academic leaders at Arizona State University powered by the State of Arizona, we recognize it takes a diversity of skills to shape tomorrow’s workforce. Together, we connect the top human talent to the top scientific programs, facilities, and industry partners so that our society can reach its full potential.

Name: Nanophotonics Application & Research Center-Sivas Cumhuriyet University (CÜNAM)
Description:
CÜNAM is the one of the national centers of Turkey in the development of III-V semiconductor lasers, photodedectors and new thin film technologies. Aim of the CÜNAM is to be leading center in national wise and global actor in the international field.

CÜNAM, III-V yarı iletken lazerler, fotodedektörler ve yeni ince film teknolojilerinin geliştirilmesinde Türkiye'nin milli merkezlerinden biridir. CÜNAM'ın amacı,  alanında ulusal lider merkez  ve uluslararası alanda global bir aktör olmaktır.

Name: Janis Research
Description:
In August 2020, Lake Shore Cryotronics acquired Janis Research. The acquisition of the Woburn, MA, based business enables Lake Shore to now offer Janis Research liquid nitrogen (LN2), liquid helium (LHe), and closed-cycle refrigerator (cryogen-free) cryostats, LHe and cryogen-free superconducting magnet systems, cryogenic and cryogen-free probe stations, and various lab cooling systems.

This acquisition by Lake Shore does not include the Janis dilution refrigerator or He-3 ultra-low temperature (ULT) system product lines. These will be available from JanisULT, a separate, privately-owned company. 

Janis combines over 50 years of manufacturing experience with extensive engineering capabilities to provide cryogenic systems for all research applications.  Application specific products include cryostats for optical microscopy, FTIR, and Mössbauer spectroscopy, continuous flow and Helium-3 cryostats, 4 K and 10 K closed-cycle refrigerators, dilution refrigerators, superconducting magnet systems, and micromanipulated probe stations.

Janis Research offers cryogenic systems for a broad range of applications, with cooling provided by liquid helium, liquid nitrogen or mechanical refrigerators. Each cooling mechanism has distinct advantages and preferred applications, and a Janis Research engineer can assist in choosing the best cooling method for your application.

Name: CemeCon Scandinavia A/S
Description:
Development and supply of advanced plasma-based surface coatings/treatments for protecting tools and components.
CemeCon Scandinavia are specialists in advanced coatings of tools, components and wear parts. We offer solutions to tribological problems (tribology is the study of wear, friction and lubrication). Our solutions include hard, hard-wearing, corrosion-resistant and self-lubricating coatings.

Name: NEI Corporation
Description:
Founded in 1997, NEI Corporation originally began as Nanopowder Enterprises Incorporated (or “NEI” for short), creating a strong foundation in the emerging field of nanotechnology. Over the years, the company’s technologies have expanded and grown, enabling NEI to become a world leader in selected markets.
 
Today, NEI develops, manufactures, and sells advanced materials for a broad range of industrial customers around the world. The company’s core competencies are in designing, developing, and producing products that meet the specific application needs of its customers. More importantly, NEI is a solutions provider, wherein we not only produce materials, but also work closely with our customers to implement them in their applications. 
 
NEI’s products, which are sold under the registered trademark NANOMYTE, are backed by a suite of issued and pending patents. NEI’s products include: Functional & Protective Coatings, Lithium-ion Battery Materials, and Specialty Nanoparticle-based products.

Name: Nanogreen
Description: A Nanogreen iniciou sua jornada se especializando na fabricação de nanopartículas através do processo físico de ablação à laser, reconhecido por ser mais limpo e não usar agentes químicos tóxicos à saúde e ao meio ambiente. Foram desenvolvidas nanopartículas personalizadas para as mais diversas aplicações, como eficiência energética, insumos agrários, biomédicos e industriais. Em 2020, a empresa passou por um processo de fusão com outra empresa do ramo de nanotecnologia, a S3nano, e teve suas operações absorvidas pela mesma.

Name: ITEP
Description:
ITEP is a multiprofile center for research and education. It's activity is focused on study of the fundamental properties of matter and their use for the development of new technologies, in particular ecologically-safe sources of energy, energy-saving equipment, telecommunications and medicine.

The main fields of research include theoretical and mathematical physics, astrophysics, high-energy (elementary particle) physics, nuclear physics, plasma physics, solid-state physics, nanotechnologies, reactor and accelerator technoligies, medical physics, computer science.

ITEP has a broad and effective educational program, mostly at the undergraduate and graduate levels, which allows to prepare qualified specialists in science and engeneering.

Name: Pervatech BV
Description:
MEMBRANE SOLUTIONS for the chemical, petro-chemical, pharmaceutical and food industry.

*dehydration of solvents with membranes *azeotrope separation with membranes *VOC removal with membranes *aroma recovery with membranes *esterification with membranes *purification with membranes *concentration of organics with membranes *recycling with membranes *debottlenecking distillation columns with membranes

Our membranes are stable in water at high temperatures, in (a)protic solvents and in the presence of acids and can be used both in stand-alone solutions and in add-on hybrid systems.

PERVATECH BV established in 1999 is a leading company in membrane technology.

The company’s core activities are the development, production and sales of ceramic
membranes for both hydrophilic and organophilic separation processes. Scientific co-operation with
leading Universities and Institutions, both nationally and abroad secures today's and tomorrow’s
ceramic membranes with superior characteristics.

We belief to have an obligation towards future generations to limit the impact on our environment
and to preserve Earth’s non-renewable resources. The membranes, products and services that Pervatech offer
enable our customers to innovate their production processes, leading to lower energy consumption,
higher efficiency, recycling of valuable resources, lower waste streams and higher product quality.

Located in Rijssen, the Netherlands close to the University of Twente, our highly motivated team
works closely together with customers all over the world to find solutions for demanding separation
processes where our membrane technology has substantial advantages over existing solutions.

Please feel free to contact us for more information:

The products of Bang Nanobiotech s.r.l are mainly used for wound repair, tissue regeneration, and skincare routine.
