Name: abberior
Description:
abberior is a company like no other. Founded as a spin-off from Prof. Stefan W. Hell’s laboratory, we have become a world-leading innovator and manufacturer of cutting-edge superresolution and confocal microscopes. We are growing and will continue to grow but we will always maintain the pioneering spirit of Prof. Hell’s lab. We do things differently. We do things better.

As the only manufacturer of superresolution microscopes, abberior develops and sells proprietary fluorescent dyes and labels ideally suited for superresolution microscopies, such as STED, MINFLUX, GSD, PALM, STORM, GSDIM, SIM, and RESOLFT. They also give outstanding results in confocal and epifluorescence microscopy as well as in single-molecule applications. Our mission is to become the leading supplier of fluorescent labels in superresolution microscopy.

Name: European Technology Platform on Nanomedicine (ETPN Association)
Description:
The European Technology Platform on Nanomedicine (ETPN Association), an initiative led by industry and set up together with the European Commission is addressing the application of nanotechnology to achieve breakthroughs in healthcare.
 
Nanomedicine exploits the improved and often novel physical, chemical and biological properties of materials at the nanometer scale. Nanomedicine has the potential to enable early detection and prevention, and to essentially improve diagnosis, treatment and follow-up of diseases.
 
The ETPN Association supports its members in coordinating their joint research efforts and improving communication amongst the members as well as towards the European Commission and the European Member States.

As an outcome of last ETPN General Assembly in October 2014 in San Sebastian, the European Technology Platform for Nanomedicine is now ready to tackle new challenges, to gain in visibility and in credibility with a new legal form.

The ETPN Association with headquarters in Paris has been officially constituted and is now operational.

Name: Fabrica Group
Description: Fabrica Group is a leader in the field of Micro-Additive Manufacturing. Serving similar users of High-Performance Electronic Devices (Hi-PEDs), Nano Dimension’s Fabrica micro additive manufacturing systems enables the production of microparts based on a Digital Light Processor (DLP) engine that achieves repeatable micron levels resolution. Fabrica systems are engineered with a patented array of sensors that allows a closed feedback loop, using proprietary materials to achieve very high accuracy while remaining a cost-effective mass manufacturing solution. It is used in the areas of micron-level resolution of medical devices, micro-optics, semiconductors, micro-electronics, micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS), microfluidics, and life sciences instruments.

Name: OPTOMAN
Description:
OPTOMAN is a manufacturer of custom high power, IBS-coated laser optics for photonic superheroes. 

Laser power keeps increasing and calls for innovative solutions. To meet that demand, OPTOMAN is opening a new office in Folsom, California. Huyen Vu has been appointed CEO and President of OPTOMAN Inc. This expansion is the next step in our growth. Our U.S. customers can now expect faster support while enjoying the same trusted IBS-coated, high-power optics used worldwide.

Complete dedication to quality, professional and reliable services, extensive characterization of optics, these things help OPTOMAN to follow his mission. 

“Serve the People and push the efficiency of lasers and laser systems to the next level.

- OPTOMAN does it right
The best possible accuracy and repeatability. 

- Ion-Beam Sputtering
OPTOMAN manufactures custom optical coatings exclusively with advanced IBS technology. It is perfect solution for small to medium scale prototyping and manufacturing. 

- Partnership
OPTOMAN educates its customers and helps in finding the best possible solution. 

Product portfolio consists of: 
- Ultra high reflectivity dielectric mirrors.
- Mirrors for ultrafast laser systems.
- High damage threshold thin film polarizers.
- High contrast wavelength separators, beam splitters and filters.

Name: Graphenano
Description:
Graphenano Fabricante Nanotecnología - Grafeno | Derivados del grafeno y productos con grafeno.

I+A de productos nanotecnológicos.  Investigación y aplicación de productos nanotecnológicos en productos cotidianos. Comercialización de aplicaciones con grafeno y subproductos.

-
 Graphenano Manufacturer Nanotechnology - Graphene |  Derivatives of graphene and products with graphene.

I+A of nanotechnological products.  Research and application of nanotechnological products in everyday products.  Marketing of applications with graphene and by-products.

Name: Nanotechnology Research Foundation
Description:
Established in July of 2008, The Nanotechnology Research Foundation (NRF) is a volunteer based 501c3 non-profit organization focused on supporting the acceleration of nanotechnology awareness, education, recognition, funding, research and American innovation. We are a catalyst serving as the hub for the advancement of nanotechnologies.

Our stakeholders include business and financial leaders as well as academic and research institutes from the private sector, in addition to government and other non-profit organizations with a common agenda.

The NRF is advocating for increased private and government funding for advanced education and research to preserve American jobs in the ever increasing global competition for high-tech dominance.

Name: TESCAN USA Inc.
Description: TESCAN USA is a trusted source of innovative and disruptive technologies for science and industries such as SEM, FIB-SEM, 4D-STEM, and micro-CT systems. Our mission is to enable scientists and engineers to address global and sustainability challenges with innovative instrumentation that discovers the secrets of matter at the micro and nanoscale.

Name: Microcity - Neuchâtel Innovation
Description:
Microcity est le pôle d’excellence et d’innovation dans les micro-nanotechnologies, la micro-fabrication et les processus d’industrialisation.
Les membres du pôle d’innovation neuchâtelois réunissent des compétences académiques, de recherche et d'industrialisation de niveau international.  Les membres sont notamment: l'Ecole Polytechnique Fédéral de Lausanne (EPFL), le Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM); la He-Arc, l'Université de Neuchâtel (UNINE),  la Fondation Suisse pour la recherche en microtechnique (FSRM), le Centre de formation professionnel neuchâtelois (CPNE), les communes du Canton ainsi que l'Etat de Neuchâtel.

Microcity c'est une SA à but non lucratif qui propose des services d'hébergement, de coaching, des événement de réseautage mais aussi des services de project management spécialisés, des assessment technologiques et la création d'écosystèmes technologiques riches pour les PME et les instituts de recherches et d'enseignement. 

 
 - un rayonnement national et international de capacités d’innovation, notamment à travers le Swiss Innovation Park - SIP.

Name: Protochips
Description: Protochips empowers scientists, engineers, and researchers to discover and analyze new phenomenon by visualizing biological, chemical and physical processes in completely new ways. Our field-proven products combine in situ experimental control with the analysis and resolution capabilities of the electron microscope, improving productivity and generating actionable data to accelerate discovery.

Name: Swiss Nanoscience Institute (SNI), University of Basel
Description:
The Swiss Nanoscience Institute (SNI) at the University of Basel is a center of excellence for nanosciences and nanotechnology. It was founded in 2006 by the University of Basel and the Swiss Canton Aargau in order to support research, education, and technology transfer in nanoscience and nanotechnology in Northwestern Switzerland. The SNI focuses on the core areas of nanoimaging and nanofabrication in order to address challenges in the fields of materials science, quantum science, life science, medicine and environment.
For alumni of the nanoscience study program, we have created a specific group: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/8278038/

Name: Custom Processing Services, Inc.
Description:
CPS loves custom toll processing and being the masters of particle technology. We use the smallest things to make big marketplace impacts for our customers. Together, we think ahead, move faster, and win more. We’ve been a custom toll processor for 20+ years, yet we’re still thrilled by new challenges, helping us become more experienced in particle reduction.

While some toll processors are simple “order takers,” we welcome a challenge, from concept to commercialization. Our fully committed problem-solvers find solutions, and our team handles customers’ needs for size reduction, contract drying, blending, extraction, extrusion, specialty services, and cGMP processing — food-grade and pharmaceutical processing. 

Our goal is to fast-forward your projects and exceed your business goals using superior particle reduction. We have the certifications and affiliations you expect from an industry leader. And, our strategic alliances with world-class organizations keep us at the top of our game. Being the Architects of What’s Possible means our particle technology mastery bridges the gap between business today and what is possible tomorrow.

Name: Intermolecular
Description:
We are the trusted partner for materials innovation.  We explore, test and develop advanced materials that are revolutionizing the next generation of electronics that make lives easier, entertaining and more productive.  

For more than 15 years, our team, methodologies and quality data have driven impactful outcomes, market opportunities and innovative product designs for our customers.  As the Silicon Valley science hub of Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany, we are perfectly positioned to break the boundaries of science and technology.  

Intermolecular is an affiliate of Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany which has 57,000 employees worldwide. Merck is 70% privately owned and 30% publicly listed. We advance digital living!

Name: Advanced Material Development
Description:
Advanced Material Development (AMD) is a UK-based advanced materials company focused on the commercialisation of proprietary nanomaterial technologies across life sciences, smart packaging, advanced manufacturing, and defence applications.

AMD’s technology platforms — including nHance, a printable functional nanomaterial system, and nLight, a polymeric photonic crystal platform — enable a wide range of applied solutions such as advanced medical diagnostics, pharmaceutical monitoring systems, non-metallic RFID, and high-performance industrial coatings.

The company works in close collaboration with global industrial partners and OEMs to bring these technologies to market through licensing, joint development, and long-term commercial partnerships, allowing AMD’s innovations to scale efficiently within existing manufacturing ecosystems.

AMD’s focus is on translating validated scientific innovation into real-world, scalable industrial solutions, supporting customers as they address complex challenges across healthcare, logistics, manufacturing, and security.

Name: Graphene-XT
Description:
Graphene-XT is an innovative startup focused on graphene applications. The company holds a new graphene production technology based on simple and green chemistry. 
Its production of graphene water based solution is suitable for many industrial processes using high rate of raw material recycling without the use of toxic products.
Graphene-XT vision is to develop and commercialize graphene applications crossing disparate categories, from electronic to medicine, passing trough composite, paint etc.
Graphene-XT main activities are materials treatment with graphene derivates and graphene master batch based on customer’s requirements.
Graphene-XT graphene is so versatile that can be applied as coating or filler on many materials, like plastics, metals, ceramic or glass and can be printed even at industrial scale on any of these substrates.

Name: Delong America
Description:
Delong Instruments is a pioneering company dedicated to advancing electron microscopy through innovation, accessibility, and precision. Founded in Brno, Czech Republic, a global hub for electron optics. Our mission is to democratize high-performance electron microscopy, making it practical and affordable for universities, research centers, and industry laboratories worldwide. For over three decades, Delong Instruments has specialized in compact, reliable electron microscopes that push the boundaries of what is possible at low accelerating voltages.

At the heart of our portfolio is the LVEM 5, the world’s first and only benchtop transmission electron microscope. Designed for powerful imaging in a remarkably small footprint, the LVEM 5 brings electron microscopy into laboratories that might otherwise lack the space, infrastructure, or budget for a conventional high-voltage instrument. Operating at low voltages, it enables fast, routine nanomaterials characterization and versatile imaging across TEM, SEM, STEM, and ED modes.

Building on this success, the LVEM 25E delivers the next generation of low voltage TEM. Combining the high resolution of field emission technology with the advantages of low voltage imaging, it produces nanometer-scale detail while preserving contrast in delicate or unstained samples. With optional EDS for chemical analysis, the LVEM 25E offers advanced performance while remaining easy to install, operate, and maintain. Applications range from clinical pathology and pharmaceutical R&D to advanced materials characterization.

What sets Delong apart is our philosophy: high-quality imaging should be within reach of every laboratory. Our microscopes require minimal infrastructure and are practical for a wide variety of environments. By lowering barriers to access, we empower researchers, educators, and innovators to explore the nanoscale with confidence.

Delong Instruments is your way to electron microscopy.

Name: Universal Matter GBR Ltd
Description: Universal Matter is a rapidly emerging global leader in advanced applied materials dedicated to transforming industries with innovative, high-quality sustainable solutions that help decarbonize our planet.

Name: Graphene-Info
Description: Graphene-Info has been the leading international graphene publication for over 5 years, with a readership of tens of thousands of professionals a month. We provide a multitude of services to the graphene market based on our extensive and up-to-date knowledge hub and close ties with industry leaders. Our consultancy services include market outreach assistance, nanomaterials brokerage, support for graphene initiatives, business development and more.

Name: AMO GmbH
Description:
AMO GmbH is a German high-tech company that acts as a research- and fabrication-service provider in the field of nanotechnology.

We offer research and fabrication services, from proof-of-concept demonstrators to final technological solutions. The main part of the nanotechnology lab is devoted to the application of advanced nanolithography techniques (UV- Nanoimprint, E-Beam lithography, Interference Lithography), and it is embedded within a highly flexible cleanroom nvironment.

As a service provider, AMO offers its advanced nanostructuring capabilities for industrial R&D in the areas MEMS, NEMS, photonics, sensor technology as well as bio-fuels and biotechnology. 

We are always keenly looking  for 

- new innovative projects 
- new partners  
- new committed employees 

If you're looking for special vacancies, please check the AMO job page:  
https://www.amo.de/en/careers/


Header picture by  www.eventfotograf.in / JRF e.V.

Name: Sanki Global
Description:
We are an organization that seeks to provide wellness through our products BelAge, Kronuit and Inner 7. Sanki is the ideal company if you want to live your dreams.

Through our innovative and unique business model, we create entrepreneurs willing to be successful in Network Marketing. We have a Comprehensive Wellness Program (CWP) unique in the industry to develop your career plan with specialized training that allows you to increase your business and leadership skills towards achieving your goals and objectives.

We have been recognized twice as a Great Place to Work and we are certified for Gender Equity Model awarded by the National Institute of Women (Inmujeres).

We have international offices in France, Japan and the United States.

Name: Nemo Lab IIT Bombay
Description:
At NEMO Lab, we are engaged in mulifaceted research. We utilize both experimental as well as theoretical approach in understanding various aspects of materials and metastructures.

Principal Investigators: Prof. Sumit Saxena and Prof. Shobha Shukla

Name: XTPL
Description:
XTPL is paving the way in innovation, revolutionising industries with our groundbreaking additive technology solutions. Our journey began with rigorous research and development, and now we're shaping the future with our technological expertise.

Our innovative approach is evident in our patented Ultra-Dispensing System (UPD) and range of nanoinks and nanopastes, which offer unparalleled precision and efficiency in microelectronics applications.

As a total supplier, we offer tailored end-to-end solutions that cater to our clients' unique needs. From concept to final product, we provide the support, expertise, and innovative solutions that ensure success.

We continuously evolve and expand our portfolio of patented solutions, showcasing our commitment to lead the microelectronics revolution. At XTPL, we don't just envision the future, we create it.

XTPL. Powering the microelectronics of tomorrow.

Name: POSITIVE COATING
Description:
Fondée en 2004, POSITIVE COATING se distingue par le développement de solutions innovantes dans le domaine des traitements de surfaces basés sur les technologies les technologies PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition) et ALD (Atomic Layer Deposition). La société compte aujourd'hui une trentaine de collaborateurs qualifiés sur son site de production et une infrastructure à la pointe de la technologie. 
Sa capacité de production industrielle, soutenue par un département d'engineering orienté vers l'innovation lui permet d'offrir son savoir-faire à une large clientèle. POSITIVE COATING possède un véritable savoir-faire sur les revêtements décoratifs pour les articles à forte valeur ajoutée réalisés en métaux et céramiques. 
POSITIVE COATING jouit d’une réputation de partenaire fiable et dynamique. L’entreprise est reconnue par les plus grandes maisons leaders dans le domaine du luxe et principalement dans le secteur de l’horlogerie. Elle propose également ses services dans les secteurs de la maroquinerie, des instruments d’écriture et de la bijouterie.
Son avenir est également très prometteur dans le domaine de la technologie médicale. POSITIVE COATING intervient dans ce secteur sur des projets liés aux dispositifs implantables et aux applications biomédicales. 
Placée au cœur d’une industrie microtechnique prestigieuse, l’entreprise contribue à l’élaboration de produits technologiques d’excellence.

Name: INCeNSE
Description: INCeNSE, the Technology Business Incubator (TBI) at The Centre for Nano Science and Engineering (CeNSE), Indian Institute of Science(IISc), Bangalore is funded by Government of Karnataka to support ideas from entrepreneurs and start-ups (not restricted to IISc alumni) in nanotechnology and other inter-disciplinary deep science & technology areas.

Name: Delmic
Description:
Delmic is a passionate high-tech company based in Delft, the Netherlands that develops powerful and user-friendly solutions for electron microscopy, bringing researchers and organizations closer to research insights across diverse application fields.  

Integration of light and electron microscopy fundamentally impacts imaging by creating the possibility to gain deep insight quickly, easily and reliably. Delmic’s mission is to empower researchers and companies by helping them achieve results that can be trusted implicitly with powerful and user-friendly solutions. Currently, Delmic has two main product lines: cathodoluminescence solutions (SPARC Spectral, SPARC Compact and JOLT) and correlative light and electron microscopy solutions (SECOM, SECOM SR, DELPHI). All the systems cater to a broad range of researchers in fields ranging from life science, geology, materials science to nanophotonics.

The company is currently working on two new projects: one is aimed to simplify the complex workflows involved in Cryo electron tomography; another project will help to increase imaging speed without sacrificing image quality with an automated multibeam scanning electron microscope.

At Delmic, we believe that imaging provides a fundamental understanding of systems and is an integral part of creating a better world, therefore we aim to continuously innovate in order to offer our customers powerful insights and make research easy with simple workflows.

Name: NenoVision
Description:
NenoVision is a Czech technology company specializing in the development and production of LiteScope AFM-in-SEM: a unique Atomic Force Microscope (AFM) designed for seamless integration into Scanning Electron Microscopes (SEM).

LiteScope broadens the present possibilities of electron microscopy and enables simultaneous scanning of samples by different techniques. The acquired images are correlated into resulting 3D image with the use of novel CPEM technology.

Name: Axoft
Description: Developing a seamless bioelectronic interface.

Name: asphericon
Description:
Bringing visions to life.
With this mission, asphericon is continuously pushing the boundaries of what is possible and establishing new milestones in the world of optics. asphericon‘s passion for revolutionizing the manufacturing of aspheric components with new technologies is changing the degree of precision and quality that is possible.

About the company:
Founded at the Friedrich Schiller University of Jena in 2001, asphericon has developed into a technological leader in the field of asphere manufacturing thanks to a great deal of expertise and passion.
asphericon combines a researcher’s pioneering spirit with the practical ability to implement customer-oriented, economical solutions.
The latest manufacturing technologies, continually evolving proprietary control software, world class measurement equipment, and highly qualified employees ensure that asphericon provides over 800 customers worldwide with innovative solutions.
We will assist you from the initial optical design, through manufacturing and coating, precision metrology and documentation, to the assembly of optical modules and optical characterization.

Learn more at www.asphericon.com

Imprint at: www.asphericon.com/en/imprint
Information on data privacy: www.asphericon.com/en/data-privacy

Name: microTAS
Description: The 30th International Conference on Miniaturized Systems for Chemistry and Life Sciences (µTAS 2030) - where minds and microfluidics meet

Name: Directa Plus
Description:
Directa Plus is one of the largest producers and suppliers worldwide of graphene-based products for use in consumer and industrial markets.

Our graphene-based products are natural, chemical-free and sustainably produced. They are found in commercial applications such as smart textiles, tyres, composite materials and environmental solutions, and they’re suitable for many other uses as well. 

We partner with our customers to enable them to offer the high-performance benefits of graphene, identified by the G+ brand, in their own products.

Established in 2005, our company has a unique and patented technology process and a scalable and portable manufacturing model. We produce graphene-based products at our own factory near Milan, Italy, and can set up additional production at customer locations to reduce transport costs, waste and time-to-utilisation.

Discover more on www.directa-plus.com.

Name: Interdisciplinary Nanoscience Center - Aarhus University
Description: The interdisciplinary Nanoscience Center (iNANO) is a collaboration between research groups at the Institute of Physics and Astronomy, Department of Chemistry, Department of Molecular Biology, and Institute for Biological Sciences at University of Aarhus, several institutes at the Faculty of Health Sciences at the University of Aarhus, as well as groups from the Faculty of Science, Aalborg University.

Name: Research & Innovation Center for Graphene and 2D Materials (RIC2D)
Description:
The Research & Innovation Center for Graphene and 2D Materials, or RIC2D as we are more known, was set up at Khalifa University in 2022 to advance the commercial deployment of graphene and other layered (2D) materials’ technologies through industry and solution focused scientific research and development in the United Arab Emirates. 

RIC2D, which represents a strategic investment of the Government of Abu Dhabi, intends to serve as the keystone of an advanced materials innovation ecosystem in the region.

Five objectives define RIC2D’s contributions to the UAE’s continuing development priorities:
• Economic: focusing on innovative companies and the knowledge-based economy
• Societal: addressing key UAE challenges including security, water, environment and energy
• Human capital: building a dynamic and sustainable R&D workforce
• Infrastructural: enabling world-class R&D and extending capabilities via global partnerships and alliances
• Knowledge: generating new scientific findings and underpinning future industrial development

Name: Bergen Carbon Solutions AS
Description: Bergen Carbon Solutions is the world’s clean carbon technology and materials company. The company is making CO2 a valuable resource, using renewable energy to turn the excessive greenhouse gas into solid carbon. With its groundbreaking, proven process for carbon capture and utilization (CCU), Bergen Carbon Solutions is enabling clean carbon for green manufacturing. The technology is ensuring stable delivery of carbon nanotubes and graphite, helping manufacturers to be less exposed to the raw material risks of geopolitical rivalry. Established in 2016, based in Norway and listed on Euronext Growth, Bergen Carbon Solutions is targeting the fast-growing battery industry globally, and other carbon intensive industries looking to replace conventional, fossil based solutions to stay relevant in their markets.

Name: UpNano GmbH
Description:
As innovation leader in the field of high-resolution 3D printing,
we see it as our mission to upscale nanofabrication.

UpNano is a Vienna (Austria) based high-tech company with the focus on development, manufacturing and commercialization of high-resolution 3D-printers. The systems are based on 2-photon-polymerization which offer industry-leading speed and resolution down to 0.2 µm. UpNano is committed to providing customers with a holistic package of hardware, software and optimized printing materials, for the fabrication of polymeric microparts as well as the unique possibility of bioprinting in a native cell environment. Using UpNano’s cutting-edge technology makes it possible to print objects with sizes ranging from the sub-micrometer to the centimeter range and up to 40 mm in height – within times and precision never achieved before. 

UpNano was founded in September 2018 as a spin off of the TU Wien and has since developed into an established provider of 3D printing systems for research facilities, universities and businesses worldwide.

Name: Scienta Omicron
Description:
Scienta Omicron is a leading innovator in surface science and nanotechnology. We provide top capabilities in electron spectroscopy, scanning probe microscopy and thin film deposition, all in ultra-high vacuum (UHV). Focusing on the race for new unique materials and solutions, in areas like – smarter batteries, next generation electronics, quantum technologies, solar energy, intelligent sensors and advanced materials Scienta Omicron drives the surface science towards the future. Please contact (https://scientaomicron.com/en/contacts) your nearest sales representative with any questions you may have.

►Follow us on 
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/ScientaOmicron 
Twitter: https://twitter.com/scienta_omicron 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/scientaomicron/
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/user/OmicronNanoTech

Name: DENSsolutions
Description:
DENSsolutions is your dedicated partner for in situ electron microscopy research. We develop and deliver state-of-the-art in situ solutions for cooling, heating, biasing, gas and liquid, allowing you to perform meaningful research at the nanoscale with a tremendous impact on a global scale.

Using MEMS-based sample carriers, DENSsolutions upgrades your TEM into a laboratory for nanotechnology that unveils the evolutionary nanoscale dynamics of your sample. With our dedicated technology, software and service, we support you from sample management to data analysis, promising reliability and accuracy every time.

Name: CTNano UFMG
Description:
O CTNano é um centro de tecnologia (CT) em nanomateriais, que tem como foco o desenvolvimento de produtos, processos e serviços a partir dessas classes de materiais de estrutura nanométrica. De portas abertas para o desenvolvimento de projetos sob demanda, o CTNano é formado a partir de tecnologias geradas na Universidade Federal de Minas Gerais (UFMG). Seu objetivo é atender demandas da indústria e, com o apoio do Governo do Estado, o Centro já conquistou vários projetos nas mais diversas áreas.
Temos como principal motivação o fomento da competitividade da indústria nacional a partir do desenvolvimento tecnológico de ponta. Nossa visão é servir como plataforma para transferência de tecnologia e geração de sociedades empresárias. Atualmente o CTNano tem uma sede de aproximadamente 3mil m² no Parque Tecnológico de Belo Horizonte (BH-TEC).

Name: Nelumbo, A Kuraray Company
Description: Nelumbo is commercializing a new platform of structural materials. The company’s current focus is on developing improved heat exchangers for the HVAC market. Our ability to improve energy performance and minimize corrosion in a single platform unlocks mass market opportunity with potential for significant carbon emission and energy use reductions worldwide.

Name: The Australian National Fabrication Facility (ANFF)
Description:
For more than 15 years, the Australian National Fabrication Facility (ANFF) has been enabling research through a mixture of training, expert support and direct access in order to assist its clients to pursue their projects in a time- and cost-effective manner. 

Established under the National Collaborative Research Infrastructure Strategy (NCRIS), ANFF's 21 Australian locations provide academia and industry with access to more than 500 state-of-the-art micro/nanofabrication facilities.

From proving principles to producing prototypes all the way through to production, the network is helping to bring about new generations of medical devices, advanced materials, electronics, photonics, and more.

Name: CDMF - Centro de Desenvolvimento de Materiais Funcionais
Description:
A característica mais importante desse centro é a multiplicidade de suas missões. Além da missão primordial de desenvolver pesquisa em materiais cerâmicos, básica ou voltada para a inovação, focalizada em objetivos específicos, o centro tem como missão não menos importante, desenvolver mecanismos efetivos de transferência de conhecimento para a sociedade.

Entre os aspectos inovadores do Centro salienta-se o componente educacional, em todos níveis, de sua missão. Além de desenvolver os programas de iniciação científica e de pós-graduação, vinculados a programas de renome como UNESP-Araraquara, Bauru, Presidente Prudente, Ilha Solteira, Guaratinguetá, UFSCar, USP-São Carlos, São Paulo, UFSCar, LNLS, UNIFESP, UFABC, FacTI, CTI/NCTI e IPEN, cabe também ao CDMF realizar atividades de extensão na área de educação básica, tais como iniciação científica para alunos e professores de segundo grau, treinamento de professores e cursos de difusão científica.

Name: Mexicana de Nanotecnología
Description:
Empresa 100% Mexicana formuladora y fabricante de soluciones químicas especializadas, con base en nanotecnología.

#MEXNA continuamente Innova el mercado de soluciones químicas enfocadas a los actores industrial, comercial y de protección para vehículos con su amplia gama de especialidades químicas, las cuales se adaptan a las necesidades y/o características de cada ambiente y proceso de operación requerido por el cliente. 

En #MEXNA contamos con tres marcas principales y enfocadas a los sectores de mayor impacto y necesidad:

GKKO | Auntomotriz & Nano Detailing Car
NANO ARQ | Soluciones Arquitectónicas
MIC | Soluciones especializadas Industriales

En #MEXNA contamos con una amplia y basta experiencia en manipulación de Nano-recubrimientos y químicos de última generación, capaces de cumplir más allá de las expectativas de mantenimiento y protección deseadas; ayudando a reducir costos por consumo de materiales y mano de obra, incorporando características de protección para la conservación de la vida, disminuyendo radicalmente o eliminando los riesgos de salud por contacto directo Humano y del medio ambiente, entre otras ventajas competitivas de alto valor.

“En #MEXNA Lo tenemos cubierto!”

Name: Elmarco
Description:
Elmarco is the world’s leading supplier of industrial electrospinning equipment for producing nanofibrous layers.

With our patented Nanospider technology, we transform laboratory innovations into scalable industrial manufacturing, enabling customers to integrate nanofibers into products across:

▻ Air & liquid filtration (HEPA, HVAC, water purification, oil separation)

▻ Medical applications (wound care, drug delivery systems)

▻ Energy storage (battery separators, fuel cells)

▻ Functional textiles (performance apparel, protective clothing)

Customers choose Elmarco because we provide more than equipment. We deliver complete solutions: polymer development, lab sampling, cost modelling, line customization, installation, training, and long-term service.

With 20+ years of innovation, 100+ patents, and over 250 installed production lines worldwide, Elmarco is the trusted partner for turning nanofiber research into industrial reality.

▻ Let’s create your competitive advantage — Think BIG, Go NANO.

Name: Swiss Cluster
Description:
Swiss Cluster AG develops innovative coating equipment for R & D and industrial production. 

Our novel and revolutionary designs challenge the industry standard in order to improve industrial process times and film quality in ALD coatings to medium to large 3D parts in different markets (semiconductor, medical, watch, optics) or fabricate new material systems by combining different deposition techniques with our SC-1 series systems. 
 
We offer R & D services and customized solutions for coating equipment, hardware/software and processes with our novel approach. 
  
Our goal is to enable researchers and companies with the tools to accelerate their next materials breakthroughs.

Name: Nanoco Technologies Ltd
Description:
Nanoco is a world leader in the development and production of cadmium-free quantum dots and other nanomaterials for use in multiple applications including LCD displays, sensing, lighting, solar cells, bio-imaging and paints and pigments.

Nanoco’s unique and patented volume production technology, referred to as the “molecular seeding process”, allows for the production of uniform, high quality quantum dots on a large scale. Moreover, Nanoco’s quantum dots, called CFQD quantum dots,  are free from cadmium and any other heavy-metals so they are ideally suited for use in all consumer electronic devices.

One of the key application areas for CFQD quantum dots is in LCD displays and LED lighting. In LCD displays, the technology can not only dramatically enhance the colour performance of the screen but also allows manufacturers to use their existing infrastructure. Similarly, CFQD quantum dots can be used to control the colour temperature and improve the colour quality of LED lighting.

Nanoco was founded in 2001 and is headquartered in Runcorn, UK. It has business development executives in the USA, with additional resource in Asia. Its technology is protected worldwide by a large and growing patent estate.

Nanoco is listed on the main market of the London Stock Exchange and trades under the ticker symbol NANO. For further information please visit: www.nanocogroup.com.

Name: American Nano Society
Description:
As the largest scientific society covering all areas of nanotechnology, American Nano Society provides a friendly environment for people involved in this emerging field to communicate with counterparts in a specialized community.

Membership in the Society is not open to public at this time. For joining the Society, you need to be sponsored by a member. You can search in the Members Directory to find a colleague to sponsor you for membership in the American Nano Society.

Name: Waterloo Institute for Nanotechnology
Description: The Waterloo Institute for Nanotechnology (WIN) is a global leader in discovering and developing smart and functional materials, connected devices, next generation energy systems and, therapeutics and theranostics. These discoveries by our scientists and engineers are fundamentally changing our world and helping solve some of humanity's most pressing issues. Our 285, 000 square foot, state-of-the-art facility meets the highest scientific standards for control of vibration, electromagnetic radiation, temperature and humidity making it a global centre of excellence for nanotechnology and its applications.

Name: shinephi
Description:
shinephi is a spin-off project incubating at ICFO - The Institute of Photonic Sciences. shinephi aims to revolutionise microscopy and high-precision imaging with its ultra-sensitive and easy-to-integrate phase-imaging technology, allowing its users to image beyond their current capabilities and expand their range of applications.
shinephi has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 101034765

Name: NanoBio Conference
Description:
The 3rd International conference on Nanotechnologies and BioNanoScience (NanoBio 2025) – aims to capitalize on the rapid growing and interconnected fields of Nanotechnology and Bioscience. The aim is to provide an interdisciplinary forum for scientists and engineers from academia and industry around the world, to discuss on the forefront of research and to address the emerging fields of Nanotechnologies and Nanosciences. The conference will focus on nano(bio)materials synthesis, innovative solar cells, nano(bio)photonics, optoelectronics, nano(bio)electronics tissue engineering, nanomedicine and safety of nanomaterials.

The main objective of the conference is to accommodate a wide range of interests and to facilitate interdisciplinary interaction in both academia and industry. The conference will also provide an ideal environment to develop new collaborations and provide opportunities to meet experts working on various areas of Nanotechnologies and Nanosciences.

NanoBio 2025 will host world leading Plenary and Invited speakers characterized by excellence, innovation, creativity, as well as ‘research lovers’ aiming at the education of young researchers. Their talks will be a platform for ‘food for thought’ for early-career researchers, young investigators and curious students. The conference encourages presentation of oral as well as poster contributions from scientists from all over the world.

Description: sp3 Diamond Technologies is a global leader in polycrystalline CVD diamond products and services. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Santa Clara, California, USA, the privately held company provides diamond products for advanced thermal management and cutting tool applications, diamond coating and material services, hot filament CVD reactors and deposition consulting services.

Description:

Principal Investigator of the Group is Prof. Hannes Schniepp.

Name: GdR Or-Nano
Description: Or-nano est un réseau de chercheurs centré sur les nanoparticules d’or, soutenu par le CNRS comme Groupement de Recherche, depuis janvier 2017 (GdR n° 2002). Il regroupe environ 800 chercheurs de 74 équipes différentes en France.

Name: Centrum NanoBioMedyczne UAM - NanoBioMedical Centre AMU
Lifesome Therapeutics
Description: Lifesome Therapeutics is a Nanotechnology Startup based in Madrid.  We have developed an innovative nanovector - LIFESOME - that for the first time combines anti-tumoral, anti-metastatic and anti-microbial activities with no associated toxicity. Lifesome is also a multifunctional nanoparticle capable of incorporating any drug irrespective of its structure or hydrophobicity (cocktail of active drugs), opening up the possibility to personalized treatments that respond to the patient individual needs.

Name: Institute of Materials in Electrical Engineering 1
Description: As part of RWTH Aachen University, we conduct research at the cutting edge of micro and nanotechnology as we strive for innovative solutions. Our international team operates in the transdisciplinary fields of biomedicine and biotechnology, environmental sensor technology, and Industry 4.0, combining the strengths of researchers from most different areas.

Name: Nano Engineered Applications, Inc.
Description:
Nano Engineered Applications, Inc.TM (NEA) is an ieCrowdTM Company established to commercialize patented and patent-pending sensor technologies based on nanomaterials that can detect airborne gases at the parts-per-billion (ppb) level. NEA’s technologies, if commercialized, could provide a product platform encompassing multiple consumer, commercial, industrial and security-related products with a variety of applications, including air quality monitoring, explosives detection, security-related uses, industrial plant toxic gas detection, gaseous chemical warfare agent detection, food and agricultural safety uses, and other applications.

NEA licenses the underlying technology, developed at University of California, Riverside, under a license from the Board of Regents of the University of California. Funding from the National Institutes of Health (NIH), Center for Nanoscience Innovation for Defense (CNID), Defense MicroElectronics Activity (DMEA) and other sources was used for research relating to this technology prior to NEA obtaining the license.

The current portfolio consists of two granted patents and four pending applications.

Name: Bonsai Advanced Technologies
Description:
Bonsai Advanced Technologies es el nexo de unión perfecto entre las soluciones tecnológicas de los mejores fabricantes del mundo y las personas que las necesitan, con el compromiso de dar un soporte y asistencia técnica de excelencia . Operamos en España, Francia, Suiza y Portugal. 

Nuestra actividad de venta y servicio técnico de equipos e instrumentación para investigación e industria se organiza en 2 segmentos:

-	ANALISIS ESPECTRAL: Analizadores NIR, Imagen Hiperespectral, Remote Sensing.

Thanks to a high level of knowledge, innovative powers and hi-tech equipment, Lasertec also has strategic and technological collaborations with R&D departments.

Name: Nanotechnology Forum India
Description: Nanotechnology forum for Indian Scientists is formed to encourage researchers in India conducting research in the field of Nanotechnology. The committee of this forum will initiate programs to enhance growth in this industry and acknowledge outstanding achievements.

Name: Institut Photonique
Description:
L'Institut Photonique est un projet soutenu par des établissements d’enseignement supérieur de premier plan (CentraleSupélec, l’Université de Lorraine, l’Université de Technologie de Troyes, l’Université de Strasbourg, l’Université de Haute Alsace, l’Université du Luxembourg), des acteurs de l’innovation (SAYENS, Grand Est Développement), une grande entreprise (GDI simulation), la région Grand Est, le département de la Moselle et l’Eurométropole de Metz. Il est le reflet d’une ambition collective visant à éclairer l’avenir grâce à la science de la lumière.

L’Institut Photonique s'est donné 4 missions principales :

Recherche
Favoriser la collaboration entre les acteurs académiques et industriels en réunissant des chercheurs de diverses disciplines pour développer des solutions qui répondent aux enjeux sociétaux actuels et futurs.

Transfert de technologies
Accélérer la valorisation de la recherche et le transfert de technologies vers le marché.

Formation
Mettre en lumière la photonique auprès des jeunes générations et attirer de nouveaux talents dans ce domaine.

Réseau
Réunir chercheurs, étudiants, entreprises, et provoquer la rencontre des ambitions.

applying advanced nanotechnology to energy storage.

Description: MATERIX is a nanotechnology-focused startup that develops and manufactures functional thin films for a sustainable future. Name: NanoLockin

Name: SURAGUS GmbH
Description: SURAGUS develops non-destructive testing devices for innovative measurement tasks based on the eddy current technology. The EddyCus TF series (Thin-Film) as well as the EddyCus CF series (Carbon Fiber) enable both inline and offline characterization of functional thin-films, metal coatings, wafer-based systems, and carbon fiber materials. There are different testing devices such as single-point measurement devices, inline systems, and mapping solutions to fulfill the respective testing task adequately. Additionally, SURAGUS offers conductivity mappings for defect identification and quality assurance of single and multi-layer systems.

Name: Zeppelin Metrology
Description:
Expertos en inspección, metrología óptica de precisión 2D/3D, microscopía electrónica y tomografía computerizada Distribuidores certificados de TESCAN, Bruker ALICONA y AshVision en España y Portugal.

Nuestras soluciones de metrología óptica 2D/3D y microscopia electrónica son ideales para los sectores aeroespacial, automoción, electrónica, fabricación aditiva, microfabricación, prótesis ortopédicas, sector farmacéutico, implantología dental, herramientas de corte, moldes de inyección, mecanizado de precisión, pulido y texturizado laser... Todo sector que requiera alta precisión a la vez que alta productividad.

Con la tecnología de Bruker ALICONA nos focalizamos en metrología óptica 3D (sin contacto) para verificación de piezas tanto en laboratorio como en entornos de producción. Medimos forma, tamaño, posición y rugosidad en superficies e interiores, incluso en miniaturizaciones complejas. En producción automatizamos los procesos de medición de dimensión, tolerancia, rugosidad y textura superficial (industria 4.0 / smart manufacturing).

Con la tecnología de Ashvision disponemos de sistemas de inspección óptica y metrología dimensional 2D, pudiendo generar o importar ficheros DXF. Ofrecemos desde una solución sencilla y económica hasta la última tecnología de inspección automática por inteligencia artificial.

Con la tecnología de TESCAN proporcionamos soluciones para el microanálisis con microscopia electrónica y tomografía de rayos X. Aplicables al control de calidad industrial, análisis forense, ciencia de materiales. Microscopios electrónicos para ciencias de la vida y aplicaciones biomédicas 2D y 3D. Disponemos de los más avanzados equipos para nanotecnología e investigación: SEM, FESEM, micro-CT y FIB para preparación de lamelas TEM y reconstrucción 3D.

Contáctenos! Le haremos un estudio gratuito del valor y ventajas que podemos aportarle, configurándole una solución específica para sus necesidades.

Email: socialmedia@zeppelinmet.com 
Tel.: +34 643 865 853
Web: www.zepelinmet.com

Name: Molecular Rebar Design
Description:
Molecular Rebar Design, LLC (MRD), based in Austin, Texas, was established to develop and commercialize a breakthrough form of modified carbon nanotubes, called Molecular Rebar. These are the world’s first CNT’s that are disentangled from the usual clumping and individualized through a patent-pending process which enables significantly enhanced performance for a myriad of high-value materials.  

Since 2009, MRD has focused on integrating our breakthrough technology into strategic markets while continuing to innovate and develop new and improved technologies. Whether it is enabling significant strength and longevity in rubber and plastic products, redefining performance limitations in both lead and lithium batteries, or working with global industry leading companies to explore new fields of application, Molecular Rebar untangles potential and enables performance.

Name: VinTech Nano Materials
Description: VinTech Nano Materials is a nanotechnology manufacturer of specialty lubricants, chemicals, protective coatings and cutting tools. The patented multi-functional products improve material performance and maximize customer productivity and profit with safe, competitively priced, nano-engineered solutions. For their oil field, transportation and industrial customers, the products provide superior longevity through reduced downtime, improved asset protection, broader operating performance and increased efficiency versus other standard technologies.

Name: UW Washington Nanofabrication Facility (WNF)
Description: The Washington Nanofabrication Facility (WNF) at the University of Washington (UW) is a publicly-accessible, full-service, micro and nanotechnology user facility in the National Science Foundation’s National Nanotechnology Coordinated Infrastructure (NNCI) network. With 15,000 square feet of recently renovated cleanroom, laboratory and office spaces, this state-of-the-art facility offers UW researchers, outside academics, government agencies, and industry users the tools and capabilities necessary to conduct basic and applied research for a wide variety of applications from silicon photonics to biomedical devices. Moreover, WNF offers users the option of contract-based remote fabrication services or self-directed on-site work. Additional information regarding WNF capabilities and services can be found on our website or by emailing us at wnfinfo@uw.edu.

An additional focus at the location in Eschbach is the use of know-how to provide solutions for highly complex, individual customer requirements. Our technologies are utilized to make products such as radiant barrier materials for energy management, protective uniforms, packaging films, ultrahigh barrier materials for OLED displays and solar cells, and polymer dielectrics for capacitive energy storage devices.

From RFID tags to radar systems for aerospace, Wootz is jumpstarting the future with a material that connects, conducts, and electrifies like nothing before. Our next-gen carbon material, Vantium, redefines performance by augmenting and replacing metals with a green alternative. We welcome any inquiries on our products or potential partnerships. We are actively seeking new partners to develop novel antennas, RF components, EMI shielding, and sensors. Please visit our website (wootznano.com) for more information on our products and technology.

Name: NSC Community
Description:
Welcome to the NanoSafety Cluster (NSC)!

Synergy among the NSC projects, collaboration for maximising impact, policy elaboration, planning of future actions, and international cooperation are the main aims of the NanoSafety Cluster. There are several NSC projects and NSC Working Groups. Each of these addresses key aspects of nanosafety, including toxicology, ecotoxicology, exposure assessment, risk assessment, standardisation, and mechanisms of interaction. 
 
This group provides a discussion and networking platform for NSC project partners and stakeholders as well as a forum for problem solving and planning R&D activities among the wider community.You can use this space to start discussions, contribute to current ones, expand your networks, share output and expertise, or announce events, training and job opportunities and publications. You are welcome to invite other members of your networks to join too.

#EU-NSC

Name: Mechanical Characterization Laboratory (MCL)
Description:
Research in MCL is specialized in MEMS/NEMS design, nanopatterning and nanofabrication, mechanical property measurement at micro and nanoscale, nano/bio interfaces and molecular diagnostics via MEMS sensors. Current projects address batch-compatible fabrication of Si nanowires and development of integration philosophies of these nanowires with micro electromechanical systems, nanowire resonators, development of resonant microcantilever-based sensors and characterization of the scale-dependence of mechanical properties.
- Fabrication innovation – Simplified, top-down, and batch-compatible approaches for scalable nanowire fabrication aligned with semiconductor processing.
- Miniaturization & hybrid integration – Bridging MEMS and NEMS with multiscale device architectures that drive the miniaturization of physical sensors.
- Atomistic and multiscale modeling – Machine learning-driven, analytical, and atomistic models to capture elastic, vibrational, and bending behavior of Si nanowires under intrinsic stress and native oxide effects.
- Nanomechanics of Si nanowires – Investigation of scale effects, surface states, and bending mechanics through combined experimental and modeling approaches.
- Nanowire-based force sensors – Development and characterization of multi-axial, high-frequency piezoresistive Si nanowire force sensors with high sensitivity.
- Surface functionalization & stencil lithography – Selective, damage-free functionalization of Si nanowires in 3D architectures for biochemical sensing and MEMS/NEMS integration.
- Knowledge building – Reviews and perspectives on Si nanowire-enabled MEMS/NEMS technologies, highlighting opportunities and future directions in physical and biochemical sensing.

Name: Mesosil
Description:
Mesosil develops and manufactures antimicrobial and bioactive additives for dental and medical materials.
Mesosil’s innovative silica-based platform enables high-capacity delivery and sustained release of bioactive agents, addressing critical challenges in infection prevention and biomaterial longevity. In dental materials, our technology reduces recurrent tooth decay by up to 80%. With applications in orthopedics, implantable devices, and other medical fields currently under evaluation, Mesosil is creating the next generation of long-term antimicrobial protection. Our platform seamlessly integrates into existing manufacturing and clinical workflows, offering a scalable, high-impact solution for the future of medical materials.

Name: Pacific Light Technologies
Description:
Pacific Light Technologies (PLT) is a world leader in light-emitting nanomaterials.  We engineer light. 

PLT’s patented quantum dot on LED technology is redefining the display and solid state lighting landscape by enabling the adoption of QD technology directly into LED packages. Being capable of direct integration into LEDs gives PLT products economic and performance advantages over current solutions.

PLT quantum dot products provide higher energy efficiency and higher color gamut solutions for display and illumination applications.  In displays, PLT QDs enable more saturated colors through a larger color gamut. In illumination applications, in addition to higher efficiencies at the LED level, PLT QDs produce a warmer, higher quality of light.

PLT is a fast growing start up with highly experienced teams in Portland OR (HQ) and Silicon Valley CA.

Name: AFMWorkshop
Description:
AFMWorkshop is a leader in the development, manufacturing and marketing of high-value atomic force microscopes. Our products include the TT-2 AFM, HR AFM, HR-2D AFM, NP AFM, LS AFM, SA AFM and the B-3 AFM.  Markets we address with these products include:

       b) Instrument innovators - Scientists and engineers using AFM as a platform to create a new instrument.

Description: Headquartered in Cambridge, UK and with its manufacturing base in Korea, planarTECH aims to enable graphene, carbon nanotube (CNT), and 2D material applications development by providing researchers worldwide with high-quality equipment for materials synthesis and characterization. In addition to its core competency in 2D materials, planarTECH also has years of experience in manufacturing high-quality vacuum systems and components. With a global network of researchers at leading institutions in Asia, the US and Europe, planarTECH has assembled the process know-how to assist customers in starting their research program as quickly as possible.

Description:

Our innovative nanoimprint lithography assisted roll-to-roll (R2R) technology, when applied to application areas such as flexible displays, solar energy harvesting, nano- and micro-electronics, photonics, etc, offer competitive advantages through lower costs, larger scale and higher volume production.

Name: SiMPore
Description: SiMPore’s mission is the creation of products for precise capture and information-rich analysis of nanoscale specimens, enabling our customers to discover the future in life and materials sciences. Over the past decade, SiMPore has developed and offered industry-leading silicon nanomembranes and BioMEMS technologies. SiMPore’s goal is to create products that bring significant value to society, its customers, employees and investors, and to do so with precision, passion and quality. SiMPore promises its innovative products will deliver superior data and superior outcomes by enabling precise capture and information-rich analysis of nanoscale specimens.

Name: Opalux Inc.

The company’s mission is to accelerate the commercialization of new technologies and applications based on the exciting platform of photonic color. Opalux works directly with industry-leading companies in a process of open innovation to collaboratively create the next generation of photonic color technology applications.

Name: NanoVerify Sdn Bhd
Description:
NanoVerify Sdn. Bhd. (NanoVerify), is  a wholly owned company under NanoMalaysia Berhad (NMB), an agency under the Ministry of Energy, Science, Technology, Environment and Climate Change (MESTECC). 

NanoVerify is mandated to monitor and facilitate nanotechnology development and commercilisation in Malaysia. 

Towards this end, NanoVerify has been designated by NMB to fully operate it’s Nanotechnology verification programme, the NanoVerify Programme. The Programme certifies processes & products with genuine nano-elements increasing consumer recognition and trust to drive industry demand and growth. 

NanoVerify also helps nanotechnology businesses thrive and capitalise on the growing nanotechnology market  by offering expert insights and consultation including technical due diligence, training services, nanotechnology event consultation, and nanotechnology product development programmes.

Name: NANOTECH KESİCİ TAKIM MAKİNA SAN VE TİC LTD ŞTİ
Description: Şirketimiz basta savunma, enerji ve sağlık sektörleri olmak üzere, sıra soğutma sistemleri, klima, motor, makine, valf, termik cihazlar, alüminyum ve çelik üretim endüstrilerine, özel amaçlı mikro hassas zımbalar (punches), kalıplar ve kalıp ürünlerinin (press dies and products) üretimini gerçekleştirmektedir.

Name: Institute for Energy and Materials Processes - EMPI
Description:
Efficient, large-scale energy conversion and storage are topics of high societal relevance, as is the generation and processing of materials that make efficient energy conversion possible in the first place and that are as sustainable as possible. For future progress on these topics, the scientific fundamentals must be well understood and incorporated into the teaching of young scientists and scholars. Therefore, so research and teaching are closely linked at EMPI.

The Institute for Energy and Materials Processes, EMPI, lays the scientific foundations for energy conversion and storage, the production and processing of functional materials and materials, and the understanding of high-temperature processes. This begins with research into the details in small experimental plants and covers the entire process chain up to the industrial scale. Experimental investigations are accompanied by theory and simulations, as preparation as well as for the interpretation of experimental results.

In research, EMPI covers a wide range of scientific disciplines, including quantum chemistry, reaction kinetics, in situ diagnostics, thermodynamics, fluid dynamics modeling and simulation, and provides an experimental platform ranging from materials design to processing, utilization, and optimization, especially for applications in the energy context. A unique strength lies in the multiple cross-connections between the disciplines, the development of versatile scientific tools, and the expertise in the targeted transfer of fundamental findings into practically usable processes. EMPI aims to provide important research impulses internationally and nationally with its work, and also to be a regional cooperation partner for companies in the Rhine-Ruhr region in research and development.

Name: Powdermet, Inc.
Description:
Powdermet, Inc., develops, matures and transitions breakthrough materials innovations that enable reduced weight, resource consumption, environmental footprint, and reduced life-cycle costs and increase energy-efficiency based on value-creation gained through engineered nano-scale features and hierarchically structures of metal and/or ceramic phases in a structure.

Powdermet is committed to maturing and transitioning clean, sustainable, energy and life-saving advanced materials solutions to the marketplace.

Name: Integrated Dynamic Electron Solutions, Inc.
Description: Integrated Dynamic Electron Solutions, Inc. (IDES) is a small, vibrant company that specializes in bringing time resolution to electron microscopy. IDES’ products use pulsed lasers, rapid beam deflection, and/or high-speed electronics integration to capture snapshots of nanomaterials in action. IDES’ systems are installed around the world and are used by physicists, materials scientists, and biologists to understand the dynamics of  nanoscale systems.

Name: NanoPhos SA
Description: NanoPhos SA invents clever materials that solve every day problems. By harnessing nanotechnology, we seek to create a more comfortable, safe and trouble-free living environment. At NanoPhos, we craft materials that effectively solve common problems. We transfer innovations out of our lab and into the hands of consumers. Our vision is clear: “Tune the nanoworld to serve the macroworld” – in simple terms make nanoparticles serve the needs of people.

Name: Plasmalex
Description:
As a fully integrated research, development and engineering group, 
Plasmalex provides complete solutions for nanoscale surface treatment, functional modification and conversion of web processed materials and objects, with the lowest possible environmental impact. The Plasmalex group combines the Europlasma and CPI division and partners with sister company Spoolex.

Name: Newnagy
Description:
纳米新能源(唐山)有限责任公司是一家在纳米技术领域进行产品研发、生产和销售的现代化高科技公司，在纳米发电机技术上具有无可匹敌的突破性技术优势。公司拥有一大批世界上和该项技术相关的核心专利，属于国际首创和领先。国家和公司所在地政府对此项技术的产业化高度重视，给予积极扶持。纳米新能源公司汇集了许多国内外知名专家和负责科研、管理、金融、财务、法律、专利等方面的优秀人才，包括毕业于世界一流学校的硕士、博士和工商管理人才。纳米新能源公司致力于通过不断的创新、独有的专利和先进的技术，打造世界一流的产品，并为员工提供现代化的科研、工作环境。纳米新能源是一家外资公司，注册资金5000万美元，总部位于中国河北省唐山市。纳米新能源在美国设有分公司，并将在全国范围内逐步建立一批分公司。
       
        纳米新能源公司重点研究纳米发电机及其应用，产品的开发涉及微纳米电源、微纳加工技术、基础设施监测、物联网、个人电子产品、医学检测芯片、生物传感器等前沿领域。纳米发电机技术不仅对传统产业有着深远的影响，而且在新兴产业中也用广阔的应用前景。从大的方面看，纳米发电机所奠定的利用纳米结构来实现能量转换的科学原理甚至有可能应用于大范围的能量收集，例如风能和海浪能等。纳米新能源因此坚信，这项技术的产业化将会对国家的经济结构转型做出重大贡献。

Newnagy (Tangshan) LLC is a modern hi-tech company that specializes in the research, development, production and sales in nanotechnology. It possesses unparalleled advantage in the nanogenerator (NG) field. The company owns many core patents worldwide associated with nanogenerator, and is a leader in the area. It gained a lot of attention and support from the government for commercialization of the NG technology. Its team consists of world-class experts and seasoned talents.Newnagy is committed to developing state-of-the-art products through relentless innovation and cutting-edge technology, and to providing employees with a comfortable and advanced R&D and work environment. The company is a wholly foreign funded enterprise with registered capital of 50 million USD. It is headquartered in Tangshan City, Hebei Province, China. The company has an office at Palm Beach, Florida in the United States.

Newnagy focuses on the nanogenerator technology and its applications. Its product development covers frontier domains such as micro/nano power sources, micro/nano machining technology, infrastructure monitoring, the Internet of Things, personal electronics, medical inspection chips, and biosensors. The scientific principles that nanogenerator is based on by utilizing nanostructures to realize energy conversion can possibly be applied to large-scale energy harvesting.

Name: Sharif Student Scientific Association of Nanoscience and Nanotechnology
Description:
Sharif Student Scientific Association of Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (Sharif Nanotech Association) founded in 2012.
انجمن نانو شریف با نام رسمی "انجمن علمی دانشجویی علوم و فناوری نانو" دانشگاه صنعتی شریف در سال 1391 و به همت گروهی از دانشجویان تحصیلات تکمیلی این دانشگاه در رشته مهندسی فناوری نانو تأسیس گردید. در ابتدای کار انجمن با موفقیتهایی همراه بود. اما پس از فارغالتحصیلی و خروج مؤسسین از انجمن، برای مدتی با افول همراه شد؛ تا جایی که صحبت از انحلال آن به میان آمد.
اما در سال 1394 با ورود و عضویت اعضا جدید، فعالیت های انجمن رنگ تازه ای به خود گرفت. تا جایی که موفق به برگزاری چندین کارگاه عمومی و تخصصی آموزش، چاپ نشریه، شرکت در مسابقه ملی فناوری نانو و برگزیده کشوری در این حوزه شد.
هم اکنون انجمن نانو به عنوان انجمن دانشجویی فعال، پویا و اثرگذار در حوزه فناوری نانو در سطح کشور شناخته می شود.

مهمترین اهداف انجمن عبارتند از:
•	ترویج فناوری نانو به عنوان یک نهاد ترویجی
•	حمایت از دانشجویان فعال در این حوزه
•	برگزاری رویدادهای متنوع
•	رابط ترویجی ستاد ویژه توسعه فناوری نانو
•	آموزش و تریبت نیروهای متخصص

با توجه به ماهیت دانشجویی بودن انجمن، طیف گسترده ای از فعالیت ها قابل اجراست که عبارتند از:
•	برگزاری سمینارهای عمومی و تخصصی
•	برگزاری کارگاه ها و کلاس های آموزشی
•	حمایت و گرنت از پایاننامه ها و مقالات
•	برگزاری رویداد های آشنایی و ترویجی
•	برگزاری اختتامیه های مسابقه ملی فناوری نانو
•	تورهای بازدید از نمایشگاه ها
•	چاپ نشریه علمی

رویداد کافه نانو
یکی از مهمترین رویدادهای ترویجی که انجمن نانو دانشگاه شریف برای اولین بار در سطح کشور برگزار کرد، برگزاری رویداد کافه نانو بود. این رویداد به معرفی کلی و کاربردهای فناوری نانو به دانشجویان و اساتید دانشگاه می پردازد.
اولین کافه نانو در بهمن ماه 1394 در دانشگاه شریف به مدت 3 روز برگزار شد. استقبال از این رویداد به حدی بود که باعث تداوم آن به صورت یک برنامه منظم و ثابت در طول ترم های آتی شد؛ به گونه ای که نام انجمن نانو شریف با نام کافه نانو بهم پیوند خورد.
پس از استقبال از کافه نانو، دیگر نهادهای ترویجی فناوری نانو در دانشگاه های سطح کشور بر آن شدند تا رویداد کافه نانو را در دانشگاه خود برگزار کنند.

Name: IEEE Nanotechnology Council Young Professionals Region 10 (Asia Pacific)
Description:
IEEE Nanotechnology Council is a platform that serves as a connection point between members involved in the field of nanotechnology. This forum serves as an enabler for many scientists and engineers and encourages closer cooperation.  All IEEE members are entitled to IEEE NTC membership.  
 
IEEE Young Professionals is a volunteer organization within the IEEE that helps young professionals transition from student to professional life. Most interact with us through their local Affinity Groups, who host events for Young Professionals members. Globally, the organization provides a range of products and services focused on networking, professional development and technical education.

IEEE Nanotechnology Council Young Professionals R10 (Asia-Pacific) is an entity within the scope of IEEE NTC which serves a linkage of young professionals working in the broad fields of nanotechnology to come together to interact, share technical knowledge and career-related activities .

If you are an IEEE Member who has received your first professional degree within the last 10 years, you are automatically part of IEEE Young Professionals! If not, we are soon changing our membership criteria those who are within 15 years of their first degree will automatically be a part of Young Professionals. Those who are not automatically members will now be given the opportunity to opt-in for the first time ever. We accept people of varying demographics, and need the help of more experienced professionals to network with and mentor Young Professionals.

For more information about each group:

IEEE Young Professionals (https://yp.ieee.org/)

IEEE Nanotechnology Council (https://ieeenano.org/)

IEEE Region 10 (https://ieeer10.org/)
