Description: An innovative start-up, engaged in developing disruptive technologies, and engineering intelligent products that learn and respond to stimuli.

Name: Mater-AI
Description: At Mater-AI we're using physics-based modelling and AI to speed up the discovery of materials optimised for electrical and thermal properties.

Name: Brightlight Photonics
Description: Brightlight Photonics is headquartered at The Engine Accelerator in Cambridge. We are looking for talented physicists and engineers, especially those with a photonics background, to join us in revolutionizing CMOS-compatible laser sources for biomedicine, metrology, and quantum technologies.

Name: EEnotech, Inc.
Description: EEnotech, Inc., is a start-up company that was co-founded by Prof. Yi Cui and CEO Meng Sui. Professor Yi Cui is a world-leading expert in nanotechnology, materials science, energy, and environmental technology. He is a highly proliferate scientist/engineer/inventor with more than 360 research publications and more than 50 patent applications. At the age of 28, he was awarded Innovators under 35 by MIT Technology Review, and he has consistently been awarded “The World’s Most Influential Scientific Minds” by Thomson Reuters. He is a Co-Director of the Bay Area Photovoltaic Consortium and a Co-Director of the Battery 500 Consortium funded by the US Department of Energy. He is a Fellow of Materials Research Society and the Fellow of Royal Society of Chemistry. EEnotech Inc is focusing on nanotechnology driven solutions to water purification, grid-scale energy storage and smart wearable textiles.

Name: NSS Water
Description: Producing Nanopure water for semiconductor manufacturing and other high tech industries

Name: NEWT Engineering Research Center
Description: NEWT ERC develops fit-for-purpose water treatment by applying nanotechnology. @RiceUniversity  @UTEP  @ASU  @Yale  | Funded by the National Science Foundation

Name: Photonic Cleaning Technologies
Description: Photonic Cleaning Technologies is the manufacturer of First Contact PolymersTM - "THE Cleaning and Protection System" and are worldwide sole source for any such technology. Our Designer Polymer Solutions are applied to precision surfaces and peeled when dry leaving the surface atomically clean. With these solutions, we provide the only way in the world to create the ultimate clean – an enabling technology for NASA missions, Lockheed’s THAAD ballistic Missile Defense System and the Caltech/MIT’s Laser Gravity Interferometer. Our non-toxic and residueless cleaning protects from scratching, is safe with optical coatings and high power laser optics and reduces solvent waste. We provide an economical method to provide surfaces better than those possible in a cleanroom manufacture - without a cleanroom.

Name: Glonatech
Description:
Carbon Nanotubes Company "Global Nanotechnologies S.A". Glonatech is a nanotechnology company, member of the ONEX Company Group, established in October 2009 in Athens.

Two years after its establishment, the company expanded its operations. The R&D offices and laboratory are located inside TE.S.P.A “Lefkippos” of NCSR “Demokritos”, while its brand new nanoparticles industrial production plant is located in the Industrial Area of Lamia.

The company’s scientific and technical team possesses several years of experience in the production and handling of nanoparticles, such as ceramic oxides and carbon nanotubes (CNTs).

Glonatech constantly refines its technologies for targeted production of customized nanomaterials and participates in research projects related to applications of nanoparticles and development of new nanomaterials.

Name: Tenutec
Description:
We provide customers with eco-frendly high-quality Graphene products, solutions, and expertise. 

Our novel production method is flexible and scalable, enabling a sustainable production of high-quality Graphene. 

Currently, we are developing Graphene to enhance TIM and thermal sheets to enhance the cooling system in electronic devices. Both to prolong the lifetime, but also to enhance the performance.

Name: NanoElectronic Imaging
Description:
NEI’s Scanning TEM Electron Beam-Induced Current (STEM EBIC) imaging system has produced several best-in-the-world results in quantitatively mapping temperature, conductivity, potential, electric field, and work function. Few alternatives can map any of these properties at high resolution, and none can map them all. Standard TEM imaging only identifies where the atoms are in an electronic device – NEI’s technology shows what they are doing.

Visit our YouTube channel for more information about what our system can do:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvIf5oomH4uNlsjTQhrvC3g

Name: LiquidNano Inc
Description: LiquidNano is using the latest Nano-Technology to develop protective coatings for a variety of consumer and commercial uses.  We are at the forefront of this exciting technology using inert, mineral based liquid-glass to give sector-leading performance with zero environmental impact.

Name: E-Spin Nanotech Pvt. Ltd.
Description: Spinning innovation into fibers is an art of science and engineering, and that is what we are at the best. Founded in the year 2010 under the aegis of SIDBI, IIT-Kanpur, E-Spin Nanotech Pvt. Ltd. has emerged as a leading high-tech enterprise for manufacturing high quality and cost-effective multi-functional nanofibers to address the unmet basic needs of the society. The company draws its strength from a strong research and development team working relentlessly in pursuit of improvement and improvisation in performance and quality of our products. On the other hand, E-Spin Nanotech Pvt. Ltd. has also re-engineered conventional electrospinning and revolutionized the prospects of nanofibers with the launch of series of advanced electrospinning units specifically designed to meet sophisticated prerequisites of research organization and industries. The company is working with undeterred commitment to achieve its long-term goal and vision.

Name: Pulead Technology Industry Co., Ltd.
Description:
Pulead operates at the leading edge of technology innovation in the development and manufacture of three kinds of electrochemical cathode active materials, namely LCO, LFP, and NCM utilized in high performance lithium-ion batteries

In 1999,  Oriental Investment Co., Ltd. and the Peking University jointly established Pulead. The company is headquartered in Beijing and operates additional production sites in Tai’an (Shandong Province) and Xining (Qinghai Province).

The R&D department of Peking University, one of China’s most reputable universities, is at the very core of the company’s technological innovation. 

Pulead is expanding its production capacity while at the same time guaranteeing consistent high quality with products that conform to industry standards with certification by the National Certification Bureau.

Pulead is closely collaborating with global battery manufacturers such as ATL and Lishen and is now the supplier of choice for a range of industry leading MNEs. The company maintains a leading market position and differentiates itself from other manufacturers through outstanding customer service, consistent high-quality products, and reliable order delivery.

Name: Kjemidagen
Description: Kjemidagen er en unik bedriftsdag på NTNU for sivilingeniørstudenter på linjene Nanoteknologi og Industriell Kjemi og Bioteknologi. Dette er en dag for og av studenter, noe som skaper et stort engasjement rundt arrangementet. Vi er opptatt av å skape god kontakt mellom studentene ved våre linjer og aktører i næringslivet. Derfor tilbyr vi Kjemidagen som et helhetlig arrangement hvor bedriftene får anledning til å komme i kontakt med studentene på flere arenaer i løpet av dagen. Kjemidagen er en populær begivenhet for studentene og derfor er oppmøtet og oppslutningen alltid stor. Dagen trekker studenter på alle årstrinn og alle studieretninger.

Name: PHORMIC
Description:
The PHORMIC project is a Horizon-European funded project that develops the next-generation wafer-scale silicon photonics platform, enhanced with low-power MEMS actuators and transfer-printed III-V laser diodes. 
Combining this photonic chip technology with advanced packaging methodologies and driver electronics, we want to create a versatile platform for programmable photonic integrated circuits.
We will demonstrate the capabilities of our platform in different application domains, including datacenter communications, optical sensing, and microwave photonics.

Name: nanoShell
Description:
nanoShell is a UK owned company with the ability to deliver cutting edge nanotechnology coatings and impregnators to clients in any part of the world. Our extensive resources and consultants can collaborate with you to establish implement and  integrate nanotechnology surface protections within your business and environment to provide aesthetic and cost improvements to your business

nanoShell is the future in high performance coatings, polishes and surface treatments. The nanoShell range of products have been developed to provide high performance surface coatings solutions that will last for many years to come.

We have truly harnessed the power of nanotechnology into a range of safe, reliable, trustworthy products that are designed specifically for each type of surface.

We recognise that it is becoming more and more important to consider how businesses and domestic environments are performing in a sustainable and environmentally friendly way.

nanoShell is constantly researching and pushing the envelope of cutting edge nanotechnology, delivering the best and most cost effective solutions to our clients, business partners and retail networks.

We believe in our products and in their contribution to safer, more hygienic and sustainable environments.

Name: Phosphorex
Description: Microspheres and nanospheres are becoming an indispensable tool for ultra-sensitive assays, multiplex detection, in vitro and in vivo imaging, drug delivery and other scientific research. Phosphorex develops and supplies various types of microspheres and nanospheres for a broad range of scientific and biomedical applications. Our standard products include pure polymer, fluorescent, phosphorescent and color microspheres with sizes ranging from 20 nm to 1,000 microns. Many of these spheres are surface modified so they can be conveniently conjugated to various biomolecules. In addition, we provide custom microspheres and bioconjugation services. We can encapsulate customer specified dyes, drugs and other molecules into microspheres and nanospheres.

Name: NanoChronia
Description: NanoChronia is a deep tech company dedicated to detecting fugitive emissions of methane, hydrogen, ammonia and hydrogen sulphide with our next-generation graphene-based nanosensors. How can you secure your infrastructure if you can't detect leaks?

Name: Super Polymer
Description:
We transform polymers into super polymers. Our technology generates a new and improved polymer that dramatically reduce carbon emissions.
Super Polymer integrates in construction and coating reducing energy costs and heat, extend EV's driving range by integration in paint layers and in various applications for the defense sector.

Our material provide the following properties:
Perfect light scattering - SP-HDPE has a refractive index of 1.96 comparing to raw HDPE that has a refractive index of 1.52.
Suspension in liquids - Stable suspension in liquids, no surfactants required.
Super Hydrophobicity - Coatings that contain SP’s particles have a contact angle of 155° or higher, and a sliding angle less than 2°.
Dry particles repulsion - Free dry particles would not adhere or attached to a surface containing SP’s particles.
High electrical insulation - Improve the already good insulation property of polyethylene.

Name: Graphene Tech
Description:
GrapheneTech is a dynamic company with high production capacity of graphene, working day after day to become a world leader in graphene technology adapted to the specific needs of each client.

Graphene manufacture as nanoplates, we specialize in large-scale production for various industrial applications using our patented method that allows us to offer suitable to industrial needs, complex and costly than other methods do not allow prices.

Six years of experience with nanomaterial of high performance are behind us. The multidisciplinary team formed by PhD in chemistry and physics of our firm has a R&D&I  extensive experience and share with the clients the vision to develop our services with accurate communication, consistent and direct.

Name: Lasertec
Description:
Lasertec is able to maintain its lead over the competition not only due to its in-house expertise but also as a result of its investments in cutting-edge equipment. 
 For example, Lasertec has a cleanroom with parallel production machines in place as well as a wide range of analytical and measurement facilities. The end result is that the production processes can always proceed in an uninterrupted fashion even in cases of overcapacity or rush orders. 
 Our laser specialists have over 26 years of experience in the field of micromachining using laser. 

 The machines are capable of carrying out serial production activities using a UV or infrared laser with a pulse in the nanosecond to picosecond range, and material surfaces can be processed of up to 2.5 m x 1.0 m. All the production machines are set up in such a fashion as to ensure that the quality can be continuously monitored and guaranteed throughout the production process.

Name: Spectrogon AB
Description:
Spectrogon manufactures and develops optical filters, thin film coatings, and holographic diffraction gratings.

Spectrogon derives its origin partly from research conducted at the Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, and partly from research and development projects at AGA AB, a former major Swedish industrial group. In 1980 we became AGA Optical, which eventually became Spectrogon in 1984. Spectrogon AB became an independent company 1991.

With over 90 % in export, our most important markets are Western Europe and North America. More than 80 % of invoiced sales go to OEM in specialised electro-optics markets. These markets include: instrumentation for medical diagnostics, industrial process control, robotics, environmental measurements, optical communications and other related instrumentation.

Name: SAW COMPONENTS Dresden GmbH
Description:
SAW COMPONENTS Dresden is a leading developer and producer of Surface-Acoustic-Wave products. We are situated in Germany for more than 25 years. 

We offer  technologies for chip manufacturing and packaging, as well as final Surface Acoustic Wave (SAW) products. This includes lithography for micro structuring with 300nm technology, thin film technology for various metals and isolators from 10 to 1500nm with a very high accuracy, as well as packaging services for SMD and metal packaging of chips with specialties in high temperature packaging for use up to 350°C.

The SAW products range from SAW Resonators, SAW Filters and SAW delay lines to SAW sensors for wireless temperature and strain measurement as well as wireless identification.

Name: ECONORM
Description:
Solide partenaire de l’industrie horlogère suisse depuis plus de 25 ans.

Nos connaissances approfondies de la technologie de couches minces et des saphirs fonctionnels  nous permettent de réagir avec rapidité et flexibilité aux exigences de l’industrie horlogère, qui ne cessent d’évoluer et de se complexifier.
Notre position de leader dans le développement de produits high-tech fait de nous un partenaire compétent dans la réalisation de nouvelles idées.

Verlässlicher Partner der Uhrenindustrie seit über 25 Jahren. 

Unsere vertieften Kenntnisse und langjährige Erfahrung in der Nanotechnologie und funktionalem Saphir erlauben uns, schnell und flexibel auf die Bedürfnisse der Uhrenindustrie - welche sich stets weiterentwickeln und komplexer werden – zu reagieren. Unsere Leader Position in der Entwicklung von High-Tech-Produkten machen uns zum kompetenten Partner, wenn es um die Realisierung neuer Ideen geht.  

Econorm, l’excellence depuis 1993

Name: nanotools
Description:
NANOTOOLS designs, develops and mass-produces high quality nanoMEMs components. Taking
advantage of our patented electron beam processing, 3D structures are directly formed in diamondlike
material quality, with ultra-high precision and reproducibility. Founded and based in
Munich/Germany in 1997, we have constantly extended our core competences in the field of
nanofabrication and have achieved scale as one of the leading experts in the production of atomic
force microscopy (AFM) tips. Since more than 20 years, we supply the world’s leading semiconductor
and nanoelectronics manufacturers with cutting-edge CD metrology and high aspect ratio AFM tips.
For more information, visit http://www.nanotools.com.

Impressum: http://www.nanotools.com/static/imprint

Name: IEEE Nanotechnology Council - Women in Nanotechnology
Description:
The IEEE Nanotechnology Council (NTC) is a multi-disciplinary group whose purpose is to advance and coordinate work in the field of Nanotechnology carried out throughout the IEEE in scientific, literary and educational areas.
The Council supports the theory, design, and development of nanotechnology and its scientific, engineering, and industrial applications.
The IEEE Nanotechnology Council is part of Division I – Circuits and Devices and is made up of the following 23 IEEE societies:
1. Aerospace & Electronic System Society (AES)
2. Antennas & Propagation Society (AP)
3. Circuits & Systems Society CAS)
4. Communications Society (COM)
5. Components, Packaging, & Manufacturing Technology Society (CPMT)
6. Computational Intelligence Society (CI) [Formerly Neural Networks (NN) Society]
7. Computer Society (C)
8. Control Systems Society (CS)
9. Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation Society (DEIS)
10. Electron Devices Society (ED)
11. Engineering in Medicine & Biology Society (EMB)
12. Electromagnetic Compatibility Society (EMC)
13. Industrial Electronics Society (IE)
14. Instrumentation & Measurement Society (IM)
15. Photonics Society (PS)
16. Magnetics Society (MAG)
17. Microwave Theory & Techniques Society (MTT)
18. Nuclear and Plasma Sciences Society (NPS)
19. Reliability Society (RL)
20. Solid State Circuits Society (SSC)
21. Robotics & Automation Society (RA)

For more than 40 years Quantum Design (QD) has been providing technology solutions to researchers in the fields of physics, chemistry, biotechnology, materials science, and nanotechnology. Name: NanoPhoton - Center for Nanophotonics
Description:
The research vision of NanoPhoton - Center for Nanophotonics – is to explore hitherto inaccessible regimes of extreme confinement of light in dielectric cavities and to apply the associated enhanced light-matter interaction to solve fundamental outstanding challenges in chip-scale information technology.

A number of recent optical cavity designs have shown a path towards confining light at deep subwavelength dimensions in dielectrics. This phenomenon - which we refer to as Extreme Dielectric Confinement (EDC) - parts with the conventional wisdom in optics and nanotechnology that light cannot be localized to volumes smaller than roughly the cubic resonance wavelength. In addition to being of theoretical interest in its own right, EDC opens up new technological possibilities for novel optical nanostructures. It is the ambition of NanoPhoton to develop the science and technology for this new class of nanostructures by combining fundamental electromagnetic and quantum optical theory with state-of-the-art nanofabrication and advanced experimental characterization techniques.

The research center NanoPhoton is funded by the Danish National Research Foundation (Danmarks Grundforskningsfond) with a DKK 62 million grant. NanoPhoton was established in April 2020 and will run for six years.

MISSION:
Develop solutions for single health through the application of nano structures and new technologies based on ferrous and non-ferrous metals.

VISION:
To be recognized as a provider of innovative and customized solutions for single health.

APPLICATIONS:
Copper nanoparticles can be incorporated into various products such as coatings, fabrics, paints, resins and polymers, among many others. The multiplicity of applications opens up a huge range of opportunities for the participation of partner companies.
In agriculture, in addition to its direct action in pathogen control, copper is also an important micronutrient for plants, playing a critical role in defense barriers and disease resistance.
In livestock, it can also be applied in animal nutrition, increasing the bioavailability of this metal, in addition to avoiding and/or reducing cross-contamination in herds and also for humans.
Recently, copper gained the spotlight during the pandemic due to its ability and speed of inactivation of the new Coronavirus. In its nanometric form, copper has its biocide activity enhanced, besides allowing its strategic incorporation in different materials, matrices and environments.

The possibilities are endless and we will always seek to create sustainable solutions!

When industrial hydrogen producers start trying to scale, they are going to face skyrocketing prices of noble materials like platinum as they play a critical role in separating hydrogen from water.

The same price increase is going to happen on the consumption side of hydrogen vehicles like airplanes, trains and cars.

Naco Technologies creates specialized nano-coatings and new materials that replace the need for platinum and other expensive materials in the production and usage of hydrogen at a massive scale.

Description:
Headquartered in Lansing, MI, XG Sciences specializes in the production of xGnP graphene nanoplatelets and the formulation of advanced materials that amplify product performance across diverse applications. 

We use proprietary manufacturing technology to produce various grades of xGnP, which enable the formulation of value-added, high-performance composites, coatings and other advanced materials. XG's products can enhance the processability and the mechanical, thermal, electrical and barrier properties of customers’ base materials. 

Since inception, we have provided our distinct high-performance products and expertise to over 1,000 customers in 47 countries for applications in automotive, consumer goods, sporting goods, aerospace, medical, electronics, energy storage, and other markets.

Name: Creative Nano
Description: Creative Nano is a research and commercial SME providing technological solutions in the field of nanotechnology and advanced materials. Cnano was founded in 2013 and got its current form after a merging during year 2018. Cnano is a research-intensive, engineering SME, based in the industrial zone in Peristeri Athens, Greece. Founded by a group of Researchers and Professionals who after collaborating together for years at various well established Research & Technology hi-level Institutions, decided to build on their extensive experience to provide cutting-edge Research & Innovation activities for the research and development of new products and services. Cnano’s core team consists of a number of dynamic and highly qualified, MSc and PhD level scientists and engineers demonstrating long experience and success records in their respective fields. The company’s main mission is to conduct basic, applied, and technological research and is orientated towards commercialisation process. Through the evaluation of new ideas Cnano determines the most effective route to ensure a successful knowledge transfer from the laboratory to the industry. Cnano has developed considerable expertise in the fields of electroplating, advanced materials and nanotechnology and is currently involved in several H2020 projects. At the same time Cnano is providing services, scientific advising and technical support to major customers.

Name: NANOGAP
Description: Novel nanomaterials for advanced products and markets. Protected technology platform based on Atomic Quantum Clusters.

Name: Material Intelligence Lab
Description:
At M19, we build smart, compliant, and user-friendly Filter Integrity Analyzers designed specifically for the pharma, biotech, and sterile manufacturing sectors.

Our flagship product, the M19 Filter Integrity Analyzer, helps quality and production teams:

• Comply with GMP, Annex 1, and FDA standards
• Run automated forward flow, bubble point, and pressure hold tests
• Eliminate manual errors with touchscreen operation and digital audit trails
• Save time, increase accuracy, and simplify validation

Whether you operate in injectables, biologics, vaccines, or sterile filtration, M19 ensures your filters are tested right — every time.

Let’s raise the standard for sterility assurance.

Contact us to schedule a demo or discuss your QA needs.

Name: Iridia, Inc.
Description: Iridia's breakthrough molecular identity technology combines cybersecurity, blockchain, molecular science, robotics, and AI to create an immutable foundation of truth in global commerce. We're not just securing supply chains, we're establishing a new bedrock of trust that will reshape how humanity conducts trade, protects innovation, and builds economic relationships. Our technology ensures the authenticity of critical materials, assets and products that power the global economy, setting new standards for transparency and security across industries.

Name: Lipomize
Description:
We are a young and flexible organization that offers products and services related to liposomal nanotechnology and Management of New Product Development.

Please take a look to our products and services we can help you find the fastest and lower cost way to incorporate liposomal technology in your company.

Mission
Provide world class products and technological development services in the field of liposomal nano biotechnology contributing to the achievement of strategic goals in our external customers.

Vision
Build a liposomal technology development platform which consolidates as a leading company nationwide and a regional level

Pharmaceutical
Lipomize develops customized liposomal pharmaceuticals products under strict quality guidelines and confidentiality.

Cosmeceutical
Liposomes favor the disposition of encapsulated active ingredients in the epidermis and dermis, while the permeation rate is decreased. This helps to fix active ingredients to the outermost skin layers as desired for cosmetic products. Simultaneously, the washing out may be delayed so that, for example, aqueous sun care products containing liposome-encapsulated UV filters are water-resistant.

Nutraceutical
The use of liposomes for the delivery of food ingredients offers a totally new technology with unique opportunities. There is nothing equivalent currently in use in the food industry. Liposomes can improve the flavor of cheese which has been ripened using accelerated methods, they can also targeted delivery of functional food ingredients, the synergistic delivery of ascorbic acid and tocopherols for enhancing antioxidant activity and the stabilization of vitamins in foods has been demonstrated.

Tailor Innovation

Currently, consumers not only want excellent products, but also want constant innovation, if your company does not have an R&D or I&R department, we can help you plan and execute your development project with our team.

Name: FabNS
Description:
FabNS is a deep-tech company dedicated to designing and manufacturing scientific-grade instrumentation for research and industrial applications. Our flagship product, the Porto System, is a state-of-the-art nanoscope based on Tip-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (TERS). It enables precise optical characterization of nanostructures, delivering unparalleled resolution and spectral signal enhancement.

Beyond hardware, FabNS provides innovative software solutions for scientific data analysis and specialized characterization services tailored for industrial R&D. Our PortoFlow software transforms hyperspectral analysis with its intuitive dataflow paradigm, empowering researchers to employ advanced methods—including PCA, ICA, Curve Fitting, Fuzzy k-means, Spectral Unmixing, and more—without the need for coding expertise.

As a spin-off from the Federal University of Minas Gerais (UFMG, Brazil), FabNS bridges academic expertise with industry innovation. Our technologies have made a global impact, with the Porto nanoscope playing a pivotal role in groundbreaking research, such as advanced materials research in a Nature cover article (Nature, Volume 590, Pages 405–409, 2021).

Name: NANOVEA SRL
Description:
3D NON-CONTACT PROFILOMETERS   /   PNEUMATIC TRIBOMETERS    /   SCRATCH TESTERS   /   NANOINDENTERS   /   MICROINDENTERS   /   LAB SERVICES

NANOVEA provides materials testing solutions for challenging applications in R&D and quality control.

With headquarters in Irvine, California, and offices in Italy, Mexico, and India, as well as a network of partners and distributors, NANOVEA has been pushing the boundaries of materials research and QC for almost two decades.

Fortune 500 companies and prestigious research institutions around the world rely on the accuracy and technical superiority of our advanced instruments, expert consulting, and laboratory testing services.

Developed and built in California.

Almae’s buried heterostructure laser technology offers a seamless solution to scale transmission speed from 10G to more than 100Gb/s. Benefits of our technology are found in lower chip to fiber or waveguide coupling losses, thanks to easy to integrate spot size converter, lower thermal resistance enabling high temperature operation, smallest size modulator and intrinsic low capacitance to give the best overall performance.

Almae is located in the south of Paris at: site Data 4 - Route de Nozay - 91460 Marcoussis - France.

After 18 years, the balance is very positive, since i3N achieved a leading position with a strong national and international impact and has been a pioneer in multiple fields such as photovoltaics, thin-film microelectronics, paper electronics, and transparent (oxide) electronics, having contributed to these areas to National and European public policies.

#mircrofluidics #nanofabrication #quantum

Description: We are the producer of blue-light emitting, heavy metal-free quantum dots, there is a demand for them in light using, advanced technology markets. Name: Sona Nanotech Inc.
Description:
Sona Nanotech, a nanotechnology Life Sciences company, is developing Targeted Hyperthermia Therapy, a photothermal cancer therapy, which uses therapeutic heat to treat solid cancer tumors. The heat is delivered to tumors by infrared light that is absorbed by Sona’s gold nanorods in the tumor and re-emitted as heat. Therapeutic heat (41-48°C) stimulates the immune system, shrinks tumors, inactivates cancer stem cells, and increases tumor perfusion – thus enabling drugs to reach all tumor compartments more effectively. The size, shape, and surface chemistry of the gold nanorods target the leaky vasculature of solid tumors, and the selective thermal sensitivity of tumor tissue enables the therapy to deliver clean margins. Targeted Hyperthermia Therapy promises to be safe, effective, minimally invasive, competitive in cost, and a valuable adjunct to drug therapy and other cancer treatments.  

Sona has developed multiple proprietary methods for the manufacture of gold nanoparticles which it uses for the development of both cancer therapies and diagnostic testing platforms. Sona Nanotech’s gold nanorod particles are cetyltrimethylammonium (“CTAB”) free, eliminating the toxicity risks associated with the use of other gold nanorod technologies in medical applications with an assessment by the Nanotechnology Characterisation Laboratory that detected neither endotoxins nor microbial contamination. It is expected that Sona’s gold nanotechnologies may be adapted for use in applications, as a safe and effective delivery system for multiple medical treatments, subject to the approval of various regulatory boards, including Health Canada and the FDA.

Name: InnoMedica Holding AG
Description:
InnoMedica is a Swiss pharmaceutical company and established player in nanomedicine that is developing a new generation of nanomedicines through an innovative nanotransport system. With the first application of nanocarriers in the field of oncology, InnoMedica intends to develop an optimized chemotherapy for cancer patients. That should reduce side effects to a minimum while maintaining good efficacy.

The strategy is to develop and commercialize novel therapeutics for the treatment of tumor diseases. This new generation of chemotherapeutics uses cytostatic drugs already in common use in a new galenic form in cancer therapy.

Another application of nanocarriers is being developed in neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson's and ALS. For this purpose, InnoMedica has optimized the nanocarrier used to overcome the blood-brain barrier and used GM1 as a regenerative active ingredient. For this approach, InnoMedica has been granted orphan drug status by the FDA, EMA, and Swissmedic for use in ALS.

InnoMedica is an independent, equity-financed Swiss pharmaceutical company with its nanofactory in the canton of Fribourg.

Name: Sparc Technologies (ASX:SPN)
Description:
Sparc Technologies Limited (‘Sparc’, ASX: SPN) is an Australian company pioneering new technologies to disrupt and transform industry while seeking to deliver a more sustainable world. Sparc has established offices in Australia, Europe and North America and is focused on three core areas of technology development.

1.	Sparc has spent over 5 years developing a graphene based additive product, ecosparc, which has demonstrated 40% anti-corrosion improvement in commercially available epoxy-based coatings. Sparc recently commissioned a manufacturing facility to produce ecosparc and is engaging with global coatings companies and asset owners to conduct field trials.

2.	Sparc is the majority shareholder of Sparc Hydrogen which is a company pioneering the development of photocatalytic water splitting (‘PWS’) green hydrogen production technology. PWS is an alternative to producing green hydrogen via electrolysis, using only sunlight, water and a photocatalyst. Given lower infrastructure requirements and energy use, the process has the potential to deliver a cost and flexibility advantage over electrolysis.

3.	Sparc is also developing sustainable sodium ion battery anode technology utilising agricultural bio-waste feedstock.

Name: Quantum Materials Corp
Description:
Quantum Materials Corp (QMC) is a world leader in the design and manufacturing of high-performance nanomaterials. QMC primarily focuses on luminescent semiconductor nanocrystals known as quantum dots and other nanoparticles for the electronics, display, lighting, and photovoltaic industries. The Company has developed an industry-first continuous-flow manufacturing processes which unlike traditional batch processing delivers highly uniform products and is quickly and easily scalable. QMC has two metric tons of production capacity, the largest of any manufacturer of cadmium-free quantum dots. 

QDX Quantum Dots are the latest nanotech breakthrough from Quantum Materials. Designed to withstand high heat, oxidation, and moisture without degrading, QDX surmount implementation constraints due to lack of environmental stability. For example, QDX will enable QD use in displays without expensive barrier films for encapsulation. In QD-LED lighting, QDX will eventually enable on-chip application increasing energy efficiency,reducing the need for bulky, expensive heat sinks for the solid-state lighting market   QDX will also lead the way in developing extremely low cost next-gen roll-to-roll printed QD solar cells. 

QMC's volume manufacturing methods enable consistent quality and scalable cost reductions to drive innovative discovery to commercial success. Quantum Materials' products are the foundation for technologically superior, energy efficient and environmentally sound LCD UHD displays, the next generation of solid-state lighting, solar photovoltaic power applications, advanced battery and energy storage solutions, biotech imaging, and biomedical theranostics. Wholly-owned subsidiary Solterra Renewable Technologies develops sustainable quantum dot solar technology. QMC produces both cadmium-based and RoHS compliant cadmium-free qd.  Founded in 2007, Quantum Materials Corp. is based in San Marcos, Texas and is traded on the OTCBB market under the symbol QTMM.

Name: Watersprint
Description: We offer safe water using patented technology providing a cost efficient UV LED based purification system to reduce up to 99,99 % of virus, bacteria, protoza, cysts and other patogenes, in close cooperation with partners and clients.

Name: Hidden Reflections
Description:
Welcome to Hidden Reflections !! I am Javier Osés, and I would love to show you a new concept of art at a nanometric scale based on the interactions of light with matter. For years I have been working in the world of materials research, thanks to which I have learned to modify their surfaces obtaining amazing colours, patterns and shapes, only appreciable through a microscope.
After years doing this as a hobby in the spare time that my jobs as a researcher and university professor leave me, I have decided to venture with enthusiasm in this innovative project as a way to spread my work, as well as to disseminate the scientific and technical knowledge that I have been collecting during my days of work in the laboratory.
I hope your visit will surprise you with the beauty of the materials in the same way that I marvel at each new sample that I create, and that you can learn about the mysteries of something so well-known, and at the same time so enigmatic, like light. I invite you to join me as a seeker of hidden reflections.

TMC products enable ultra-precision research, measurements, and manufacturing in the fields of photonics, semiconductor manufacturing, life sciences, drug discovery, and nanotechnology.

Our products are designed and manufactured at our state-of-the-art, vertically integrated manufacturing facility and are backed by a customer commitment to provide superior global service. 

To contact us, please email TMC.Sales@ametek.com.

Description:
Henniker Plasma is a leading manufacturer of plasma treatment systems for cleaning, surface activation to improve adhesion, and functional nano-scale coating.

Test & Development - We operate extensive laboratory facilities to assess, test and investigate all aspects of plasma surface modification on a wide range of materials.

Driven by expertise - Coupled with extensive in-house and real-world knowledge, we can deliver a tailored treatment to suit your individual product or production needs quickly and efficiently. Our standard configurations cover most applications but we understand too that no two samples or surfaces are the same. That’s why, uniquely, we offer a wide range of options that allow us to customise any standard system for your exact requirements.

Respected by trusted customers - We have worked with some of the most recognisable global brand names and world-leading academic institutes. We work hard to develop long-lasting customer relationships which in turn help us to create the best customer experience possible. Whilst, like many businesses, we measure our success in growth, our core value is developing trust between ourselves and our customers.

Name: SUPERBRANCHE
Description:
SUPERBRANCHE : Nanomedicine as a breakthrough to treat cancer

We bring answers to the following issue: intratumoral injection of nanomaterials implying both accessibility and detection of the tumor beforehand.
We position ourselves as a producer dedicated to the synthesis of architectured nanomaterials as simple as possible and of controlled size.
Our modular technology platform presents both therapeutic and diagnosis applications. It consists in a magnetic nanoparticle coated by a tailor made branched architecture.
Thanks to their optimized shape and stability, our compounds are well tolerated and can be administered intravenously,
thus helping to the early detection and treatment of metastases.
The main advantage of the SUPERBRANCHE technology stem from
our specific  processes and synthesis know-how which are at the heart of our innovations.
Superbranche is a Biotechnology company characterized by two main activities:
- the sale of dendrimers and nanoparticles as research products to any laboratory in the field of nanomedicine
- conducting development partnerships to bring to the clinics targeted diagnostic and image-guided therapy nanomaterials based on its proprietary technology.

Name: Norcada
Description:
Norcada specializes in the development and manufacturing of MEMS and Photonic products for a wide range of industrial and scientific applications. Founded in 2001, Norcada has a state-of-the-art MEMS design and photonics packaging facility in Edmonton, Canada, home to one of the largest micro/nano technology clusters in North America.

Norcada’s business focus and product offerings are:

+ MEMS devices for scientific and high-tech applications
+ Photonic devices for industrial sensing applications
+ MEMS and nanotechnology solutions for health and bio-pharma

Our MEMS product portfolios are ultra-thin membranes for electron and x-ray microscopy, in-situ microscopy solutions, x-ray optics, and nanopore devices for genomics and proteomics. 

Norcada's photonics products include near-infrared and mid-infrared DFB lasers for spectroscopy, silicon optical bench devices for telecom, as well as polarizers and gratings for Terahertz applications.

Norcada has a broad international presence and provides high-tech products to thousands of customers worldwide.

Name: Nanoview Nanotecnologia & Arantius

diversos setores industriais.

Name: Prospective Instruments
Description:
Prospective Instruments is a start up located in Dornbirn, Austria, and Regensdorf, Switzerland. 
Our goal is it to provide the research and clinical community with turn-key, flexible, modular and highly compact multimodal imaging platforms and associated light sources.
Prospective manufactures and sells multi-photon microscopes, as well as ultrafast lasers for the biomedical, pharmaceutical and basic research community.

Name: Optonas
Description:
Precision optics manufacturer specializing in advanced thin film coatings and custom optical solutions. With decades of experience in laser and defense optics, we deliver high-performance coated optics for demanding applications across industries. Our capabilities include coating optics up to 600 mm in diameter, covering a broad spectrum from 180 nm to 20000 nm.

We engineer optics for performance and durability. Our in-house environmental and durability testing, conducted according to MIL and ISO standards, enables us to develop stronger, more resilient optics that meet the highest reliability requirements. Our versatile pool of coating equipment gives us the flexibility to select the most suitable coating technology for your specific application - ensuring optimal results across diverse use cases. From R&D prototypes to large-scale production, our team of experts ensures every component meets exacting standards. 

Let’s build optics that go further, last longer, and perform better. Contact us today to explore how Optonas can support your next breakthrough.

Name: Deeplight
Description: Deeplight produces ultra-narrow linewidth laser that can achieve MHz-rate chirping with high linearity. Our lasers are powered by Photonic Integrated Circuits (PICs) and can serve customers from the scientific community, to LiDAR and manufacturers of fiber sensors.

Name: Lionano
Description:
Lionano is a material manufacturing company that focuses on improving the key components of lithium-ion batteries.
 
Currently, Lionano specializes in an advanced drop-in electrode material, which the company commercially produces for major battery manufacturers. The material has been nano-engineered to create batteries with greater capacity and cycle life. This technology can enable lighter and lower cost consumer electronics, and longer range for electric vehicles.
 
Lionano was founded by a group of PhDs and MBAs and a distinguished professor in electrochemistry research from Cornell.

Name: Silicon Microgravity Ltd
Description:
Silicon Microgravity Limited (SMG) is a technology company focused on the inertial navigation and gravity markets.  

Over 10 years of research and development, first at Cambridge University's Nanoscience Department and then carried on inside SMG, has gone in to our resonant MEMS technology.  Today SMG's team of scientists and engineers are based in our Technology Centre in Waterbeach, just north of Cambridge, England.

SMG focuses on two markets.  The inertial navigation market where our MEMS accelerometers and gyroscopes presently under development will provide higher accuracy, smaller form factor and lower cost than existing sensors.  Then the gravity sesning market where our MEMS gravity accelerometer sensor can detect changes down to 10 nanoG.

Name: Bruker BioAFM
Description:
Bruker's BioAFM expertise has its roots in the extensive development capabilities of JPK Instruments AG, which joined the Bruker Nano Surfaces and Metrology division in 2018. JPK was founded in 1999 as a spin-off from Humboldt University in Berlin, Germany,  and quickly established itself as a pioneer in the commercial development of atomic force microscopy (AFM) and optical tweezers for life science applications.

Key JPK innovations that set new industry benchmarks include:

The ability to investigate challenging biological samples (e.g., living cells and tissues) in liquid and under controlled environmental conditions;
High-speed scanning capabilities that enable the real-time visualization of dynamic molecular mechanisms;
Multiparametric quantification of nanomechanical properties, such as adhesion and stiffness; and
The ability to combine BioAFM instruments with advanced optical microscopy techniques, such as confocal and super-resolution microscopy.
JPK's flagship product line, NanoWizard BioAFMs, launched in 2001 and has remained at the forefront of innovation in biomolecular and cellular imaging. The ForceRobot and  CellHesion systems launched in 2007 and 2008, respectively, and specialized on single molecule force and single cell spectroscopy. Finally, the NanoTracker optical tweezers system launched in 2013.

Since 2018, these technologies have been integrated with Bruker's proprietary advances, such as PeakForce Tapping, and worldwide reach to deliver the highest performance commercial BioAFMs avilable today.

Imprint: https://www.bruker.com/en/legal/imprint.html
Privacy Policy: https://www.bruker.com/en/legal/privacy-policy.html

Name: Biorism

Biorism is a trailblazing innovator specializing in nanotechnology-infused solutions that can be seamlessly integrated into textiles and more, providing diverse wearable wellness, woven into daily life.

The diverse application of our functionalised solutions ranges from infusing fabrics, enhancing laundry detergents, formulating creams, or creating effective surface sprays. Our versatile solutions adapt seamlessly. Leveraging cutting-edge nanotechnology, each application boasts long-lasting effectiveness, ranging from 48 hours to one week or up to 30 washes, depending on specific use and application.

Name: Electron Microscopy Innovative Technologies, LLC (Emit, LLC)

Phone: (203) 978-3648

Our company is located in East Hartford, CT and offers daily rentals at our office. We also offer weekly, monthly or longer-term rentals where we will deliver the microscope to your facility and show you how to use it.  Our weekly rates are less expensive than what most outside labs charge for a single day! Why not keep your R&D in-house while saving valuable time and resources?

Name: NANONET Foundation
Description:
The NANONET Foundation was established as a source of reliable information allowing to discover and experience the nano-world. Created by volunteers, it aims at promoting the development of entrepreneurship based on new technologies and, furthermore, at popularising the results of research and development works conducted in the field of nanotechnology. The Nanonet
Foundation, established in 2006, includes scientists, young researchers, PhD and undergraduate students, business leaders, research organizations from public and private sectors and many other professionals with various backgrounds. It aims to achieve its goals through taking part in public meetings, science fairs and conferences, stimulating public debate on related topics, publishing
activities, running the chief portal focused on nanotechnology in Poland, networking between different actors in order to assist collaboration. The Foundation has received prestigious awards and
has been recognized by academia, business and government circles as an ever active and competent partner in the field of nanotechnology in Poland.

Name: Active Membranes
Description:
Improving a desalination membrane is simple. Just activate the membrane. With our patented electro-active membranes, we minimize scaling and fouling, reducing desalination costs and environmental footprint by up to 50%.

Founded in 2022 with one clear purpose: to redefine membrane separation and deliver more fresh water at a lower cost. We believe desalination should be a first choice, not a last resort, in addressing global water scarcity. Multiple industry awards confirm we’re on the right path.

That’s what we do. That’s what drives us. That’s what Active Membranes is about.

Name: Modumetal, Inc.
Description:
Modumetal is a new class of materials that represents a disruptive change in both the performance and manufacturing efficiency of advanced materials. Modumetal is addressing the limitations of current metals in corrosion, wear, fatigue and weight.  The market for Modumetal materials exceeds $2 trillion worldwide.  

Modumetal is a layered alloy, similar in structure to plywood, but comprising layers that are on the order of nanometers in thickness.  Modumetals are superior in mechanical, corrosion, wear and thermal performance, but are comparable in cost to conventional non-layered metals.

Nanolayered alloys cannot be produced by conventional heat-based metallurgical processes.  Modumetal is produced by a patented, sustainable manufacturing process that uses electricity as the direct input form of energy and enables the production of the nano-layered materials at an industrial scale and a competitive price.  The Modumetal process represents a major shift in the way metals are manufactured. It affords inherent efficiencies in end part manufacturing and reduces demand for metal raw materials.

Name: Northern Nanopore Instruments
Description: We specialize in research tools to support single-molecule experiments with solid-state nanopores. Our instruments provide customers with a bench-top system for rapid, inexpensive, and automated fabrication and experimentation with precisely sized solid-state nanopores for use in a variety of life and health sciences research. Our products are simple to use, customizable to your existing workflow, and fit into any research budget.

Name: Endor Technologies
Description: On the way of developing new cancer therapies and solutions for skincare.

Name: InnovCoat Nanocoatings and Surface Technologies Inc.
Description:
We develop and manufacture multi-functional coating solutions for industrial application. Our products can be applied on any surface in order to provide new functionalities or enhance existing ones. 
We can work together with clients from every industry to create innovative and customized solutions, which are easy to apply and long lasting.

Compared to traditional organic products our coatings are inorganics, which makes them capable to provide several functionalities at the same time in just one very thin layer and to last much longer.

If you are looking for a solution to solve an issue and to create new value for your material or product, we are ready to provide a free analysis and application trial. 

Just contact us!

Name: Nanoacademic Technologies Inc.
Description:
Nanoacademic Technologies develops and commercialize licenses of advanced atomistic simulation tools to predict the properties of next generation materials and electronic devices. Our set of DFT-based software is being used around the world by Universities, Government labs and private R&D companies to reduce development costs and time-to-market.

We have also developed an innovative and unique spin-qubit modeling tool to simulate the envelope functions of elementary particles in semiconductors, in the design of your choice and study its quantum properties to design the components of future quantum computers. QTCAD calculates the envelope functions and energy levels of electrons or holes confined in nanostructures within k.p theory by using non-linear Poisson, Schrödinger, and many-body solvers. 
The next evolution of QTCAD will consist in implementing more exclusive features for spin-qubit modeling with in particular Tight-Binding (TB) simulation capabilities and also a new software module to simulate superconducting-qubit based device parameters with finite element modeling and Josephson junctions material properties with atomistic methods which is a multiscale approach, truly unique on the market by bridging our atomistic and quantum design code ecosystems.

It is commercially available for academic and corporate users and a classroom version for graduate students is available, it is QTCAD EDU available for free-trial testing and through semester/year-long licenses.

Our customers' fields of research are semiconductors, nanomaterials and devices, battery technologies, solar cells, metals, topological materials, quantum computing technologies and telecommunication among plenty of other innovative materials science and applicative domains.

Direct contact email: info@nanoacademic.com

Online documentation portal (User manuals, tutorials and much more):
https://docs.nanoacademic.com/

Follow us on Twitter: 
@nanoacademic

Description: With UN nations call on  sustainable development goals, every possible country is trying to make their economics in resonance with SDG's in order to give the planet it's previous resemblance. This not only vibrates the countries but also the companies across the globe and shift their paradigm towards SDG's. We develop competitive safe, durable and measurable energy-efficient engineered solutions which are both temperature and fire resistant; as part of our thermal engineering services, we provide thermal and carbon emission calculations of complex linings translated into ROI analysis to guarantee the best solution is selected and optimal total cost of ownership is always achieved.

Interested in having a conversation ? Reach us at info@siltherm.eu

Name: QSens - Quantum Sensors of the future
Description: QSens is dedicated to the development of quantum sensors, a new technology in the field of measurement technology and sensor technology that has a sensitivity on the edge of what is theoretically possible. This new type of sensors can be used, for example, in medical technology, autonomous navigation and renewable energies. Quantum sensors are still in their infancy. Promising prototypes have been tested in the first successful feasibility studies, and this is exactly where QSens comes in: Based on the results of basic research, the future cluster finalist is working on the field in an interdisciplinary manner with partners from the natural and engineering sciences and industry.

Name: nanome
Description:
Semiconductor colloidal quantum dots, also named as nanocrystals, are unique materials having physical sizes on the order of nanometers. These nanoparticles can be excited by an external optical or electrical source to generate light at specific colors depending on their size. Having an expected market size of over 20 billion USD in the coming 10 years, colloidal quantum dots are being used in lighting, display, energy and biotechnology applications.

At nanome, we are developing colloidal quantum dots for niche applications in optoelectronics including lighting and displays. Having 10+ years of mature academic expertise in the field, our aim is to be a leading high tech company in the world.

Please do not hesitate to contact us at info@nanome.tech for business opportunities.

Name: Psigryph
Description: Psigryph Inc. is redefining the way we deliver bioactive molecules across the cell membrane. Our patent-pending Nanopect nanodelivery system can transport large numbers of molecules across cell membranes of plants, animals and humans increasing bioavailability many fold. Psigryph was founded in 2018 and identified product opportunities in many industries such as Food and Beverage, Agriculture, Pharmaceutical and Cannabis. Our plant-derived nanostructures and food powders have been researched extensively in the lab and are ready to enter the market. We seek collaborations with innovative and industry leading companies to provide technology to improve the health of Humans, Animals and the Environment.

Name: Alien Photonics
Description: Alien Photonics provides advanced optical products and solutions. Company is known for delivering complex, high-precision optical components tailored to the needs of institutes, universities, laser manufacturers, quantum photonics developers, and other cutting-edge photonics companies.

Name: Materials Physics at SINTEF
Description:
In the Materials Physics groups at SINTEF (www.sintef.no) we work to understand fundamental physical and chemical properties of materials. Together with our Norwegian and international partners, we use this understanding to develop and improve processes and materials that meet the needs of tomorrow. With our two main hubs in Trondheim and Oslo, we have close proximity to important innovation centres in Norway.

We use several key materials characterization and modelling competencies going all the way down to atomic resolution in combination with material physics understanding in order to achieve this.

Some of the techniques we are working with are:
* High resolution structural analysis of materials (SEM, TEM, FIB, APT)
* Surface analysis (XPS, AES)
* Lithography/thin film deposition
* Optical characterization
* Materials modelling (DFT, MD, Optical modelling, etc.) 
* Interoperability and ontologies

We work towards a wide range of applications:
* Structural materials (Light metal alloys, Steel, Concrete)
* Materials and devices for production/storage of renewable energy (Silicon solar cells, Hydrogen production, Batteries, BIPV)
* Thermoelectric materials
* Catalysts
* New, functional and safe nanomaterials. 
* Lighting technology including laser and LED technology

Name: Raymor Industries Inc.
Description:
Raymor Industries Inc. comprises Raymor Nanotech and  Nanointegris, a subsidiary now located in Boisbriand, Québec.

Raymor Nanotech produces very pure and highly graphitized single-wall carbon nanotubes (C-SWNT). Raymor Nanotech uses a patented plasma technology to synthesize C-SWNT at low price and with an industrial capacity of 1 kg per day. Raymor Nanotech C-SWNT can be used in many applications such as conductive polymers and transparent conductive films and coatings for electro-static charge dissipation, anti-corrosion, EM shielding or capacitive touch screens. 

NanoIntegris is the world's leading supplier of high mobility semiconducting inks. These inks contain high-purity, sorted metallic and semiconducting single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWNTs), graphene or HiPco nanotubes. They are formulated for inkjet and aerosol printers and used in the development of the next generation of high-mobility transistors, transparent conductors and photovoltaics, as shown by over 250 published scientific studies using these advanced materials.

Name: Center for Molecular Modeling
Description:
Who are we?

The CMM is an interfaculty research center with researchers belonging to the faculties of Engineering and Architecture and Sciences of Ghent University. Our general research ambition is to model molecules, materials, and processes on the nanoscale with important technological applications. Our research is situated at the cross-roads of physics, chemistry and (bio)engineering. This synergistic research vision was from the start of the center the DNA of the CMM and remains the key point to pursue scientific excellence.

What do we do?

By applying the most advanced molecular modeling tools, we have the ambition to contribute to important technological solutions for societal problems related to clean energy conversions, sustainable chemistry, processes based on bio feedstocks, etc. Our research is performed within the framework of a very strong national and international network, and we have various collaborations with industrial stakeholders.

At Tec Star we believe in the extreme effectiveness and versatility of our nanotechnological products and therefore we work to ensure their application to a wide number of fields with the purpose of helping our customers in reaching their maximum potential.

We are also truly conscious of our social responsibility. All of our solutions are developed aiming to reduce carbon emissions, in order to give the environment the respect it needs and deserves. 

Let’s enter the future together!

Name: Luna Nanotech
Description:
Luna Nanotech is a nanotechnology manufacturing company that offers a range of products and solutions for nanomedicine and biomedical research. Our product lines includes biocompatible nanoparticles, molecular biology tools and equipment for DNA and RNA purification, and diagnostic solutions for detection of infectious pathogens. We also offer custom services in nanoparticle synthesis and characterization, and analytical services to quantify nanomaterial distribution in environmental and biological samples.

Luna Nanotech is also actively developing diagnostic products that range from simple and highly cost effective point-of-care assays that can be used by research labs and for clinical screening in resource-poor settings, to more sophisticated portable all-in-one devices capable of simultaneous detection of multiple pathogens geared towards centralized and field laboratories. These technologies are expected to improve patient outcomes by facilitating earlier disease detection to help guide informed treatment choices.

Our core team of founders and scientists have more than 20 years of experience in the fields of nanomaterial development and biomedical research. We use our extensive expertise to ensure the quality and reproducibility of nanomaterial and molecular biology products offered by Luna Nanotech. Our client base include North American and international research laboratories in such academic institutions as Harvard University, Stanford University, and Berkley National Labs.

Name: FELMI-ZFE Institut für Elektronenmikroskopie und Nanoanalytik - Zentrum für Elektronenmikroskopie
Description:
FELMI-ZFE besteht seit 65 Jahren und ist ein Forschungsverbund, der sich aus dem Institut für Elektronenmikroskopie (FELMI TU Graz) und dem Zentrum für Elektronenmikroskopie (Mitglied: ACR-Gruppe) zusammensetzt. Die Forschungs- und Dienstleistungseinrichtung bietet allen Interessenten aus dem universitären und industriellen Bereich ein umfassendes Spektrum modernster elektronenmikroskopischer Untersuchungsmethoden zur mikrostrukturellen und mikrochemischen Charakterisierung von anorganischen und organischen Materialien, elektronischen Bauelementen, pharmazeutischen und biologischen Systemen. 

FELMI-ZFE (Institute for Electron Microscopy & Nanonalysis/Graz Centre for Electron Microscopy) is one of the leading microscopy facilities in the physical and biological sciences; interdisciplinary research, teaching and industry services are the key to our success. We house some of the world’s best microscopes. Our broad field of activity – ranging from fundamental to applied research – sets us apart. We work in close cooperation with universities and enterprises throughout Europe.

Know-how Transfer: we are constantly creating new knowledge and methods. We believe that it is our responsibility not only to support knowledge creation but also to share generated know-how and expertise. That is the reason why we put know-how transfer at the top of our list of priorities.

Training & Microscopy Courses: we provide training on in-house instruments for SMEs who wish to purchase microscopes or use these instruments on a regularly basis and see the need to upskill their staff to become self-sufficient with their analysis (SEM & microanalysis, TEM & EELS).

Teaching: our scientific staff gives lectures at the University of Technology covering different fields of research. We supervise bachelor, master & PhD projects. 

Microscopy Library: our library offers a large number of state-of-the-art textbooks and scientific journals about microscopy of materials & related subjects.

Name: COPE-Nano
Description:
COPE-Nano is an ambitious project which addresses the Call HORIZON-WIDERA-2022-ACCESS-01-two-stage that will leverage on the internationally acknowledged track record and innovation activities of the Nanotechnology Lab LTFN/AUTh to establish a new, autonomous, and self-sustained Centre of Excellence for Or-ganic, Printed Electronics & Nanotechnologies (COPE-Nano). 

This CoE will become a leader in EU in basic, applied and industrial research in the rapidly growing fields of Flexible Printed Organic Electronics (FPEs), Bioelectronics and Advanced Nanomaterials for applications in Green Energy, Electronics, ICT, Medicine and Agriculture. COPE-Nano will enhance scientific and technological capacity of Greece, raising the research profile of the Greek and EU scientists, researchers and entrepreneurs based on close and intensive collaboration, networking and know-how exchange with University of Bordeaux (LCPO & ELORPrintTec), and University of Cambridge, which are institutions worldwide acknowledged for excellence and leading in FPEs & Nanotechnology. 

COPE-Nano is strongly supported by the Greek Government (GSRI) to expand the Nanotechnology Ecosystem of LTFN and turns them into purely growth initiatives and business activities, strengthens and highly impacts the regional, national and EU scientific, technological and economic growth to lead in the Green Energy and Digital transformation priorities.

Name: Mechion Electrospinners
Description: Mechion produces lab and industrial-scale electrospinning devices. No matter which type or size of the device you need, Mechion can fulfill all needs of your nanofiber research. You can easily design your own machine and get info and quote from https://www.mechionlab.net/get-quote-for-model-a/ Mechion has three models of electrospinning equipment Model A: Basic electrospinning setup Model B: Advanced device for nanofiber researchers Model C: Industrial machines for mass production

Name: Swiss NanoAnalytics
Description:
The Swiss NanoAnalytics platform within the Adolphe Merkle Institute's BioNanomaterials group offers high quality services for the analysis of nanoparticles. This includes characterization of the physicochemical properties of nanomaterials, isolation and analysis of the occurrence of particles in food products and cosmetics, testing the stability and size of nanomedicines in biological fluids (e.g. blood serum), and testing material composites used in electronics or construction materials.

Furthermore, in connection with the development of Covid-19 vaccines or therapeutic nanocarriers (e.g. liposomal or polymer capsules), we offer characterization services, support the characterization of such vaccines or nanocarriers and provide relevant information at an early stage of development.

We offer a number of services related to the detection and analysis of nanoparticles:
- Characterization of raw materials like powders and nanoparticles (Particle size, shape or elemental composition )
- Detection of particles in consumer products such as cosmetics or food (Particle presence, size and elemental composition)
- Characterization of nanomedicines (vesicles, liposomes, polymer particles, mRNA vaccines, biomedical products, drug carriers). Particle size and colloidal stability can be analyzed in specific suspensions (physiological suspensions, plasma, cell culture medium)
- Filter efficiency measurements or on other virucidal systems (with MS2 bacteriophages)

We also offer development of procedures, consulting, training workshops (e.g. light scattering, electron microscopy, cell culture).

Name: Nova Graphene
Description:
Nova Graphene is a global advanced materials company with a focus on research, production and marketing of proprietary CarbonEra graphenes, as well as graphene-enhanced materials and products for the manufacturing sector. Our novel graphenes and graphene-enhanced formulations are intended to be integrated into the production process of other products to make them stronger, harder, lighter, and more fire resistant & energy efficient. 

Our R&D strength lies in creating proprietary graphene blends and formulations that target specific product enhancements sought by manufacturers, while achieving competitive price/performance ratios. We devote our R&D efforts to graphene applications that bring high performance, best economic value and reduced carbon footprint to a variety of industry sectors.

Name: SILTEA SRL

Lux Labs' ongoing distribution partner is Visual Feeder. Please visit www.visualfeeder.com for access to ClearBright and other cutting edge display products.

Name: Instituto de Nanotecnología Aplicada.
Description:
El Instituto de Nanotecnología Aplicada – INA es una empresa mexicana privada que promueve, capacita y desarrolla las Nanociencias y Nanotecnología en el: 

- Sector académico: Universidades, centros de investigación y posgrado.

- Sector productivo: Industrias nacionales e internacionales compatibles con el desarrollo e integración de materias primas nanoestructuradas.

- Servicios de ingeniería: Informáticos, de manufactura avanzada y caracterización de materiales.

Name: Visualise.bio
Description: At visualise.bio, we aim to standardise expansion microscopy (ExM) through automation and quality control solutions. Our goal is to make high-resolution 3D imaging accessible and reliable, enabling detailed and reproducible ExM.

Name: BESTMEDGRAPE
Description:
“BESTMEDGRAPE project aims at supporting the creation of new start-ups/SMEs by transferring scientific/technological knowledge on local grape cultivars and exploitation of wine by-products as a source of bioactive compounds that are transformed in innovative commercial health products when appropriately incorporated in ad hoc nanocarriers. 
Living labs/seminars/round tables/meetings/business creation trainings (Contamination lab CLab)/technology platforms will favour the involvement of stakeholders and business creation. Direct beneficiaries are: local farms, wineries and cosmetic and functional food SMEs, researchers, selected potential entrepreneurs and local Institutions, which will benefit from extensive capacity building during and after project performance.
According to ENI CBC Med programme - A.2.1 priority, the proposal fully responds to the Overarching Objective of fostering sustainable economic, social and territorial development. It takes stock from over 10-year R&D of the partners in the fields of grape valorisation, waste exploitation and development of nanotechnological antioxidant/anti-inflammatory/anti-neurodegenerative formulations, areas where international patents have been obtained.
Following a long-standing scientific relationship, a working partnership has been established with institutes were joint investigations on grape have already been carried out:
University of Cagliari (Applicant)
Italian National Research Council (PP1)
National Institute of Health and Medical Research (PP2)
University of Carthage (PP3)
Saint Joseph University of Beyrouth (PP4)
National Trade Union Chamber of wine, beer and spirits' producers (PP5)
Berytech Foundation-Business Innovation Centre (PP6)
Jordan Society for Scientific research (PP7).
The joint group expects to produce a substantial change in areas where winemaking could not profit from recent technological applications due to generalized economic slowdown and conflicts affecting the Med area.

Name: GATTAquant - DNA Nanotechnologies
Description:
The company focuses on probes for fast, easy and precise quantification of super-resolution systems.

These probes allow you to check the resolution of your microscope with a precision of a few nanometers. This level of precision is reached by using special nanostructures (so called DNA origami structures) as breadboards for placing single dye molecules in an exactly defined pattern.

Our innovative nanorulers allow a fast, easy and precise determination of resolution values in any common super-resolution system (STED, SIM, GSD, dSTORM, Blink microscopy, DNA-PAINT). On the one hand the instantaneous visible, significant result makes it easy for users to proof their microscope’s resolution and on the other hand provides an opportunity for microscope manufacturers to demonstrate the performance of their products in an eye-catching way.

To ensure an optimized gain as well as a maximal performance of the probe, we design our products individually according to your wishes, requests and intentions.

Therefore the continuous extension of our portfolio is the main focus of our company’s research.

If you need more information, you're welcome to visit www.gattaquant.com.

Name: Imagine Intelligent Materials
Description:
Imagine Intelligent Materials develops conductive smart materials from graphene.

Our functionalised graphene transforms ordinary materials into electrically conductive, “intelligent” materials. These materials sense and report real-time changes in stress, temperature and moisture.

Our graphene composites and coatings create high value-added products for high-volume manufacturing. We tailor graphene for individual products, processes and supply chains.

Our pilot graphene plant is able to produce up to 10 tonnes of graphene per annum. We develop “drop-in” solutions for large-scale manufacturing.

Name: Icon Analytical

Name: The Nanomed Lab

Name: IQS nano
Description:
IQS nano is a technological company belonging to the IQS Group holding.
