We have more than 25 years of experience in use of electron beam lithography and laser lithography for synthetic holography, micro-optics and other micro and nanotechnology applications.

Name: atech innovations gmbh
Description:
Seit über 25 Jahren produzieren und vertreiben wir keramische Membranen für die Mikro- und Ultrafiltration an unsere Kunden aus den Bereichen Chemie und Biotechnologie, der Ernährungs- und Getränkeindustrie sowie Abwasserbehandlung und Recycling.

Im Bereich der Membrantechnologie gehören wir zu den Weltmarktführern. Wenn es um Weiterentwicklungen geht, sind wir bestens aufgestellt. Unsere Abteilung für Forschung und Entwicklung spielt für uns eine wichtige Rolle, denn wir arbeiten täglich akribisch daran, unsere Produkte besser zu machen und unserem Firmenamen atech innovations gmbh in vollem Umfang gerecht zu werden.

Unsere Mission: 
Als führender Anbieter von keramischen Membranen gestalten wir die Zukunft der Mikro- und Ultrafiltration. 
Durch innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Lösungen setzen wir Maßstäbe im Umweltschutz und in der Wasseraufbereitung.

Unsere Vision: 
Wir streben danach, weltweit als kompetenter und zuverlässiger Partner anerkannt zu sein, der durch höchste Qualität, kundenorientierten Service und fortschrittliche Technologien einen nachhaltigen Beitrag zur Schonung unserer Ressourcen leistet.

Unsere Werte: 
Qualität: Wir verpflichten uns zu höchster Präzision und Langlebigkeit unserer Produkte.
Innovation: Wir fördern kontinuierliche Forschung und Entwicklung, um unseren Kunden stets die besten Lösungen zu bieten.
Nachhaltigkeit: Wir handeln verantwortungsbewusst und setzen auf umweltfreundliche Technologien.
Kundenzufriedenheit: Wir stellen die Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden in den Mittelpunkt und bieten individuelle Beratung und erstklassigen Service.
Teamgeist: Wir fördern ein offenes und respektvolles Arbeitsumfeld, in dem Zusammenarbeit und flache Hierarchien schnelle Entscheidungen ermöglichen.

Wir sind ein Tochterunternehmen der in Bad Schwartau ansässigen ARAN Holding GmbH und gehören zu dem Geschäftsbereich Technologie der ARAN Unternehmensgruppe.

Name: NANOPROGRESS
Description: Nanoprogress covers in its member base the most important and active actors from nanotech and complementary sectors including the representative of excellent KET centres, universities and R&D institutions and key industrial players. The members employ more than 10 000 workers and are responsible for the turnover higher than 200 billion of Euros covering three European countries. The focus of the cluster and its members include but is not limited to circular economy, resource efficiency, KETs and service innovation where all these categories are crucial for industrial modernization. Nanoprogress is a member of National Innovation Platforms that serve as a mains source of information for the revision of National RIS3 strategy through entrepreneurship discovery process. Nanoprogress aims at boosting innovation and competitiveness of its members and its activities are focused on four main strategic areas: research and development, technological improvement of its research centres, improvement of managing processes and internationalization. Currently, Nanoprogress is based in Pardubice and operates in five cities in the Czech Republic. Beside the headquarters, the cluster has three research centres in Buštěhrad, Liberec and Roudnice nad Labem.

Name: Nano Energy Lab
Description: We are interested in developing multifunctional carbon based nanomaterials for its energy and environmental applications. We intend to develop materials using green and safe technologies and modify it's properties for various applications. We develop nanomaterials for fabricating electrodes for energy storage applications like supercapacitor, PEM fuel cell and battery applications. Here, we are applying the principle of charge storage on capacitive desalination and developing devices for flow through and flow between CDI setups. We are also into the development of photothermal materials for solar steam generation and use this for modifying solar still for water purification applications. Recently, we ventured into the area of photocatalysis were we study hydrogen evolution reactions. We are studying the effect of various heterostructured visible light photocatalyst on water splitting and hydrogen evolution reaction.

Name: SiOx
Description:
High performance anti-stiction coating

SiOx is a Danish technology company that enables fused quartz coating of metal based on it’s patented, unique NANOmel Technology. The thin film provides a dense and flexible coating, which prevents fouling and scaling.

The coating furthermore effectively protects the metal from corrosion while at the same time being a barrier that prevents unwanted interaction between the metal and the agent/environment. Further, the coating provides surface optimization like better polishing, hardness and wearability.

Across every industry and market segment we work with optimization of industrial process applications where we strive to assist CTO’s, Process Engineers and Application Specialists looking for new solutions that can better ROI of the process plant/equipment – and COO, Production & Operations Managers who aims at increasing the operational productivity of the process line itself.

The SiOx coating is a strong quartz coating with extraordinary mechanical strengths and durability while at the same time providing high heat conductivity and chemical inertness at a competitive price point.

In short, we do quartz coatings to improve industrial processes.

Get in touch to learn more.
Mail: info@siox.dk

Name: Holding Company Composite
Description: Holding Company Composite is Russian a large-scale manufacturer of carbon fibre and carbon fibre-based fabrics and prepregs.  Our products are used around the world in aerospace, wind energy, construction, transportation, shipbuilding, power industry, sporting goods, and medicine.  Our carbon fibre products production facilities are located in Moscow, Elabuga, Saratov, Chelyabinsk and Dubna, Russia.   In 2010 the company established an R&D Center to develop new products and improve the operational performance of existing production facilities.  In 2011 a Nanotechnology Centre of Composites (NCC Russia) was created to provide comprehensive engineering support for customers.  In 2013 the company will open significant new production facilities and capacity for carbon fibre, weaving and prepregs and the company will expand its international distribution channels.

Name: ImagineOptix Corporation

Making Impossible Optics Possible!

ImagineOptix creates innovative solutions for optical and opto-electronics challenges in displays, telecommunications, imaging, optical storage, spectroscopy and many others.

In collaboration with organizations across a wide range of industries, ImagineOptix applies proprietary technologies to control and capitalize on the properties of light in unexpected ways, resulting in dramatic improvements to optical efficiency and performance.

Our patented thin-film wavefront and spectrum control technologies have been successfully applied to imaging systems, telecom switches, LCDs, and have enabled the world’s smallest, most battery-efficient projectors.

Name: Next Tip

On the side, we are quietly developing a new means of carbon capture.

Name: Cavitation Technologies, Inc.
Description:
Cavitation Technologies, Inc. (CTI); (OTC: CVAT); is a “Green-Tech” company, established in 2006 to become a world leader in the development and licensing of new cutting edge technologies for the, biodiesel, vegetable oil refining, renewable fuel, petroleum, water treatment, wastewater sanitation, petroleum, food and beverage, and chemical industries. Our mission is to apply our technology in order to make the world better through renewable energy. 
Our technology is based on continuous flow-through, hydrodynamic cavitation-based reactor systems that use our patent pending, state-of-the-art, multi-stages, NANO cavitation reactors. Our applications can cost effectively and quickly replace many competing industrial methods that generally require: more energy, more manufacturing space, more equipment, more time and more physical labor. This translates into higher margins for manufacturers and lower costs to consumers across a wide variety of end products.

Name: Quantum Silicon Inc.

Taking the world from the transistor to quantum age of computing.

Name: NASAT Labs
Description:
Nanotech Analytical Services and Training (NASAT) Corporation was started due to the increasing clamor for local provider of advanced analytical and training services of customers from both the industry and the academe. This demand is either due to customers existing need but no budget for equipment acquisition or they wanted a faster cycle time by sending the request locally instead of overseas. Also some organizations who already have equipment installed on-site wanted their laboratory personnel to be trained in-depth on the use of the tools by sending them to a training facility which will provide not only theoretical knowledge but also of hands-on skills.
NASAT is addressing this customer demand by putting up a capability locally but in internationally-accepted systems and practice. The highly advanced tools and competent people available on-site will cater to the needs of customer in the fastest and cheapest way but in a world-class facility and laboratory standard.
NASAT with its advanced tools such as Atomic Force Microscope (AFM), Field Emission Scanning Electron Microscope (FESEM), Energy Dispersive Spectrometer (EDS) among others will provide customers the appropriate tool in this technology-driven world.

Name: sp3
Description:
For over 20 years, sp3 Diamond Technologies pioneered the development of CVD diamond technologies. The company has developed hot-filament reactors and processes that are allowing the practical application of diamond coatings in cost effective ways.
Today, the company is comprised of a team of experts in the fields of chemistry, physics, material science and manufacturing, all of which play a role in its efforts to provide the best service and solutions to our customers. sp3 was acquired by MAPAL in 2019 in order to become a fully integrated supplier of precision tools, whist also supplying various customers in multiple regions of differing products:

•	silicon wafers,

A long side our CVD diamond coating services we also supply CVD diamond equipment. We design and manufacture our Hot Filament CVD equipment in order to be a secure consistent supplier to our coating service customers as well as being an equipment provider of HF reactors, spare parts and training services.

Name: Applied Nanostructured Solutions, LLC
Description: Applied NanoStructured Solutions, LLC (ANS) is dedicated to the rapid development and commercialization of nanotechnology. ANS has developed a revolutionary way to grow Carbon Nanostructures (CNS) directly onto various substrates through a continuous and production-scalable process. This process allows a tunable percentage of CNS to be incorporated into composites. ANS was formed in April 2010. The primary focus of the effort is to commercialize the CNS infusion technology for glass substrates, which can then be utilized in conductive thermoplastics, multifunctional laminates, and high performance composites. In addition, ANS has the capability to grow on other substrates such as ceramics and metals.

Name: MEDAD TECHNOLOGY
Description: The technology arm of Medad Holding, Medad Technology  is dedicated to developing novel therapeutic solutions that are smarter, safer and more effective for the prevention and treatment of chronic conditions. Name: Applied MicroStructures, Inc. (Acquired by SPTS Technologies)
Description:
AMST was acquired by SPTS Technologies in 2016. For more information on SPTS, please go to:  http://www.linkedin.com/company/spts-technologies-ltd 

Applied Microstructures, Inc. (AMST) pioneered Molecular Vapor Deposition (MVD), a unique technology used to deposit anti-stiction and other ultra-thin films used to build MEMS devices, like digital displays, motion sensors, microphones, and accelerometers. Customers rely on MVD to achieve exceptional film uniformity with very high reliability -- advantages that increase yield, boost device performance, and lower overall costs.

Name: Ambature
Description:
Based in Waterloo, Ontario and Scottsdale, Arizona, Ambature, Inc. is the world leader in a-axis high-temperature superconducting technology. We design superconducting materials and quantum devices to power an innovative, sustainable future. Nearly half of Ambature’s intellectual property portfolio is dedicated to computing and sensing, while the other half is dedicated to clean energy—the generation, transmission and storage of electricity. Our unique materials and scalable device architectures enable two important shifts in the advanced chips industry: mass production and higher operating temperatures of superconducting devices.

If the transistor is the most fundamental semiconductor device, then the Josephson junction (JJ) is the most fundamental superconductor device. JJs are building blocks for sensors, quantum computers, and high-performance or energy-efficient classical computers. Our JJs can be manufactured at scale in any major foundry in the world using standard silicon fabrication equipment.

Semiconductors conduct electricity with some resistance. Resistance generates heat in wires, batteries, antennas, smartphones, computers, etc. Heat is the major barrier to advancements in semiconductor power, speed, density, efficiency, and reliability. Our materials address the heat problem because superconductors conduct electricity without resistance. Superconductors typically operate at very cold temperatures, but Ambature’s roots are in research towards room temperature superconductivity.

There are low-temperature superconductors (LTS) and high-temperature superconductors (HTS). HTS devices reduce cooling power consumption and coolant costs by an order of magnitude. Ambature’s a-axis HTS JJs will be the first commercially available, mass produced HTS tunnel junctions. They are viable because we have overcome historical technological barriers by mastering a-axis rather than conventional c-axis epitaxial growth.

Name: GRAPHENATON Technologies SA
Description:
Our DNA is the printing of 2D materials on polymer substrates to create innovative films used in industrial applications that contribute to the energy transition. GRAPHENATON Technologies SA has the patented know-how to offer unique solutions.

Our films offer ultra-innovative functionalities, are recyclable and inexpensive; the applications developed cover heat production, heat dissipation, energy storage and energy production through the manufacture of photovoltaic cells. 
 
In concrete terms, our films are used in soft mobility systems, as well as heating and de-icing systems for buildings and in the automotive and aviation sectors. They also incorporate the components used in the manufacture of tomorrow's solar panels.
 
At GRAPHENATON, we are fully committed to the Net Zero Carbon objectives for 2050. Like Nvidia in the field of artificial intelligence, we aspire to complement the applications made by today's leading industrial players with our technology.

Name: ODL Coating Srl
Description:
The history of ODL (Optical Deposition Laboratory) begins in 1987 with the foundation of a research laboratory by two physicists in Bergamo (Italy) having years of experience in the thin film coating technology for optical applications.

Today, ODL has grown into an international high-tech enterprise, with advanced equipment and 40 high skilled professionals working in a dynamic and customer-oriented organization, with offices in Italy and China.

In 2006, the company opened a branch in Guangzhou (China) devoted to products cutting and shaping finishing for Asian customers who require Made in Italy products that meet the specific requirements of their local markets.

The production capability is over 900 sqm of glass coating per month.

Through its projects ODL operates in 5 continents.

Name: Georgia Tech 3D Systems Packaging Research Center
Description:
The Georgia Tech 3D Systems Packaging Research Center (PRC) is a leading university driven center that focuses on advanced packaging using 2.5D and 3D heterogeneous integration technologies. The center conducts research and education in all aspects of advanced packaging and heterogeneous integration that includes materials, design, processes, thermal management, power delivery, and assembly and integration driven by applications that include high-performance computing, artificial intelligence, automotive, mm-wave communication, and photonic connectivity.

The 3D-PRC has access to unique cleanroom facilities and characterization tools enabling the center to pursue glass-core panel-based as well as silicon wafer-based technologies and processes. The center features up to 300 mm x 300 mm glass-core package panel facility that includes lithography, lamination, RDL/TGV metallization, laser drilling, and chiplet embedding. In parallel, the center also has access to up to 200 mm silicon wafer processing including lithography, physical vapor deposition, chemical vapor deposition, deep reactive ion etching, and electroplating processes. Together, these facilities provide our faculty, staff, students with state-of-the-art capabilities to advance heterogeneous integration and advanced packaging research.

The Center’s interdisciplinary faculty are affiliated with the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE), George W. Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering (ME), School of Materials Science and Engineering (MSE), and Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI). With our approximately 30 member companies, 3D-PRC serves as a leading national center for university-industry partnership on advanced packaging and heterogeneous integration. Education is a core mission of the Center with several graduate and undergraduate courses devoted to advanced packaging and heterogeneous integration offered each year by the Center’s faculty.

Name: 3DSecret
Description:

The foreseen technological and scientific discoveries will revolutionise cancer treatment and diagnosis, and therefore set the basis for a game-changing path in one of the current holy grails of European research.

Name: CRS Gene Delivery and Gene Editing Focus Group (CRS GDGE)
Description:
Controlled release delivery systems play essential roles in gene therapy and gene editing applications. During the past few decades, great advances have been made in the delivery of nucleic acids to generate therapeutic proteins (e.g. pDNA, mRNA) and to eliminate disease causing proteins (e.g. siRNA, miRNA, ASOs). More recently, the development of gene editing technologies including CRISPR/Cas, TALENs, and zinc finger nucleases (ZFNs) has opened extraordinary opportunities to precisely edit the genome. Because of the ability to target disease causing mutations, these methods hold incredible promise for one-time cures of genetic diseases. 

Despite this potential, therapeutic success still largely depends on the development of viral and non-viral carriers to deliver and edits genes within targeted cells. Current efforts in the gene delivery and editing community are focused on creating a better fundamental understanding of the barriers to delivery, the design of improved carriers, and realizing opportunities for therapeutic intervention. This is particularly true for emerging therapeutics including mRNA and CRISPR/Cas, for which much remains unknown. There is also a present challenge to develop viral and non-viral carriers that can deliver and edit genes in specific organs and cells. 

Thus, a great future lies ahead for the field of gene delivery and editing, while equally great challenges also stand in the way. The Gene Delivery and Editing Focus Group will serve investigators from universities and research institutes with scientific interests on development of viral and non-viral carriers, examination of delivery barriers, fundamental and applied gene delivery and gene editing applications, industrial researchers from companies with R&D in the vast fields of protein therapies, gene therapies, gene silencing, and gene editing, and experts from regulatory bodies, each under the umbrella of CRS.

Name: SOLARUP - EU HORIZON EIC 2021 PATHFINDER OPEN
Description:
SOLARUP directly addresses the ever louder call for sustainable energy production. The proposed solar energy conversion technology will reduce dependence on critical raw materials and overcome efficiency thresholds to unlock the future of flexible photovoltaic (PV) solar cells for mass deployment in smart buildings, soft robotics, wearable electronics, and other consumer products. Our main innovations lie in nanoengineering zinc phosphide (Zn3P2) for use as an earth-abundant direct bandgap semiconductor absorber and combined with a novel device architecture we target cell efficiency enhancements of up to 15%. Advanced manufacturing techniques such as nanoimprinting and metal-organic vapour-phase epitaxy will be explored to open industrially scalable routes to synthesise high quality Zn3P2 films. Moreover, our approach will allow reuse of the growth substrate, making sustainability another core element of SOLARUP’s radical vision. Optimisation of both the absorber structure and device architecture will be achieved through a holistic interplay of first-principles calculations and atomic scale structural and electronic characterisation.
The main outcome of SOLARUP will be the demonstration of an ultrathin-film PV technology that is scalable, cost-effective, and environmentally sustainable, complete with a comprehensive life cycle analysis. In this sense our aim is to feed and inspire the development of Zn3P2-based solar cells towards a market ready technology. The project consortium unites six European teams with complementary expertise at the forefront of Zn3P2 research, device architecture, and life cycle analysis, who will approach the ambitious challenges from a flexible and interdisciplinary perspective. SOLARUP has clear potential to make science-based contributions to energy security and high- quality job creation, while also connecting to industry and boosting the trajectories of early-career team members.

Name: Porous Liquid Technologies
Description:
Porous liquids are a new class of liquid materials that contain microscopic cavities (pores), each the size of a single molecule. They contain up to 10 000 times the number of cavities that are found in conventional liquids, and up to around 20% of the liquid is actually empty space.

Thanks to these cavities, porous liquids can absorb large amounts of gas and they can be tuned to selectively absorb one gas over another — as for porous solids, which are widely applied in industry. However, a critical advantage over porous solids is that porous liquids can also flow through pipes, meaning that they can be applied in a host of different ways.

We will help to solve your company’s problems and realise new opportunities using our team’s expertise in porous liquids and related materials combined with our industry knowledge and commercial experience. We provide solution-focused consulting, research and development, and technology licensing.

The consortium of the cluster, coordinated by Hahn-Schickard, consists of well-known players such as the Freiburg University Hospital, the University of Freiburg, the Max Planck Institute for Immunobiology and Epigenetics, Freiburg and start-ups such as Ionera and Actome as well as other partners from Baden-Württemberg research organisations. Name: Molecular Vista, Inc.
Description:
Molecular Vista, Inc. develops nanochemical spatial mapping tools that probe and analyze matter at the molecular level through quantitative visualization. Its flagship product, Vista One, combines atomic force microscopy (AFM) and IR spectroscopy to acquire both topography and chemical signature(s) at the nanometer scale. Through its patented technique of Photo-induced Force Microscopy (PiFM), the photo-induced polarization of the sample is measured directly in the near-field by detecting the time-integrated force between the tip and the sample. PiFM demonstrates exceptional spatial resolution (~5 nm), excellent sensitivity (monolayer), a good correlation to bulk FTIR spectra, and universal applicability to a wide range of organic and inorganic materials. By imaging infrared wavelengths specific to different chemical entities, PiFM can resolve the nanometer-scale distribution of each chemical species in diverse multi-phase and multi-component systems, thereby revealing an unprecedented and spectacular molecular vista to the researcher. The Vista One can be equipped with scattering SNOM, Raman, or other options as well.

Molecular Vista was founded by two industry veterans, H. Kumar Wickramasinghe, Ph.D., and Sung Park, Ph.D. (co-founder of Park Scientific Instruments).

Description: Empresa privada mexicana especializada en promover, capacitar y desarrollar las nanociencias y nanotecnologías para el desarrollo de la nano industria mexicana.

Name: Genoptic LED

We have now transformed such research into various commercial ready applications as such, Genoptic is in a unique position to commercialize these disruptive technologies into various industry sectors; one of them was the development of a breakthrough technology to fully integrate a complete solar solution into a slim light weight SolarGEN Tile format.

Name: Institute of Nanoscience and Nanotechnology, Kafrelsheikh University
Description:
Description:
The Institute of Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (INST) at Kafrelsheikh University (KSU) is a leading center for nanoscience and nanotechnology research and education in Egypt and Africa. INST is dedicated to advancing the field of nanotechnology through cutting-edge research, innovative applications, and the development of a highly skilled workforce.
INST's research activities span a wide range of areas, including:
•	Nanomaterials
•	Nanomedicine
•	Nanoelectronics
•	Nanoenergy
•	Nanoenvironmental science
INST is also committed to promoting knowledge dissemination and technology transfer in the field of nanotechnology. The institute offers a variety of educational programs, including undergraduate and graduate degrees, as well as short courses and workshops. INST also hosts a number of conferences and symposia each year, bringing together researchers, industry leaders, and policymakers from around the world.
INST is a key player in Egypt's drive to become a regional leader in nanotechnology. The institute is working closely with government agencies, industry partners, and other academic institutions to develop and commercialize nanotechnology-based products and services. INST is also committed to using nanotechnology to address some of Egypt's most pressing challenges, such as water scarcity, energy security, and healthcare.
Location: KAFR EL SHIEKH,  Kafr El-Sheikh Governorate

Name: Nanoksi
Description: Nanoksi tarjoaa konkreettisia ratkaisuja globaaleihin ongelmiin. Tarttuvien virusten ja bakteerien nopea leviäminen kosketuspintojen kautta on vaikuttanut merkittävästi maailmanlaajuisiin terveysongelmiin. Me haluamme tarjota pitkäkestoisen suojan, mahdollistaa kohtaamisen ja turvallisen kosketuksen. Siksi kehitimme valokatalyysiin perustuvan Fotonit-pinnoituksen, joka saa pinnat desinfioitumaan itsestään, ilman kemikaaleja.

#deeptech #startup #innovation #LowCarbonTransition  #climatechange #energy  #batteries #AnodeMaterial

Name: HELLO NANO - coating of the future
Description: Our group is happy to participate in this life saving race by proposing Hello Nano: a uniquely designed antimicrobial agent, with its main functionality built around a group of carefully selected nanoparticles with clinically proven antibacterial and antiviral properties, in form of a wearable coating, with many possible protective applications.

Name: iCampus Cottbus
Description:
Der Innovationscampus Elektronik und Mikrosensorik Cottbus – iCampµs – ist eine Forschungskooperation zum Thema Mikrosensorik. Der iCampus vereint das anwendungsorientierte Know-how von außeruniversitären Forschungseinrichtungen und die Forschungsstärke der Universität.

Ziel ist es, Produkte im Bereich der Mikrosensorik und Digitalisierung gemeinsam mit kleinen und mittelständischen Unternehmen (KMU) in der Lausitz zu etablieren. Der iCampus ist ein Wissenschaftsprojekt aus den Sofortmaßnahmen des Bundes zum Strukturwandel der Kohleregionen.

Name: Aavalor
Description:
At Aavalor Greentech, we’re building the world’s most energy-efficient water filtration technology — using a patented combination of graphene oxide and ceramic membranes.

Our membranes operate at ultra-low pressure (<1 bar), removes nitrates, and phosphates with over 90% recovery, and use 5x less energy than conventional nanofiltration — with zero chemicals required.

Born in India and scaling in Europe, Aavalor is redefining sustainable water treatment for utilities, dairy and data centers

We’re backed by IP, driven by purpose, and focused on creating global impact through clean water, energy savings, and scalable infrastructure innovation.

Join us as we pioneer a cleaner, more sustainable future. Let's transform water and energy efficiency, one membrane at a time.

Name: SPAOM
Description:
SPAOM Congress (former REMOA meeting) has had editions in 2012 (Barcelona), 2014 (Madrid), 2016 (Bilbao), 2018 (Granada), and now happens every year between Portugal and Spain: 2019 (Coimbra), 2020 (Valencia, online), 2021 (Lisbon, online), 2022 (Salamanca), 2023 (Faro), Toledo (2024) and Braga (2025), with an attendance of approximately 200 participants in recent editions. SPAOM is a three-day event focused on advances in optical microscopy and image analysis, applied to several fields in biological sciences (e.g. neuroscience, cancer biology, development biology, ...). The meeting’s signature features are all plenary lectures, community workshops by scientists and industry workshops. 
We aim to bring people from the Iberian and European scientific communities together and foster inspiring networking around new developments in the field. This year, our event is to be held on 5-7 of November 2025 in Braga, Portugal. 
Check our event at: https://spaom2025.org/
Site: https://spaom2025.org/

Name: Spectral AB
Description:
Electron Microscopy and Surface Analysis.
Our team has been working in the Nordic region since 1995, and since then, we have placed over 200 systems and numerous accessories in the Nordic microscopy market. Our customers benefit from our broad range of experience in both the microscopy and materials sectors, meaning that we can work closely with customers across a range of applications.

Name: Coloursmith Labs Inc.
Description:
Coloursmith is pioneering the future of vision care with advanced light-filtering technology that elevates contact lenses to new levels of functionality and performance. Our patented platform enables the development of lenses that not only enhance visual comfort but also support eye health and lifestyle needs, meeting the growing demand for specialized vision solutions.

Rooted in cutting-edge R&D and driven by a commitment to excellence, Coloursmith partners with leading industry players to bring innovative lens solutions to market. We focus on creating value through technology that empowers vision care companies to differentiate their products and offer enhanced visual experiences to consumers.

Join us on our mission to redefine vision care and make groundbreaking vision technology accessible worldwide.

Name: Research Triangle Nanotechnology Network
Description: The Research Triangle Nanotechnology Network (RTNN) is a collaborative network of laboratories and facilities at UNC Chapel Hill, Duke University and NC State University. The RTNN is an innovation hub for transformative nanotechnology research, discovery, education, commercialization, and workforce development. The RTNN is one of 16 sites in the National Nanotechnology Coordinated Infrastructure (NNCI). The NNCI is supported by the National Science Foundation (NSF).

Name: The Real Green
Description:
Somos una empresa que desarrolla productos innovadores,100% naturales y respetuosos con el medio ambiente para el cuidado de plantas, huertos y jardines. 
También contamos con servicios de jardinería con nuestra marca @urbanbotanix, donde fusionamos lo mejor de la sustentabilidad y la tecnología para crear áreas verdes urbanas más resilientes.
A través de la nanotecnología y biotecnología hemos desarrollado fórmulas que son más efectivas, más rápidas y seguras para las personas, las plantas y los animales, que los productos tóxicos que existen en el mercado.

Name: Quasi-S (Official Page)
Description:
Quasi-S is the leading analytical laboratory equipment distributor of globally well-known manufacturers founded since 2002 . Presently we have expanded to having 3 active branches situated in Singapore (HQ), KL (Bangi Branch) Malaysia and Penang Malaysia. We also provide engineering services and support to our customers as we strive to strengthen and broaden our capability and technology advances.

Quasi-S aims to establish ourselves as the preferred independent consulting company and service provider for the supply of efficient and creative solutions with rapid turn-around time. Our people and equipment have proved to provide our customers with technologically advanced solutions to diverse and complex challenges in the fields of semiconductor, new material research and quality assurance. Quasi-S commits to continually provide our customers with quality products and services. Delivering great value to customer is our promise.

Name: Holographix LLC
Description:
Holographix is the industry leader in nano- and micro-replication. Our proprietary replication technology enables us to produce state-of-the-art structured surfaces and optical devices that are used in a wide array of industries including spectroscopy, life sciences, semiconductor, aerospace, defense, display, telecommunication, metrology, and precision measurement. 

Over the past 20 years, Holographix has earned a reputation for providing customers with high performance, custom designed and manufactured photonic solutions for a wide range of applications. Custom binary and blazed gratings, microlens arrays, and diffractive waveguides are our main focus. However, knowledge gained during the development of these custom devices has enabled many technological advancements in other areas requiring nano- and micro-replication. Therefore, if you require a custom nano-or micro-structured surface of any kind, please contact us! 

Holographix is located 25 miles west of Boston in Marlboro, Massachusetts (see directions https://holographix.com/Directions.html ). Our 15,000 square foot facility is equipped with Class 1000 cleanrooms, environmentally isolated holographic labs, custom UV replication labs, thin-film coating and sputtering systems, phase-shifting interferometers, laser systems, spectrophotometers. Holographix uses a variety of software tools for modeling performance of optical systems and components such as diffraction gratings and microlens arrays.

Name: spotLESS Materials
Description:
Our founding team has long had a passion for addressing global challenges facing humanity. One of our ultimate goals is to transform technology with great potential into technology with great impact. A few years ago, our co-founder, Dr. Tak-Sing Wong, was approached by a group working under the Gates Foundation’s Reinvent the Toilet Challenge looking for a solution to human waste residue in low-water toilet systems. He found that most hydrophobic and superhydrophobic coatings are excellent at repelling liquid, but fall short in their ability to repel sludge-like material. So we set out to make coatings specifically to repel human waste. The results of this work has been recently published in Nature Sustainability. We quickly learned that “sticky problems” are not unique to sanitation. From automotive application to medical, surfaces get dirty, so they need to be cleaned or replaced. Our aim is to make surfaces that keep themselves clean.

Our ultimate goal is to use what we have -- our skills, our technology -- to make the world a better place. The concrete ways we’d like to “make the world a better place” is to help address the Global Grand Challenges [1] laid out by the National Academy of Engineering.

[1] http://www.engineeringchallenges.org/

Name: Silson
Description: We manufacture specialist consumables and components for x-ray microscopy, electron microscopy, accelerator mass spectrometry and related fields. We specialise in the manufacture of ultra-thin membranes and related micro and nanotechnologies. We hold thousands of membranes in stock, which are available for immediate delivery. Feel free to drop us a message if you have any questions or if you'd like to make an enquiry!

Name: CROMA  ("UMR 5130": CNRS, Grenoble-INP, UGA, USMB)
Description:
Les activités de recherche de CROMA couvrent un spectre très large allant de la microéléctronique CMOS à la photonique en passant par les ondes millimétriques et térahertz.
Elles concernent aussi bien des recherches en micro et nano-technologies, qu’en conception de circuits, microsystèmes et systèmes ou qu’en caractérisation de ceux-ci.

Name: Drywired
Description:
About Us

DryWired is an award-winning Los Angeles based clean tech firm that distributes advanced surface nanotechnologies globally. DryWired’s mission is to harness nanotechnology as a catalyst for change. Our eco-friendly protective thin-film coatings make buildings more energy efficient, surfaces cleaner, lessen e-waste, reduce city air pollution and make food last longer. We are committed to solving global challenges through scientific partnerships with private laboratories and leading universities.

Company History

Founded in 2012 and launched at CES in January 2013 as a start-up offering a single nanocoating solution for protecting mobile phones, DryWired has grown into a global nanotech firm with over 100 complimentary licensed nanocoating technologies. We search the world’s laboratories for revolutionary nanotech and help bring innovation to market in collaboration with the award-winning scientists behind the discoveries. Unlike other companies who offer a single technology solution regardless of a client’s needs, we leverage our team of chemical engineers and materials scientists in order to find the best solution for each individual customer. Our customized, non-toxic, nanocoating products are all made in the USA, Europe and Japan.

The DryWired Team

Our team’s distinctive background ranges from materials science, chemical engineering, military support operations, plasma processing and media production. We wanted diversity in our team to match the diversity in our technology’s range of applications. Our nanocoatings and surface modification technologies were developed and refined over the last 30 years by Award-winning American, European and Asian scientists. DryWired’s licensed technologies are protected by dozens of international patents and trade secrets.

Name: Advanced Electronic Materials and Devices (AEMD)
Description: The Advanced Electronic Materials and Devices group aims to explore fundamental electronic and electrochemical phenomena of novel materials, with a current particular emphasis on graphene and other 2D materials (e.g. MoS2), and to develop the fabrication and processing technologies necessary to prepare advanced electronic devices and systems based on them. A major focus of our work are applications related to neural interfaces and neuroelectronics.

Name: StatNano
Description:
StatNano was established in 2010 as a gateway to the latest information and statistics in nano-based Science, Technology and Industry. The mission of StatNano is to monitor the status of nanotechnology development and policies in the world.
StatNano’s main objectives are:
- The collection, processing and dissemination of information about nanotechnology development in different countries around the world
- The international and regional ranking of countries and institutions in the field of nanotechnology based on various indicators 
- Providing information that would guide actions and policies for nanotechnology development

Working to the objectives, it provides information for our target audiences that include but is not limited to researchers, policymakers and managers of public organizations, universities and industries active in the area of nanotechnology.

Name: Sustainable Nano

Spanish Network with the aim of working together towards generating safe and sustainable nanosciences and nanotechnologies, by involving sustainability criteria along the whole process. 
Network funded with Grant RED2022-134129-T by MCIN/AEI/10.13039/501100011033.

Name: PrimeNano Inc.
Description: PrimeNano is an early stage, privately held analytical instrument company. We have commercialized a patented microwave impedance imaging technology developed at Stanford University. PrimeNano’s ScanWave Pro product, enables imaging of electrical properties of materials (conductivity and permittivity) at nano scale dimensions, using atomic force microscopes (AFMs).

Name: The Kavli Nanoscience Institute at Caltech
Description:
The Kavli Nanoscience Institute (KNI) at the California Institute of Technology emphasizes nanoscale research at the frontiers of electronics, photonics, quantum matter and technology, medical and bio-engineering, and sustainability. Since its inception in 2003, the KNI has been an intellectual hub and facilitator of cross-disciplinary research in the area of nanoscience and nanotechnology. It is the home of an advanced nanofabrication facility which helps support this research. The KNI has been critical to realizing exciting breakthroughs in nanoscale photonics, materials science, and biotechnology.

The KNI assembled its centralized nanoscale fabrication and characterization facility in 2008 through the generous support of the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation and the Kavli Foundation. The multi-user facility provides equipment for researchers at Caltech, the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), other universities, government, and industry. Within Caltech itself, there are more than 100 researchers spanning more than 30 faculty research groups across five academic Divisions. 

Through its prize postdoctoral fellowships and its sponsorship of various institutional, national, and international programs, the KNI serves as the center of Caltech’s nanoscience community, bringing together scientists and engineers to exchange ideas and to develop partnerships.

Name: COnovate, Inc.
Description:
A Revolutionary Material for Evolutionary Solutions
Commercializing a novel carbon-based nanomaterial that is the world’s only form of solid carbon monoxide (CO)—offering cost-competitive scalability and the opportunity for seamless adoption as a battery anode material and ultimately many other applications.

Name: Coral Innovations
Description:
At Coral Innovations, we are pioneering sustainable solutions to address some of the world's most pressing environmental challenges. Founded on breakthrough research in nanotechnology, our focus is on developing advanced, accessible solutions for water remediation and environmental protection. Just as coral reefs play a crucial role in sustaining marine ecosystems, we aim to restore and preserve Earth’s most valuable resources, bringing clean water and resilient technologies to communities worldwide.

Our Mission: To harness cutting-edge nanotechnology to provide innovative, scalable solutions for clean water access, environmental restoration, and sustainability. We believe that true impact is achieved when technology empowers individuals, businesses, and governments to create lasting environmental change.

Our Technology: Coral Innovations is dedicated to developing nanotech-based solutions that target pollutants, reduce environmental harm, and support resource sustainability. From portable water-quality testing units to versatile filtration materials, our products address immediate needs while fostering long-term environmental resilience.

Our Impact: We are committed to improving access to clean, safe water for resource-limited communities, promoting health, environmental justice, and sustainable development. As we scale our solutions, our goal is to empower those who need it most with the tools and knowledge for a cleaner, healthier future.

Name: SolarFoil
Description: At SolarFoil, we develop light-converting nanomaterials to optimise sunlight for plant growth. Our products help growers increase productivity, contributing to more sustainable horticulture.

Name: Innova Dynamics (Acq: TPK Holdings Co. Ltd.)
Description:
Innova Dynamics is a rapidly-growing, venture capital-backed nanotechnology company serving the touch screen and display industries. This University of Pennsylvania spin-off is commercializing a far reaching materials platform, Innlay, that can transform device performance by embedding nanoparticles directly into the surfaces of polymers and creating new polymer functionalities. 

Innova is unleashing the full-potential of touch screens by leap frogging the performance of existing materials, enabling sensors of any size and shape, and dramatically reducing the overall manufacturing costs. Future opportunities to leverage the Innlay platform include OLED lighting, OLED displays, thin-film solar, smart windows, anti-infective medical devices, and numerous other application spaces. 

We are actively collaborating with the top companies in touch screens, consumer electronics, and the chemical/materials industry to bring our first touch products to market over the next 1-2 years. A team of highly successful industry veterans paired with the founding entrepreneurs leads the company. 

We are seeking high-performance, committed individuals that want to make a difference while building a great, new technology company.

Name: NanoStruct GmbH
Description:
Traces of contaminants remain invisible and can have a huge impact on your products quality. An early detection allows you to identify problems in your process so that you can take action against it. 

You want to achieve the fast, easy and safe detection of  microbiological contaminants, microplastics or chemical substances?

Feel free to contact us !

For more information visit our webpage www.nanostruct.eu.

Name: MapMetrics
Description:
Maps aren’t just roads and routes—they’re living directories of places, people, and experiences. MapMetrics captures that rich metadata layer by empowering anyone to contribute granular details: store hours that actually match reality, wheelchair‑friendly entrances, seasonal menus, pet‑friendly patios, pop‑up markets, and community events. Each micro‑update is verified through a consensus model and rolled out worldwide in minutes, keeping our dataset perpetually fresh.

We built MapMetrics because the world changes faster than legacy mapping giants can keep up. Traditional map providers rely on slow, top‑down data pipelines; by the time an update appears, it’s often stale. MapMetrics flips the model: local contributors supply updates in real time, retain full ownership and privacy over their data, and see direct recognition for their input. Meanwhile, developers, cities, and businesses access this dynamic metadata via powerful APIs, unlocking smarter local search, personalized recommendations, and agile smart‑city applications.

Our mission is simple: make location data as responsive as the communities it represents—open, accurate, privacy-first, and owned by the people who know their neighborhoods best.

Name: Xallent
Description: Xallent develops proprietary nanoscale measurement hardware and software tools for imaging, testing, and analyzing semiconductors and thin film materials.

Name: Double Helix Optics
Description:
Double Helix's breakthrough 3D nano-scale imaging dramatically extends the precision and accuracy limitations of current 3D imaging technology.

Precision and Accuracy.
Our technology is already accelerating disease and drug discovery in life sciences labs. The possibilities are endless.

We developed our technology to provide a modular solution that makes precision 3D nano-scale imaging accessible, easy to use, and affordable to every lab. We created a simple, adaptable system based on our patented phase mask technique integrated into a high precision optical module with powerful software that will affordably extend your imaging capabilities to sub-diffraction 3D imaging.

What Is It?
The SPINDLE has been designed from the ground up to integrate with your existing microscope. Compact in size and optimized for every emission wavelength, the SPINDLE attaches directly between your camera and microscope using standard C-mounts. No adjustment to your existing set up is required for operation. The imaging software has been optimized for processing and analyzing captured images to provide detailed 3D information and full structure datasets for analysis. Each part of the SPINDLE has been designed and engineered by the Double Helix team.

How It Works
The Double Helix SPINDLE uses Double Helix’s patented Point Spread Function engineering to capture localization information in 3D, leveraging existing microscopes and cameras. Using proprietary algorithms the information delivered by the rotating lobes is converted into three-dimensional particle localization information providing sample measurement with the highest precision – depth of field combination.

Description: At Halion we have developed advanced colour changing nanomaterials for intelligent surfaces.

Name: LEE-BED-H2020
Description:
LEE-BED brings together world leading European RTOs to establish an Open Innovation Test Bed to de-risk and accelerate the development and manufacturing of nanomaterials and lightweight embedded electronics for the benefit of European industry.
Access to such a Test Bed will help establish the industrial eco-systems, R&D investments and new supply chains to support market entry as well as providing a global competitive advantage, leading to growth and job creation.
For Europe to be more competitive compared to the US and Asia, digitalisation of both the development and manufacturing of functional nanomaterials and printed components needs to be established, as well as addressing cost, productivity and capacity. The digitalisation of the European production industry (industry 4.0) through digital based production technologies, such as ink-jet printing, additive manufacturing, robotically assisted in-mould labelling, laser processing, stereo lithography, etc., requires functional nanomaterials and formulations and is a major bottleneck due to high prices and limited volume production.
LEE-BED will address these challenges by building European infrastructure for rapid development and pilot production of nanomaterial, inks, adhesives and composites as well as digital based pilot production lines. LEE-BED will unify the entire value chain from raw materials to embedded electrical components, providing tailored solutions for European entrepreneurs, start-ups, SMEs and large enterprises, with the main objective of going from concept to prototype within six months. Unique to LEE-BED will be the development of tailored services, including technical, business, patent mapping, safety and life-cycle analysis modelling. LEE-BED will also provide funding services for LEE-BED access to SMEs and post-project capital. LEE-BED will guide European industry towards sustainable implementation of innovative technologies, making them stronger and more globally competitive.

Name: Iris Light Technologies
Description: Iris Light’s mission is to accelerate the growth of the $1 billion/year silicon photonics (‘light chip’) market driven by applications in optical communications, photonic AI, medical devices, autonomous vehicle LIDAR, optical chem-bio sensors, quantum, and beyond. To realize this vision, we are pioneering a breakthrough chip manufacturing approach for ‘difficult to integrate’ opto-electronic components, including the perennial challenges of on-chip lasers, LEDs, and photodetectors. Our technical innovation is a new class of photonic ink based on active nanomaterials that can be tuned to a variety of ‘colors’ to address the wavelength needs for these diverse applications. Iris Light is a spin-out of Argonne National Lab and Northwestern University funded by private and government capital.

Name: SPIO Systems
Description:
SPIO Systems was founded in 2020, build upon a long history of successful companies in the optics industry in Denmark. We are enabling Integrated Optical engines utilizing Planar wave guiding wafers Stacked into modules with a cost-effective mass production by polymer imprint technology and wafer level assembly of optical modules. 

Our vision is to serve our clients by developing and manufacturing optical engines with a high degree of complexity, quality, and stability. We will become the technology leader in advanced optical engines based on Stacked Planar Integrated Optics (SPIO) and Nanoscale 3D structures.

Name: Innofocus Photonics Technology Pty Ltd
Description: Innofocus aims at providing innovative products, solutions and services, leveraging our key competencies in photonics, optoelectronics, big data analytics, and intelligent image recognition. We are particularly focused on bringing more application value to Frontier Scientific Research, Clinic and Diagnostical Assistance, Green Energy Alternatives, Smart Farming, Advanced Manufacturing Support, and Healthcare Data Analytics, etc.

Name: Highlander Enterprise S.A.
Description: Highlander Enterprise S.A. (formerly Advanced Graphene Products S.A.) specializes in the production and sale of hybrid nail polishes under the 8belle brand, as well as a wide range of cosmetic products and accessories. Our second area of activity is the distribution of graphene for applications such as carbon composites, 3D filaments, paints, and polymers.

Name: Nanesa
Description:
Nanesa is an Innovative SME involved in the production of Graphene-based nanomaterials. Nanesa's team worked for over 4 years on the development of innovative technology for the production of graphene-based nanomaterials; currently, the company hit the market with different graphene nanoplatelets, commercially available in industrial batches (GXNan grades).
Nanesa operates as a strong research partner cooperating with final customers for developing new multifunctional products exploiting graphene-based materials properties. From these products Nanesa can develop homogeneous dispersion in different resins and solvents, facilitating the application of nanomaterials in consolidated industrial processes.
Properties such as electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity and diffusivity, gas barrier, fire barrier, and EMI shielding, can be transferred from nanoscale to macroscopic dimensions, creating high added value applications with new multifunctional materials.
A multidisciplinary team of highly skilled researchers and engineers can solve complex technologic problems by partnering with companies aiming to use GRMs in their products and/or processes.
Nanesa from 2014 is a partner of the Graphene Flagship Consortium, the most important EU core Research project on Graphene Related Materials.
Nanesa is the only European SME involved in three different spearhead projects related to the development of GRM solutions for automotive, aeronautical, and energy applications.
For more information visit us on our website www.nanesa.com or contact us.

Name: Analog Quantum Circuits

Name: InnoFlex B.V.
Description:
A deep-tech solution to air pollution. 

InnoFlex is deep-tech venture that has the ambition to solve the challenging "stikstof crisis" and make nano-material advancements accessible. 

We are developing a thin-film product which mitigates Nitrogen Oxides in the air.

Name: Rede NanoSaúde
Description: NanoSaúde is one of the Nanotechnology networks supported by the Carlos Chagas Filho Research Support Foundation of the State of Rio de Janeiro (FAPERJ). With 28 laboratories from universities and research centers, the network operates in all stages of development of a nano product, from its design, synthesis and processing, to physical-chemical characterization at the multiscale (macro, micro and nanometric) to efficacy and safety, pre-clinical and clinical assessments, taking the Brazilian and international regulatory system as a reference.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: Marine NanoTech
Description:
Marine NanoTech specialise in the high quality restoration, renewal and protection of paint/gelcoat, glass surfaces and more for the Marine Industry. All products and processes are based on nano technology. 

The cutting-edge technology of our products and processes is designed in-house and exclusively for the marine industry, where Superyachts need the highest standards of quality, facing extreme challenging conditions, such as extreme sun exposure, salt, chemicals and abrasion on a daily basis. 

Marine NanoTech offers full flexibility around the busy itineraries in the yachting industry. MNT works globally, all services can be performed whilst the yacht is still in the water, no tenting is necessary. 
It is not in what we take away, but in what we add to the treated surface that sets us apart. Instead of using abrasive polishing to restore gloss in damaged surfaces, our restoration process repeatedly adds specially formulated nano-fillers that fill in the gaps between molecules and thereby smoothen the treated surface on a molecular level. The result is a gently renewed surface with a high shine, which is then sealed (optionally) and frozen in time with our highly formulated protective coatings ULTRA SHINE ARMOR and CERAMIC ARMOR.

We add to restore, we add to protect and we add long term value. 

We stand behind our technology- this is why we offer long term global warranty programs (12 months / 24 months / 5 years).

Name: NanoComputing Research Lab
Description:
Welcome to our NanoComputing Research Lab, where we delve into materials, devices, and circuits to pioneer energy-efficient computing solutions. 

Our research activities are focused on nanoscale device and interconnects with advanced CMOS and novel materials for implementing novel analog/mixed-signal circuit designs. Our core expertise are in device physics, circuit design, physical design, design space exploration and optimization methods for the design of energy efficient circuits and architectures. 

Over the years, we have widened my research scope to cover material and device fabrication and characterisation combined with circuit and IC design to investigate novel computing paradigms such as physical computing, neuromorphic computing and monolithic 3D integration. 

Additionally, we apply circuit design techniques in quantum computing for quantum circuit design such as transpilation, error mitigation and fault tolerance. By combining my knowledge and expertise in diverse areas, we strive to contribute to the advancement of nanotechnology and drive innovation in the field of nanoscale devices and computing architectures.

Head of NanoComputing Research Lab is Prof. A. C. Todri-Sanial, TU/e

Name: Materials Oceania 2025
Description:
The Sixth International Conference on Materials Science and Engineering, Materials Oceania 2025, extends a warm invitation to researchers, professors, early career researchers (ECRs), and students to participate in this highly anticipated event. The conference is scheduled to take place in 2025 in Seoul, South Korea from September 16-19, 2025

Organized by the Australia-Japan Foundation (AJF), a cultural council fostering people-to-people connections, this conference reflects the enduring collaboration between Australia and Japan, emphasizing the significance of international partnerships in the field of materials science.

Name: Nano Magic
Description:
We turn science into everyday magic. Our mission is to create nanotechnology powered solutions that clean, protect and enhance everyday products for peak performance. We take nature’s own building blocks and turn them into innovations that are accessible and practical for our daily lives.

We have a deep, innovative history in creating cutting-edge liquid nanotechnology solutions: where our formulas have been making waves as a premier cleaning solution for over three decades. Our diverse line of products are formulated in the USA and offer cleaning and protecting optimization for everything from eyeglasses to phone screens and windshields to shower doors. Other applications include solar panels, glass countertops, display cases, and bathroom surfaces. Our products form a lightweight, invisible forcefield of protection against the dreaded fingerprint smudge on any surface from china and porcelain to glass.

Name: Smart Material Solutions
Description: Smart Material Solutions creates nanopatterned films with advanced optical properties that improve solar panel efficiency, combat counterfeiting, and enable next-generation camouflage. Its advanced nanomanufacturing platform delivers truly large-area nanofabrication for the first time, powered by its core technology, nanocoining, which seamlessly nanopatterns cylindrical molds for high-throughput roll-to-roll production. This capability empowers partners to integrate high-performance metamaterials into real-world products at unprecedented scale.

Name: Nanophoton - A Bruker Company
Description:
Nanophoton is manufacturer of high performance analytical and imaging instruments with major focus on innovative Raman systems by using most updated nano- and photonics technology. Nanophoton’s instruments include high performance Raman Imaging Systems, fast Raman Screening Systems (widefield imaging and analysis) as well as Tip Enhanced Raman Systems (TERS) for most demanding applications. These products are integrated into a broad range of high-performance solutions for academic and governmental research as well as industrial applications. 
Technology and Innovation is part of our DNA and we believe that our customers are keen on high performance,  innovative technology.

Our product lines RAMANtouch, RAMANview and TERSsense are currently the most innovative systems in Raman imaging. RAMANtouch provides ultra-fast Raman imaging with a diffraction limited spatial resolution. RAMANview provides ultra-wide field-of-view, deeper depth of focus, and longer working distance, which open up new dimensions of Raman imaging, especially for fast screening.

Name: DIVINCELL
Description: DIVINCELL is a BioPharmaceutical NanoMedicine company pioneering a novel drug delivery technology for the treatment of cancer and genetic diseases. DIVINCELL is specialized in the preclinical and pharmaceutical development of therapeutics using a patented peptide-based delivery platform. DIVINCELL offers innovative solutions to tackle nucleic acid/protein/peptide-based drug cellular uptake limitations, based on 20 years of research in the field of transfection. DIVINCELL is developing and plans to commercialize its innovative drug delivery platform through strategic relationships with established laboratories and pharmaceutical companies. The therapeutics developed by DIVINCELL have been validated using cancer and other therapeutic models (hemophilia, metabolic disorders) and provided an efficient alternative to existing drug delivery methods for a wide range of diseases. This breakthrough technology will have therapeutic potential enabling optimization of treatment accuracy and increasing patient safety.

Name: Fibertech Group
Description:
Production of innovative high-tech composite solutions.

A considerable advantage is being able to manage directly and internally all the different production phases: from weaving the fibres and formulating "solvent free" resins to impregnating the fabrics.

The deeper knowledge of the Composite sector allows Fibertech Group to understand the needs of the customers providing technical support.

Name: Advanced Graphene Products
Description:
Advanced Graphene Products is a nanotechnological venture focused on large-scale production and supply of graphene materials and development of graphene applications. Founded in 2012, AGP has prepared a highly efficient graphene manufacturing process and established industrial recognition as a fully-fledged partner. 

High Strength Metallurgical Graphene (HSMG) is a large-area polycrystalline graphene sheet, characterized by unique stability and durability. Our patented manufacturing method is based on the graphene crystallization on the surface of liquid metal. Liquid matrix enables grain rotation and rearrangement during nucleation process which results in larger grain sizes and improved graphene properties.

Name: Smart Material Solutions
Description: Smart Material Solutions is revolutionizing nanofabrication with its patented nanocoining technology — a scalable, high-speed nanopatterning process that’s hundreds of times faster than electron-beam lithography. Nanocoining enables industrial-scale production of advanced metamaterials and plasmonic structures by seamlessly nanopatterning cylindrical tools for high-throughput, roll-to-roll manufacturing. This breakthrough unlocks substantial commercial opportunities across rapidly growing markets, including solar energy, next-generation displays, and premium decorative and security films.

Name: Occam Dx
Description:
The impossible made simple!

Conventional microbiology requires laborious amplification to even detect anything. We are the first to introduce a technology that is sensitive enough to simply count the pathogens one by one directly from the sample. 

Thanks to our patented nano-electronic biosensor, we are able to detect even one single virus or bacterium, resulting in unprecedented sensitivity. We identify the targeted  pathogen and disregard other components in the sample, for superior specificity. This gives you better insights to make the right diagnosis and determine the correct regimen.

The test is performed as close as possible to the patient. The data is brought as close as possible to the clinician, through the cloud. This ensures a secure mode of communication between the medical professional in the ER, OR, ICU and GP’s office and the microbiologist in the lab.

We significantly reduced the logistical complexity of a diagnosis. Less hands-on time, easy-to-use for all practitioners, online accessible, resulting in an effective and cost efficient procedure.

Name: OrelTech/ATMOcoat
Description:
ORELTECH is an innovation leader and expert in metallization and functional surface coating.

ORELTECH was founded in 2015 by alumni of Weizmann Institute of Science in Israel. Our founder, Natalia, found a way to implement reductive properties of cold plasma to solid metal coatings for electronics industry. This led her to develop a new platform technology for applying functional thin metal coatings on almost any substrate.

Today, the core of ORELTECH’s success is the proprietary formula of nanoparticle-free metal solutions, which has grown into an extensive library of unique formulations developed for specific technological challenges, and a committed tech team with combined 25+ years of experience in chemical research and product development. 

Customer and market needs have led ORELTECH to develop solutions to metallization challenges in industries including printed electronics, PCB production, photovoltaics, medical coatings, catalytic and antibacterial layers, plating on plastics and smart textiles.

Together with our customers, we get to the core of any metallization challenge, tailor our products and optimize the production process to meet their specific demands. We are satisfied when the customer masters our technology in mass production.

ORELTECH solutions and products are developed and manufactured in Berlin, Germany, where the company has relocated in 2018.

Name: MAEBL
Description:
The Meeting for Advanced Electron Beam Lithography (MAEBL) comprises a geographically dispersed and notably vendor-segmented community of engineers, scientists, and students who use electron beam lithography (EBL) to enable cutting-edge research, development, and innovation. MAEBL networks its global members through annual workshops/meetings and social media, which is made possible with sponsor support. Devoid of conference formality, MAEBL meetings provide an intimate space for its community, spanning academia, industry, and government, to disseminate foundational knowledge, common challenges, and best practices in electron beam lithography and related areas of process integration. MAEBL's hallmark of continuous active engagement brings together the collective living knowledge of an esoteric field of nanotechnology and nanofabrication. 

Follow us here on LinkedIn and join us online or in-person to participate in an active and growing community!

Description:
Hydrosome Labs is a Chicago-based B-to-B biotechnology company on a mission to change lives by changing water.  Our technology, Hydrosome H2O, is a natural and chemical-free process that improves the power of water through the emerging science of ultrafine bubbles. The innovative process delivers active ingredients to cells more efficiently and completely with multiple applications across personal care and cosmetics, beverages, precision fermentation, and controlled environment agriculture.  Uses range from helping plants grow faster and healthier with less fertilizer, improving nutrient uptake in skin care, delivering faster, longer lasting, and improved hydration in performance drinks and better yields for precision fermentation.   

Hydrosome H2O also allows manufacturers to do more with less, use fewer chemicals, reduce environmental impact, and shorten supply chains.   The company and advisors include business and R&D execs from Fortune 500 companies like P&G, Goldman Sachs, and Nestle Health Sciences, as well as the Intercontinental Exchange, owner of the New York Stock Exchange.   

For more information visit www.hydrosomelabs.com

Name: Nikalyte Ltd

Nikalyte Ltd designs and manufactures advanced Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) systems and high-performance Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (SERS) substrates for research and industrial applications.

Our deposition techniques include magnetron sputtering, e-beam evaporation, thermal evaporation, and gas condensation, ensuring ultra-pure, well-controlled deposition processes for advanced battery and energy materials, electrochemistry and catalysis, quantum materials, and sensing applications.

In parallel, our gold and silver SERS substrates deliver reliable, high-sensitivity detection for chemical, biological, environmental, pharmaceutical, food safety, and forensic analysis.

With deep expertise in vacuum engineering and SERS, Nikalyte supports universities, R&D laboratories, and technology innovators worldwide.

Les équipements LASSE extrêmement complexes utilisent un laser UV de très forte énergie unique au monde et intègrent les technologies associées : systèmes optiques complexes, métrologie de précision, robotique, électronique et informatique embarquée. Ils permettent la réalisation de traitements thermiques très spécifiques (recristallisation, activation…) sur des couches minces constitutives des composants semi-conducteurs les plus avancés.

Les machines LASSE sont intégrées aux chaînes de fabrication d'acteurs majeurs de la micro et nano-électronique en Asie, Europe et USA.

Name: German BioImaging - Gesellschaft für Mikroskopie und Bildanalyse e.V.
Description:
Microscopy and the quantitative analysis of microscopic images lie at the heart of modern biomedical sciences and are a motor of technological innovations. 

German BioImaging - Gesellschaft für Mikroskopie und Bildanalyse e.V. (GerBI-GMB, Society for Microscopy and Image Analysis) is a scientific society representing the interests of researchers and professionals as well as core facilities in Germany involved in microscopy and image data analysis for the life sciences. 

The society fosters knowledge exchange within the community, and interaction between scientists and engineers both from academia and industry. Additionally, GerBI-GMB promotes communication with the public about imaging in the life sciences.

Name: SEMTech Solutions, Inc.
Description: Our mission is to provide the highest quality used SEMs, scanning electron microscope accessories, and analytical laboratory microscopy services.  Our trained engineers are located throughout the country ready to repair and upgrade most SEMs manufactured by various corporations.

Name: Midel Photonics
Description:
Aiming for e-mobility, sharper displays or ever-smarter solutions using semiconductors: our modern information society faces major challenges that can only be mastered through advancing innovation. To make the development of new machining processes with lasers agile and efficient, a quickly available, high-quality and flexible solution is needed.

We develop beam shaping optics for use with high-power and ultrashort pulse lasers in manufacturing industry. Our mission is to provide high quality and fast availability. A beam shape adapted to the manufacturing step increases the efficiency of existing processes and paves the way for new technologies.

Name: CASL Surface Technologies / Kymera International
Description: CASL’s purpose is to continuously improve the performance of high value, mission critical parts through the application of wear resistant metallic or ceramic alloy coatings.  How do we do that?  We collaborate to determine your desired component performance and assess the operating environment, from there selecting the alloy, preparation process, application technology and finishing operation needed to achieve the expected result.  Once we’ve defined the outcome, our focus shifts to getting your critical parts back on time and per specification.  CASL’s unique combination of design collaboration, technical capability and vertically integrated, quick response manufacturing helps minimize owning and operating costs to get more performance out of your high value component parts.

Name: Advenira Enterprises, Inc
Description:
Advenira Enterprises is an innovative thin film deposition technology startup offering hardware, precursors, and processing that enable many critical high tech and clean tech applications.

Advenira Enterprises has authored a novel Solution Derived Nanocomposite SDN technology that allows for low cost - high throughput nanocomposite multifunctional coatings deposition. SDN technology possesses immense potential for an extremely diverse scope of applications, including but not limited to the production of energy-efficient, low-emission architectural glass, solar cells and modules, flat panel displays, advanced process equipment for the semiconductor and microelectronic industry, as well as food packaging and antibacterial materials.

Name: Brisk_AFM

Name: BIOMAT Project
Description:
The main objective of BIOMAT is to establish an Open Innovation Test Bed (BIOMAT-TB) for providing services to a wide range of European industries and SMEs to accelerate and facilitate the uptake of innovation in nano-enabled bio-based cellular materials. 

BIOMAT consortium has identified a large demand for nano-enabled bio-PUR foams with huge markets. Thus, the BIOMAT project selected five of these targeted markets for proof of concept: Building, Construction, Automotive, Furniture & Bedding. 

BIOMAT-TB will be based on a technology transfer platform to produce advanced nano-enabled polyurethane (PUR) foams and novel active NPs (antibacterial or antifungal and flame-retardant silica, hydrophobic lignin, etc.), as well as technologies for inline monitoring, digital twin pilots, and nanosafety. These technologies will be transferred to the relevant SMEs and large industries. They will be backed up by services such as relevant business plans, life cost and life cycle assessments (LCC and LCA), and assistance in funds raising.

Biomat is based on a multinational consortium of 26 partners, including universities, research centers, SMEs, large industries, and associations.

This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grand agreement N.º 953270.

Our groundbreaking optical microscope uniquely integrates spatial and temporal super-resolution for structural and functional imaging in living samples with unprecedented detail. 

Our mission is to make high-resolution imaging accessible to all, fostering scientific discovery and advancing healthcare research.

Name: Strain 2 Data
Description: Introducing Strain 2 Data - the leading manufacturer of high-quality strain sensors that are designed to meet the most demanding industrial needs. Our cutting-edge technology and precision engineering allow us to deliver strain sensors that provide the most accurate and reliable data. At Strain 2 Data, we understand that the reliability of data is paramount in today's fast-paced industrial landscape. That's why we have developed a range of strain sensors that are designed to deliver the most accurate and consistent data in even the toughest environments. Our strain sensors are made using the highest quality materials and are rigorously tested to ensure they meet our stringent quality standards. Whether you need to measure strain in a simple application or a complex one, Strain 2 Data has the right sensor for you. Our strain sensors are easy to install and integrate seamlessly with your existing systems. And with our exceptional customer support, you can rest assured that you'll receive the help you need whenever you need it. So why choose Strain 2 Data?
