Name: Universal Photonics, Inc.
Description:
Name the Application - We Provide the Solution. With more than 9,000 products for optics fabrication, we meet the surfacing requirements of a variety of industries including Precision Optics, Ophthalmic, Flat Glass & Mirror, Semi-Conductor & Electronics.

Universal Photonics is a world-leader in critical surface preparation materials. Our dedication to the advancement of surfacing and polishing technology in all phases of fabrication spans over 94 years, and is supported with state-of-art laboratory, manufacturing and distribution facilities throughout the United States, Europe, and the Far East.

Our memberships and affiliations include National Glass Association, The International Society for Optics and Photonics (SPIE), American Precision Optics Manufacturers Association and New York Photonics Rochester.

Our goal as a manufacturer and global distributor is to provide you with the most advanced surfacing and production products available. To assure this, we are committed to research and product development, maintaining in-depth knowledge of the latest advancements across a variety of surfacing technologies, and ongoing, comprehensive field research.

More than offer an extensive line of quality products, UPI applies the Science of Surfacing to all applications. Contact our Account Services team or arrange to consult with a Field Applications Engineer. As we have for nearly a century, UPI stands ready to meet your specific application needs quickly and thoroughly, while always providing fast, dependable service and accurate, technical consultations.

Name: LABORATORIO NACIONAL DE NANOTECNOLOGIA LANOTEC CENAT
Description:
El Laboratorio Nacional de Nanotecnología (LANOTEC), adscrito al Centro Nacional de Alta Tecnología (CeNAT), Contribuye con el desarrollo de tecnologías que permitan el mejoramiento de productos y procesos en el sector industrial. Y coadyuven a disminuir la brecha de la nanotecnología entre los países desarrollados y sub-desarrollados.
Política de calidad: El LANOTEC se compromete a satisfacer a sus clientes brindando el máximo compromiso de calidad en todos sus servicios, a través de la mejora continua de nuestros procesos, la trazabilidad de las mediciones, la confiabilidad de los resultados, la confidencialidad, la imparcialidad y la competencia técnica del laboratorio y su personal.

Name: point electronic GmbH
Description:
point electronic GmbH specialises in the development and manufacture of turnkey technologies and instruments for electron microscopy.  

Our portfolio includes detectors, electronics and microscope controls systems for SEM, FIB, TEM and Microanalysers. We complete our hardware with tailormade software, including offline software for data processing.

With over 30 years of expertise, point electronic GmbH is a market leader in signal acquisition and processing for electron microscopes. 

As the sole worldwide provider, we also offer custom electronics development and manufacturing services for OEMs and column manufacturers.

All our systems provide maximum interface flexibility, and accessibility thanks to transparent software, APIs and SKDs.

Name: Tesla NanoCoatings, Inc.
Description:
Tesla NanoCoatings, founded by Todd Hawkins, is a nanotechnology research and development company with a focus on carbon nanotubes (CNTs). For the first time since their discovery in 1991, Tesla NanoCoatings has successfully brought carbon nanotubes out of the lab and into the protective coatings industry where they demonstrate incredible prowess. We offer a “2-coat” corrosion control coating that is an ideal replacement for the traditional “3-coat” zinc-rich epoxy coating systems, thermal sprayed aluminum coatings, or galvanizing. Our full line of CNT products includes high-strength primers and topcoats, steel pit fillers, our award-winning non-skid deck coating, and more.

With a tensile strength 50 times greater than that of steel, our carbon nanocoatings provide offshore steel structures with the highest level of impact resistance available today. If their immense strength wasn’t impressive enough, CNTs also serve as incredible conductors of electricity with roughly 1,000 times the conductivity of copper. This CNT technology makes our protective coatings the most effective against corrosion and oxidation by far, even in harsh offshore environments. Our coatings are engineered and proven to maximize life cycle and ROI as demonstrated, validated, and recommended by the U.S. Army Corps Engineer Research and Development Center.

Name: Shilps Sciences
Description:
Shilps Sciences, a deep science company based out of Bangalore, India, develops novel solutions for imaging and characterization at the nano & micro scale. Our products enable our clients in life sciences, advanced materials and nano scale manufacturing to build their next generation products. Shilps Sciences is a recipient of several national grants and holds multiple patents around its technologies. We believe in Partnering for your Success.

Lucent AFM
Our Lucent series of Atomic Force Microscopes combine AFM with Laser Scanning Microscopy, White Light Interferometer & Optical Coherence Tomography for correlational imaging, newer insights and faster results. Covering a range of millimeter to nanometer, topography to sub-surface, profile to material analysis, our product comes with a vast suite of data acquisition and analysis features. 
Whether it’s life science research, micro fabrication metrology or novel material development, stay ahead with Lucent.

Dexter platform
Analysis of living cells and finding the right cells is of critical importance for therapy development. The Dexter Microfluidic Platform is the perfect choice for functional screening of live single cells and cell retrieval for downstream processes. Using tiny droplets as nano bio-reactors, the product enables singulation, time lapse cell tracking and a range of assays to suit your requirements. 
Dexter, an affordable and robust solution, ideally suited for research applications in cell therapy, antibody development and cell line development.

Name: TESCAN D-A-CH
Description:
Seit 2018 Ist die TESCAN GmbH (ehemals EO Elektronen-Optik-Service von 1979-2018) für den deutschsprachigen Raum als Vertriebs-, Servicegesellschaft zuständig. Mit einer eigenen Niederlassung in Österreich sowie einer Fiskalvertretung in der Schweiz stehen wir seit über 40 Jahren für ausgezeichnete Beratung und zuverlässigen Service mit kürzesten Reaktionszeiten.

Servicekonzept 
Die TESCAN GmbH bietet einen umfassenden Service für das gesamte Portfolio der TESCAN Rasterelektronenmikroskope. Dies beginnt mit der Installation des Mikroskops, der anschließenden Geräteschulung und im weiteren Verlauf auch den Wartungen durch Ihren persönlichen Servicetechniker. Unser dezentralisiertes Netzwerk von Servicetechnikern im Außendienst gewährleistet kurze Anfahrtswege und somit schnelle Unterstützung. Weiterhin stehen Ihnen unsere Techniker in der Niederlassung in Dortmund bei technischen Fragestellungen via Telefon oder per Fernwartung zur Seite. 

Durch langjährige Erfahrung und regelmäßige Schulungen der Techniker gewährleisten wir ein gleichbleibend hohes Serviceniveau. Fragen bezüglich Ihrer Anwendung beantwortet Ihnen unser Team von Applikationsspezialisten in Dortmund ebenso.

Name: BRAVE Analytics GmbH
Description:
It all started with a BRAVE idea – to achieve continuous (nano)particle sizing on a single-particle basis, for the lab and online during production. This idea became BRAVE Analytics’ patented OF2i technology and is now in use measuring particles in a range from 50 nm to 5 µm (sample-dependent). 
But it didn't stop there: add an optional Raman module to identify particles directly in liquids with no or minimal sample preparation. This device detects nanoparticles (<1 µm) and microplastics (1 µm to 50 µm) continuously and directly in-flow.
Or how about aggregation monitoring with a module using static light scattering, covering the size range 5 nm to 50 nm (sample-dependent)?
For micron-sized particles we have an in-flow large particle detector (2 µm to 80 µm size range) which is ideal for PFAT5 evaluation and detecting contamination.

Learn more about our BRAVE new technology on our website and stay tuned for new developments, including more automation options.

Name: SmarAct Group
Description:
With the development and production of market-leading solutions in the field of high-precision positioning, automation and metrology, the SmarAct Group reliably accompanies their customers in achieving their goals. The broad product portfolio – from single positioning stages to complex parallel kinematics, miniaturized robots, control systems and measurement technology – is complemented by automated microassembly solutions. Even the most challenging customer requirements can be met by maximum adaptability and complete in-house production.

Since its founding in 2005, SmarAct has steadily grown from a small team of engineers to a group of companies with three independent business units and over 200 highly skilled members. Today, SmarAct relies on years of experience and, above all, on a very passionate team with unconditional customer focus.

Name: N&P
Description:
Nanotecnologia especializada em GRANDES RESULTADOS

Para pensar grande é preciso se atentar aos detalhes, principalmente quando se trata de tecnologia 100% verde, sustentável e orgânica. A N&P - Deep tech de nano e biotecnologia, desenvolve formulações seguindo os princípios de Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) para o agronegócio e
indústria a partir de matérias-primas naturais.

Conheça as nossas soluções inovadoras e revolucione o seu negócio.

Name: MSCA Doctoral Network - STRIKE
Description: STRIKE brings together 12 institutes of academic and non-academic sectors from 8 EU countries with the aim to STRIKE malignant tumors using innovative magnetic nanomaterials. New experimental aspects on design and synthesis of nanostructured magnetic materials are applied to the development of innovative magnetic field-responsive nanotherapeutics for osteosarcoma (OS) combinational therapy. Moreover, the peculiar features of STRIKE nanomaterials are exploited for liquid biopsy using biological samples of BRCA+ breast cancer (BC) patients. The cooperation among outstanding universities, research institutes and industries provides the ESRs with trans-national exchanges and the most innovative research trainings in novel anticancer therapeutic strategies, including in vivo biological evaluation, imaging in OS and early diagnosis in BC. STRIKE will raise excellence in research and training in nanomedicine/precision medicine creating new generation of entrepreneurial and innovative multidisciplinary Early Stage Researchers (ESRs).

Name: Hilton Head Workshop
Description:
The 22nd  in the series of Hilton Head Workshops on the science and technology of solid-state sensors, actuators, and microsystems will take place May 31 to June 4, 2026 at the Sonesta Resort on Hilton Head Island, SC. This exciting multidisciplinary event has occurred biennially since 1984. Hilton Head Island, the beachfront workshop hotel, and the casual, comfortable, seaside environment with nearly all meals included allows for relaxed exchanges, collegial interactions, and many fruitful discussions. The Hilton Head Workshop has historically been a regional meeting restricted to professionals living in North or South America as well as past attendees of previous Hilton Head Workshops. Beginning in 2020, the meeting opened to international colleagues beyond the Americas. We continue to welcome researchers worldwide to submit high-quality abstracts (contributed and late news) for consideration by the submission deadlines. 

This meeting is sponsored by Transducer Research Foundation: https://www.transducer-research-foundation.org/

Name: Z Future
Description: Z Future offers strategic innovation consulting based on nanotechnology and prototyping solutions.

Name: Sol Voltaics
Description:
Sol Voltaics creates nanomaterials to improve the efficiency of solar energy capture, generation and storage. A fast-growing, venture-backed company with a strong intellectual property portfolio, we are developing a high-volume production platform for our patented Aerotaxy nanowire thin-film process, which will dramatically improve the efficiency of conventional solar modules while maintaining very competitive pricing.

The company made a major technological breakthrough with the successful alignment of gallium-arsenide nanowires in a thin film. When integrated in a tandem-junction architecture on mainstream crystalline silicon panels, Sol Voltaics’ nanomaterials innovations will enable photovoltaic (PV) module efficiencies of well over 27% — representing a > 50% enhancement in energy generation compared to today’s solar panels. 

Sol Voltaics has also progressed through several generations of development of its Aerotaxy technology. This patented process, originating out of Lund University in Sweden, is the foundation for producing nanowire solar cells and films in a cost-effective manner, a key parameter in the commercialisation process. 

The company is backed by the Swedish Energy Agency and has also received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation program.

Sol Voltaics is based in Lund, Sweden.

Name: EsaNanotech
Description:
We are an Italian entity, but above all, we are reality.
Over those who merely speak of innovation, We prefer those who realize it: someone like us.
We are committed to developing graphene-based technologies for radiant systems, advanced electronic devices, sensors, and supercapacitors.
Because the future of the industry lies in the industries of the future.

Name: FideNa [2007-2013]
Description:
Ex miembros de la antigua Fundacion I+D en Nanotecnologia.

FideNa fue un centro de investigación en Nanotecnología que funcionó en Navarra ente 2007 y 2013.

En 2013, fruto de una reorganización de la I+D Navarra, FideNa se extinguió y sus investigadores pasaron en su mayoría a ser parte de otro centro de investigación llamado CEMITEC, actual NAITEC. Actualmente se puede contactar con la mayoría de ellos a través de esta página.

Name: SENSE in
Description:
SENSE in is a monitoring specialist of composite parts during both manufacturing (PHM - Process Health Monitoring) and usage (SHM - Structural Health Monitoring). Our sensors are non-intrusive and integrated at the core of the composite during its fabrication, enabling the tracking of various parameters throughout the material's lifespan, including resin flow, temperature, and damage.

SENSE in has also developed a unique solution for ceramics monitoring. This monitoring technology allows for the detection of microcracks related to the manufacturing process, usage, or transportation. The sensor is undetectable, of negligible weight, and non-intrusive.

Name: Terracarb
Description: Terracarb has developed a library of chemical precursors for the production of graphene. By utilizing high production volume chemicals and conventional industrial processes, Terracarb strives to enable the techno-commercialization of graphene.

Name: PIK Instruments Sp z.o.o.
Description: We are a company experienced in the distribution and servicing of modern and advanced laboratory equipment. We specialize in the field of electron microscopy and solutions dedicated to the preparation of microscopy samples. Our team of highly qualified specialists provides you with professional advice and comprehensive services for both commercial and service.

Name: Advanced Diamond Technologies, Inc.
Description: Sold/closed

Name: TAU.nano - The Center for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology at Tel Aviv University
Description: Tel Aviv University Center for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology is a multidisciplinary research center committed to advancing society by conducting cutting edge research and by engaging in close and sustainable cooperation with industry and society. Established already in 2000, we pioneered Nanotechnology in Israel and since then we are the leading center in Israel in the field.

Name: Rede NanoSAÚDE
Description: A Rede NanoSAÚDE é uma rede multidisciplinar de pesquisadores em nanotecnologia capaz de agregar conhecimento em instrumentação, desenvolvimento de protótipos, controle de qualidade com viés regulatório, ensaios pré-clínicos e clínicos.

Name: NuNano
Description:
NuNano is a UK-based company specialising in the design and manufacture of probes for atomic force microscopy and cantilever-based sensor devices. Building on over 30 years of experience in atomic force microscopy, proprietary microfabrication processes enable NuNano to manufacture AFM probes with the tightest dimensional tolerances in the market at present. Furthermore, NuNano is passionate about providing AFM users with the best possible customer service, from the first time you visit our website to storing your probes after a successful day on the microscope.
NuNano is always interested in hearing from potential distributors. Please contact us at info@nunano.com.

Name: BioAxial, part of Telight
Description:
BioAxial is now part of Telight. Please follow our news at Telight LinkedIn page https://linkedin.com/company/telight

TELIGHT specializes in a field of instruments employing light optics, in particular, providing innovative types and solutions for light microscopy.

Name: NanoScale Corporation
Description:
NanoScale Corporation (NanoScale) developed chemical decontamination and indoor air quality systems under the brand names of FAST-ACT, ChemKlenz and OdorKlenz, as well as odor elimination solutions for consumers. 

NanoScale was founded as an independent commercial research and development organization that would further develop nanotechnologies from Kansas State University. The company originally acted as a self-funded advanced research facility focusing on government sponsored programs and developing unique solutions for decontamination and environmental remediation.

Name: Institute of Nanotechnology
Description:
About the Institute...

Our Vision:
"To be the foremost global nanotechnology information provider and membership organisation."

Our Mission:
"To foster, develop and promote all aspects of responsible nanoscience and nanotechnology that have the potential to benefit society and the environment"

Background:
The Institute of Nanotechnology (IoN) was founded by Ottilia Saxl in January 1997. It is a registered Charity, whose core activities are focused on education and training in nanotechnology. It grew out of the Centre for Nanotechnology, part funded by the DTI through the UK 's National Initiative on Nanotechnology (NION). The Institute was one of the world's first nanotechnology information providers and is now a global leader.

The Institute works closely with governments, universities, researchers, companies and the general public to educate and inform on all aspects of nanotechnology. It also organises various international scientific events, conferences and educational courses that examine the implications of nanotechnology for industry, society, health, energy and the environment, and the disadvantaged communities across the globe.

Name: Veneto Nanotech
Description:
Founded in July 2003, Veneto Nanotech aims at co-ordinating the Italian Nanotech Cluster’s activities and implementing its strategic goals.
Veneto Nanotech focuses on technology transfer and acts as a single point of contact for the companies it collaborates with, for which it develops projects, specific jobs and joint initiatives.
It also promotes actions to support technology start-ups and to attract companies that may be interested in investing in the regional territory.
The aim of Veneto Nanotech is to identify and develop new applications for ongoing research lines, accomplish technology transfer projects with businesses, prepare and co-ordinate national and Community-level projects, foster new start-ups and promote awareness and dissemination of nanotech issues. 
These activities are mainly carried out in its three research facilities: 
LaNN - Nanofabrication and research lab (focus: lithography and nanodevices);
ECSIN - Research centre actively researching the impact of nanotechnology on health, environment and society;
Nanofab - Research lab focusing on knowledge transfer in the field of surface treatment and new nanostructured materials

Name: Quantum Plasma Technology
Description:
Atmosferik plazma ile hidrofilik yüzey elde edilerek, malzemelerin hemen işlenmeye devam edilebilmesini sağlar. 
Tüm malzeme yüzeylerinde nano çukurlar açılarak, yüzey hidrofilik hale gelir ve yüzey boyama ve kaplama için en uygun hale gelir.

Name: NanoTC
Description:
Nanotecnología aplicada para mejorar materiales industriales existentes

En NanoTC ayudamos a la industria a mejorar el desempeño de sus materiales sin cambiar procesos productivos ni formulaciones base.
Desarrollamos y producimos nanopartículas de cobre y óxido de grafeno, que integramos como aditivos funcionales en recubrimientos, pinturas, polímeros y materiales industriales.

Qué problemas abordamos

• Corrosión y degradación prematura de materiales
• Crecimiento de hongos, bacterias y biofouling
• Pérdida de desempeño térmico y eficiencia
• Altos costos de mantención y baja vida útil

Qué hacemos en concreto

• Incorporamos propiedades antifúngicas, antimicrobianas y antifouling
• Reforzamos durabilidad, resistencia y estabilidad de materiales existentes
• Mejoramos eficiencia y desempeño térmico en aplicaciones donde el control de calor es crítico
• Todo sin rediseñar productos ni interrumpir la operación

Cómo trabajamos

Operamos bajo un modelo Probar → Validar → Transferir → Escalar.
Desarrollamos proyectos aplicados a medida, validamos desempeño en condiciones reales y entregamos soluciones listas para integrarse y escalar, con impacto técnico y económico medible.

Para quién

Trabajamos con empresas industriales de energía, construcción, minería, pinturas y recubrimientos, polímeros, logística y ambientes marinos que necesitan extender la vida útil de sus materiales y reducir riesgos operacionales.

 Valparaíso, Chile
 contacto@nanotc.cl
 |  www.nanotc.cl

Name: 2D fab
Description:
2D fab is a large-scale manufacturer of green graphene. We offer the world’s most cost-efficient high-quality graphene products, with years of experience and research behind our manufacturing process.

Our business is based on the enormous potential of graphene and its incredible properties. The material is stronger than steel, harder than diamond and conducts electricity better than copper, to name a few of its superior properties.

2D fabs production process is designed for customization, meaning that we can tailor the price/performance ratio to fit your products need. It is also very cost-efficient since we have managed to create a process where we have a very low energy consumption compared to other manufacturing methods. Besides the fact that 2D fab’s manufacturing process is cost-efficient and customer adapted, it is also easily scalable and therefor suitable for industrial volumes. The scalability also enables licensing and other very exciting business concepts. Additionally, our process is hydromechanical and very energy-efficient – both making the manufacturing more environmentally friendly.

2D fab strives for an automated, high-tech, self-adopting and AI-based production of two-dimensional materials. The graphene market is expected to explode, and we aim to become the largest, and best, graphene producer in Europe within a decade.

Name: MS Technologies Inc.
Description:
MS Tech is a global leader in designing, manufacturing, and innovation of advanced sensors. MS Tech operates through three specialized business divisions: MS Food Safety, MS Bio-Medical, and MS Detection. 
Our Sensor Technology powers innovative products and solutions for a wide range of global industries, including Food Safety & Product Inspection, Bio-Medical Diagnostics, Homeland Security & People Screening, Fire & Smoke Detection, Water & Air Monitoring, and Aerospace. 
Our diverse customer range includes government agencies, airports, airlines, military and law enforcement authorities, hospitals, petrochemical companies, equipment manufacturers and service providers across multiple sectors worldwide.
At MS Tech, our mission is to make the world a safer, healthier, and more efficient place through advanced, reliable, and environmentally friendly technologies.

Name: Kaleido Technology
Description:
Kaleido Technology innovates in optical design and manufacturing to enable next generation imaging experiences. With a focus on proprietary and ultra-precise solutions, greater optical design freedom can be achieved thus shortening lead-times, while enabling high volume mass production with greater consistency.

Kaleido Technology was founded in 2001, and AAC Optics commenced investment and collaboration in 2010. Since engaging with AAC Technologies, the innovation has been brought to mass production and may now be found inside smartphone cameras while also exploring further markets. One of the key technologies is Wafer Level Glass (WLG) lenses that enable restoration of natural colors, improved image clarity and images that are consistent regardless of ambient temperature variations.

Kaleido Technology today employs unique expertise within a range of optics fields and ultra-precision manufacturing, and offer exciting opportunities to make a real difference in next generation imaging.

Name: NanoBios Lab
Description: NanoBios Lab can be termed as the perfect breather for a passionate researcher. A learning hub where creative ideas are translated into successful simulations with a positive impact. Every candidate is trained resourcefully to lead research projects and contribute towards smooth operational activities of lab. The lab group members associate with each other as family and believe in achieving success as a unit combining their expertise coming from various disciplines. Our guide (Prof. Srivastava) has always motivated us to explore solutions for the under-researched challenges majorly impacting our country. This is the place where we are empowered to be skilled independent researchers in future, develop solutions for the healthcare industry and contribute to the maximum towards bringing up a healthier India.

Name: ANNEALSYS
Description:
Annealsys designs and manufactures Rapid Thermal Processing (RTP) and Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD) systems. Our customers are companies for production applications and R&D laboratories in the fields of silicon, compound semiconductors, nanotechnologies, MEMS, etc.
With 300 systems installed worldwide, Annealsys is a leading manufacturer of RTP systems for special production applications using substrates up to 200 mm.
Our cold wall chamber RTP furnaces can perform processes up to 1450°C. Pulse annealing process is available to anneal thermally sensitive substrates. It is possible to carry out process under any types of gases, from atmospheric pressure down to high vacuum.
Applications: implantation annealing, contact annealing, rapid thermal oxidation, nitridation, selenization, sulfurization, CVD of graphene and h-BN, etc.
A high temperature RTP furnace for processes up to 2000°C is available for silicon carbide implantation annealing and graphene generation by sublimation of silicon from SiC.
Our Direct Liquid Injection (DLI) CVD and ALD deposition systems offer the highest process versatility for development of new materials by utilization of low vapor and thermally unstable precursors. They can handle most of the materials of the periodic table and offer unique process and material development capabilities. The 50 mm machine (MC-050) uses a lamp heating and allows in-situ annealing. All DLI-CVD reactors can perform DLI-ALD and pulse pressure CVD processes inside the same chamber.
Applications: Semiconductor, micro batteries, BAW and SAW filters, 2D materials, energy harvesting, etc.
Our philosophy involves building up a long-term relationship with our customers, provide high reliability and high quality tools, insure low cost of ownership and offer outstanding customer support and service. To achieve this, we have created a worldwide network with trained engineers for sales and service.

Name: Epic Advanced Materials
Description: We are an AI-driven advanced manufacturing company focused on democratizing the material science of nanotechnology. We're taking material science from trial and error informed by materials insight to data driven breakthroughs that change our world.

Name: Fluid-Screen
Description:
Fluid-Screen is revolutionizing the way to analyze bacteria, challenging the status quo, bacterial culture, that has been a standard for quality assurance in the pharma industry for over 130 years.

At Fluid-Screen we believe that people and companies make better decisions when they have access to the right information. These decisions impact their health, the well-being of their families, enabling them to work more efficiently.

Our artificial intelligence-based microchip technology provides information about bacteria present in fluid samples in minutes instead of days. We help our partners, pharmaceutical companies, accelerate development and manufacturing of new therapeutics. It is time they get access to the right information when they need it. 

Fluid-Screen has raised over 7 million USD Series A round of financing. Our goal is to bring a family of products to market that will fundamentally improve the pharmaceutical manufacturing process. We are currently engineering our first prototype systems which will be deployed for validation testing at up to five identified customers.

Name: PureFize Technologies AB
Description:
PureFize is a simple, fast and extremely efficient way to
eradicate bacteria, viruses and other germs. It can be used to
improve hygiene or disinfect surfaces, foods, liquid and air.
PureFize is mercury-free and developed with sustainability in
mind to minimize impact on people and planet.

Visit us at https://www.purefize.com

Name: Semplor
Description:
Semplor is an innovative high-tech company based in Eindhoven, The Netherlands. Eindhoven is the crucible of Electron Microscopy with its origins at Philips Electron Optics during the middle of the last century. Semplor was founded in 2021 by seasoned electron microscopy experts. The NANOS has been developed by passionate innovators with an extensive network and deep-understanding of EM technology and engineering. All together the team has more than 200+ years EM experience with respected electron microscopy manufacturers.

Our mission is to make SEM accessible for anyone, everywhere. Driven by passion and experience a new generation tabletop SEM has been created. During development of the NANOS the latest insights and smart engineering have been used. The latest technology makes the NANOS comprehensive and affordable. Engineered by a team with broad experience, completely dedicated to the tabletop SEM, has resulted in the NANOS. A robust and high quality SEM; the next generation tabletop SEM.
Engineered in Europe, designed for the World.

A true analytical tabletop scanning electron microscope.
The NANOS offers SEM imaging at a low cost of ownership for high-resolution imaging and integrated energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) for rapid elemental analysis. It is designed for easy installation, ease of use and easy servicing.

Name: LAYERTEC
Description:
LAYERTEC is your reliable partner for high-quality optical components and customized solutions for laser applications. Our extensive range of products includes optics, coatings, and services for various industries such as laser research, medical technology, machine tool manufacturing, and semiconductor manufacturing.

Visit our website for more information.

Name: Bristol Nano Dynamics
Description:
Our innovative video-rate scanning probe microscope images cm2 areas with nanometre resolution and can track dynamic processes in real time. The product of 10 years of intensive development, our technology is fundamentally different from traditional atomic force microscopes but produces images and data with similar resolution and accuracy to the best competing high-speed AFMs in only a 1,000th of the time!

Creative researchers are already using our instruments to carry out analyses that were previously thought impossible across diverse fields from genomics to 2D materials, batteries and semiconductors.

We continue to develop new technologies and will be launching a scanning probe (patent pending) version of our microscope soon for semiconductor wafer measurements.

Contact us for more details and a demonstration on your own samples.

Name: Nick Holonyak, Jr. Micro and Nanotechnology Laboratory
PI BeNeLux B.V.
Description:
As PI Benelux, we are proud to be part of one of the leading players in the global market for precision positioning technology. Located in Sint-Oedenrode, we joined the PI Group in 2015.
With long term experience, in-depth knowledge and a passion for technology, our experts develop customer and application-specific products. By creating bright ideas together, our customers get the most advanced drive technologies and system solutions.

Want to know more? Visit physikinstrumente.nl

Name: CEOS GmbH
Description: CEOS GmbH is the world's leading manufacturer for aberration correctors in electron microscopes. The company is concentrating on research, development and production of advanced optical components for electron microscopy such as correctors for spherical and chromatic aberrations, monochromators and imaging energy filters.

Name: ZMN @ TU Ilmenau
Description:
The ZMN is the Center of Micro- and Nanotechnologies at the TU Ilmenau. Since 2006 it exists as a central operating unit of the university and as a technological center for the associated Institute of Micro- and Nanotechnologies (IMN MacroNano).
The foundation of the interfaculty research center was already driven by 9 departments in the mid 90s of the last century. With the aim of providing the community of researchers and university staff with a place for top-level interdisciplinary research, the high-tech building Feynmanbau was opened as ZMN in 2002. With the acquisition of a second building by the TU Ilmenau, the Meitnerbau, further (clean room) areas could be developed for the center.

The main task of the ZMN is to support and promote scientists in the field of micro- and nanotechnology. The ZMN fulfills this task by purchasing equipment and new facilities, operating and maintaining them, and through financial project management. In particular, the support and technological safeguarding of the equipment is organized in close coordination with the technologically driving departments of the center and institute.

As a "Core Facility", the ZMN provides direct access to its infrastructure as well as the possibility to provide processes as services. The Center is open to the research community of the TU Ilmenau as well as to external researchers from academia and industry.

The physical proximity of the various departments and research groups creates an intensive scientific exchange in a creative environment. Interdisciplinarity is lived at the ZMN.

Name: temicon GmbH
Description:
Worldwide series producer of nano and micro structured films and metal parts for innovative displays, head-up-displays, LED, Solar, Optics, Sensors and Life Science applications.

The company temicon is a pioneer in lithographic production of micro- and nanostructures and parts on large, seamless surfaces and replication by roll-to-roll or roll-to-plate nanoimprint processing, micro injection molding and electroforming.

#microlensarrays #prisms #diffusors #augmentedrealitydisplays #holographicstructures #microfluidicstructures #microstructuredtiterplates #micromembranes #vibratingmesh #micrometalparts #encoderdisc

Name: Ebatco
Description: Exponential Business and Technologies Company (Ebatcoᵀᴹ) is a nanotechnology service and development company. Ebatco is a Minnesota Corporation incorporated on July 3, 2007. Its worldwide headquarters is located in the beautiful, convenient and industry-intensive City of Eden Prairie in the Twin Cities Metro Area. It serves the world's business and technology communities through its nanotechnology expertise, business savvy, best-value products and satisfactory services.

Name: Richter Precision Inc
Description:
Richter Precision Inc. is North America's leading PVD, CVD, and DCD coating company. For more than 30 years, our coatings have been helping customers realize the full potential of their tools and components, thereby improving the efficiency and profitability of their operations. We offer a wide range of thin-film processes because we know that there is no "one size fits all" coating.

The variety of surface treatment technologies available to us allows our team to select the ideal deposition method and thin-film coating to fully optimize your tool or component's performance.

RPI manufactures coatings for application in many areas such as cutting, forming, stamping, automotive, aerospace, medical, firearms, decorative household products, oil & gas, semiconductor, and power generation industries.

Name: Quantum Circuits Group Bariloche
Description:
The Bariloche Quantum Circuits Group, led by Leandro Tosi, explores the limits of quantum mechanics using superconducting circuits. We specialize in hybrid systems that combine superconducting and semiconducting materials to develop devices with unique properties, such as the entanglement of fermionic and bosonic degrees of freedom. Currently, we are building a small-scale quantum processor to advance quantum computing in Argentina and worldwide.  

Experience
Bariloche Quantum Circuits Group 
Research Laboratory in Quantum Physics  
Since December 2019  

Key Activities  
- Design and simulation of superconducting circuits.  
- Fabrication of high-quality factor microwave resonators.  
- Development of superconducting quantum circuits.  
- Quantum simulation and characterization of superconducting devices.  
- Radiofrequency measurements at low temperatures (10 mK).  

Equipment  
- Bluefors LD250 Dilution Refrigerator with an 8T magnetic field capability and RF lines up to 20 GHz.  
- 2-Port Vector Network Analyzer up to 26.5 GHz.  
- Radiofrequency Generator up to 20 GHz.  
- Access to the Clean Room at the Bariloche Atomic Center.  

Team  
The group, led by Leandro Tosi, includes:  
- Kelvin Ramos (PhD)  
- Valentín Reparaz (Master’s)  
- Cristopher Tene (Master’s)  

We collaborate daily with other researchers from the Department of Devices and Sensors (Dyspo), directed by Hernán Pastoriza.  

Notable Alumni  
- Ignacio Lobato (Internship)  
- Erick Potosí (Master’s)  
- Louise Noelle (Internship)  
- Ivana Curci (Master’s)  
- Joan Cáceres (Internship)  

Collaborations  
We work with local, national, and international institutions to advance research and technological development in quantum physics.
Location: San Carlos de Bariloche, Río Negro Province

Name: Floatech
Description: Floatech, a spin-off of the IMDEA Materials Institute, is a high tech company specialized on designing and producing silicon anodes for lithium-ion batteries. We have developed a commercial competitive process for the fabrication of >75% nanostructured Si anodes without the use of solvents and polymers during processing, hence reducing costs and emissions. The method combines manufacturing scalability, sustainability and, most importantly, leads to high-performance properties in LIBs.

Name: Materials Science and Technology (STM) @ Roma Tre
Description:
The Materials Science and Technology (STM) group is part of the Department of Civil, Computer Science, and Aeronautical Technologies Enginnering at the University of Roma Tre which was selected by the Ministry from among the 180 winning Departments of Excellence in the selection for "Departments of Excellence 2018-2022" of MIUR for the area “08 - Civil Engineering and Architecture”.

The group is made up of four staff units (a full professor, an associate professor, a researcher, a specialized technician) and a team, funded mainly through research projects, composed of about 5 units with the following profiles: two PhDs , three PhD students; the group is also assisted by undergraduates in Mechanical Engineering, Bio-Engineering and Aeronautics, who are carrying out their thesis, engaged in training activities related to research projects supported by industrial companies.
The group boasts a wide range of state-of-the-art tools for microstructural, compositional and mechanical characterization that are integrated and complementary, on which methods and techniques for analyzing and manipulating original and innovative materials have been developed. Among these it is worth mentioning electron beam microscopies (in scanning - SEM and in transmission - TEM -), ion microscopy (FIB) and stylus microscopy (AFM), nanomechanical and surface energy characterization, ray diffraction X. Characteristics such as durability and operating behavior can then be simulated thanks to other available resources, such as a salt spray chamber and a newly developed tribometer developed and patented by the group itself.

Name: Cornell NanoScale Science and Technology Facility (CNF)
Description: CNF is a world-class facility for micro and nanofabrication. Operating as an open user facility we assist users from across the country and around the world. Over 500 different individuals use CNF facilities every year. They include undergraduates, graduate students, postdocs, and professionals, both those experienced in nanotechnology as well as novices. Projects range from pure university research to product development for small and large companies. Our flexible equipment and policies allow us to undertake projects that would not be possible in many other nanofabrication environments.

Name: Spectrum Scientific, Inc.
Description:
Spectrum Scientific, Inc (SSI) has been manufacturing high volume flat, aspheric and freeform reflective optics and holographic diffraction gratings since 2004. We primarily use the optical replication process in our manufacturing allowing us to supply high fidelity, high specification precision optics at a fraction of the traditional cost of volume manufacturing.

One of our key capabilities is the manufacture of freeform optics, off-axis paraboloids and ellipsoid mirrors with surface figures down to λ/10 or better. We also manufacture plane, concave and convex holographic diffraction gratings, which can be supplied as blazed gratings using our proprietary blazing technique, which not only offers high efficiency in the UV, but lower stray light than conventional ion etched gratings.

Our high precision replicated optics, include spherical and aspheric mirrors (on or off-axis parabolic mirrors, elliptical mirrors, toroidal mirrors and cylinders) as well as retroreflectors, nanostructures, hybrid optics and complex optics. 

In addition to components, Spectrum Scientific offers a comprehensive design and manufacturing service, supplying custom designed UV spectrograph modules for OEM customers.

Name: The BIG-nano Corporation
Description:
At BIG-nano Corp, we specialize in manufacturing nanofiber meltblown materials using our own patented Canadian technology. Our mission to drive innovation has led us to become the first company to produce nanofibers on an industrial and commercial scale.

As a Canadian manufacturing company, we take pride in our diverse team consisting of professionals with several years of experience in research & development, management, and engineering.   
Not only are we committed to becoming #1 in the high-performance nonwoven market, we are also dedicated to fulfilling our social responsibilities by mentoring and supporting local entrepreneurs entering the field.

Name: Toshniwal Brothers (SR) Private Limited
Description:
Empowering Indian Research & Industry with Advanced Scientific Solutions
We are India channel partners for globally renowned brands, delivering cutting-edge technologies to researchers, academia, and industry.
Our offerings cover advanced tools and instrumentation across material science, nanotech, life sciences, photonics, metrology, and more.
Advanced Microscopy & Spectroscopy
• Confocal Raman + modular add-ons (AFM, SNOM, SHG, THz, etc.) – WITec (Oxford Instruments), Germany
• High-res AFMs – Asylum Research (Oxford Instruments), USA
• Confocal Microscopes – Andor Technologies (Oxford Instruments)
• Nano-IR & PiFM – Molecular Vista, USA
• DarkField & Hyperspectral Imaging – CytoViva, USA
Bioanalytical & Life Science
• Circular Dichroism, Stopped-Flow, DSF – Applied Photophysics, UK
• ITC, DSC, GCI for biomolecular studies – Malvern Panalytical
Nanofabrication & Metrology
• 3D Lithography – Nanoscribe, Germany
• 3D Surface Profilers – Sensofar Metrology, Spain
• Optical Inspection (medical) – Sensofar Medical, Spain
Materials Processing & Testing
• SPS, Hot Press – FCT Systeme GmbH, Germany
• Slot-Die Coating – FOM Technologies, Denmark
• Ceramic Extrusion – ECT-KEMA GmbH, Germany
• Polymer Extrusion & Testing – OCS GmbH, Germany
• Planetary Roller Extruders – Entex, Germany
• Ball Mills, Crushers, Particle Analyzers – Fritsch GmbH, Germany
Graphene Derivatives & Applications
We now manufacture graphene derivatives in-house as a strategic expansion.
• Developing graphene-based solutions for cement/construction to enhance strength, durability & sustainability
• Collaborating with academic & industry partners for new graphene applications across sectors
With a focus on scientific excellence, innovation, and customer support, we bridge global technology with Indian needs.
Interested in collaborations, demonstrations, or consultations? Let's Connect

Name: Preciseley Microtechnology Corp.
Description: Preciseley Microtechnology Corporation (“PMC”) is a global leader in optic MEMS solutions for advanced communications, automotive and intelligent sensing applications.  The company offers innovative MEMS solutions that help design engineers solve complex problems for 5G telecommunications infrastructure, data center networks, automotive LiDAR and laser lighting systems, industrial LiDAR and 3D machine vision systems, AR/VR, and biometrics.  For more information, visit www.preciseley.com.

Name: Emisshield, Inc.
Description:
We provide the highest quality, energy-saving products and technologies to all
industry market areas around the world. Utilizing advanced high-emissivity
technologies from NASA's space program, we are driving a new era of reduced
energy costs, improved combustion, and reduced carbon intensity across the full
spectrum of heat transfer and high-energy applications.

Name: Gambetti Kenologia Srl
Description:
Gambetti Kenologia is a commercial reality driven by a youthful, dynamic and versatile spirit, that is not limited to sales activities alone, but that tries to promote profitable collaboration and consulting work with its clients.
The company’s history had its beginnings thanks to the entrepreneurial spirit of its founder, Glauco Gambetti, current president, who in 1974 perceived that the sector dealing with vacuum and thin films could become a stimulating market, taking into account the period in which scientific research enjoyed an up and coming moment from the investments viewpoint.
Besides the commercial, technical and administrative offices, an ample and modern laboratory equipped for checking on and repairs to the marketed instrumentation, a clean room that hosts several instrumentations for low pressures and a capacious warehouse for consumption materials and spare parts are hosted here.
For more than three decades, Gambetti Kenologia has been dealing with top-technology products in the high-vacuum technologies sector, in the analytical sector and surface characterisation.

Name: Crest Group
Description: Crest was established in Kuala Lumpur, June 1999, with a focus on supplying inspection and measurement instruments to industries and government research centers.  We have since grown to become a leading solutions partner for our customers operating in China, Malaysia, Singapore and Thailand.

Name: Innovnano
Description:
Innovnano, an expert manufacturer of high performance ceramic powder, produces high quality nanostructured zirconia-based powders and aluminium zinc oxide sputtering targets for a variety of advanced technological applications. 

Industry Sectors: 
•	Aerospace
•	Renewable Energy
•	Medical Devices
•	Electronic Devices
•	Automotive
•	Process Control
 
Our new, purpose-built and modular production centre means that we can scale-up production to 1000 tpa to meet the rapidly growing demands of industries taking advantage of our high performance ceramics.

As well as our core stock, we can customise our products to client specifications, ensuring complete customer satisfaction.

Innovnano is actively seeking representative companies for business opportunities in various worldwide locations. If you are interested please send a brief summary describing your business and its relevance to Innovnano by email to: info@innovnano-materials.com

Name: TioTech
Description:
We enable the green shift to happen in new markets! 

TioTech is a Norwegian producer of titania (TiO2) nanomaterials and a developer of titania-based technology. We know how to optimise the production process and properties of titania nanoparticles. 

After successfully developing and commercialising a series of photocatalytic surface treatment products (www.joma-int.com), we are directing our R&D efforts towards a new area; the use of titania as anode-material in Lithium-ion batteries (LIB). 

A battery with our material on the anode will charge faster, last longer, be safer and tolerate temperature variation better than conventional batteries which have graphite on the anode, and outcompete LTO-batteries on capacity. 

The development work takes place in our in-house laboratory in Bergen, where our dedicated team play around with titania, giving life to innovative solutions.

Name: Neptune Nanotechnologies Inc.
Description: We are an early-stage tech Startup active in the bio-nano material space. Specifically, we are commercializing a technology that can convert organic fishing waste into ultra-high value nanocrystals. These nanocrystals are stronger than steel, lighter than plastic, entirely bio-based, non-toxic, bio-compatible and biodegradable. They function as physical additives where a small quantity of nanocrystals added can drastically increase the strength, stiffness, crack resistance and barrier properties of aerospace and automotive composites, structural adhesives, films, coatings and 3D printed materials,

Name: Nobio Labs
Description:
At Nobio Labs, we’re reshaping restorative dentistry with a singular purpose: to develop smart dental materials that do more than restore — they actively protect.

Powered by proprietary QASi antimicrobial filler technology, our flagship platform, Infinix, integrates long-term antibacterial functionality into the core of the restoration. This scientific breakthrough transforms the way restorations interact with the oral environment — helping reduce bacterial colonization, preserve marginal integrity, and extend restoration longevity.

Clinically validated, FDA-cleared, and trusted by leading practitioners, Infinix marks a new era in restorative care — one where materials are not passive fillers, but intelligent participants in the healing process.

We collaborate with forward-thinking clinicians, researchers, and institutions to bring the science of protection to the frontlines of dental practice — combining research, development, and real-world clinical utility.

We’re building more than a product. We’re building a platform for the future of smarter, safer, science-driven dentistry.

Name: nanoEMI
Description: We are producer of graphene flakes and composites that block electromagnetic radiation in broad spectral range.

Name: Rolling Optics AB
Description:
Rolling Optics provides cutting edge security solutions to protect and authenticate brands and other products from counterfeiting. Our innovative and patented technology allows brands to be authenticated with world-class protection in one simple view. No tools or readers needed.

From birth at the University of Uppsala and Ångström laboratory (world leading micro-optics and nanotechnology lab) to today´s mass production, we combine key skills from high end design, material physics and chemistry to cutting edge innovation, printing and manufacturing to produce unique micro optical 3D films to customers globally.

Our 3D films can be used in labels and other applications to secure and protect brands and valuable documents.

Our micro printed 3D films can display both printed and covert graphics providing several levels of security. Our  products are used to beat counterfeiting, tampering, diversion and warranty fraud. 

Please visit our website for more information, contact and inquiries: www.rollingoptics.com

Name: Bionanoplus
Description:
We are a drug delivery company specialized in providing polymeric nanoencapsulation solutions for different fields (pharma, food, veterinary, cosmetic, etc.)

Our propietary technology provides the advantages of traditional nanotechnology methods while avoiding their main drawbacks:
- No expensive laboratory equipment needed
- Easy to scale-up
- GRAS listed excipients
- No volatile organic solvents (no drying steps necessary)

Bionanoplus' strenths relay on the developments based on our different technological patented platforms that perfectly fit market needs.
 
We are developing our own product porfolio as well as providing R&D services to third parties and collaborating in several European Projects.

Applications of our technology:
- Hair loss
- Fungal infections
- Vaccination (human & veterinary)

Name: Lilliputian Systems Inc.
Description: Lilliputian Systems, Inc. has developed the world’s first Personal Power solution for Consumer Electronics ("CE") devices, a revolutionary and disruptive family of products targeted at the $50 billion portable power market. The Company’s breakthrough solution delivers the only viable small form-factor battery replacement that provides the enormous run-time improvements demanded by today’s CE devices.

Name: NanoMesureFrance Association
Description:
NanoMesureFrance contributes to structuring the industrial nanomaterials sector by bringing together all relevant stakeholders around reliable and recognized data.

Our ambition is to build a shared foundation of trust, serving both the competitiveness and safety of innovation.

Our missions are structured around six key areas:
 Identify:
o	The dimensions of objects that may constitute (nano)materials
o	The needs of industrial players in terms of both measurement methods and materials to be considered
 Characterize:
o	The “identity profile” of nanomaterials
o	The specific requirements of industries according to their sectors, material types, and intended uses
 Assess:
o	The emission of nano-objects at various stages of product life cycles
o	The capabilities of service providers and analytical platforms
 Share:
o	Ongoing projects and initiatives in France, Europe, and worldwide
o	Reference methods and guidance documents through a dedicated knowledge base
o	A mapping of available analytical resources and expertise
 Connect:
o	Our members with all stakeholders across the ecosystem
o	Through working groups, webinars, workshops, and other networking events
 Coordinate:
o	The development, validation, and harmonization of testing methods
o	The promotion of the association’s work within national, European, and international networks of influence

Chemistry & Materials – Cosmetics – Nanomedicine – Instrumentation & Sensors are the four main thematic areas structuring our members’ activities.

They collaborate within dedicated working groups as well as during workshops.

The topics addressed, along with the documents produced, directly support R&D objectives and regulatory expectations across the relevant sectors.

Interested in joining us?

Find out how to become a member here: https://www.nanomesurefrance.fr/pourquoi-et-comment-adherer/

Name: Nano Bubble Technologies
Description:
Nano Bubble Technologies (NBT) was created out of a desire to provide a much safer, effective and environmentally friendly solution to various industries.

NBT have spent the last 14 years in the Research & Development of this process of containing Nano Bubbles in Liquids to remove bacteria, fungi, mould and other micro-organisms.

To date, NBT have trialed our Patent pending Nano Bubble injector in a wide range of industries using gases other than Ozone such as oxygen, Co2 and Hydrogen.

NBT is an emerging and disruptive technology business with a unique and highly adaptable solution across a number of verticals. The business has the capability, capacity and credentials to be a significant player and partner of choice.

Name: INNOMEM Project
Description:
INNOMEM has evolved into a commercial entity while continuing to operate within the framework established by the European project. Coordinated by EMI Twente BV, INNOMEM is backed by five key partners: Fundación Tecnalia Research & Innovation, VITO NV, Helmholtz-Zentrum Hereon GmbH, Fraunhofer Gesellschaft, and Ciaotech Srl.

INNOMEM is dedicated to developing a sustainable Open Innovation Test Bed (OITB) to accelerate the deployment and scale-up of innovative nano-enabled membranes and their applications. It provides a comprehensive ecosystem covering various membrane materials—including polymeric, ceramic, metallic, and nanocomposite—as well as surface modifications, membrane morphology, and geometries.

At the core of INNOMEM is its Single Entry Point (SEP), designed as a one-stop-shop for businesses in the membrane sector, particularly SMEs. Through this SEP, European companies gain streamlined access to cutting-edge expertise and technologies, enabling them to develop, test, and integrate high-performance, multifunctional, safe, and environmentally friendly nano-enabled membranes. This approach fosters cost-effective and sustainable innovations while facilitating real-world demonstration of advanced nanomembranes in industrial applications at Technology Readiness Level 7 (TRL7), thus accelerating market adoption.

INNOMEM unites some of Europe's most recognized membrane research institutions as well as specialists in technology transfer, corporate finance, funding, and business coaching. The Test Bed offers:

- Access to the most advanced and promising manufacturing pilot lines,
- State-of-the-art characterization techniques and modeling, and
- A range of non-technical services to support commercialization.

Name: Nemo Nanomaterials
Description:
NEMO Nanomaterials Ltd. develops game changing, nanocarbon-based industrial additives, enabling next generation performance and properties for a variety of material-based applications.
 
The science and know-how is based on novel technological advancement in processing and dispersing nanomaterials, relevant for a wide range of industrial materials: plastics, composites, coatings, and much more. 
NEMO's products and proprietary know how take industrial materials to the next level. The Nemo Blend is ready to be used at industrial manufacturing processes, opening new possibilities for design and advanced functionalities, weight reduction, while enhancing sustainability throughout the production process.
 
Established in 2018 and rapidly growing since, NEMO is bringing the promise of high-end carbon nanomaterials into industrial reality.

Name: FANDA Scientific
Description:
Welcome to FANDA Scientific, where expertise meets dedication. Founded in 2018, we proudly bring unparalleled scientific knowledge and services to the GCC region. Unlike simply representing brands, our focus is on delivering top-tier scientific solutions.

As the exclusive distributor of HORIBA Scientific instruments in the UAE and Oman, we specialize in an array of cutting-edge technologies: from Raman Microscopy to Fluorescence, Ellipsometry to Particle Size Analysis, and more. Our expertise extends to elemental analysis through ICP-OES, Carbon/Sulfur analyzers, O/N/H analyzers, and Sulfer-in-Oil systems.

At FANDA Scientific, our team of specialists is committed to providing comprehensive support. From seamless installation to thorough training and reliable maintenance, we ensure our clients receive the utmost in scientific instrument care. 

Our mission is simple yet profound: to contribute humbly yet significantly to the advancement of scientific research in our community and beyond!

Empowering your scientific journey.

Name: Magnaplate
Description:
Magnaplate's surface-enhancement coatings have been engineered to provide metal parts and other substrates with corrosion and chemical resistance, super-hardness and dry lubrication. These coatings impart properties that ensure longer life spans and greater reliability, consequently reducing replacement part expenditure and minimizing downtime needed for cleaning and repair.

As the world leader in surface-enhancement coatings, Magnaplate is committed to the conservation of the environment through a series of company-wide 'green' initiatives. Because Magnaplate's coatings significantly increase the life of parts and tooling, they have a positive effect on the environment through the reduction of energy required for manufacturing new, replacement parts. The increased life span of old parts through coating and repair also significantly reduces the amount of scrap created, while the added dry lubricity property of some of the coatings also eliminates the need for expensive, environmentally damaging petroleum-based lubricants.

Name: SmartCoat - titanium world technology s/b
Description:
Our company is the manufacturer of a highly advance nano technology coating call SmartCoat. It has been widely used in some parts of the world in the hygiene sector and building and construction sector. 

SmartCoat is a multi functioning liquid solution that is used as an invisible coating for any surfaces for the purposes of antimicrobial, self deodorizing, self cleaning and elimination of VOCs. All these functions is from 1 time application of SmartCoat. The effective period is 1 year. Meaning, when a surface is applied with SmartCoat, it will self sanitize and self deodorize for 1 year. 

This application can be used in a very wide sector from Hospitals, to Green Buildings to Automotive, residential, commercial offices, educational facilities, added value product and many more.

Name: Nano Healthcare Europe
Description:
Our patented technology revolutionizes the beauty, preventative health and medical industries. 

Our focus lies with preventing chronical pain, inflammation and age-related problems through strengthening the cell structure in its core. We provide the necessary nutrition for your cells to rebuild and rejuvenate, enabling them to do their primary “job”.

Nano CellCare is not a medical device or treatment, it’s a process in which we assist your body restore itself, and works incredibly well with other treatments – medical, beauty or physical.

Body Plaza Europe is a dynamic and innovative conglomerate within the beauty & health industry. With the revolutionary and patented Nano CellCare product line, we have established as a recognized partner for Doctors, Therapists, Health Professionals and Beauticians. Our personal approach provides us with vital market information, to ensure that our marketing activities support the needs of our partners. We take partnership a step further!

info@nanohealthcare.eu
info@bodyplaza.eu

www.bodyplaza.eu

Name: Graphenemex
Description:
Graphenemex is a pioneering company in Mexico and Latinamerica involved in graphene production and applications development.

The company is committed to be a low cost provider and to assure quality, scalability, replicability and standardization of the graphene-based materials produced.

Graphenemex has achieved three prestigious international certifications: ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015 and OHSAS 18001 related to its quality, environmental and safety management systems, respectively.

Graphene, a Nobel Prize-winning nanomaterial, is both the strongest and the lightest material known. It is more than 200 times stronger than steel and the best conductor of electricity and heat. In addition, graphene has an incredible surface area of over 2500 m2 per gram.

Currently, the company has an annual production capacity of four tons of graphene-based materials. In addition, we develop solutions for multiple applications, considering both technology that is advanced enough to satisfy market requirements and the existence of clear demand/market for the innovation.

In that regard, Energeia Graphenemex is getting engaged with research centers, universities and industrial partners to realize the full benefits of graphene and other nanomaterials.
Location: Ciudad de México, Ciudad de México

Name: ANAXAM - Technology transfer center for advanced manufacturing
Description: ANAXAM is a technology transfer center focused on applied materials analytics using neutron and synchrotron radiation. Our analytics go far beyond what is possible at laboratory scale. We provide industry access to state-of-the-art material analytics originally developed for basic research, but now made available to address industrial challenges. We are a one-stop-shop for consultancy, measurements, analysis and interpretation of results, such that industry profits from the most advanced analytical techniques in the fields of imaging, diffraction and spectroscopy to solve their industrial problems.

Name: TriNANO Technologies
Description: TriNANO Technologies provides Nano Coatings for Solar Panels for better power output & reduces O&M costs, thence advancing the performance ratio.

Name: h2020 SUNSHINE
Description: SUNSHINE aims to develop and validate Safe & Sustainable by Design strategies for products enabled by Multi-component Nanomaterials and facilitates their implementation at an industrial scale.

Name: Luz WaveLabs
Description: Founded in 2013, Luz Wavelabs is a spin-off of the Carlos III Madrid University, based on Terahertz (THz) and Photonics technology.

Name: SNS Nano Fiber Technology, LLC
Description:
SNS Nano Fiber Technology LLC is a producer of specialized nanofiber matrices.  Using proprietary technology, a variety of polymer nanofibers can be fabricated.  Nanofiber layers can be spun directly onto a substrate, such as a nonwoven or perforated film.  Thicker mats can also be produced that are self-supporting without the need for a substrate. Particles can be entrapped in the nanofiber matrix or encapsulated within the nanofibers. Many additives can be incorporated in the nanofiber products, such as superabsorbent particles, antimicrobials, drugs, APIs, pigments, vitamins, proteins, and so on. With so many different polymer options and functional additives available, the possibilities for new nanofiber products are endless.
SNS Nano Fiber Technology is certified to the ISO 13485:2003 (w/Design) and ISO 9001 (w/Design) International Quality System Standards.  The entire quality system was audited and certified by Smithers Quality Assessments Inc. (SQA), a leading independent accredited registrar.
SNS Nano Fiber is part of a family of companies, which includes Struktol Company of America (www.struktol.com), Schill+Seilacher "Struktol" GmbH (www.struktol.de) in Hamburg, Germany, Schill+Seilacher GmbH (www.schillseilacher.de) in Böblingen, Germany, and Struktol Canada, Limited.  Utilizing Struktol’s experience with additives and the polymer expertise of the University of Akron, we have developed a unique niche that did not formerly exist within the realm of nanofiber production. 


.

Name: Silklab
Description: The Silklab research focuses on materials that can be employed at the interface between technology and life sciences. The laboratory’s goal is to provide innovation and solutions of global societal impact through advances in naturally derived, abundant, sustainable materials. The core research is based on advanced material processing, prototyping, and manufacturing based on structural proteins, with specific focus on silk and other biomaterials. Over the past decade, the laboratory has pioneered biomaterials-based applications in edible and implantable electronics, food preservation, energy harvesting, wearable sensors, compostable technology, distributed environmental sensing, medical devices and therapeutics, biospecimen stabilization, advanced medical diagnostics, and structural components.

Name: Cupola Plastic Cards Limited
Description:
Cupola Plastic Cards Ltd. is a world class Visa International and MasterCard International accredited facility based in Jebel Ali, Dubai, U.A.E. 

Operational since 1999, CPCL manufactures high quality printed and laminated plastic cards including mag-strip and chip embedded, credit cards, ATM cards, membership/loyalty cards, identity cards and access control cards. We also supply pre-paid scratch-off cards printed on plastic and paper.

Name: Hyderabad Consulting Group
Description:

Name: Carestream - Contract Manufacturing
Description:
With a history of innovation in precision multilayer coating technology spanning over 100 years, Carestream applies our aqueous and solvent coating proficiency at bench, pilot and production-scale manufacturing for optimal product design, technology integration and manufacturability. Partners leverage our coating expertise, manufacturing assets and new product commercialization processes to develop precision-coated and cast film applications for a wide range of energy storage, display, medical, optical, electronic and other advanced materials products.

Our multilayer coating approach delivers process development advantages that can dramatically improve performance and reduce cost by allowing additional layers to be coated in one pass through the coating machine – enabling manufacturing partnerships that bring disruptive products to market. It also provides the design freedom to improve product performance with additional layers, and allows higher line speeds for reduced manufacturing cost.

We actively seek growth partnerships combining customer product IP and market channels with our high-speed roll-to-roll processing, quality systems and worldwide presence to bring new products through commercialization quickly and efficiently. Partnership agreements include joint development, manufacturing partnership or full joint venture.

Backed by exceptional Lean Six Sigma manufacturing practices, our engineers apply deep knowledge and unique analytical capabilities to identify and develop optimal coating solutions to meet partnership requirements, with planning, logistics, finance and purchasing competencies available. 

Contact us today to learn how a Carestream multilayer coating partnership can increase your product development, scale-up and commercialization success. For additional information, please visit www.tollcoating.com.

Name: NanoFixit
Description:
Nanofixit liquid screen protection provides a protective multi-purpose solution can be applied to any device (smartphone, tablet, laptop) and its invisible nature means that it has no adverse effect on the responsiveness of a touch screen. In fact, enhances the vividness of the smartphone screen, its colours and brightness due to the fact that the glass surface is now much smoother. NanoFixIt Inc. has developed a liquid screen protector using nanotechnology to form a unique mix of Nano scale organic silicon (SiO2) and highest quality Nano grade Titanium particles that penetrate and seal any electronic glass surface and make it Ultra Scratch Resistant. NanoFixIt Titanium is the hardest liquid screen protector available in the market and is even stronger than tempered glass.

Benefits And Features

- Ultra Scratch Resistant screen
- Enhances the colours of the screen (HD effect)  
- Enhances the effectiveness of the camera lens and quality of pictures taken
- Helps reduce radiation (92.7%) 
- Anti bacterial protection (99.8% KR) 
- Resistant to both acid and alkaline

Name: BalticNet-PlasmaTec
Description:
http://www.balticnet-plasmatec.org/legal-notice/

The BalticNet-PlasmaTec registered association stands for international technology and market-oriented cooperation of science, research and economics in the field of plasma technology and adjacent disciplines. One cluster aim is to increase the perception of the plasma technology in the society. It also helps interested parties in the Baltic Sea region assess their potential for using this technology. 
As an international cluster, BalticNet-PlasmaTec supports the creation of new jobs in the long term by introducing and disseminating the plasma technology in the universities, industry, SME and public facilities. This will be realised by development of new business segments, spin-offs and an intensive common marketing.
The BalticNet-PlasmTec based on 5 workgroups where different types of project will be realized. The work in the workgroup Plasma& Environment focused on plasma application for environment protection. The focus of the workgroup Plasma & Bio is the combination of physical plasmas with life science and medicine. Within the workgroup Vacuum Technology the members work with low pressure plasma applications. The Education workgroup organize education events for employees and students like summer schools, placements and workshops. All activities to increase the visibility of plasma and the growing of the cluster are bundled in the workgroup Marketing.

Name: Svaya Nanotechnologies
Description:
Svaya turns the common into the extraordinary.  Imagine a world where common surfaces are functionalized to:

•	Turn your windshield into climate control - leading to reduced car temperatures and increased MPGs
•	Prevent staph infections on hospital surfaces;
•	Increase the power capability of Li-ion batteries by 10x.

Svaya strives to make common, everyday things into active, functional and extraordinary products that make our lives better.

Svaya develops and delivers an integrated solution for the deposition of nanostructured thin films that enable our customers to make their products more valuable and to create revolutionary new products.  The company is funded by NEA and Sigma Partners and is located in Sunnyvale, CA.

One of our Technology Advisors, Professor Valentina Snitka, Director, Principal Scientist, Research Center for Microsystems and Nanotechnology, Kaunas University Of Technology, Kunas, Lithuania, is an active researcher in Biotechnology and Nanotechnology and the recipient of several EU (FP) Grants worth tens of Million Euros.

Name: NeoCoat
Description:
NeoCoat SA is a high-tech company active in the field of CVD Diamond Coating Technology.
Its thin diamond films have various applications and the company focuses its offer of CVD-diamond coating on Microsystems, mechanical or micromechanical parts and doped-diamond electrodes.
 
NeoCoat’s headquarter and production facility is located in the Science and Technology Park Neode, in La Chaux-de-Fonds (NE), Switzerland.
 
The company does business worldwide by developing new applications and products, either with industrial partner or OEM, by selling components, products, processes and engineered equipments, directly or through partners and sales representatives.
 
Main products and applications:
- Diamond coated tools (drill, routers, inserts, etc.)
- Conductive diamond AFM probes
- Diamond electrodes (BDD – Boron-Doped-Diamond)
- Diamond coated rotary seals
- Diamond coated micromechanical parts
- Crystalline synthetic diamond parts

Name: NanoFAB.SDSU
Description: NanoFAB.SDSU Industrial Consortium and Research Center operates a leading center for micro and nanofabrication located in the EIS building of SDSU. The state-of-the-art facility has a 2500 sq. ft. ISO 9001-certified Class 1000 Cleanroom with 4” and 6” wafer processing capability with 10 micron lithography feature sizes. The Center provides a comprehensive and expanding selection of state-of-the-art process equipment for lithography, etching, thermal processing, thin-film deposition, and characterization. The vision of the Center is to become a leading national and international center of integrated silicon and polymer/carbon-based micro and nanofabrication facility to support user needs in newer areas of nanoscale science, engineering, and technology domains. The Center has a strong focus on educating undergraduate, masters, and doctoral students in the various areas of nanotechnology.

Name: Panoval (Asia) Ltd
Description: Panoval is an industrial process manufacturing company. With emphasis on R&D. Providing solutions to the Medical, electro technical and the  packaging industry.

Description:
Driving Innovation Since 1998
