Our products are based on a unique technology for embedding and processing nanoparticles in glass. In addition to our colorPol polarizers, our portfolio includes polymer polarizers, as well as wafers and substrates for analytical applications.

 Trusted by industries and researchers around the world.

Name: Carbodeon Ltd Oy
Description:
Super-property nanomaterials for metal plating, durable low friction coatings, and thermal and mechanical polymer applications.

"Hard as hell, but cooler"

Carbodeon supplies superhard materials for applications where toughness is at a premium. Our nanodiamond products each possess the desired properties fine-tuned for a growing number of dedicated applications. These grades are sold under the name uDiamond.

Similarly, our Nicanite graphitic carbon nitride can be converted to carbon nitride film coatings with unique properties.

If you’re looking for high-quality superhard materials, contact us to see how we can help you with your particular application.

Name: Raith Laser Systems BV
Description: Raith Laser Systems BV is an innovative engineering company with a proven track record in the optical media industry. We combine optics, electronics, motion and automation. A powerful combination that perfectly fits your request. Not only state of the art in technology, but also highly cost effective. Whether it’s a complete automated production machine or a simple high power light source. And in addition to our engineering services we offer a range of ready to use products. Such as laser and light sources and high precision distance sensors.

Name: NANOSPRESSO-NL
Description:
NANOSPRESSO-NL (website in preparation) is a transformative project aimed at developing technology for the small-scale, decentralized production of personalized nucleic acid nanomedicines, targeting the treatment of rare and orphan diseases. Challenging the traditional large-scale industrial manufacture of treatments, this approach aims at enabling hospital pharmacists to locally prepare these medicines at the bedside, revolutionizing patient-specific treatment.

This initiative is driven by a very diverse consortium, bringing together SMEs, industrial partners, RTOs, academic reserachers, clinicians, nano-technology experts, regulatory experts, and pharmacists. Each contributes vital expertise in areas like nanotechnology, microfluidics, biology, medicine, pharmacy, and regulatory sciences, essential for the project's success and translation to the clinic.

Coordinated by UMC Nanomedicine, NANOSPRESSO-NL addresses critical societal challenges, aligning with the NWA routes such as 'Personalised Medicine: The Individual at the Centre' and 'The Quantum/Nano-Revolution'. This project underscores a societal mission to improve healthcare accessibility and affordability, particularly in developing countries.

NANOSPRESSO-NL, funded by the Netherlands Science Agenda, project number NWA.1389.20.096, represents a collaborative effort towards a more personalized, effective healthcare system.

Name: NMW | NO MORE WATER

Name: NXTNANO
Description:
NXTNANO was formed in 2013 with a vision to solve historical industry limitations and engineer a process allowing nanofibers to be manufactured at a commercially viable rate. Our advancement has resulted in many new industries and applications now benefiting from the unique properties of nanofibers. NXTNANO's production capability in material width, speed, volume, & fiber versatility is simply unmatched. We have the ability to produce fibers from polymers of PU, PVDF, PAN, PES, and others. This versatility combined with a unique ability to simultaneously spin multiple polymers provides unprecedented flexibility. Additional research is being committed to adding "functionality" to polymers, enhancing nanofibers characteristics to not only preform filtration tasks but to enhance or preform additional function's such as odor and light absorption, even piezoelectric properties.

We are driven by a commitment to deliver exceptional, industry leading nanofibers on an unprecedented scale. Our production technique is a revolutionary departure from the normal methods of fiber spinning that allows us to put down more & finer fiber, with greater consistently and bonding. NXTNANO currently produces a range of nanofiber medias, from very light surface coatings all the way up to full membranes for the apparel and micro filtration industries.

Name: Nano Guard Puebla

Name: Nanolyse Technologies Ltd
Description:
Nanolyse Technologies is a Cleantech Materials Innovation company dedicated to creating sustainable solutions for the future.

Our adept team of specialists is dedicated to engineering cutting-edge solutions tailored for pivotal industrial applications, driving sustainability and efficiency.

What Sets Us Apart:
• Material Innovation: Our creations are not just new but are pivotal in environmental sensing, waste management, water resource recovery, and green construction.

• In-depth R&D: In alliance with scientists and industrial entrepreneurs, we deepen the understanding of smart materials and broaden their application horizons. We welcome collaborations on innovation and research grant applications.

• Expert Consultancy: We’re not just innovators but also advisors, offering bespoke materials and environmental monitoring expertise.

• Collaborative Growth: Open to synergies, we join forces with corporations and institutions amplifying R&D outcomes.

Our Mission: 
At Nanolyse Technologies, our mission transcends innovation. We are stewards of a sustainable supply chain transformation aiding businesses to be not just profitable but also responsible. Every material engineered and every consultation rendered is a step towards a greener economy and a preserved environment.

Examples of our ongoing work and impact:
1- Engineered solutions for pollutant extraction from water bodies and industrial outlets.

2- Process development facilitating efficient waste management and resource recovery.

3- Innovation in chemical separation and sensors enhancing environmental monitoring and industrial water quality monitoring.

4- Co-development of bio-based, microalgal bitumen as a sustainable binder for asphalt.


Join us in weaving sustainability into the fabric of every industry!

2020-2024 Nanolyse Technologies Ltd. Company no: 12658132

Name: Wide Bandgap Device Research Group @ CeNSE (WBDRG@CeNSE)
Description:
We are a group of researchers from the Centre for Nanoscience and Engineering (CeNSE) at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore.

With Prof. Digbijoy Nath as the principal investigator, we explore the wide, ultrawide bandgap and 2D Materials (GaN, Gallium Oxide and memory materials) towards realising state-of-the-art semiconductor devices.

The facility is equipped with state-of-the-art class 100 and 1000 cleanrooms and a characterisation facility where we create and explore the World at the nanoscale.

Name: ICeNd
Description:
We create UNanoD to enable universal smart detection of the smallest
movements.

We are ICeNd - a newborn startup with an ambition to be the next generation leader in artificial lives (AL). We have created sub-25 nanometer nanofluidic channels,  quantum materials, and physics-driven AI as building blocks of artificial lives. While our vision of AL is futuristic for the sustainability of humanity on earth and outside earth, the individual building blocks of the AL have highly demanding markets. Our current MVPs Universal Nanodevices or UNanoD enable real-time molecular studies for drug discovery, structural biology, material discovery, and pollution sensing at the precision of single molecule level. UNanoD Fluid is at TRL8 performing real-time single molecule detection with photons and electrons. UNanoD Solid is at TRL6 performing real-time nanomechanicoelectrical signals. 

We aim to be an independent IP heavy semiconductor IDM to produce quantum nanotech products. Our market entry is through microscopy and energy sectors. We partner with giant microscopy companies. 


ICeNd's headquarter is at Eindhoven, The Netherlands.
ICeNd's engineering site is at Bengaluru, India.

Name: Incoatec GmbH
Description:
GETTING THE MOST OUT OF X-RAY ANALYTICAL SYSTEMS

We at incoatec enable our customers to make better use of their resources and improve their results. We develop, produce and supply components for X-ray analytical systems, including tubes, optics, and microfocus sources.

We offer solutions for heavy industry, pharmaceutical industry, chemistry, semiconductor industry, life science and nanotechnology. Applications include X-ray spectrometry, X-ray diffraction, and X-ray scattering.

Being experts in thin film and X-ray source technology, we produce highly reliable, efficient and sustainable equipment. In short: Made in Germany.

Located in northern Germany, incoatec was founded in 2002  as a spin-off of what today is called the Helmholtz Center Geesthacht (formerly GKSS) and Bruker AXS, Karlsruhe, one of the worldwide leading manufacturer of X-ray analytical systems. In addition to being an OEM supplier for Bruker AXS, we also offer tailor-made solutions directly to end customers.

We guide our customers through the whole process - from selecting the most suitable solution until their system is up and running. And even after installation our experts are at our customer’s disposal, face to face.

For more information about our company, products and services visit https://www.incoatec.de/.

Name: ADJ Nanotechnology
Description: ADJ Nanotechnology is Polish SME, strongly oriented to R&D. The company’s business concept is based on nanotechnology, in particular, composite nanoparticles and nanohybrid structures, which properties are driven by quantum effects. Therefore, compared to bulk macroscopic materials, ADJ nanomaterials exhibit specific features such as unique optical properties, extraordinary surface activity, corresponding chemical reactivity, and increased antibacterial activity. That is why they are new promising building blocks for developing multifunctional materials of the future. At ADJ Nanotechnology, we use bottom-up techniques as well as top-down to produce our high-quality nanoproducts. By integrating the vast fundamental knowledge with the highest level of care and long-term practical experience in nanotechnology, we offer expertise in nano-R&D and scaling-up. The innovative nanotechnology used in our own production allows great flexibility in design and engineering of various nanomaterials and nano-hybrid systems.

Name: EuroNanoForum
Description:
The EuroNanoForum 2017 conference (ENF 2017) will take place on 21-23 June 2017 in Malta under the auspices of the Maltese presidency of the European Union.

It is organised in cooperation with the European Commission’s Directorate-General for Research and Innovation.

More on the event: European competitiveness and jobs depend on strengthening European manufacturing capabilities by providing essential technology building blocks in the form of high value-added products and their manufacturing processes in strategic European value chains.

The aim of the conference is to contribute to these efforts of strengthening European competitiveness and supporting the renewal of its manufacturing industries, by the aid of nano- and microtechnologies and advanced materials.

ENF2017 will review the status of European technology development in nanotechnology and advanced materials industries, discussing the latest progress in nanoscience and nanotechnology, and their contribution to innovation in manufacturing across all industrial sectors.

In addition, framework conditions like education, standardisation, regulations, IPR and safety issues, as well as entrepreneurship and industrial policy will be discussed. The program also features sessions on finance and funding through European and National programs, in particular Horizon 2020, smart specialisation and public-private partnership initiatives.

The conference builds on the agendas set by previous conferences and will also review the first activities launched under H2020 and provide feedback for the coming calls.

Our confirmed ENF2017 speakers include Audi, ST Microelectronics, NXP Semiconductors, Infineon Technologies as well as Avantium, UPM Biochemicals, KONE Corporation, Canatu, nanoSPACE, Water Services Corporation, Abertax Technologies Ltd, Elastopoli, Crystalsol, Selena, Lionex, Aditech Corp., Thought 3D, Nanoscribe, NanoTech Analysis and more!

Name: Nano-AI Factory
Description:
Somos uma iniciativa para promoção e avanço das Máquinas Moleculares através da Inteligência Artificial e para o progresso de longo prazo em direção à manufatura de precisão atômica para aplicações em medicina, meio ambiente, energia, agricultura, cidades inteligentes, desenvolvimento espacial e ciência dos materiais.

Nós acreditamos que a IA pode impulsionar a criação, design e desenvolvimento molecular de produtos, objetos e sistemas em nível atômico, em escala global, com baixo custo e pegada zero de carbono - desde um nanorôbo capaz de fagocitar células cancerígenas até supercomputadores, carros, naves espaciais e alimentos.

Description: Exergy revolutionizes water and recycling solutions to confront pervasive overconsumption in the manufacturing industry. Purifying and recycling resources like water at the point of use, Exergy’s sustainable response brings valuable water back into the production process while minimizing the water treatment and disposal costs

Name: Silicon Carbide (SiC) Materials & Devices Workshop
Description: A fascinating family of wide-band-gap (WBG) semiconductors, SiC crystals are technologically important materials that not only received great research attention in recent few decades, but also continue to generate new and increasing interests at emerging new frontiers.  This workshop serves as a 'brainstorming' & 'working' meeting for discussing some of the latest research progresses in SiC materials and devices.  The workshop's scope spans a spectrum of scientific aspects of SiC and other related WBG materials & devices - including materials growth and characterization, physics and modeling, processing, electronics, sensors, MEMS/NEMS, SiC and other related materials for photonics, optomechanics, and quantum technologies.

Name: Luxel Corporation
Description:
For more than 45 years, Luxel has been a pioneer in the development of new optical filter materials and techniques. We regularly expand the limits of the field by producing filters and windows larger, stronger, and thinner than previously thought possible. We offer filters in more than 70 elements, compounds, and organics that provide researchers with the ability to better discriminate regions of the electromagnetic spectrum.

Luxel has an extensive flight heritage, spanning from Skylab in 1973 to eRosita in 2019 and including such missions as EXOSAT, the Chandra X-Ray Observatory, the Solar Dynamics Observatory, and the Mars Curiosity Rover. We also provide laser targets, photocathodes, CCD filters, TEM supports, pressure windows, and soft x-ray and high energy physics filters to support cutting edge research in a wide variety of fields. One of our newest product lines is GridTape, a reel-to-reel tape that combines automated serial sectioning with high-throughput TEM imaging.

Our filters are of the highest quality available and most can be provided light tight and pinhole free. Our films demonstrate remarkable strength for their size, sufficient to withstand spacecraft launch, pressure differentials, and maintain gas cell integrity. We offer an array of standard frames but can also manufacture frames to meet any custom need.

In addition to thin-film filters, Luxel offers thermal evaporation equipment. Our RADAK Furnaces are resistance heated vacuum evaporators that offer unparalleled control in material evaporation up to 1500°C. Luxel’s revamped RADAK Power Controller incorporates many new innovative features including programmable inputs and outputs, compatibility with external devices, and integration into automated deposition systems.

We pride ourselves in helping our customers succeed in meeting their research or production objectives.  Our team of scientists and engineers can help develop a product to meet any of your custom needs.

Name: NanoEarth (The National Center for Earth and Environmental Nanotechnology Infrastructure)
Description:
The National Center for Earth and Environmental Nanotechnology Infrastructure (NanoEarth) is a node of the National Nanotechnology Coordinated Infrastructure (NNCI), an NSF-funded network of 16 centers spread throughout the United States serving as user facilities for cutting edge nanotechnology research. 

NanoEarth is part of Virginia Tech's Institute for Critical Technology and Applied Science (ICTAS) and proudly headquartered in Blacksburg, Virginia.

NanoEarth is funded by NSF Award #1542100 (2015-2020) and NSF Award #2025151 (2020-2025).

Name: INASCON 2024
Description: The 18th edition of the International Nanoscience Student Conference (INASCON)  will take place in Enschede, the Netherlands in August 2024! Stay tuned!

Name: VINNY Project
Description: The primary goal of VINNY is to create environmentally friendly nanobiopesticides and nanobiofertilizers to revolutionize viticulture practices. By using bio-actives extracted from grapevines and industrial by-products, VINNY aims to decrease dependency on harmful agrochemicals and encourage sustainable vineyard management.

Name: MINIGRAPH
Description: The overall goal of this project is to demonstrate the functionality of a new generation of brain implants with closed-loop capabilities based on graphene ground-breaking material and a minimally invasive robotic implantation procedure for the treatment of brain disorders.

Name: ExactBlue Technologies Inc
Description:
Exact Blue’s Technology develops and commercializes cost-effective, simple, and fast solution for testing and monitoring the microbiological and chemical contamination of water. The platform technology that we have developed has allowed us to create rapid test kits as a portable solution i.e. a quick and simple qualitative test for the water quality, or as a quantitative detection system i.e. part of a monitoring or treatment system, and can provide fast and reliable information on water quality. 
Our ExactBlue Mobile Lab Platform allows users to perform quantitative testing in field conditions, without sending samples to a lab and waiting for days for results, at a faction of the price.

Name: OPTAGLIO
Description:
OPTAGLIO GROUP is a world leading manufacturer of holographic and optical security features for government issuers of passport, ID and travel documents, state printing works, commercial high security printers and significant international brands all over the world.

OPTAGLIO GROUP has developed and pioneered many successful innovative security techniques and holds over 20 active worldwide patents covering a wide range of security solution. Company is certified by ISO 9001:2008, member of International Hologram Manufactures Association (IHMA) and 
Document Security Alliance (DSA).

Name: Plasma Quest
Description: Plasma Quest Ltd is a solutions provider for thin film sputter-deposition specialising in a unique plasma generation process that enables high rate, exceptional quality coatings of many different materials at ambient temperatures. PQL has developed a variety of deposition tools suitable for a wide range of end user applications from small R&D through to large area, high throughput coatings. The recently enhanced linear deposition process is capable of highly uniform coatings over a 14 inch web. The exclusive technique enables an array of new applications, materials and substrates. A key example of the exceptional attributes of the process is the low temperature deposition of indium tin oxide that has a sheet resistance of less than 10Ω/o with >97% peak transmission. Furthermore, the ITO is well adhered to a variety of substrates including planarised PEN and PET substrates and can be repeatedly flexed with a bend radius of <1cm whilst demonstrating no reduction in conductivity. Other materials deposited at ambient temperatures include a wide range of high breakdown dielectrics with 100% peak transmission and high mobility (>20cm2/Vs) transparent semiconductors suitable for flexible electronics applications. PQL offers a complete range of services from contract R&D through to complete bespoke deposition systems.

Name: Navork Innovations Private Limited
Description: Navork Innovations is an Indian startup focused on developing advanced technology to extend the shelf life of fruits and vegetables while preserving their freshness. By using innovative solutions, Navork ensures that produce stays fresh longer, reducing waste and improving supply chain efficiency. Our technology helps maintain the quality, nutrition, and taste of fruits and vegetables, providing a sustainable approach to food preservation.

Name: Nanotech Innovations, LLC
Description:
Located in Oberlin, Ohio, Nanotech Innovations (NTI) is at the forefront of nanotechnology research in Northern Ohio.  The injection CVD system at the core of our product line was originally investigated at NASA Glenn Research Center.  We recognize the demand for a reliable and affordable source of high purity carbon nanotubes. Our single-step process and instrumentation deliver nanotubes with low residual catalyst and virtually no amorphous carbon impurities. The Nanotech Innovations SSP-354 is a benchtop system that provides convenient access to exciting nanomaterials for education, research and product development.  

Nanotech Innovations can also provide Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) imaging services at a cost much lower than university labs and testing facilities.  Our Phenom SEM provides imaging at 20-20,000X magnification and an ultimate resolution of 30nm.  With real-time viewing available, NTI can perform a wide array of analytical imaging such as inspection of materials for imperfections, determination of uniformity of plating layers, etc.  Detailed imaging of nanomaterials and photovoltaic structures is also available.  

Nanotech Innovations, simple solutions for your nanotechnology needs!

Name: MikroMasch
Description:
MikroMasch offers AFM probes and test structures.

AFM (Atomic Force Microscopy) is a widely-used instrument and technique for non-destructive surface investigations as well as for surface modifications at the nanoscale. Besides topography, AFM is capable of collecting information that relates to local material properties such as adhesion, stiffness, elasticity, conductivity, coercitivity, etc.

The principle of AFM consists in registering the force experienced by the probe from the sample as the probe scans its surface. There are other scanning techniques like STM or NSOM, where the probe is designed to register other physical values. All these techniques and instruments are referred to as SPM (Scanning Probe Microscopy). So AFM is a part of SPM.

Mikromasch is a trademark of Nanoworld AG operated by ISB Ltd.

Los nanomateriales aportan a los productos base en los que se aplican diferentes funcionalidades y propiedades convirtiéndolos en productos de alto valor añadido. Sin embargo, los procesos actuales de síntesis de nanomateriales tienen un elevado coste económico que encarece el producto final, limitando su aplicación a determinados sectores.
 
Con dicha visión, LAURENTIA TECHNOLOGIES nace como puente entre las ideas y las oportunidades en el ámbito de los nanomateriales sostenibles y las TICs, siendo un facilitador para empresas y organismos de investigación que apuesten por la innovación como vía para el crecimiento.
  
LAURENTIA TECHNOLOGIES desarrolla soluciones basadas en los siguientes nanomateriales:

- Nanomateriales sostenibles de baja energía embebida a partir de subproductos industriales o agrícolas y de  fuentes naturales:  nanoarcillas, nanocelulosa, nanosílice, nanocargas fotocatalíticas. 

- Encapsulación de sustancias activas: biocidas, nutrientes, colorantes, antioxidantes, reparadores.

- Recubrimientos funcionales basados en nanomateriales: propiedades de autolimpieza, hidrofóbicas, biocide, descontaminante, autorreparadores).

Ofrecemos también servicios de consultoría para empresas que quieran participar en proyectos del H2020:

- Preparación de propuestas y definición de consorcio
- Gestión de proyectos
- Desarrollo de planes de diseminación y explotación.

Description:
Eonum Inc. is a cutting-edge research and development company established in 2019 and headquartered in Irvine, California. Eonum’s vision is to drive Quantum Environmental Transformation, setting new standards in sustainable technology. By simulating and designing advanced materials, such as nanoporous silica and metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), we aim to create the most efficient and scalable carbon capture solutions. Our innovative technology employs unprecedented methods validated through computer simulations and experiments. It is workable, ready for market application, and has proven effectiveness. It is scalable and adaptable for global deployment, meeting diverse environmental needs. It is disruptive, offering energy-efficient direct air capture adsorbents surpassing industry standards. Eonum provides a range of services designed to support the deployment and optimization of our carbon capture technology. Our consultancy offers expert advice for companies aiming to lower carbon footprints, specializing in implementing and optimizing carbon capture technologies. We collaborate with industries, particularly the petrochemical sector, to provide cost-effective, energy-efficient carbon capture solutions. Our ongoing R&D investment ensures continuous quantum computation and carbon capture innovation, keeping our solutions at the forefront of technological advancements. 
Additionally, we secure government and private sector contracts and grants from organizations such as the NSF and DEO to support climate change mitigation initiatives.

Microspheres will become a super material at the heart of some of the most important innovations in the optics space.

We developed the world’s most powerful brightfield microscope; the NANORO M, which can achieve 50 nm resolutions purely through glass and light. This utilises SMAL (Super-resolution Microsphere Amplifying Lens) technology to provide new insights into nanostructures - non-destructively, in full colour, rapidly and with no sample preparation. This has major applications in materials science research, microelectronics and 2D materials imaging. We launched this product in June 2017 through our imaging brand NANORO to international media aclaim.

We are also developing a best in class particle lens array that can produce nanoscale patterns rapidly and repeatably with high precision over a wide area, with applications in microfluidics, semiconductors and bank note security.

Name: Soft Matter Laboratory

Our approach is multidisciplinary, involving concepts and expertise from chemistry, physics, biomedical engineering, and materials science. We pursue the goal of understanding the relationship between molecular structure, molecular function and macroscopic properties. In this context, we are particularly interested in designing and building soft materials and devices with unprecedented properties and functions. The research projects on which we are presently working involve the integration of functional molecular systems into devices and platforms for different specific purposes. The devices of current interest are solid-state nanopores, graphene-based field-effect transistors and bioelectrochemical sensors.

Description:
SabiNano is a privately owned South African nanotechnology company that manufactures and supplies carbon-based nanomaterials such as carbon nanotubes (CNTs), graphene and other nanotech and biotech-based materials and technologies for research and development (R&D) and industrial applications. We are  a research and innovation-driven company with experienced staff and partners to conduct in-house R&D for various industries. Our main focus is to service the demands of the rising local market while developing new applications for various industries such as those require raw materials and chemicals in their R&D and production processes.  including the manufacturing of carbon materials that consist of multiwalled CNTs, nitrogen-doped CTNs, carbon spheres, and graphene. 

Our core competencies include:

i) Customization of manufactured products (e.g. carbon nanotubes and graphene): The company has the capacity to customize these products to suit specific customer requirements.
(ii) Application innovation (including R&D): The company can identify application of its nanomaterials in end products/technologies especially in technologies that have not been explored before.

Name: Sputtek Advanced Metallurgical Coatings
Description:
We are a specialized PVD, DLC and Thermal Spray Coatings Company, providing surface technology services for various manufacturing industries including Automotive, Plastic, Oil & Gas, Aerospace, Mechanical Engineering, Consumer Goods and much more. Additionally, we design and build coating equipment for all types of industrial and commercial applications.
Our continuous inhouse R&D and decades of experience enables us to provide advanced industry leading coating technologies to improve the performance and life cycle of manufacturing, machinery and tooling. Our coatings significantly reduce downtime from process issues, tooling change overs and breakages by improving the substrate properties such as hardness, adhesion, lubricity, resistivity to chemicals, abrasive wear, corrosion, and extreme working temperatures. 
Cutting, Machining, Stamping, Punching, Die-Casting, Extruding, Embossing, Welding Fixtures, Injection Moulds and Spray Nozzles are some of the typical applications for manufacturing tooling.
Typical functional and decorative component type coating applications include bearing housings, camshafts, valves, piston rings, conveyor shafts, gears, rotors, saw blades, knives, golf clubs, skates, firearms, marine hardware, utensils, and anti-microbial hardware for industrial and consumer goods.
As an ISO 9001:2015 certified company we have a diversified portfolio of coating solutions. This includes customized new-generation coatings for specific metallurgical application requirements, along with a full spectrum of generic and decorative coatings.
Our large capacity facility in Woodbridge Ontario, provides for significant volume throughput offering short turnaround services of 12-48 hours. We are proud to be Canadian Owned and Operated since 1994.

Name: IKI-Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology
Description:
The research support labs are part of the Ilse Katz Institute for Nano-Science and Technology (IKI) at Ben-Gurion University of the Negev (BGU) founded in 2006.
The IKI brings under one roof a community of scientists working on aspects of the fields that are related to the understanding and manipulation of matter at reduced dimensionality. We provide a “one-stop shop” for state-of-the-art technology and expertise in nano-scale fabrication and characterization.
Our characterization facility includes electron microscopy, X-ray and light scattering, vibrational spectroscopy (FTIR, Raman), surface analysis (XPS and AES), advanced microscopy (optical and SPM), bio-imaging and flow cytometry, thermal analyses, mass spectrometry and more. We also provide solutions for life sciences and bio-medical research through our Genomics unit, Bioinformatics, Cryo-EM and Crystallography unit.

Name: Xergy Inc
Description:
XERGY is the world leader in the development of advanced composite ion exchange membranes. It is primarily involved in the design, development, manufacture, sale and service of a range of devices, stacks, modules and systems employing its XION brand advanced membranes. 

XERGY products include ECCO Heat Pumps, X-CELL electrochemical compressors, XUMIDOR and XUMIDOX micro-climate control modules, XIZONE ozone generators, XIGEN fuel cells, XIPURE membrane desalination systems, XISERV ERV’s, XISENSE sensor modules and other products in the pipeline. These systems are being introduced to large (multi-billion-dollar scale) applications including home appliances, water and power utilities, automotive, medical, electronics and industrial processes. XERGY has a strategic alliance with the world’s largest appliance manufacturer Haier (China) and its U.S. subsidiary GE Appliances (Haier USA). 

The company was founded in 2010 and is headquartered inDE. XERGY won the GE Ecomagination award in 2011.

Xergy’s Ion exchange membranes conduct ions (Cations (such as Protons) and Anions), that are ultra-thin, ultra-strong, and provide ultra-high ionic transport performance due to their thinness and ability to support highly functional ion exchange materials that would otherwise not be strong enough on their own to be free-standing films. Xergy then takes these advanced membranes and creates high-value, leading-edge products with them. Xergy’s technologies are disruptive and transformational for many critically important markets and can make a huge contribution to the reduction of global energy demand, the reduction in the use of GWP refrigerants, and the reduction of atmospheric ozone.

Our vision, is to become the new “Intel” of our age. We believe that controlling the movement of ions and molecules through nanoscale channels offers revolutionary pathways for development of home, energy, industrial, medical, and sensing products. Xergy wants to build these products.

The company has 12 associates (2016), and is privately funded (without VC’s) by its founder.

XERGY Firsts:
-	Xergy’s first patent for Electrochemical Compression (ECC) technology was in 2009, and now has over 50 patent cases in U.S. alone
-	Xergy’s won GE Ecomagination in 2011, and was a finalist in many other clean-tech competitions.
-	Xergy built the world’s first low pressure electrochemical compressor for heat pump applications
-	Xergy built the world’s first (electrochemical) compressor with water as a working fluid
-	Xergy built the world’s first micro-climate control systems for oxygen and humidity control
-	Xergy developed the world’s first stable composite anionic membrane for alkaline fuel cell applications.

Description:
The Australian National Fabrication Facility was established under the National Collaborative Research Infrastructure Strategy (NCRIS) in 2007 to provide Australian researchers from both the public and private sectors with access to micro- and nano-fabrication tools and services. One of eight Nodes within the national network, the NSW Node (ANFF-NSW) offers advanced nanofabrication capability in cleanrooms and laboratory spaces spanning 750m2.

At the heart of the ANFF-NSW laboratories are three electron beam lithography systems, all capable of producing sub-20nm device features. This capability is supported by a full Si-MOS process line (high temperature furnaces for oxidation, diffusion and annealing; photolithography; a range of deposition and etching tools; and dicing, packaging and bonding capability) as well as additional tools allowing MOS-incompatible processes to run in parallel. The Node also operates a suite of molecular beam epitaxy systems producing ultra-low-disorder III-V heterostructure material as well as a wide range of high quality complex oxide layers and nanocomposites.

One of the key services provided by ANFF-NSW is the provision of advanced nanofabrication training for research students and staff. Training programs are customised to suit each researcher’s individual project requirements and are most often delivered in one-on-one sessions with ANFF-NSW process engineers. ANFF-NSW staff are also available to perform processing and development work on behalf of researchers.

Around 250 researchers make use of the ANFF-NSW laboratories each year, representing a diverse range of research disciplines including electronics, quantum technologies, photonics, clean energy technologies, biomedical devices and medical sensor technologies.

Name: NanoPrintek, Inc.
Description:
When it comes to printing electronics, sensors, micro-batteries/supercapacitors, etc.., what if instead of wet printing technologies (i.e., ink-jet and aerosol-jet) there was a dry printing additive nanomanufacturing technology that could enable:
1.	On-demand and in-situ generation of various pure nanoparticles (e.g., metals, insulators, semiconductors, superconductors, ceramics, composites, etc.), 
2.	In-situ and real-time laser sintering of dry nanoparticles jet on various substrates (polyamide, PET, PDMS, paper, metal, ceramic, etc.), with no further post-processing. 
3.	Multi-material printing of hybrid and composite materials and structures.

Wet Printing Technologies Challenges
•	Complex, costly, and eco-unfriendly ink formulation processes.
•	Limited availability of functional inks.
•	Transportation, shelf life, and storage difficulties of inks 
•	Contaminations and impurities due to the use of solvents and additives for ink formulations.
•	Environmental pollution due to the necessity of removing and recycling solvent and surfactant after deposition. 
•	Post-processing steps for curing the prints and removing surfactants and ink additives. 
•	Limited or no multi-material printing of hybrid materials and composites. 
•	Clogging and cleaning hassles.

NanoPrintek Dry Printing Advantages
•	No need for inks and liquids. Requires solids targets (pellets) of the desired materials.
•	Easy transportation, shelf life, and storage. 
•	On-demand and in-situ formation of dry and pure nanoparticles. 
•	Real-time sintering and integration. 
•	No post-treatment processes.
•	Environmentally friendly process due to the on-demand formation of nanoparticles as needed. 
•	Advanced and Multifunctional materials printing. Hybrid, multimaterial and composite printing. 
•	Suitable for printing electronics, sensors, micro-batteries/supercapacitors, catalysis, etc. 
•	Simultaneous subtractive and additive processes for trenching/printing and localized repair.

Name: Carbon Nanotechnology

The main focus of the company is R&D of CNT and biocompatible electroactive polymer nanofiber actuators used in many applications, especially in artificial muscles and tissue engineering.

We produce high quality and high yield single walled and multi walled CNTs that are suitable for many applications with their superior mechanical, electrical and thermal properties. CNTs offer exciting opportunities for the development of new composite materials. Polymer/CNT composites shows improved mechanical, surface and multi-functional properties. By incorporation of CNTs to electroactive polymer based systems, their electrical and mechanical properties are enhanced. Addition of CNTs also improve dramatically the actuation response of the electroactive polymers and decreases their actuation voltage.

Please contact us for your CNT and electroactive polymer actuator needs. We supply high quality products to academic and corporate researchers

Name: EU H2020 NEURONN
Description:
EU H2020 ICT NEURONN 2020-2022 
Two-Dimensional Oscillatory Neural Networks for Energy Efficient Neuromorphic Computing

Coordinator: Dr. Aida Todri-Sanial, CNRS
Partners: 
CNRS, FR
IBM Research Zurich, Switzerland
Fraunhofer, Germany
CSIC/University of Seville, Spain
Silvaco, UK
A.I. Mergence, France

Advisory Board:
Intel, Germany
Prophesee, France

Neuro-inspired computing employs technologies that enable brain-inspired computing hardware for more efficient and adaptive intelligent systems. Mimicking the human brain and nervous system, these computing architectures are excellent candidates for solving complex and large-scale associative learning problems. 

The EU-funded NeurONN project will showcase a novel and alternative neuromorphic computing paradigm based on energy-efficient devices and architectures. In the proposed neuro-inspired computing architecture, information will be encoded in the phase of coupled oscillating neurons or oscillatory neural networks (ONN).

Name: NT-MDT
Description:
Nanotechnology is exploding. The world of small sizes has never been as vital for so many directions in science and industry. With this new direction comes a greater need for measurement on the atomic scale and new demands for reliable, precise, and multi-purpose instrumentation. NT-MDT enjoys a 30-year history in instrumentation created specifically for nanotechnology research, leading the field in originality, quality, and high tech development.
We strive for next-generation SPM technology, whether it be in pure modularity that allows a university or industrial lab to start with a cost-effective core product and build to a grand, multi-user research center or the ultimate amalgamation of SPM with related technologies that has resulted in ultramicrotomy for nanotomography and spectroscopy-based instruments that meld the world of imaging with the world of chemical analysis. We believe passionately in pushing the envelope for rapid innovation while still delivering superb customer service.

NT-MDT offers expert service and applications development through more than 20 representative offices and distributor centers around the globe. In the past years, our installed base has grown to over 1000 instruments, promoting growth of both lab and research programs world-wide.

Whether you are engaged every day in nano research or just contemplating it, coupling your specific scientific knowledge and expertise with our competence in instrument creation will produce the highest quality research results currently available.

Mission
Our mission is to enable researchers, engineers and developers to conduct nanoscale research by creating ever more perfect nanotechnology instrumentation. Along the way, we maintain a global perspective, always taking into consideration the needs of student in the classroom, the researcher at the cutting edge in the laboratory, and the practicalities of industrial R&D.

Name: Nanophotonica, Inc.
Description: NanoPhotonica provides innovative nano-materials and fabrication techniques that enable electronic displays to have high resolution, pure, vivid colors, and high efficiency at significantly reduced manufacturing cost.  NanoPhotonica’s system of light emitting nano-materials, including Quantum Dots (QDs), and method of layering these materials together, form the core technology for a new generation of electronic displays.  NanoPhotonica’s award-winning nano-materials, outstanding and motivated team, and effective growth strategy have gained us world-wide recognition and caught the eye of several large display manufacturers who have become our development collaborators. Together, we are bringing “Quantum Dot-Light Emitting Diode (QLED)” displays to this large and important market – from smartphones, to tablets and notebooks, to TVs.

Name: NOA/ Salomé group @ INL
Description:
Established in 2017, the NOA group is at the forefront of integrating nanotechnology into optoelectronic devices, focusing on industrial relevance and innovation. The group leverages the advanced nanofabrication processes of INL and engages in international collaborations to develop cutting-edge, industry-applicable devices. Core strengths include bottom-up nanofabrication and the characterization of optoelectronic materials.

With a strong focus on high technology readiness level applications, the NOA group collaborates closely with industry, introducing new technologies and aligning with market needs, while training the next generation of researchers and creating valuable expertise. Dedicated to advancing nanotechnology-based optoelectronic device concepts, the group strives to enhance the performance of devices through tailored nanofabrication solutions.

The group’s strategic approach involves mastering prototyping and transforming technology concepts into real-life devices. This commitment is demonstrated in the development of devices for sensors, energy harvesting, and IoT applications, as well as exploring integrated photovoltaics (BIPV). The NOA group also cultivates extensive expertise in large-area nanofabrication techniques, positioning itself as a sought-after collaborator for industrial partners. Through international partnerships, the group ensures that its innovative technologies are integrated into cutting-edge industrial devices on a global scale, fostering high innovation potential.

Name: CytoViva, Inc
Description:
CytoViva is a leading provider of advanced optical microscopy and hyperspectral imaging technologies. These technologies have been specifically designed for nanomaterials, nanomedicine and life science related research. 
 
CytoViva's patented illumination technology integrates onto a standard optical microscope, creating a high signal-to-noise, darkfield-based image. This specialized capability enables fast observation of a wide range of nanomaterials and pathogens along with unlabeled or fluorescently labeled cells and tissue.

The CytoViva Hyperspectral Imaging System attaches to the CytoViva Microscope System. It serves to quantify the presence of a wide range of nanomaterials in cells and tissue or in composites. The system captures the VNIR (400-1000nm) or SWIR (900-1700nm) spectrum within each pixel of the scanned field of view. Advanced analytical software then provides detailed spectral analysis of the scanned materials.

For additional information, contact us by email at info@cytoviva.com or by phone at 1-888-737-3130.

Name: Orbital Electronics
Description:
Electrons in matter possess various quantum degrees of freedom, among which the orbital angular momentum which features the self-rotation of the electron wave packet. In recent years, it was discovered that orbital currents can be used to convey information very efficiently. Most importantly, they can be generated in light metals that are cheap and abundant on Earth, thereby opening entirely new paths for their exploitation in competitive electronic components.

The EU-funded OBELIX project aims at advancing the physics of “orbitronics” and launching an entirely new technology based on the electronic orbital degree of freedom.

OBELIX will drive the development new families of electronic components, from memories to sensors and logic, and THz emitters and radio-frequency nano-oscillators free from scarce heavy metals. This technology has the potential not only to outperform existing devices but also to be strategically resilient and environmentally responsible.

Our mission is integration and cross-fertilization between these clusters to address tomorrow’s challenges in the fields of Telecommunications & Connectivity, Quality of Life, Mobility and Sustainability. 

LIMNI’s overarching perspective, integration of a high-end infrastructure with expertise that stretches across disciplines, stimulates the creation of ground-breaking projects and numerous innovative researches beyond. LIMNI is an excellent integrated point of access for not only consortium building but also to provide win-win valorization opportunities for both KU Leuven researchers and external partners.

Name: SAFENANO
Description:
Established as a Centre of Excellence in Nanosafety in 2006 at the UK’s Institute of Occupational Medicine (IOM), SAFENANO provides industry, academia and governments with independent authoritative expertise and state-of-the-art facilities to enable effective risk management.

We bring together multidisciplinary expertise in hazard assessment, exposure, toxicology, occupational hygiene and risk assessment, facilitating the responsible development of nanomaterials and nanotechnology-enabled products by making use of an integrated approach combining research, review activities, expert opinion and best practice training.

These services are supported by a free-to-access Knowledge Base, focusing on interpreting and transferring the emerging scientific evidence about the potential risks to health and the environment arising from the development and use of nanotechnology.

Effective risk management protects your business and gives you confidence in developing and realising the potential of nanotechnology by:
- maintaining investor, workforce and consumer trust;
- guarding against potential future liabilities and prosecution;
- protecting your reputation and brand.  

With global coverage from our UK and Singapore offices, IOM has unrivalled expertise and capability in risk management designed to support research, regulation, and commercialisation of nanotechnology.  With unparalleled expertise, facilities and services, there is no one better placed to help you manage the increasingly complex risk presented to your business.

For further information, please visit our website http://www.safenano.org

Name: Spinbow S.R.L.
Description:
Spinbow s.r.l. was founded in 2012 as a result of collaboration of companies located in Bologna and research centers. The shareholders of Spinbow are entrepreneurs whose companies work in the field of mechanical engineering, mechatronics, electronics and precision mechanics and by researchers in chemical science, mechanical and electrical engineering as well as in the nanostructured materials science area.

Spinbow has three main production lines:
- Electrospinning machines and devices for research laboratories;
- Continuous electrospinning machines for mass production of self-standing nanofibrous mats or for the continuous coating of substrate membranes;

Thanks to the company capability and expertise, Spinbow can offer high quality support for selecting the proper polymers and substrates tailored to the specific requests and needs of our customers.
Spinbow srl is part of a large network of companies whose name is INNOVANET where different clusters work developing new technologies and materials which benefit the companies in the network:
- Cluster eHealth,
- Cluster robotics

Name: Advanced Lithium-Sulfur batteries with ultramicroporous carbons
Description:
Energy storage plays a crucial role in reducing strain on the environment while meeting growing energy demands. As one of the most versatile energy storage technologies, batteries are essential to achieve the decarbonization of the transport and the power sector. Lithium-sulfur (Li-S) batteries are a strategic candidate to outperform established  Li-ion systems in energy density, cost, and materials sustainability. So far there are two major issues preventing Li-S batteries from widespread practical application. First, the poor cycle life caused by polysulfide shuttling and irreversible reactions at the metallic lithium anode. Second, insufficient cell capacities due to high electrolyte amounts needed to dissolve large amounts of sulfur. ALISA will solve these limitations by optimizing the solid state sulfur to lithium sulfide conversion in ultramicroporous/macroporous carbon cathodes with liquid carbonate electrolytes. The combination of carbonate electrolytes with ultramicroporous carbons allows in principle for high cycle life (several thousand cycles) and lean electrolyte conditions. By developing novel carbide-derived and hierarchical macro-/ultramicroporous carbons with tailored pore size distributions and surface functionalities, ALISA aims for high sulfur loadings, optimized Li ion transport pathways and thus gravimetric capacities twice as high compared to nowadays intercalation-type Li ion battery materials - with practical cycle life and at practical rates. Advanced characterization techniques will quantify structure and transport from atomic scales to macro scales and provide rational design criteria for improved end-device performance, higher cycle life and thus decreased degradation.


This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 958174  M-ERA.NET network: Ministrstvo za visoko šolstvo, znanost in inovacije for ALISA project (project number 9359)

Nanotechnology is a key enabling technology at the basis of many modern processes and devices. By using a consolidate knowledge deriving from the intersection of the fields relative to Physics, Chemistry and Biology, nanotechnology aims to master the manipulation of matter at a very fine scale in order to impart new and unexplored properties needed for the production of devices with improved performances and/or better efficiency.

Nanotechnology is at the basis of many industrial developments where this modern approach opens new and unexplored business opportunities.

Nanoshare 4.0, an innovative small spin-off company, born from the experience and know-how of researchers with a long career in the public research institutions and in the University, setup according to a public call from Regione Lazio, aims to open new business opportunities conjugating energy efficiency and environmental sustainability in the development of new and innovative products for advanced electric energy storage.

In particular, nanotechnology is used to impart new and unexpected properties and efficiency to modern devices, in particular Li-ion batteries, developing, engineering, producing and commercializing advanced materials for their internal components.
Our mission is to contribute to environmental sustainability providing components for batteries with more efficient energy storage and management.

Name: SolarSkin
Description:
Certified long-lasting Nanotechnology anti-soiling solution for PV glass restoration,self/easy-clean & protection.

PV modules consist of solar cells which are covered by a piece of protective glass. Since PV modules are exposed to environmental conditions their protective glass requires periodic cleaning. Staining,dirt and grime, leaves, pollen, dirt droppings, lime-scale and other pollutants prevent solar radiation from reaching the cells of PV modules and as a result, the efficiency of the solar system is reduced.

By SolarSkin the glass surface of a solar modules becomes anti-adhesive, therefore dirt and pollution can no longer adhere to it. PV modules which have been coated by SolarSkin acquire both hydrophobic (water-repellent) and oleophobic properties. As a result, the various contaminants are easily washed away by water, i.e. either by rainfall,or by rinsing with potable water (distilled water is not necessary).

The most important advantages of SolarSkin at a glance:
Easy cleaning of spots and stains. Self-cleaning under rainfall
PV Glass restoration.
Increment of clarity and transparency-improvement of power efficiency.
High Resistance to hard water and cleansers.
Protection from acid rain and environmental pollution. Reduction of mildew,  protection from pollen, bird droppings,etc.
High Endurance to high temperatures >400°C
High Resistance to temperature changes.
Reduction of mechanical degradation (e.g. scratches) and chemical wear or   corrosion of the protective glass of solar modules.
Reduction of downtime during cleaning cycles
High endurance, long-lasting properties for at least seven years or 15000 cleaning cycles.
Very easy implementation-Do It Yourself.
Protection of the environment, due to reduced use of water, detergents and cleansers. 
Certified by NCSR Demokritos, National Technical University of Athens & TUV
User and environmentally friendly

The Nano-Science Center houses research groups from the Department of Chemistry, the Niels Bohr Institute, the Department of Public Health, the Department of Food Sciences, and the GLOBE Institute.

Name: Quantum Materials and nanoDevices Group at Chalmers University of Technology
Description:
Our group explores the frontiers of condensed matter physics by investigating quantum nanomaterials with a focus on two main research directions:

- Topological Insulators and Weyl Semimetals;
- High-Temperature Superconductors.

We adopt a comprehensive, interdisciplinary approach that spans the entire research pipeline — from material synthesis to device physics. Our work begins with the growth of high-quality thin films and nanostructures, including novel topological and superconducting compounds. We perform advanced structural and surface characterisation to understand and tune material properties at the atomic level.

Our team is also actively engaged in the nanofabrication of quantum devices in a cleanroom environment, developing custom architectures to probe quantum phenomena such as spin-momentum locking, proximity effects, quantum coherence, charge density waves, and more.

The final stage of our research focuses on electrical transport measurements, with experiments conducted down to the mK regime to uncover quantum phenomena that emerge only at ultra-low temperatures.

These experiments allow us to probe fundamental quantum effects and explore new phases of matter, with an eye toward future quantum technologies.

Name: Meerkats World
Description:
Vision
 Enhance the operational life of electronic devices that has become imminent part of work, education and life in general, for optimum utilization depleting natural resources. 

Almost all the thermal interface material currently in use rely on Phonon based thermal conduction. Only exception is Liquid Metal. This has become a limiting factor in electronics, where owing to degradation of the functionality of these material the heat gets accumulated, resulting in fast ageing of electronics. Similar problem is observed in many industrial application thermal interface material. Our innovation has brought in electronic thermal conduction where electron become main carrier of heat. With application of nanoscience, vertically aligned metallic nanocylinders are synthesized and provides the 6X increased surface, along with electron heat carriers.

Advantages Estimated: The product shall make electronics regain its power efficiency, stop heat related degradation, increase the operational life of electronic products, consumer electronics by many years. This shall have considerable contribution towards achieving Climate Action objectives.

Prof. Ali Bumjdad

Description:

FNM's products and services are design and production of electrospinning machines in lab, pilot and industrial scales as well as melt, force and blown electrospinning systems, with various accessories (High Voltage power supplies, Syringe Pumps and collectors), with focus on producing of respiratory face mask, power plant and automotive air and oil filters, window screen, vacuum cleaner bags, cosmetic face masks, wound dressing and etc. based on electrospun nanofibers.

FNM's lab-scale electrospinning machines are excellent for research and development of several kinds of ceramic, polymer, composites nanofibers. These machines have reasonable and competitive price with high quality and FNM has exported this machines to foreign countries. FNM's large scale electrospining machines can be used in coating of filters' papers by a layer of polymeric nanofibers. A thin layer of nanofibers increase the filtration efficiency, filter life-time and dust holding capacity without increasing the pressure drop. The produced nanofilters by this large scale machine are useful in cars' filter, power plants' filters, respiratory face masks, etc. The produced nanofilters passed required tests and standards and got needed certificates from some institute such as VTT (Finland) and DMT (Germany). Additionally, FNM is going to develop many kinds of technologies related to nanofibers including ceramic nanofibers, composite nanofibers, scaffold nanofibers and core-shell nanofibers.

Our mission is to provide our customers with state-of-the-art image processing software for microscopy including:

•	Correction tools for creating the most accurate presentation of the "true" surface

•	Automated analysis techniques assuring high accuracy, quality and cost efficiency

•	Visualization and reporting tools enabling convincing and impressive communication of results

This mission is fulfilled by development of our main product the Scanning Probe Image Processor, SPIP. SPIP supports a wide variety of microscope types and their file formats including Scanning Probe Microscopes (SPM), interference microscopes, Scanning Electron Microscopes (SEM), confocal microscopes and profilers.

Image Metrology is a highly innovative company constantly developing new solutions meeting the demands from our high-tech customers. Image Metrology is supplying its products directly to the end users or via our distribution network, and having an export rate of 98%.

Name: Centre for Programmable Biological Matter
Description:
At the Centre for Programmable Biological Matter, we are interested in understanding, designing and building natural and artificial bionanomachines.

Bionanomachines are fascinating nanoscale machines made from biological molecules, typically proteins, lipids, RNA and DNA. In nature they carry out an amazing array of tasks, from copying our DNA to converting light into energy. 

We use a mixture of biochemical, structural and computational techniques plus the most important ingredient: imagination. 

Currently we are particularly interested in the natural machine DNA gyrase both because of its fascinating and intricate mechanism but also because it is an important target for development of new antibacterial drugs.

We are also building artificial structures from proteins and DNA with the aim of developing Programmable Biological Matter. This describes artificial nanoscale biological material which can be programmed to move on demand. This will be useful for new materials and particularly medical use for example in effective vaccines and smart drug delivery systems which may one day be used as effective disease treatments in particular for age-associated diseases.

Funded by The Leverhulme Trust and Academy of Medical Sciences.

Name: CERC interactions lumière-matière/light-matter interactions

***
The high-level vision of the Canada Excellence Research Chair (CERC) in Light-Matter Interactions is to deliver new knowledge of the properties of microscopic systems made of many interacting particles in the solid state, which will empower the discovery and implementation of new materials for photonics and quantum technologies.

Electrophoretic technologies play a central role in scientific investigations in clinical, basic, and applied disciplines from life sciences through chemistry and physics, to engineering. Our goal is to promote excellence in electrokinetic technologies, thus improving the overall quality and sophistication of scientific research.

The AES Electrophoresis Society:
- Is dedicated to the development and refinement of electrokinetic and related technologies;
- Was formed to promote excellence, generate scientific knowledge, and engineer techniques;

Name: EternAloy Corporation
Description:
EternAloy (formerly Allomet) produces EternAloy TCHP (Tough-Coated Hard Powders), a nano-engineered powdered metal product line that offers combined hardness and toughness beyond any other material ever produced and enables extreme wear-resistance.

We utilize a flexible, proprietary, globally-patented, nanotechnology-based process to carefully coat 2-50 micron-sized hard core particles (ceramics, superhards, carbides) of any shape with 50-500 nanometer-thin tough coatings and an extremely strong outer shell coating to create EternAloy TCHP.  Employing conventional methods, TCHP is then applied as a surface coating (via thermal spray or laser metal deposition) or pressed and sintered into solid body shapes (cutting tools, wire dies, etc.).  The resulting applications exhibit unsurpassed levels of extreme wear-resistance over a very wide temperature range for high-value applications where productivity, reliability, efficiency, and safety are a critical priority to the customer.

The TCHP production process is customizable to meet desired specifications, allowing a hard core particle to be selected from a range of 30 types to provide desired thermal conductivity/insulation, strength, wear, fatigue life, and toughness characteristics.  Applications can include: 

Machine tools
Wire dies
Ballistics
Turbine and aircraft components
Oil and gas drills
Automotive engine components
Bearings
Rollers/extruders
Hazardous waste storage containers
Heat exchangers
Valves
Pipe interiors/exteriors
Medical devices

Name: Axium Nano
Description:
Axium Nano is a revolution in nanoscale engineering and fabrication. Graphene, graphene oxide, nanoparticles and other nanoscale raw materials exhibit tremendously exciting structural and functional properties. However, the cost of producing these materials are very expensive and quality control is poor. Moreover, the costs of manufacturing nanocomposites (thin films) from graphene and nanoparticles are also very expensive, limiting applications to niche markets. Axium has developed several proprietary processes that establish a comprehensive and efficient supply chain to fabricate graphene, nano and micro particles, ceramic and metallic nanofibers at significantly lower cost with tight quality control. The Company's downstream nanocomposites manufacturing platform can produce these thin films at a rate over 10,000 times faster than chemical vapor deposition (CVD) while maintaining precise deposition and dispersion control.

Axium's mandate is to industrialize nanomaterials manufacturing and commercialize nanocomposites for high volume commercial applications, such as solar, energy storage, water treatment, coatings, among many other applications. The Company has developed a directly deposited silicon/graphene anode that meets or exceeds the specifications of Tier One lithium ion battery manufacturers and could be offered at a price competitive with synthetic graphite. Silicon is considered a breakthrough material for energy storage due to its incredibly high energy density, but the development of a commercially-viable composite anode has alluded industry and academia for almost a decade. Axium is scaling its production and plans to build manufacturing capacity over the next 24 months. 

Axium has been developing a pipeline of progressive materials for other energy storage applications and also advanced battery chemistries, such as lithium sulfur and lithium air, technologies that store far more energy than lithium ion batteries.

Name: DCN Corp
Description:
DCN Corp is a Nano-tech company seeking to commercialise equitable solutions.

Our technology is validated to TRL 3 and suitably protected.

What we do -
• Developing & raising awareness of a new Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) process technology. It will later become a measurable tool that provides accurate, real-time & quantifiable solutions.

Our Mission -
• To pioneer both the development & understanding of using Nano-tech to produce solutions. Through extensive research & sound science, we aim to demonstrate how advancing Nano-tech applications, especially within healthcare, could contribute to a significant positive impact on humanity by producing life-changing innovations.

Our Vision - 
o We all know someone who has been impacted by a life-changing illness, say cancer or a cardiovascular condition. These illnesses respond better to effective treatment if identified & precisely categorised earlier, meaning an increased chance of survival, as well as much reduced costs in treatment plans.

Our vision is to develop a handheld bio-kit to help with this objective. This vision is supported by future trends in healthcare & at-home rapid diagnostic requirements.

The SPR tool will be scalable & suitable for mass production, with the unique ability to:-
1. Accurately provide real-time diagnostic results of a condition or disease

2. Provide numerical information on the severity of a diagnosed condition or disease

This tool will be a companion for both new & existing laboratories that employ Raman & Infrared instruments both desktop & handheld settings.

What we need to achieve this -
o DCN Corp are seeking both technical collaborations & financial relationships

o Our next R&D milestone is to apply the Nano-tech onto plastic polymer substrates, which will enhance the bio-sensitivity of the SPR tool

To achieve this were raising a Pre-seed fundraising round of above £500k ($645,866).  So, if you are interested please ask for our Executive summary.

Name: SILSEF
Description:
SILSEF is a French start-up, founded in 2010, based at Archamps Technopole on the French-Swiss border near Geneva. We share a 20 000 sq ft plant with industrial wafer manufacturer Sil'tronix Silicon Technologies.
The facilities include clean rooms to provide the quality required for micro/opto-electronics and bio-medical applications.  The team includes 10 highly skilled staff, mostly qualified engineers and PhDs.

Our key technology is Nano Imprint Lithography (NIL) invented in 1995 in the U.S, to reduce the size of microelectronic circuits. It is ranked among the revolutionary technologies by the MIT ("Ten Technologies That can change the world"), thanks to its generic and large variety of applications.
The invention is to replicate nanoscale designs over a wide range of materials in volume and at minor costs. Our challenge is to implement the two following options to offer tailor-made solutions : 
           -NIL with an etching step
           -Nano Embossing Polymer (NEP)

The technology allows also double scale patterns such as macro-channels (100 µm wide) with nano-structured areas (100 nm in size).
Our skills cover the whole technology chain to meet your expectations : 
           -Design of patterns including simulation and modelling
           -Manufacture of stamps at micro and nanoscale 
           -Transfer patterns on a range of materials
           -Geometric and functional characterizations

It is supported by the NILAB (Nano Imprint Laboratory), a technology platform that brings together first class academic and industrial partners. Thus, in addition to its internal resources, SILSEF can call upon wide scientific and technological resources to assess your requirements and best respond to your specifications.

Description: Nanvio is a CleanTech startup from Chalmers School of Entrepreneurship and has a patented nanotechnology platform for air and water purification. The timing of this platform technology is essential since clean water is scarce, and air is becoming increasingly polluted. As an example, it is estimated that 16% of all deaths that occur each year is linked to pollution and according to WHO, half of the world’s population will be living in water-stressed areas in 2025. Clean air and water are essential to life, and access to it shouldn’t be depended on where you live. With increasing contamination it is more important than ever to cut down on pollutants in the environment. We have created a solution that can clean our two most essential natural resources. Our technology is a platform technology, modular in design and application. We offer a solution with few constraints to whoever faces the problem of pollutions in water and air.

Name: Detectica
Description:
Welcome to Detectica, your partner in innovation and progress. We are a forward-thinking company dedicated to revolutionizing industries through the power of nanotechnology. Our journey began with a vision to bring cutting-edge solutions to businesses and organizations seeking transformation.

Our Mission
At Detectica, our mission is to harness the potential of nanotechnology to create products that redefine industries and drive technological advancements. Our commitment to innovation fuels our passion to develop solutions that make a real difference in the world.

Our Expertise
With a team of dedicated experts, we are focused on pioneering new frontiers in nanotechnology. Our journey started with the exploration of graphene's remarkable properties, and it has now evolved to encompass a broader spectrum of applications. Our dedication to research and development has led us to create innovative paint solutions that are set to make waves in various sectors.

Nanotechnology-Powered Paints
Our journey into nanotechnology has led us to formulate groundbreaking paint products that offer exceptional performance and value. From Thermal Insulation Nanopaint to Antibacterial Nanopaint, our innovative formulations have the potential to revolutionize industries by addressing key challenges and delivering tangible benefits.

A Vision for the Future
As a startup with a relentless drive for progress, we are excited to pave the way for a new era of nanotechnology applications. Our nanotechnology-powered paints are just the beginning of our journey to transform industries and create a positive impact.

Get in Touch
Explore the exciting possibilities of nanotechnology with Detectica. We invite you to learn more about our innovative paint solutions and how they can enhance your projects. Connect with us to discover the future of industries powered by nanotechnology.

We are using atomic force microscopy and accessible organic synthesis techniques to assemble diamondoid structures directly, using off-the-shelf tools and well-understood chemistry.

We are also developing a software environment for creating and validating engineering designs of atomically precise parts and mechanisms, with the goal of growing the community of nano-mechanical engineers in advance of the manufacturing process being ready.

The company was founded in 2009 and is located at Feevale Techpark, Campo Bom.

Name: Vitec Microgenix
Description:
Hospital Acquired Infections are a global problem. Cases increase every year in both quantity and severity due to the growing resistance to antibiotics. The goal of Vitec Microgenix is to significantly reducing HAI's by eradicating indoor microbes on surfaces and airborne. 

Surfaces are protected for up to 12 months with one treatment using our patented antimicrobial chemical. Air is purified of 99.99% of microbes using our unique Air Sterilisation System.

Join us in shaping the future of nanotechnology and making a positive impact on the world!

Name: NanoRD
Description:
NanoRD’s vision sees a new world, where atomic-level manipulation of materials brings dreams to reality. Our pillars are
power, adventure and wealth which are strong forces in the markets where competition is high or will become high
when we help clients carve an entirely new market.
Our service, at their core, guide creative thoughts into real-life products. To stimulate the creative workflow, we offer a
few broad ideas that intend to spark ambition in our clients. The semiconductor industry example below shows a silicon
transistor which has a single (a binary output). Through NanoRD, our client can create heterostructures with chips of the
same size having multiple capabilities.
In layman’s terms, the sun may power this device, alongside the heat generated by the electricity flowing through it. A
chemical sensor can be integrated to monitor the environment of pollutants and a strain sensor can help diagnose
mechanical damage (instead of the current, crack it open and have a look) and it also acts as a transistor. These
possibilities are realized into the same volume as a classic transistor through the power of nanotechnology and 2d
materials.

Name: NANOTRANSMED
Description:
NANOTRANSMED is a European project, led by a french-german-swiss consortium and cofinanced by the INTERREG VA Upper Rhine Programme.

The NANOTRANSMED consortium focuses in particular on issues concerning early, reliable and fast diagnosis but also personalized treatment of diseases such as cancers and inflammation as well as issues regarding nosocomial infections contracted by 5% of hospitalized patients.

Description:
Born in 2007, we are a young company with rich experience and exposure. Companies galore in the similar business and missions, what separates us from the rest is - the passion to create and innovate and to provide the best services to our customers.

A strong believer in these principles, Anarghya Innovations and Technology Private Limited is involved in providing high-end and State-of-the-Art Technological Solutions to support a wide range of research. We are involved in both sales and quality service of high-end research equipments for a host of applications such as Nanoscience and Nanotechnology, Nano-Biotechnology, Microscopy, Semiconductor processing, Epitaxial material and device processing, Nano-structural analysis, Cyrogenics and Surface sciences.

Name: CONCERTO PRIN2020
Description: CONCERTO will develop a suite of harmonized multiscale materials modelling and characterization methods, to support and accelerate the production of Ce-TZP nanocomposites and coatings, with unprecedented combination between strength and toughness. A multi-scale design of selected ceramic components will be performed, to demonstrate the ability to engineer the materials from the nano/microstructural features to the macro-structure, and through all processing ceramic steps, from composite powder elaboration, to shaping and sintering of the final parts. An emerging 3D printing technology for technical ceramics, stereolithography, will be used for the first time to fabricate simple and complex-shaped demonstrators, in parallel with the exploitation of thermal spray technology to fabricate coatings. Finally, two impactful case studies are selected to demonstrate the relevance of the research conducted, namely optimization and functional validation of (a) Ce-TZP materials for application in dental prostheses and (b) Ce-TZP composite thermal barrier coatings for high-temperature applications.

Name: EP Laboratories, Inc.
Description:
Independent testing laboratory that specializes in mechanical testing at low loads, including nano and micro levels.  We are committed to providing confidential, professional and detail-oriented services at competitive prices.  

Testing services include the measurement of coefficient of friction; wear and abrasion; nanoindentation for hardness, modulus, creep and fracture toughness; nano scratch testing for adhesion and scratch resistance; “push-pull” mechanical testing at low loads; hardness (nano, micro, Vickers, Rockwell all scales, Brinell low load, Mohs, Shore Durometer)

Materials tested: metal, polymer, plastic, glass, ceramic, alloy and composites.

Applications and testing include thin films and coatings, small electronics, medical devices and tools, packaging and containers, wires, cables and connectors; semiconductors, components, testing for puncture resistance, seal strength, tensile and compressive properties.
