Name: Quertech
Description:
Quertech is undoubtedly a new industrial leader in treatment surface industry introducing a breakthrough technology named Hardion+ : micro particles accelerators for ion implantation. Ions directed at a surface of a material, with high energy embed themselves in the material. This is used to change the properties of the material by adding another species to the material.

By continuously advancing ion implantation technology, Quertech helps customers to improve environmental performance, decrease energy consumption, extend component life, improve productivity, minimize downtime, reduce operating costs, and produce higher quality products. 

Hardion+ process can be used to improve the friction coefficient, adhesive wear and surface hardness of polymers, glass and metals by bombarding surfaces with a high energy ion beam. 25 patents cover process, machine, composition of matter.

Name: 파크시스템스
Description:
파크시스템스는 순수 국내 원천 기술로 첨단 나노계측장비인 원자현미경 및 계측 기술의 혁신을 선도하고 있는 글로벌 기업입니다. 
파크시스템스의 원자현미경은 국내외 수의 대학교와 연구소는 물론, 최고의 글로벌 반도체 기업들로부터 최적의 솔루션으로 높이 평가받으며, 원자현미경 분야에서 독보적인 기술력과 브랜드 인지도를 구축하고 있습니다. 
1997년 설립되어 현재까지 가장 오래된 원자현미경 기술의 역사적 뿌리를 기반으로 전세계에서 가장 앞선 신기술들을 개발하여 시장을 리드해 오고 있습니다. 수원 광교에 본사를 비롯하여 전세계에 법인 및 연락사무소를 두고 있으며, 연구용 장비부터 반도체 계측 검사 자동화 장비에 이르기까지 다양한 원자현미경 제품 라인을 보하고 있습니다. 
파크시스템스는 앞으로도 매년 지속적인 기술 혁신을 통해 독보적인 기술로 세계를 선도하는 원자현미경 회사로 거듭날 것입니다.

Name: InovisCoat GmbH
Description:
InovisCoat is known as a competent partner for high-tech functional coating. Core competency is the multilayer coating, where up to 9 different layers are applied simultanious but mixing free. Naturally each individual layer can have its own functionality. Dry coating thicknesses of 300 nanometers up to 100 micrometers can be achieved.

InovisCoat can offer its customer everything from chemical development, as well as paste preparation and the actual coating, to ensure a successful and quality wise exclusive production of high-tech products. InovisCoat understands itself not as a simple contract or toll coater, but as a highly competent partner for the development of new products or new economical production processes.

Formerly specialized on photographic films and papers, InovisCoat broadened its business activities especially into the nano-technologic fields of optical films (e.g. OLEDs, Displays), pharmacy- or medical technologies (e.g. wound curing), security systems (eg. identification, copy protection) as well as energetic systems (e.g. barrier sheets). Here InovisCoat can support its customers through its profound experience as well as its high innovation potential to find ever new solutions.

The existing production and r&d facilities fulfill highest requirements on precision and flexibility even for light-sensitive, multilayer products. Different materials and substrates can be coated with our multilayer expertise.

Name: fionec GmbH
Description:
fionec GmbH has been developing, manufacturing and distributing innovative fiber optic measurement systems and components for precision measurements in the nanometer range since 2007.

With its unique miniature measuring probes, fionec GmbH is technology leader in high-precision measurements of tight or hard-to-access spaces and of sensitive surface structures.

Thanks to flexible modular design, configurable system settings and integrated interfaces, fionec provides customized measuring systems for demanding measurement tasks and reliable quality assurance in precision and ultra-precision manufacturing.

Services include prototyping and development of customized measurement solutions, simulations and contract measurements, as well as construction, assembly and distribution of fiber optic measuring probes and optical fibers.

In addition, as a long-standing distributor of Corning, fionec offers a wide range of optical fibers for metrology and laser technology, life sciences and telecommunications.

Name: Atomic Mechanics
Description: Atomic Mechanics Ltd is a new spin-out business of the University of Manchester. It has been created to develop, manufacture and sell a completely new range of highly sensitive, ultra-robust pressure sensors, transparent, flexible, variable-pressure touch interfaces and actuation devices.  These innovative products are built around a patented core technology which uses single layer graphene and 2D materials films supported by a thin flexible polymeric membrane. The new products will have significant competitive advantage over existing technologies and bring enormous benefits to customers in a wide variety of global markets.

Name: BioForce Nanosciences
Description:
BioForce Nanosciences, Inc. develops and commercializes nanotech instruments, consumables, and applications for the life sciences. For over a decade we've been a leading provider of consumable support products for atomic force microscopy (AFM). In 2005 we introduced our own flagship instrument platform, the Nano eNabler system. 

The Nano eNabler system is a molecular printer that rapidly and precisely delivers liquids to surfaces such as silicon chips in droplets that are ten billion times smaller than a drop of blood. This unique capability offers researchers in industry and academia a practical solution to problems they encounter when developing ultraminiaturized devices and methods, and creates opportunities for further commercialization of nanotechnology. For example, BioForce’s technology is being used to create biosensors and to study cell biology at the single cell level. Additional applications include printing proteins to guide neural cell growth, printing signaling proteins to study stem cell differentiation, “inking” of micro-stamps for soft lithography, and bio-functionalization of microfluidic devices.

Name: Nanoscope Instruments

Die nascit GmbH bietet Ihnen verschiedene Lösungen an, um das Nanoscale Sculpturing Verfahren gewinnbringend für Ihr Unternehmen zu nutzen. Von der Entwicklung maßgeschneiderter Anlagen bis hin zur Fertigung großer Stückzahlen bieten wir für jedes Problem eine passende Lösung. Unser Entwicklungsteam erarbeitet täglich Lösungen für unsere Partner und Kunden und wird auch für Ihre Anliegen passende Lösungen finden.

Our team has many years of practical experience. We provide you with relevant information regarding your challenges in failure analysis, quality assurance and product development. You will receive the results quickly, typically within 6 - 10 days.

4TunaTES will develop a TCES-prototype that can be also used for domestic use and addresses different challenges in terms of materials used, heat exchanging components with a high degree of manufacturing flexibility, and revolutionary systems with electricity adapted thermodynamic cycles.

Pathfinder project funded by EIC active from 2024-2028.

Name: NanoProtection
Description:
Nanoprotection est une société française, basée en Franche Comté, spécialisée dans les solutions professionnelles de nano-revêtement pour la protection des surfaces. 
Nous sommes fiers d'être le leader du marché dans le domaine de la protection et du traitement de surfaces grâce à notre expertise en nanotechnologie. 

Chez Nanoprotection, nous comprenons l'importance cruciale d'une surface protégée dans le monde professionnel. C'est pourquoi nous avons développé une gamme complète de solutions de nano-revêtement qui offrent une protection inégalée pour une large variété de surfaces : verre, pierre, métaux, plastique, textile, bois…

Professionnel de l'automobile, de l'industrie, de la construction, du secteur médical, des énergies renouvelables, du nettoyage, nos produits sont conçus pour répondre à vos besoins spécifiques. 

Grâce à nos technologies de pointe, nous pouvons prévenir l'accumulation de saleté, calcaire, graisse et autres contaminants. Vos surfaces deviennent hydrofuges et offrent une résistance exceptionnelle aux rayures, aux produits chimiques agressifs, aux UV, à l’abrasion...  Nos produits sont conçus pour résister aux rigueurs de l'environnement professionnel offrant une durabilité prolongée et une apparence impeccable pour vos équipements, vos installations et vos infrastructures. 

Nous sommes également fiers de contribuer à la préservation de l'environnement. Nos produits réduisent la consommation de produits chimiques agressifs et permettent un nettoyage facile et écologique. 

La plupart de nos produits n'ont pas d'équivalent à l'heure actuelle sur le marché français.

Rejoignez-nous et découvrez comment Nanoprotection peut vous aider à améliorer la durabilité, la performance et l'apparence de vos surfaces. Suivez-nous pour rester informé des dernières avancées technologiques, des études de cas inspirantes et des témoignages de clients satisfaits.
Faites confiance à Nanoprotection pour une protection de surfaces de qualité professionnelle.

Name: AdaptEM
Description: AdaptEM is company providing fully programmable phase plates for electron microscopes. Our patented technology is based on individual Einzel lens controlling each of the 48 independent pixels forming the phase plate. Each pixel can apply a constant phase shift to the coherent electron wave front giving unprecedent control over the quantum wavefunction of the electron beam. Applications include but are not limited to formation of exotic beam shapes, adaptive aberration correction, contrast enhancement, symmetry selection in inelastic scattering, solution of inverse scattering problems, determination of chiral structures, seeing through thick samples, depth sectioning, increased depth of focus, and many more!

Name: International and Inter University Center for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (IIUCNN)

Eight years ago, Mahatma Gandhi University established International and Inter University Centre for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (IIUCNN) to carry out intense research in the field of Nanotechnology. However, the Centre extended its attention to comprise both research and teaching in Nanotechnology. IIUCNN have always sought to bridge the researchers of University and industry by offering them a place to exchange their thoughts and encourage the scholars of the University to work on product development at industrial level. 

Our students are passionate about the development of the cutting edge technology in the field of nanotechnology research and aspire to make a lasting global impact. During their time at IIUCNN, they immerse themselves in rigorous research, mastering fundamental theories and creating a personalized course of study that encourages experimentation. They collaborate closely with many scientists across the globe through conferences, workshops, exchange programs and activities. This melding of theory and practice touches everything we do. For these years, IIUCNN graduates have accomplished great things. I am proud to be part of an institution that inspires the cutting-edge thinking and innovative ideas that will prepare a new generation of principled scientists to make the world a better place. 

Dr Nandakumar Kalarikkal

BebCom Software to turn around better Analog/RF faster and with less risk

In the last years, digital verification has outgrown actual SoC design time and the verification flows have gone through major productivity changes. Meanwhile, analog/RF designer is still closer to art: engineers still craft transistors one by one, run simulation almost interactivelyand make visual comparisons of their change results. This makes the fine tuning process tedious, costly and error prone. We have designedthe software tools to boost productivity and track automatically mismatches.

Name: CENS Materials
Description:
CENS – Clustered Engineered Nano Space is the first company chosen to be financed by Incubit Ventures.

The Start-Up was founded by Michael Bromfman and Dr. Niles Fleischer in 2013. CENS is developing breakthrough technology for increasing the energy and power of super-capacitors and lithium ion batteries by connecting carbon nanotubes (CNT) with various electrode materials to create highly conductive 3-dimensional structures for meeting the increasing demands of the electronics, transportation, medical device and defense markets. A range of CENS products will be sold as electrode materials to battery and super-capacitor manufacturers.

The addition of CNT to electrodes improves their electric conductivity and can buffer volumetric changes that Li-ion batteries suffer during use. However, the potential of these benefits are not fully realized with currently used CNT preparation techniques due to problems of mixing, expensive processing, and poor performance efficiency.

The CENS technology, on the other hand, enables significantly greater utilization of the CNT properties including much improved power and energy by creating a very efficient nanostructured network with various types of electrode materials.

The CENS CNT-particle clusters provide super-capacitors with about 50% more power than equivalent type cells produced with conventional CNT mixtures thereby enabling desirable features such as fast charging, longer cycle life, and better reliability. Power sources made with the CENS 3-dimensional structured electrodes will be attractive to battery and super-capacitor producers enabling them to offer better products for powering the next generation of electronic items, medical devices and electric vehicles.

Name: DELIGHT - EU project
Description:
DELIGHT focuses on nanomaterial functionality that is activated, triggered or modulated by light. Our approach is to explore the synergies of the different nanoparticle types, functional organic and biomaterial matrices, and hybrid perovskites to create optically active nanomaterials with designed properties and to boost their potential for a broad range of applications that span from photonics over energy conversion to biosensing and healthcare.

The project is based on a truly interdisciplinary approach that unites key expertise in several research fields, and leading groups Europe and America. With such unique competence we aim to develop sustainable materials for the society of the future.

DELIGHT is coordinated by Istituto Italiano di Tecnologia and it is a project for scientific exchange of researchers between leading academic institutions in Europe, United States of America, Canada and Argentina funded by the Marie Skłodowska-Curie actions (MSCA) in the Horizon Europe framework programme of the European Union. 

The scope is to stimulate long-term scientific exchange between different sectors and countries, giving young scientists the opportunity to broaden their horizon in long term stays at host labs, contributing to an interdisciplinary and international education.

علاوه بر تلاش در ترویج فناوری نانو در سطح عموم مردم، دانش آموزان، متخصصان و فناوران، لازم است جایگاه تک تک بازیگران عرصه این فناوری در کشور، از دانشگاهها و مراکز علمی گرفته تا شرکتها و سازمانهای تجاری نیز معین گردد. برگزاری جشنواره های فناوری نانو می تواند علاوه بر توجه به این دو مهم، اهداف زیر را نیز دنبال کند:

    تقویت همکاری بین صنعت و دانشگاه
    نفوذ فناوری های توسعه یافته نانو در صنایع موجود
    زمینهسازی برای حضور شرکتهای نانو در بازارهای بینالمللی
    ارتقای دانش عمومی در حوزه فناوری نانو
    تقدیر از برترین های فناوری نانو

Name: Plataforma EnsiNANO
Description:
O Projeto Ensinano foi criado a partir das experiências didáticas reunidas nos últimos 6 anos, onde cursos e palestras foram ministrados em instituições públicas e privadas do Estado de São Paulo, com o objetivo principal de promover o ensino e a divulgação da nanotecnologia.  Por meio de atividades teóricas e experimentais, foram realizadas inserções em todos os níveis de ensino onde as experiências acumuladas deram suporte a construção de proposta pedagógica com enfoque na interdisciplinaridade, trazendo a adaptação do conteúdo como eixo central da construção do processo de ensino e aprendizagem sobre nanociência e nanotecnologia.  O objetivo é promover a cultura da educação científica nas escolas, explorando um tema que está na vanguarda da pesquisa científica e vem expandindo os limites de nosso conhecimento científico coletivo. Portanto, é necessário incentivarmos a inserção deste tema no currículo do ensino médio e técnico para explorar as interconexões entre diferentes áreas do conhecimento, permitindo que os alunos compreendam os fenômenos de modo integrado, ao mesmo tempo que se amplia o debate sobre as relações entre Ciência, Tecnologia, Sociedade e Meio Ambiente.
Com o lançamento nacional da cartilha "NANOTECNOLGIA PARA TODOS!", material didático gratuito para ensino e divulgação da nanotecnologia nas escolas, estamos dando asas ao sonho de criar ummovimento de popularização da nanotecnologia no ensino brasileiro.
A versão digital da cartilha pode ser baixada gratuitatmente no celular e computador através do no site!
Acesse e saiba mais sobre nosso projeto!
Nanotecnologia na educação é Nanotecnologia para todos!

Name: OneShield
Description:
At Oneshield we believe that customers need a mechanism to make better informed decisions to protect themselves from everyday evolving technologies and their effect on our health. The ever growing electronic emissions environment is further threatened by the introduction of 5G – a system that is at least 150 times stronger than the current 3G/4G.

Oneshield is a thin nano-technology sticker that when applied to the phone deflect the torsion waves and protect against it. Test data have shown that the ”OneShield” device considerably reduces the effect of the torsion component of cellphone’s electromagnetic radiation on immunological blood parameters of users, and that its use could be associated with a favorable sign of stabilization of condition of the immune system, on which the incidence of cold-related infectious, allergic and oncological diseases depends to a considerable extent.

For more information about the health effects of cellphone radiation see the Cell Phone Radio Frequency Radiation Studies from the National Toxicology Program 2018

The Oneshield team sent the nano sticker to be assessed in a blood test study run by Dr Gottried Scholer and his team in Salzburg, Austria. The results confirmed the effectiveness of the patented product in combating the harmful waves emitted from technology and cancelling the negative effects on the human body.

Oneshield is the protection you need.

Name: Nanoscale Function Group
Description:
At the Nanoscale Function Group, we are advancing the frontier of nanotechnology by enhancing Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM) to go beyond structural imaging—toward functional analysis at the molecular level. Our mission is to unlock the potential of nanoscale science in biological and medical systems, where understanding and manipulating interactions at the single-molecule or single-cell level can pave the way for breakthroughs in disease detection, prevention, treatment, and biomimetic material development.

By actively developing AFM instrumentation and pushing measurement capabilities, we enable researchers to explore complex biological systems under physiological conditions, with unmatched resolution and sensitivity. Our innovations in AFM technology empower fundamental discoveries at the intersection of physics, biology, and engineering.

Name: Quantum Battery Labs
Description:
Quantum Battery Labs has a clear vision to develop new battery technology, based on quantum mechanics. 

It is our belief that a safe, cost efficient, powerful quantum battery can help decarbonise the grid and help the world achieve net-zero by 2050. 

The electricity that we use today is primarily generated by coal, nuclear and gas-powered electricity stations. However, to meet the government CO2 emission requirements, the government is planning to end coal powered stations by 2024 and increase the usage of renewable sources, e.g., solar and wind power plants. However, wind and solar are intermittent and batteries are required to store these energy sources. 

We need a super battery that can store an unlimited supply of electricity…. A battery that is a key enabling device, which allows us to draw electricity from the sun and wind, even when the sun does not shine, and the wind is unavailable. 

We believe a Quantum Battery can solve this problem for future generations.

If we, as a society, are going to live in a world free from CO2 that is literally poisoning our children, we will need to think differently and take action!

We cannot just eat less meat…
We cannot just recycle more…
We cannot just fly less to reduce our carbon footprint…
We need to innovate and develop novel solutions to solve the existential threat of climate change! 

If you are a researcher doing work on a "quantum battery", please feel free to drop us a line. We have investors who would be interested in funding your research. jl@quantumbatterylabs.com 

#climatechange #batterytech #quantumtech #cleantech

Name: KiloLambda Technologies Ltd.
Description:
Established in 2001, KiloLambda has developed a unique optical layer, based on its proprietary, patented nano-technology. 

KiloLambda has surpassed the traditional techniques of light control by developing its own nano-technology and applying it in effective manner to everyday applications. It is a technology that controls optical power for all wavelengths and does so in a totally passive manner, as opposed to conventional solutions of limiting or blocking specific wavelengths and the use of active methods. The company is now applying its breakthrough technology in the advanced development of next-generation non-linear components and devices, to control and regulate optical power, passively.

The implications of KiloLambda’s technology are enormous in their ability to protect the human eye and expensive equipment against damage by destructive light sources, from sunlight to lasers. It is used in optical sensors, high-end communications devices and other light conducting applications, with enormous significance to industries in the photographic, laser, defense, security, digital imaging, automotive, building and eye wear fields. 

KiloLambda is characterized by a dynamic team of post-doctorate scientists and engineers applying out-of-the-box thinking and a management team dedicated to integrating the company’s unique technology into an ever-growing scope of applications.

Name: Koperski Lab @ IFIM, NUS
Description:
The Institute for Functional Intelligent Materials (I-FIM) at the National University of Singapore (NUS) was newly established on 1 September 2021. Headed by world-renowned physicists Nobel Laureate Prof. Sir Konstantin Novoselov and NUS Distinguished Prof. Antonio Castro Neto, I-FIM will be the world’s first institute dedicated to the design, synthesis, and application of Functional Intelligent Materials (FIMs). Its global vision is to create a platform to develop FIMs with predetermined properties and autonomous, dynamic functionalities which can respond to changing environmental conditions. I-FIM will then investigate the use of such materials for smart applications in various sectors of technology and medicine.

The Koperski Lab investigates optoelectronic, vibrational, magnetic, and structural properties of layered 2D materials. We engineer and characterize van der Waals heterostructure-based devices as a platform to study excitonic physics and quantum phenomena in transition metal dichalcogenides and 2D magnetic materials, with potential applications in single photon source creation and emerging telecommunication technologies.

Visit our career website for more information on our open positions today!

Name: nM2_Lab
Description:
The Nanostructured Magnetic Materials Laboratory (nM²-Lab) is a dynamic research unit combining expertise from the Institute of Structure of Matter (Roma, Italy) of the Italian National Research Council) and the Department of Chemistry and Industrial Chemistry of the University of Genova (Genova, Italy). Our multidisciplinary team of ~20 members, including researchers, technicians, postdocs, PhD and master’s students, drives innovative research in magnetic materials.
Founded in 2018 as the evolution of the Nano-Magnetism Laboratory (est. late 1980s by D. Fiorani), nM²-Lab has built a strong international reputation in fundamental and applied magnetism. Led by Prof. Davide Peddis and Dr. Gaspare Varvaro, we design and study functional nano- and micro-scale materials, including single-phase magnets, magnetic composites, and hybrid/multifunctional systems in forms such as nanoparticles, thin films, nano-patterned structures, fluids, and bulk materials. Our work spans applications in energy, environment, ICT and biomedicine, wth a recent focus on sustainable and responsible materials.
Our research is structured around four integrated lines:
- Hybrid magnetic nanoarchitectures
- Functional magnetic thin films and heterostructures
- Magnetic alloys and multifunctional nanocomposites
- Advanced materials and processes for sustainable bulk magnets
Beyond research, nM²-Lab members actively contribute to education and outreach, training students at all levels and organizing events that engage both the scientific community and the public. We aim to advance knowledge, foster innovation, and create real-world impact through magnetic materials science.

Name: IConS: Institute for Connected Sensor-Systems
Description:
Mission:
The mission of the Institute for Connected Sensor-Systems (IConS) is to lead, integrate, and deliver sensors, sensing, and systems solutions for and by the research community at NC State University. In carrying out this mission, IConS is committed to providing, facilitating, and enhancing (1) partnerships and collaborations among researchers and (2) multidisciplinary training opportunities for the University’s student body under the mentorship of an internationally-recognized membership of engineers and scientists.

Vision:
The vision of IConS is to be the center of gravity at NC State University for developers and customers of sensor systems to enable innovative, value-driven, real-world solutions, which will enable NC State University to acquire, execute, and grow new research programs.

IConS will accelerate/catalyze convergent sensor research at NC State making it a Center of Excellence and positioning it as a national and global leader in helping address societal problems through innovative and transformative technologies.

Focus:
Connected Sensor-Systems are multi-node and multi-modal technologies, in which information from multiple sources can be combined and correlated to infer conclusions about manifest and latent problems, via real connections between nodes in a distributed network or combined in software to create a soft network. The predictive and actionable power of Connected Sensor-Systems will be realized by integrating novel sensors, electronics, networking technologies, and demonstration of performance at individual to global scale.

Name: Next-2Digits
Description:
Next-2Digits is an Horizon Europe funded project, that aims to develop the next generation of sensors and imagers enabled by 2D materials digital integration.
With a consortium of 11 partners from 8 different countries, Next-2Digits aims to tackle the challenges associated with Photonic Integrated Circuits (PICs), Optoelectronic Integrated Circuits (OEICs) and 2D materials. 
Next-2Digits benefits from the presence of well-reputed academic, research and industrial teams, whose areas of work span from photonics, material science, application-based integration technologies and validation. 
The proposed technology to integrate graphene into PICs, being one-step and free of chemical processing, will have significant advantages in terms of cost, material and energy saving, and reduced resulting waste. 
Next-2Digits envisions to develop the first hybrid Graphene-based photodetectors (PDs) and modulators (MDs).
Next-2Digits is involved in the GrapheneEU CSA project, under the Graphene Flagship Initiative.

Name: Ceramic Pro Brasil
Description:
Ceramic Pro Brasil, distribuidora oficial da Nanoshine, busca inovar no mercado com produtos altamente qualificados e de tecnologia avançada.

Em 2010 a Nanoshine Ltd introduziu Ceramic Pro – uma série de produtos para o mercado automotivo, aéreo e marítimo, adequados para proteção de superfícies, tais como pintura, vidro, alumínio, tecido, couro, plástico e borracha. Em 2014, a Ceramic Pro Brasil, trouxe os produtos para o mercado, possuindo exclusividade para revender em todo território brasileiro. O que torna Ceramic Pro diferente é a tecnologia de ponta à base de compostos moleculares de cerâmica (nanotecnologia).

A linha de produtos profissionais da Ceramic Pro foi desenvolvida pela líder global em tecnologia Nanoshine Ltd, de Taiwan. Em sua essência estão compostos moleculares únicos (nanocerâmica) que formam revestimentos sólidos.

A linha Ceramic Pro garante possibilidades inigualáveis de proteção para superfícies de carros, veleiros, barcos, aeronaves, construções, móveis e ítens industriais, vestuário, acessórios, etc

POR QUÊ ESCOLHER CERAMIC PRO?

- Proteção permanente: O Ceramic Pro não é uma proteção de pintura, cera ou selante que será lavado ou  que quebre com o tempo. É um revestimento nano-cerâmico que forma uma adesão permanente à tinta e só pode ser removido por abrasão. Nenhum produto químico pode dissolver o revestimento.
- Dureza acima de 9H: Quando o revestimento endurece, cria uma superfície que pode suportar lascas de pedra e todos os tipos de arranhões leves. O revestimento de vidro está acima de 9H na escala de teste Lápis. Esta escala é usada na indústria de revestimento para determinar a dureza de um mineral, sendo a 9H a mais alta na escala. O casaco de couro vai ficar "novo" por toda a vida se mantido corretamente.
- Super Gloss: O brilho deste produto não é comparável a qualquer outro produto no
mercado. Tem que ser visto na vida real.
- Super Hidrofóbico
- Resistência química
- Oxidação e Resistente à Corrosão
- Proteção UV

Name: LaFSI
Description: At LaFSI we deal with interfacial phenomena occurring in confined or micro patterned structures characterized by large surface-to-volume ratios. Name: SoluBest Ltd.
Description:
Founded in 2001 and privately owned and funded, SoluBest is an Israel based formulation solution provider specializing in a broad spectrum of formulation and delivery challenges involving:
- Bioavailability
- Stability
- Solubility
- Particle design the optimisation
- Scale up and industrialisation of formulation technologies 

SoluBest is pursuing a Two-Pronged Business Model:
a) Solubest provides custom services by leveraging its expertise in the development of novel formulations for solubility challenged molecules, using either proprietary or off the shelf drug delivery technologies.  This activity allows SoluBest to both generate short term income and/or long term royalty arrangements, as well as opportunities as a custom supplier for Pharma and non-Pharma applications.  

b) Solubest develops its own speciality pharma products, which it spins as projects mature.  Projects that have progressed into clinical stages (or in some cases, pre-clinical proofs of concept) are spun out into new ventures for their commercialisation through equity-based joint ventures, and/or licensing-out agreements based on down- and milestones-payments.  Several JVs are currently being formed.

In this context, SoluBest  has developed a proprietary, Industry validated  drug delivery platform for significantly improving the bio-availability of small molecule drugs by generating proprietary smart drug-polymer sub-micron particles in which drug molecules are interwoven within FDA-approved polymers. Once in the body, these formulations disintegrate into stable colloidal dispersions, significantly increasing drug absorption, thus improving the performance of active substances.

Name: CLIMB Doctoral Network
Description:
The CLIMB Doctoral Network is a consortium of leading European research institutions aimed at training the next generation of researchers in the physics and chemistry of complex lipid-based systems.  

The objectives of the CLIMB core research project are to investigate how lipid composition governs membrane structure, molecular interactions, and collective behaviours. Through a multidisciplinary approach, the network will design and characterise complex lipid assemblies for advanced applications in mRNA delivery, biosensing, and sustainable formulations.

This project is funded by the European Union’s Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA), Grant Agreement No. 101169269.

ARGE çalışmalarına aralıksız devam etmekte üretim ve kaliteyi her gün artırma yolundadır. 

Genç, dinamik, çalışkan, deneyimli, yenilikçi, samimi, dürüst ve sevilen satış ekibiyle kısa zamanda beklentilerin üzerinde emin adımlarla adını duyurmaya başlamıştır. Temsil ettiği tüm firmalarla olan iyi ilişkileri sayesinde ürün gamını en hızlı ve başarılı şekilde geliştirerek piyasadaki yerini sağlamlaştırarak yeni sektörlerde de büyümek amacındadır. 

Müşteri için katma değer yaratmak, teknik destek sağlamak, uzun vadeli iş ilişkileri kurmak Nanokar ailesindeki her ferdin hedefidir. Bu doğrultuda her geçen gün yeni müşteri, yeni ürün, yeni çalışan üçgenini kuvvetlendirerek köklü bir şirket olma yolunda ilerleyecektir. 

Müşterilerinin sağlığına, konforuna yönelik çalışmaları kazancından önce düşünmektedir. Alanında uzman çalışmalarda bulunan firmalarında bayiliklerini de üstlenen NANOKAR, ARGE çalışmaları ve Nano teknolojik ürünler ile sizlerin hizmetindedir.

We use this pioneering techniques to investigate the optical and electronic properties of novel nanomaterials, namely III-V and II-VI nanowires and topological quantum materials, with the aim of harnessing their potential for development of terahertz devices.

Name: NANOTEL
Description:
NANOTEL is a company that produces electrospun nanofibers from synthetic and natural polymers. It was established as a start-up at Istanbul Technical University Techno Park in 2014 by Prof. Dr. Filiz Altay.
NANOTEL had a grant in 2015 from TUBITAK (The Scientific and Technological Research Council of Turkiye) for a 1512-Entrepreneurship Support Project (no: 2140151) called “Commercial Production of Nanofibers used in Foods and Pharmaceuticals.” In this project, NANOTEL manufactured electrospinning equipment producing nanofibers at various scales.
NANOTEL had a TUBITAK project (no: 1150062) called “Reducing fat content in oil-in-water emulsions by addition of electrospun nanofibers” during 2015-2017. In this project, the stability of fat-reduced food emulsions increased by addition of nanofibers.
NANOTEL had another TUBITAK project (no:1150783) called “Implants from or covered with drug-loaded nanofibers” in 2017. In this project, the targeted delivery of drug-loaded nanofibers has been studied.
NANOTEL was sponsor for the “International Symposium on Food Rheology and Texture” organized at ITU in 2018.
NANOTEL has conducted special projects for industry on the utilization of nanofibers as a functional packaging layer and decreasing the amount of raw materials by addition of nanofibers.
NANOTEL is the first Turkish company that produces nanofiber cell and tissue cultures commercially.
NANOTEL offers consultancy to companies on nanotechnology applications and manufactures nanofiber cell/tissue cultures, nafiber encapsulated bioactive materials, nanofiber additives for various applications and carbon nanofibers. Customized nanofibers can be manufactured as needed. For more information send your inquiry to lokumcu@itu.edu.tr

Name: Twisted Nano
Description:
Drug discovery demands reliable, ultrasensitive and fast techniques to test small drug volumes. The efficacy and biotoxicity of drugs is heavily affected by their chiral configuration. In turn, pharmaceutical companies routinely encounter a looking-glass world of mirror-image molecules – enantiomers - where chiral sensing of small drug volumes plays a pivotal role for drug discovery and nanomedicine. 

TwistedNano addresses this need with a new generation of integrated nanophotonic devices enabling the characterization of small quantities of chiral drugs with volumes of a few nanolitres through light, revolutionizing at source the technological toolbox for drug discovery and nanomedicine.

This project has received funding from the European Union under grant agreement No 101046424. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or the European Innovation Council. Neither the European Union nor the European Innovation Council can be held responsible for them.

Description: NanoBloc’s aim is to provide reliable Antimicrobial, Antiviral, and Antifungal Nanocoatings, by sustainable synthesis methods, suitable for application on all surfaces and textiles. Description:

- innovative Thin Film deposition technologies and processes;
- nanostructured Thin Film methods and diagnostics;
- organic/inorganic spintronics.

Os has developed a unique Thin Film fabrication technology, i.e. Pulsed Plasma Deposition (PPD), an innovative and low cost technology for the fabrication of inorganic Thin Films for optical, mechanical and electronic applications that exploits the benefits offered by organic semiconductors.

Having developed a novel approach to the use of organic semiconductors by achieving room temperature operation, OS is on the forefront of spintronics-based innovation for sensing applications.

The name of the company reflects its origin since the PPD technology was instrumental in making colossal magnetoresistence thin films for spintronics. Since then, the PPD technology has become the major commercial asset of OS.

Name: Plasma-Tailored Nanomaterials (PLaTaN)

Description:
The Nano-Imaging and Spectroscopy Laboratory (NISL) at Ontario Tech University is a cutting-edge interdisciplinary research group focused on advancing fundamental and applied science at the nanoscale. Led by Dr. Nisha Agarwal, NISL integrates expertise from materials chemistry, physics, biophysics, and spectroscopy to explore the interactions of light and matter in complex systems.

Our research spans a broad spectrum—from plasmonic nanostructures and surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) to radiation-sensitive biosensors, laser-deposited materials, and vibrational spectroscopy for medical diagnostics. We are committed to innovation at the intersection of nanotechnology, photonics, and life sciences.

At NISL, we combine state-of-the-art instrumentation with hands-on training for undergraduate and graduate students, fostering the next generation of interdisciplinary researchers. We welcome collaborations and new team members passionate about solving real-world challenges using nanoscience.

 Key Areas:
• Nano- and bio-materials characterization
• Surface-enhanced spectroscopy (SERS, SEF)
• Radiation effects on biomolecules
• Optical and energy sensor development
• Medical and environmental diagnostics

Name: BlackHole Lab
Description:
BlackHole Lab presentation:

BlackHole Lab has been created to provide turnkey soft lithography stations to research laboratories. In 2008, during their Phd in microfluidics, BlackHole Lab founders saw the need for numbers of laboratories to have their own microfabrication facilities in order to make their own chips quickly and easily. They developed in 2009 their first generation of softlithography station for researches institutes in Paris and Philadelphia. After several years of improvement, Black Hole lab now provide a full range of turnkey soft lithography station all over the world.

Usually microfluidic chips are made in a clean room but the purchase and maintenance of one is really expensive. So the tricky idea was to propose a set of equipment usable out of a clean room and with the same efficiency. BlackHole Lab offer well-thinking set which is working together to give the best answer for microfluidic fabrication. But it’s not limited to it and BlackHole Lab is still improving or adapting the offers to keep at the cutting edge of the technology. It has been done the choice to gather numerous PhD inside the company to be as close as possible of the researchers, like that the need is really understood and a real exchange on a wide range of subjects is possible. Finally, the BlackHole Lab dynamism enables to be really responsive and flexible to any researcher’s requests.

In one sentence, the aim of BlackHole Lab is to open the microfabrication and so the microfluidic to anyone.

Name: Challenges project
Description:
The project CHALLENGES – Real-time nano-CHAracterization reLatEd techNoloGiEeS – aims to develop innovative Non-Destructive Techniques (NDTs) for reliable inline multiscale measurements down to the nanoscale, and fully compatible with different factory environments. The developed metrology technologies will enable the increase of speed, resolution, sensitivity, spectral range and compatibility within different nano-related production environments, finally improving products performance, quality and reliability, with the consequent bosting of competitiveness. The CHALLENGES’s innovation will be developed exploiting the plasmonic enhancement of optical signals. It will provide a non-destructive approach based on the use of multipurpose nano-optical techniques to enable a reliable real-time nano-scale characterization in the factory floor, using plasmonic enhanced Raman, InfraRed (IR) and Photoluminescence signals.

This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 861857

By leveraging our world-class infrastructure and local and international ecosystem of innovation labs across a multitude of industries, we create groundbreaking innovation in application domains such as healthcare, smart cities and mobility, logistics, and industrial testing and monitoring. RG brings together close to 40 brilliant minds from over 10 nationalities, and is headquartered in Singapore. RG has distributed R&D groups at a number of universities and private-labs, in Germany, Israel, Singapore, and has offices in Singapore and Germany.

Ram Group operates multiple independent R&D and micro-nano-fabrication facilities in Germany and Israel. RG's primary state-of-the-are innovation labs and CMOS fab is located in Zweibrucken, Germany where the Company develops bio-sensor technologies, wireless autonomous sensor technologies and wearable electronics in an open innovation setting. A key feature of RG’s Innovation Center is its development model structured around industry and academia in which RG fills a critical gap between both, and facilitates accelerating commercialization of products into the market. This is a key component to RG’s technology being years ahead of its competitors. It is this kind of cross-fertilization that enables Ram Group to tune its scientific strategy to industrial needs and solve tomorrow’s consumer needs in an agile manner shifting from volume to value based products.

Name: RUSALOX
Description:
RUSALOX is a high-tech developer and manufacturer of high thermal conductive PCB and substrates. Manufacturing process is based on unique aluminum oxide (al oxide) technology, which provides high cooling efficiency of any heat-dissipating electronic components. RUSALOX al oxide PCB and substrates have performance comparable to standard ceramics (Al2O3/AlN) with mechanical features of IMS (Insulated metal substrate) materials at a very competitive price to the alternatives.

Considering characteristics of multi-functional electronics products, various electronic components shalle be mounted on a single substrate. Recently, a heat dissipating problem caused by heat generating elements mounted on the substrate has been an issue. The unique heat-radiating substrate including an insulating layer formed on a metal core layer and a circuit layer formed on the insulating layer have been commercialized and patented up to now by RUSALOX LLC. The heat-radiating substrate has excellent heat-radiating property as compared to a general organic PCB; moreover, it is possible to implement high density/integration of the heat-radiating substrate.

In addition, when heat generating elements such as LED and electronic components vulnerable to heat are simultaneously mounted on a single substrate, the al oxide heat-radiating substrate has high integral thermal conductivity (120 W/mK), such that heat generated from the heat generating elements is transferred to the entire heat-radiating substrate (in particular, through a metal core layer) and is also transferred to an area where heat is not transferred, thereby degrading the performance of the thermally weakened elements.

Applications
- General LED lighting
- WireBond Chip-On-Board (COB)
- Chip-On-Board
- Flip Chip
- Horticultural LED Lighting
- Automobile LED lighting
- Power Electronics
- Power modules (substrates for IPMs, MOSFETs, IGBT)
- High-frequency Industry

More information is published on our website http://rusalox.ru/en/product-category/pcbs/

Name: Light-G-Comp

The project addresses the increasing demand for sustainable and lightweight composite structures that enhance fuel efficiency and reduce environmental impact through energy-efficient and circular manufacturing technologies.

The core aim of Light-G-Comp is to transform conventional and non-recyclable waste materials into graphene-based structures. These are further used to develop recyclable, high-performance thermoplastic composites with tailored, multi-scale reinforcement architectures. The project promotes circularity by increasing the bio-based content of composites and providing lightweight, cost-effective, and functional material solutions for diverse applications.

In particular, the consortium contributes to waste reduction efforts in the UAE by upcycling thermoset and thermoplastic waste streams into value-added graphene-based compounds. Light-G-Comp targets critical industrial sectors such as aerospace, automotive, and plastics.

Name: DISRUPT Project
Description: DISRUPT aims at revolutionising the field of biomedical imaging by developing a radically new lab-a-on-chip technology: integrated tomographic microscopy. This unprecedented technique will be enabled by pushing forward the science of on-chip wireless photonics and tomography, in combination with microfluidics and artificial intelligence (AI). The CMOS compatibility of this technology will represent a paradigm shift as it assures the realization of tomographic microscopes that are dramatically cheaper, lighter, and smaller than current approaches. Moreover, the singular features of the proposed solution will introduce key advantages in terms of resolution, sensitivity, throughput, parallelisation, and energy efficiency. To illustrate its potential, we will show that on-chip tomographic phase microscopy (TPM) can be used for cancer detection and the identification of infected cells. This novel device will be suited for many applications, such as early cancer diagnosis, cell characterisation, research on cancer and infectious diseases, immunocyte phenotyping, stem cell multipotency identification, tissue pathology, haematopathology, and analysis of infected cells. Its intrinsic mass-producible, compact, low-cost, mechanically robust, and energy-efficient feature will make this technology a future innovation driver for new developments in many biomedical application fields, and offers an alternative toolset addressing some of the emerging needs of microscopic analysis and diagnostics in low-resource settings, telemedicine applications and point-of-care, having a potentially huge societal impact fostering early diagnosis of cancer and other diseases and infections.

Name: Carbon Continuum
Description:
We make carbon nanotubes from industrial carbon emissions. Yup, the kind of carbon emissions spewing from factories that's linked to global warming, or more accurately, climate change. Carbon nanotubes (CNTs) are the material of the future. Similar to carbon microfiber, this material is orders of magnitude stronger and more light-weight than aluminum. Applications for such properties are abundant and limitless; for example, sporting goods, construction, electronics and all modes of transportation, particularly for space travel. Since we are using waste carbon emissions (mainly methane, which is 20x more potent in global warming effects than CO2), we can offer low-cost CNTs, enabling the use of this material on a commercial scale. 

Our technology work likes this: First, we analyze that composition of the waste gas, and then affix a customized reactor to convert the gas into nanomaterials. Our reactors are uniquely designed to facilitate a chemical reaction promoting the growth of CNTs and other nanomaterials. 

Currently, we are honing in on methane as the primary source to make multi-walled CNTs and sending samples for quality analysis and application R&D. We are aiming to secure funding to build a larger reactor to increase production capacity.

Description:
The creation of Lipid Nanotechnologies, LLC is based upon the research of Dr. Steve Lenhert, Assistant Professor, Department of Biology & Integrative Nanoscience Institute at Florida State University. Dr. Lenhert leveraged the talent of his students, who created a business plan for his invention, which won first place in the 2012 Florida State University, Jim Morgan Institute for Global Entrepreneurship & Florida State University College of Business. Dr. Steve Lenhert’s students not only beat all of the other competitors at the Florida State University competition, but also created a new entity that may revolutionize drug discovery.

Lipid Nanotech, LLC, has developed a revolutionary and novel method to increase the efficiencies of drug discovery by further miniaturizing the process of high-throughput screening through their patented Liposome Microarray Technology or LiMiT.  High-Throughput Screening (HTS) involves the miniaturization and
automation of bioassays so that millions of variable drug compounds can be tested in a short amount of time, which is one of the most critical steps in the discovery of new medicines and chemical entities. According to Global Industry Analysts, Inc., the HTS market is expected to reach $19BB by 2017.

LiMiT will have three prospective applications that will increase the efficiencies associated with drug discovery via high throughput screening. LiMiT has applicability to research conducted in academia, pharmaceutical/ biotech labs, and government research. Based upon initial feedback, it is perceived that LiMiT will have the following future applications: Gene Therapy, Personalized Medicine and Molecular Diagnostics.

Name: European FIB Network
Description:
We are a collaborative network dedicated to advancing Focused Ion Beam (FIB) processing and related technologies. Our mission is twofold: to enhance existing concepts, technologies, and partnerships, and to foster new, interdisciplinary approaches and collaborations across Europe and beyond.

The network’s origins trace back to 2007, when the “D-A-CH FIB Arbeitskreis” (D for Germany, A for Austria, CH for Switzerland) held its inaugural meeting in Graz, Austria. Since then, annual workshops in Germany, Switzerland, and Austria have steadily grown in participation and garnered interest from other European countries. In response to the increasing international involvement, the Steering Committee has rebranded the group as the “European Focused Ion Beam Network” (Eu-F-N). From 2021 to 2024, the Eu-F-N was in close partnership with the FIT4NANO COST Action (links to https://www.fit4nano.eu and https://www.cost.eu/actions/ca19140), during which two joint workshops were organized in 2021 and 2024. Moving forward, it will be designated as EU-FIB.net to better reflect its expanded scope and ongoing global engagement.

Name: Keshe Foundation
Description:
O que é plasma?

Todo objeto físico (elétron, átomo, grão de areia, planta, animal, humano, planeta, estrela, galáxia, universo etc.) nada mais é do que um acúmulo de campos magnéticos aos quais Keshe se refere como campos magnéticos plasmáticos . Esses campos magnéticos plasmáticos consistem em uma enorme quantidade de campos magnéticos individuais. Os campos magnéticos plasmáticos são tridimensionais, cujo movimento e área são determinados pelos campos magnéticos individuais dentro do objeto. Cada um desses objetos plasmáticos é referido como plasma e é definido pelo conteúdo do campo dentro dele.
A Fundação Keshe não usa a palavra plasma da mesma maneira que é usada na física padrão. A Fundação não se refere ao estado de um gás ionizado quando diz "plasma". O plasma é definido pela Fundação como todo o conteúdo dos campos que acumulam e criam um objeto. NÃO é definido por suas características físicas como ionização e temperatura, por exemplo. O plasma refere-se exclusivamente às propriedades dos campos nos quais um objeto consiste.
Na maioria dos casos, os campos se estendem muito além dos limites físicos do objeto em questão. Assim, existem composições de campo (plasma s ) que já está separado da sua fonte e física estão movendo-se em uma forma não-física (como um corpo puro campo) através do espaço para outro objecto físico (ou não físico). O movimento dessas conglomerações de campos é considerado um tipo de "fluxo". A Fundação, portanto, define energia como campos que se movem e fluem no espaço do ponto A ao ponto B.


O que a maioria dos Cientistas estão buscando hoje?

O Tele transporte??? 

Sim!.. Eles buscam realizar o Teletransporte e com a Tecnologia de Plasma isto esta sendo possível.

Tecnologia de Plasma Keshe 
A Tecnologia da Nova Era.
Uma Nova Forma de se Viver se Inicia

Que a Paz e o Amor Esteja em  Nossos Corações.

Description:
IEEE Nanotechnology Council is a platform that serves as a connection point between members involved in the field of nanotechnology. This forum serves as an enabler for many scientists and engineers and encourages closer cooperation.  All IEEE members are entitled to IEEE NTC membership.  
 


IEEE Nanotechnology Council Hyderabad Section Chapter is an entity within the scope of IEEE NTC and IEEE Hyderabad Section which serves a linkage of student members, graduate members, young professionals, senior members, LSM, Fellow IEEE working in the broad fields of nanotechnology to come together to interact, share technical knowledge and career-related activities .

The IEEE Hyderabad Section is one of the most active sections of IEEE in India. IEEE was formally introduced in Hyderabad, in June 1981, when a group of engineers in Hyderabad decided to form an IEEE sub-section here. This sub-section was rapidly elevated to a full section, in 1984 (the year of IEEE Centenary celebrations). All IEEE members residing in the states of Telangana, India and Andhra Pradesh, India are automatically members of the IEEE Hyderabad Section.

For more information about each group:
IEEE Nanotechnology Council (https://ieeenano.org/)
IEEE Region 10 (https://ieeer10.org/)
IEEE Hyderabad Section (https://ieeehyderabad.org/)

Name: Jikan nano structured paint and coating
Description:
In 2020 researchers at SNE research center were succeeded to formulate a superhydrophobic paint for the first time in the Asia. In the past, different superhydrophobic coatings were developed at the center, but formulating a superhydrophobic paint is way more difficult due to the following reasons. A coating is usually formulated to remain for a couple of weeks on the surface, whereas a paint should properly adhere to its substrate and last for at least a couple of years. Also, despite coatings, paints come in different colors.
In late 2020, Jikan nanostructured Paint and Coating Co. was founded to commercialize the superhydrophobic paints under Jicolour trademark. This company is one of the few companies in the world producing superhydrophobic paints.

Description:
Olimpíada Nacional de Nanotecnologia (ONANO)
 Brasil |  Educação Científica |  Nanotecnologia |  Inovação

A ONANO é a primeira olimpíada científica brasileira dedicada exclusivamente à nanotecnologia, com o objetivo de popularizar o conhecimento sobre nanociência e suas aplicações no cotidiano. Voltada a estudantes do ensino fundamental, médio e técnico de escolas públicas e privadas, a ONANO é um evento gratuito, 100% digital e inclusivo, que integra ensino, divulgação científica e inovação educacional.

Nossa missão é democratizar o acesso à nanotecnologia e formar uma nova geração de jovens críticos, criativos e preparados para os desafios da ciência e tecnologia no século XXI. Através de uma trilha de aprendizagem acessível, oferecemos materiais didáticos adaptados aos diferentes níveis de ensino — como os NanoBOOKS, videoaulas, jogos de tabuleiro educativos, simulados e aulas interativas — permitindo que estudantes participem da olimpíada mesmo sem conhecimento prévio sobre o tema.

Além da competição, a ONANO promove lives com especialistas da indústria, pesquisadores e pós-graduandos, forma educadores por meio de cursos de capacitação e estimula o uso de metodologias ativas com foco no aprendizado significativo.

Name: Harvest Nano
Description:
Harvest Nano Inc: textile-to-cellulose/nanocellulose IP. 
Harvest Nano Inc is an inventor-led company developing textile recycling → cellulose/nanocellulose materials and systems. Our work supports domestic circular manufacturing and high-value reuse of textile waste.
In 2023, Harvest Nano licensed its Textile waste IP/technology to Goodwill Industries of West Michigan to build a textile circularity system, converting unsellable cotton textiles into higher-value cellulose and nanocellulose products. 
In October 2024, Goodwill publicly announced our joint effort to create and operate Michigan’s first cotton waste textile circularity operative processing and recycling plant, turning non-saleable textile waste into cellulosic raw materials and nanocellulose. 
That milestone matters because the world is producing fiber at record levels, 124 million tons in 2023, while true textile-to-textile recycling remains a small fraction of the system. 
We are also grateful to Grand Haven Community Area for a grant. A $250,000 Community Impact Grant supported Goodwill’s “Cellulose Creation from Waste Textiles” project, specifically noting the collaboration with Harvest Nano. 

https://wghn.com/2024/12/18/8022251/

Name: مجمع اقتصاد فناوری نانو | Nanotechnology Economy Forum
Description:
در مهر ماه سال ۱۳۹۱، نخستین مجمع بینالمللی اقتصاد نانو توسط ستاد ویژه توسعه فناوری نانو برگزار شد. هدف از برگزاری این مجمع، آشنایی با آخرین تحولات اقتصادی و مدیریتی در حوزه فناوری نانو بوده است و در طی سه دوره برگزاری آن، آخرین تجربیات و روندهای اقتصادی و مدیریتی فناوری نانو در سطح ایران و جهان در این مجمع بررسی گردیده است.
ستاد نانو با استفاده از تجربیات سه دوره برگزاری این مجمع، به یک بازنگری اساسی در ساختار، فلسفه و محتوای این مجمع پرداخته است. طی جلسات کارشناسی با صاحبان صنایع، فناوران، صاحبنظران و اساتید دانشگاه، برنامهریزی نوین و اثربخشی برای چهارمین مجمع اقتصاد نانو که با محوریت «نوآوری باز: انتقال دستاوردهای فناورانه از شرکتهای کوچک به صنایع بزرگ» برگزار خواهد شد، طراحی شده است.
مخاطبان اصلی این رویداد، عرضهکنندگان فناوری (SMEهای تولیدی نانویی)، شرکتهای متقاضی فناوری (صنایع)  و واسطین انتقال فناوری میباشند. همچنین این مجمع میتواند برای اساتید، دانشجویان و کلیه افرادی که در حوزههای مرتبط با فناوریهای نوین و کاربرد آنها در صنعت علاقمند هستند نیز مفید و جذاب باشد.
برای کسب اطلاعات بیشتر به لینک ذیل مراجعه نمایید.
http://nanoecoforum.ir

Name: LIFNano Therapeutics
Description:
It is known that LIF (Leukaemia Inhibitory Factor) is a protein that can enable the repair of damaged neurons, and has promised the possibility of treatment for Multiple Sclerosis and other neurodegenerative diseases. The challenge has always been to find a way to deliver LIF to the precise location of the damaged neurons, and this is the basis of the LIFNano Rx breakthrough. Our research has developed a method of packaging a measured amount of LIF into tiny particles that can be delivered into the bloodstream (via injection or nasal spray), where they will circulate until they reach the blood-brain barrier. A unique antibody coating allows them to pass through this barrier exclusively and, once assembled at the treatment site, the completely inert particles will disintegrate, leaving only the LIF molecules and a small amount of water and carbon dioxide.

The LIFNano Rx system is a very elegant solution to several problems: it enables precise targeting of the treatment area – a more focused and economical approach; and it delivers the treatment in a completely clean, contaminant-free, easily reproducible molecular formula, eliminating the need for expensive and difficult cell-based treatments.

Grazie all’impiego del processo di elettrofilatura, realizziamo materiali nanostrutturati costituiti da substrati  e nanofibre di polimeri con ampie possibilità di applicazione. Ci rivolgiamo quindi a tutte le aziende che hanno compreso l’importanza di dare spazio a materiali d’avanguardia.

Utilizzando l’Electrospinning abbiamo realizzato qualcosa che prima non c’era: Neatess, la membrana filtrante su base di cotone che coniuga le caratteristiche naturali di questo materiale a un’eccellente efficienza filtrante e a un’elevata traspirabilità. 

Una soluzione evoluta e di alta qualità, con potenzialità applicative estremamente ampie, rispettosa dell’ambiente e della persona, nata per contribuire a un futuro più salubre e più sostenibile.

Name: Cornell Nanoscale Science & Technology Facility
Description:
The Cornell NanoScale Science & Technology Facility (CNF) is a national user facility that supports a broad range of nanoscale science and technology projects by providing state-of-the-art resources coupled with expert staff support. CNF has been serving the science and engineering community since 1977 -- 2017 marked our 40th anniversary! Research at CNF encompasses physical sciences, engineering, and life sciences, and has a strong inter-disciplinary emphasis. Over 800 users per year (50% of whom come from outside Cornell) use the fabrication, synthesis, computation, characterization, and integration resources of CNF to build structures, devices, and systems from atomic to complex length-scales.
The research accomplishments (under "Publications" above) will give you a short introduction to the research, "Lab Equipment" offers more details on our facility capabilities, and our short course (under "Events & Seminars") gives a practical introduction to the technology. Other links and our search engine below will lead you to a variety of information related to nanoscale science and technology. Click on the "Getting Started" tab above to find out what the CNF can do for you!

New users come to us from academia, industry, and government laboratories and learn to use the tools available in the facility to carry out their research projects. CNF is open 24 hours a day, and provides the interactive and exciting learning and practicing environment critical to successful cutting-edge research. External users typically spend a week or two at CNF to complete their work with strong staff support. Many projects can also be accomplished remotely.

A video tour of the facility is available for viewing over the web. This ten-minute video will give you a brief introduction to what types of equipment and processes are available at the facility.

Name: B+H Solutions GmbH
Description:
The company develops metal-based nanofertilizers which possess trace elements, such as copper (Cu), silver (Ag), iron (Fe), boron (B), calcium (Ca) and magnesium (Mg) with nanotechnology.

These products are being used to provide the necessary amounts of trace elements to the plants, which, thanks to their physical and chemical properties, are more bioavailable.

The key is the higher surface area of the materials within our fertilizers that provides more reaction site among the molecules, which facilitates the metabolic reactions in the plant system.

The reduced size of the particles allows more opportunity of contact per unit area, which leads to more penetration into the plant and uptake of nutrients.

This technology allows the use of very small amounts of fertilizer material to cover a large area, which helps saving resources and have less environmental impact. Our concept provides perfect tools for sustainable agriculture.

Name: Luminesys
Description:
Our goal is that Luminesys contributes to the detection of pathologies earlier, faster and at lower cost than before.

Luminesys IPD Increased Photon Density coatings increase the light perceived and emitted by target molecules by a factor of 3 to 25 times.
The photonic, nanostructured coatings are applied to the surface of glass slides or other supports for use with microscopes, microarray scanners and whole-slide imagers.
The coatings precisely control the increase in photon density, making the detection of molecules much more sensitive.
Our proprietary IPD technology was developed at EPFL, Switzerland to enable new generations of analyses for the detection of molecules at lower concentrations.

More light and more sensitivity for your molecular analyses

Name: Joel Yang's NanoLab
Description:
Our group is interested in the science and engineering of making small things and in using them to control light. How small? Between roughly 1 nanometer to several micrometers. Materials at these length scales interact with light in remarkable ways, not seen in the bulk. For instance, polished aluminum and silver are well known to be reflective metals in the bulk. But tiny little particles of the same materials start appearing colorful, with colors that depend sensitively on their actual size, shape, and arrangements. The result is a unique approach to produce colorful prints at unprecedented levels of detail. At 100,000 dots per inch (dpi), this method is a thousand times higher resolution than a desktop color printed. Importantly, the ability to generate colors on demand provides an appealing alternative to pigments and dyes that might be less sustainable.

Through advanced techniques in nanotechnology, we make nanostructures out of a broad range of materials. These materials range from metals such as gold, silver, and aluminum to transparent polymers, 2D materials and semiconductors like silicon. We have probed them with electron beams, shot pulses of energetic photons at them, and performed numerical simulations to investigate their behavior. The fascinating findings are reported in our research publications.

We believe in making the world a better place through our innovations in the field of nanotechnology. Utilizing the craft of miniaturization, we strive to make devices smaller, better, and faster.

Name: PHASTRAC
Description: PHASTRAC is a Horizon EU project. PHASTRAC aims to develop a novel analog-to-information neuromorphic computing paradigm based on oscillatory neural networks (ONNs). We offer a first-of-its-kind and novel analog ONN computing architecture to seamlessly interface with sensors and process their analog data without any analog-to-digital conversion. ONNs are a biologically inspired neuromorphic computing architecture, where neuron oscillatory behavior will be developed by innovative phase change VO2 material coupled with synapses developed by bilayer Mo/HfO2 RRAM devices. PHASTRAC will address the most critical issues, namely 1) novel devices for implementing ONN architecture, 2) novel ONN architecture to allow analog sensor data processing, and 3) processing the data efficiently to take appropriate action.

Name: ThunderNIL Srl
Description:
ThunderNIL offers an extremely fast version of thermal nanoimprint lithography (NIL), enabling the patterning of 2D and 3D micro and nano-structures on thin films and surfaces for a broad range of applications.

ThunderNIL develops a whole technological solution for nanolithography, based on its Pulsed-NIL technology, from tools, stamps and processes.

Pulsed-NIL enables the patterning of surfaces in a single ultrafast thermomechanical cycle of a few tens of microseconds, outperforming in speed – by orders of magnitude – the conventional thermal Nanoimprint Lithography (NIL) and offering advantages in several other respects.

Such a disruptive technology also ensures the patterning of sizable surfaces (>100 square centimeters, scalable to larger areas) at extreme resolutions (down to the 10-nm scale), in very short times (< 1 ms, where the only limitation is the speed of the robotic systems handling the substrates or the goods).

Pulsed-NIL approach fits in a large class of applications fields where efficient patterning of surfaces at the micro/nanometer scale is required, such as biotech (microfluidics, point of care devices), optoelectronics (photovoltaics, lighting devices, displays), telecommunication (integrated optics, refractive or diffractive optical elements), micro/nano-mechanical systems, products anti-counterfeiting, surface finishing and surface aesthetics , etc…

La vocation d’EDEN Instruments est de proposer aux académiques, aux industriels, aux plateformes scientifiques une solution technique globale d’imagerie et d’analyse avec une grande compatibilité entre tous les équipements In-Situ/ in-Operando et Ex-Situ utilisés pour la microscopie électronique.

Expertise de l’environnement
Expertise sur matériel Spatial
Technologie des semi-conducteurs et micro fabrication 
Géosciences et Matériaux 
Matériaux du génie Civil 
Matériaux du Patrimoine Culturel
Nanomatériaux

Marché de l’analyse de l’amiante suivant normalisation COFRAC norme NFX 43050
Sciences du vivant

Toutes les études scientifiques et industrielles nécessitent la compréhension de taille, de morphologie, d’orientation cristallographique, associées à la composition chimique qualitative et quantitative d’un échantillon.

Les solutions de nano caractérisation proposées par EDEN Instruments comprennent des équipements pour l’étude des échantillons en micro-analyse MEB EDS, EBSD, WDS, en micro-XRF (ORBIS), pour la microscopie électronique MET/MEB In-Operando, pour la microscopie corrélative (SCLEM), pour la cathodo luminescence, pour des essais électriques/ mécaniques (traction, flexion, compression,) pour du positionnement/ manipulation à l’échelle micro et nano ainsi que pour la préparation des échantillons.

Name: PATROLS
Description:
Exposure to engineered nanomaterials (ENM) poses a risk to human and environmental health. However, at present, the test methods used to assess this risk are inadequate and unrealistic. Current test systems utilise scientific models that lack the detail and complexity of the environment and the human body, and only consider the effects of short-term ENM exposure which does not reflect realistic extended or repeated exposure scenarios. 
The primary aim of PATROLS is therefore to establish and standardise a set of innovative, next generation safety testing tools that more accurately predict adverse effects caused by long-term ENM exposure in humans and the environment. PATROLS will achieve its aim by: 
•	Producing realistic and predictive cultured 3D tissue models of the lung, gastrointestinal tract and liver for ENM safety assessment.
•	Developing innovative methods for safety assessment in ecologically relevant test systems and organisms, selected according to their position in the food chain
•	Characterising ENM under relevant experimental conditions dictated by the advanced human and environmental models developed.
•	Creating robust computational methods for ENM exposure, dose modelling and hazard prediction.
The cutting-edge models, hazard reporter tests and computational methods developed through PATROLS will allow ENM to be categorised based on their human and environmental risk. The improved predictivity of the tools generated will help to minimise uncertainty in ENM safety, thereby reducing the extent of animal testing in current use. Furthermore, the PATROLS toolbox   will provide effective data to support and enhance the current ENM safety evaluation frameworks.

Name: Purdue MTEC
Description:
Research in the MTEC Lab intersects heat transfer, energy conversion, and materials science to enable advances in technologies where energy conversion and thermal transport are key factors in performance. Prof. Marconnet has developed an interdisciplinary research program to evaluate, understand, and control the physical mechanisms governing the multi-functional properties of materials, machines, and systems. Cross-cutting all the specific research directions, work in the MTEC lab focuses on

Design, development, and validation of novel experimental metrology tools for characterizing multi-functional properties of materials across length and temperature scales;
Enhanced understanding and control of fundamental transport and energy conversion mechanisms through multi-scale computational modeling; and
Strategic, physics-based design and development of materials with multi-functional capabilities 
The combined experimental and computational research approach enables the MTEC lab to make contributions across a range of fields including materials discovery, high power dissipation energy storage, mobile and high-powered electronics cooling, and renewable energy and green buildings.

Name: ShimyaTech Ltd

ShimyaTech is a company focused on scientific innovation, providing highly specialised scientific and technical consultancy, as well as educational and training services to businesses and organisations. Built on over 25 years of academic and industrial experience, the company provides specialist nano science related products and services to disciplines such as Chemistry, Biology, Medicine and Pharmacy. The founder has 16 years academic and 15 years of industrial leadership experience at UK and foreign universities and industries and worked as lecturer, innovator and senior research scientist and project manager. His main interests have been on: surface modification, nanomaterials, electrocatalysts, functionalised materials, sensors, sonochemistry and exploiting emergent technologies in the medical device, nanotechnology, modified electrodes and nano electrochemistry, chemical and advanced materials industries. His first successful commercial product was a nanoparticle synthesis in non aqueous media. The invention received extensive socioeconomic impacts including over 25 publications and patent generations and attracting around 1 million grant funding in total for his research.

He has been developing innovation strategies and programs in nanotechnology, nanomaterials, nanocoating and advanced materials.

Name: NanoScience Solutions
Description:
NanoScience Solutions, Inc. (NSS) is a cutting edge company aimed at the development and manufacturing of advanced products for quantitative atomic force microscopy. At present, we offer two families of products used as extensions to the existing AFMs:

Ringing ModeTM attachment adds multidimensional imaging of physical properties of a sample surface. This modality allows users to obtain unique information of a sample surface which cannot be obtained with any of the existing modalities (including other types of microscopy beyond AFM). Eight quantitative channels of physical information can be recorded simultaneously within a single scan.  
      Compared to the rival (sub-resonance tapping) modes, Ringing ModeTM is up to 20x faster, with fewer artifacts, and produces higher lateral resolution.

FT-NanoDMA attachment is a fast dynamical mechanical analyzer (nanoDMA), which allows for simultaneous recording of mechanical dynamical properties at different oscillation frequencies (several oscillation frequencies are sent to the sample simultaneously). This patented technology is based on the Fourier Transformation, which is similar to the applied in Fourier transform nano rheology and FTIR spectroscopy. 
       This approach allows users to measure dynamical mechanical properties of materials (storage, loss moduli, loss tangent, etc.) about 100x faster and allows for more than 100x better spatial resolution compared to the existing nanoindenters. This increase in resolution is comparable with the transfer from optical to electron microscopy when applied to soft materials!

Name: Stat Peel
Description:
Today’s advances in man-made nanomaterials pose new and unprecedented risks to employees along the whole value chain. Airborne, inhalable fiber-shaped nanomaterials like carbon nanotubes (CNTs) and graphene nanoplatelets (GNPs) can pose asbestos-like health risks when inhaled. Today’s available aerosol measuring devices are not capable of discerning CNT from less harmful airborne particles like pollen, dust or carbon black from diesel exhaust. 

Our high-tech but easy to use detection system can monitor an individual’s long-term exposure to nanotubes in workplaces. It allows university labs, R&D departments, start-ups, fiber producers and any company using material containing CNTs to monitor every individual employee.

Name: Project Move2Treat - EU HORIZON EIC 2024 PATHFINDER OPEN
Description:
Move2Treat aims to develop a theory of how we generate movement by investigating neuronal population activity across central brain regions and the spinal cord. This will be achieved through the following:

-Bi-directional soft-fiber platform: A novel technology capable of stimulating and recording multiple signals from both the brain and spinal cord.

-Disruptive science: Developing a new brain–spinal cord interface to explore the associated neuronal circuits.

-The “pacemaker of the nervous system”: The first complete, minimally invasive, wireless, biocompatible, and soft multifunctional fiber-based neural interface that enables real-time, in vivo mapping of brain–spinal cord activity with high spatial and temporal resolution.

Objectives of Move2Treat:

-Investigating how different neuronal circuits in the brain interact with the spinal cord and identifying which spinal networks are involved in the “execution” of brain “commands”.

-Achieving this breakthrough through a high-performance bi-directional interface.

Innovative Approaches:

-Optogenetics: Unlocking the full potential of optogenetics to control neuronal activity.

-Infrared Neural Stimulation (INS): Exploring a genetic-free neuromodulation approach that relies on infrared light absorption to induce neuronal responses, providing a safe and effective method to control neural activity without genetic manipulation or exogenous substances.

Future Impact:

Move2Treat aims to establish a disruptive technological paradigm for the central and peripheral nervous system.

Upon successful in vivo validation, the project will lay the foundation for therapies targeting Parkinson’s disease, tremors, and partial spinal cord injury.
