Its current flagship product is Nanopositioning Systems. Precision applications require the ability to position objects accurately within nanometer resolution or less over a few micron range of travel. Ultra-precise positioners and motion controllers are key components of larger instruments, such as critical-dimension measurement tools, profilometers, scanning probe microscopes, optical microscopes, tools used in manufacturing / testing operations that involve ultra precision machining, inspecting, and testing of parts. Materials scientists, physicists, chemists, biomedical scientists, mechanical and electronic engineers, as well as other researchers who are active at the frontiers of this diverse and multidisciplinary field are invited to take this opportunity to join us at this event to exchange ideas, network with scientists from around the world, and hear the latest about various disciplines in ultrafine grained and nanostructured materials.

Name: NanoSpire Inc.
Description:
NanoSpire’s business and technology focus is the harnessing of high speed liquid cavitation reentrant microjets. NanoSpire’s technology is based on cavitation, a boiling phenomenon that involves a change of phase from the liquid to the gaseous state of a particular substance. A laser, ultrasound or other energy source is used to create small high-energy vapor bubbles through phase transition. The collapse of cavitation bubbles in close proximity to a wall generates supersonic liquid microjets naturally pointed towards a wall or other restriction to the collapse of the bubble.

NanoSpire’s patented methods build on, but go beyond the energetic process seen in nature. The size, strength and direction of cavitation reentrant microjets can be controlled by our patented methods to a very high degree of accuracy. Cavitation microjets can travel at up to Mach 4 and are capable of drilling a hole as small as a few nanometers in a diamond. Multiple controlled bubbles are also possible allowing lines to be machined as well as other form factors and machining applications. Cavitation allows a high aspect ratio machining to be obtained and is very repeatable. The technology can mill materials ranging from as soft as plastic to as hard as diamond. The technology can reach across numerous markets including many applications in the membrane/sensor area, (healthcare, automotive, aerospace, etc.), fuel cells, semiconductors, MEMS/NEMS, biotech, microfluidics, instruments and microsurgery markets, among others.

We supply OEM’S; processors and finishers; own label brands and leading retailers in quantities ranging from bulk IBC’s down to single litres. Textile

Description:
At Solar Ethos, we are pioneering next-generation thin-film solar technology to tackle global warming and the energy crisis, helping the UK to achieve the Net-zero target by 2050. 

Our thin-film solar cell combines cutting-edge perovskite technology with graphene coatings, making it flexible, lightweight, semi-transparent, and highly efficient. The graphene coating with a state-of-the-art barrier property replaces the conventional precious metal electrodes in thin-film solar cells, increasing their lifetime and reducing manufacturing costs. Our graphene-based prototype device has demonstrated a lifetime double that of devices made with precious metal electrodes.

It has great potential for portable and alternative markets such as electric vehicles, building-integrated systems, and flexible applications. More excitingly, our technology can be integrated with silicon solar panels to enhance their sunlight absorption and generate more electricity, making solar energy cheaper and more efficient for customers.

Name: ZnOrgBAT
Description: Efficient and long-duration stationary energy storage plays a key role in the transition towards a carbon-neutral economy. The storage demand is experiencing intensive growth due to the booming increase of EV market. Due to the lack of tailored devices, modified versions of Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) originally developed for mobility applications are extensively used for this purpose. However, these devices are not capable of meeting the desirable requirements of energy density (350 Wh/kg), power density, service life (10 years) and fire safety. Additionally, Li is on the Critical Raw Material (CRM) list, exposed to the supply disturbances and high price volatility. A new generation of Zn-based batteries will offer an optimal alternative. In the project a prototype with an outstanding energy density will be tested in a relevant environment. The early-stage prototype will demonstrate an energy density of 500 Wh/Kg. The market price target is within 30 - 50 €/kWh, which is 5 times lower than commercial LiBs. Technological progress will enable addressing the requirements of community building and the connection of relevant stakeholders from the ESEE RIS region in the battery value chain, building capacity in students and professionals, also targeting potential customers. The main objectives of the project are to create Zn-battery prototypes and to establish a start-up company, taking the demonstrated batteries forward on the market.

Name: Deutsche Plattform NanoBioMedizin (DP-NBM)
Description:
Trotz immenser Fortschritte in der modernen Medizin besteht immer noch großer Bedarf an neuen diagnostischen und therapeutischen Innovationen, um Krankheiten früher und gezielter diagnostizieren sowie besser und kostengünstiger therapieren zu können.

Eine Schlüsseltechnologie auf diesem Weg ist die Nanotechnologie, weil sie einerseits die Instrumente für eine noch detailliertere Analyse und ein daraus resultierendes besseres Verständnis der Krankheitsursachen und -abläufe liefert, und andererseits die Materialien zur Verfügung stellt, Krankheiten gezielter behandeln zu können. 

Megatrends wie Personalisierte oder Regenerative Medizin sind ohne nanotechnologische Lösungen nicht zu realisieren. 

Die Integration der Nanotechnologie in biomedizinische Anwendungen, kurz NanoBioMedizin genannt, erfordert neue Forschungsdisziplin übergreifende Konzepte und Wertschöpfungsketten. Dazu müssen Technologieentwickler aus den Materialwissenschaften, Chemie, Biologie und Physik mit medizinischen Anwendern wie Pharmazeuten, Klinikern und Industrieunternehmen zusammenarbeiten, um am Ende bedarfsgerechte Innovationen für Patienten auf den Markt bringen zu können. Als Rahmen für den erforderlichen Dialog all dieser Akteure ist eine deutsche NanoBioMedizin Plattform gegründet worden. Das Ziel, das mit dieser Plattform erreicht werden soll, ist eine integrierte Forschungsförderung von der Nanomaterialentwicklung bis zur Klinik, mit der das zweifellos vorhandene wissenschaftliche und wirtschaftliche Potenzial in der deutschen NanoBioMedizin durch nachhaltige Forschungsthemen und Strukturen weiterentwickelt werden kann. Es soll eine Spitzenposition Deutschlands in dieser Zukunftstechnologie erreicht und gefestigt werden.

Name: CrystalGrower
Description:
CrystalGrower is a sophisticated software tool designed to enhance your understanding of crystal growth at the molecular scale. Utilising advanced Monte Carlo methodologies, CrystalGrower simulates the growth of any type of crystal, allowing for a comprehensive exploration of crystal habit and surface topography across a broad spectrum of environmental conditions. This software is versatile and can be applied to a wide range of material types, including organic, structural, ionic, and metallic framework crystals.

CrystalGrower is invaluable for sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agriculture, food, energy, technology, manufacturing, and mining. By offering thorough insights into the molecular details of surface topography, accessible through scanning-probe microscopies, our software integrates this data with crystal habit information. This combination delivers a wealth of experimental information, essential for understanding and controlling crystal performance and functionality.

With CrystalGrower, you can extract critical information about your crystal system's behaviour, enabling you to tailor crystallisation conditions for optimal results. Whether you aim to improve drug formulation, enhance agricultural products, develop new food textures, innovate in energy storage materials, advance technological applications, streamline manufacturing processes, or optimise mining operations, CrystalGrower offers the tools you need to achieve your goals.

Name: Noivion Coating
Description:
Noivion designs and produces thin film deposition equipment and components based on a innovative technology: Ion Jet Deposition (IJD). 

The IJD method consists of a pulsed electron source placed in vacuum able to generate repetitively very short and intense electric discharges supported by a gas jet and directed into a solid target. The target is composed by the desired coating material. When a discharge hits the target generates a superficial explosion and consequent emission of very energetic material in the form of a plasma. The plasma condenses on the objects if finds on its path coating the object surface with a film made by the same material of the target.  

IJD shows consistent advantages over conventional techniques such as:
- Conservation of stoichiometry
- High growth rate
- Fine control of thickness and roughness
- Low temperature deposition 
- Compatibility with a large set of materials
- Low cost and robustness
- The IJD technique is protected by international patents.

With IJD technology we create thin layers, nano layers, surface layers, surface treatments, low-temperature coating, multi-material layers, multilayers, hard layers, sliding layers, self-lubricating layers, abrasion-resistant layers, colored layers, corrosion-resistant layers, chemically resistant layers, optical thin layers,  antibacterial layers, thin layer coating equipment, laboratory systems for thin layer coating, industrial systems for thin layer coating. 

Physical Vapor Deposition, PVD coating, PVD technology, PVD processes, PVD systems, Nanolayers, Thin films, Thin film deposition, Nanotechnology, Nanocoating, Multilayer coating, Surface engineering, Wear resistance, 
Corrosion resistance, Hardness enhancement, Friction reduction,

Description:
WNC is a student-organized conference that provides an opportunity for students, employers, and entrepreneurs to learn from one another, strengthening our mutual understanding about how nanotechnology is taught and how it is used in industry, in innovative research, and how it is powering new ventures.
We will educate and inspire our participants with exciting speakers, an informative industry panel discussion, and other interactive activities.

Name: Surflay Nanotec GmbH
Description:
Surflay Nanotec GmbH is an interdisciplinary team of scientists transforming surface modification, characterization, LbL technologies, fluorescent polymers, nanoparticles, micro-particles, and micro-sensors into valuable products assessing pharmaceutical and non-pharmaceutical applications. Dr. Lars Dähne leads Surflay Nanotec since 2008, after the increased success of the development of the Layer-by-Layer encapsulation at the Max Planck Institute of Colloids and Interfaces. Surflay is at the cutting edge of polymer and surface science as well as micro-sensors, helping academic and industrial partners to develop highly valuable technologies and products. 

At Surflay Nanotec GmbH, our focus revolves around three interconnected scientific solutions. Firstly, we specialize in contract research and surface solutions, offering nanoscale surface modification and functionalization, as well as Layer-by-Layer (LbL) coatings. These advanced techniques allow for precise control over surface properties and can be tailored to meet specific requirements. Secondly, we excel in the development of fluorescent polymers, nano- and micro-structures, beads, and microbubbles. These innovative materials find applications in various fields, including imaging, diagnostics, and sensing. Lastly, we are experts in label-free microsensor systems, enabling the real-time monitoring of important parameters such as bioanalysis, pH, temperature, and oxygen levels, among others. 

If you are interested in any of our solutions or services, please do not hesitate to contact us. We offer tailored research and development services, and we are more than happy to provide further information about our products. Additionally, we provide analytical services and offer opportunities for licensing our cutting-edge technology. Furthermore, we are open to engaging in third-party-funded joint development projects to collaborate on innovative solutions in partnership with other organizations.

Description:
Dipole Materials is an electrospinning company, based in Baltimore, MD, that specializes in custom nanofiber manufacturing to meet a range of industry applications, including cell culture, tissue engineering, water filtration, technical textiles like piezoelectric yarns, catalysis and more.

DiPole Materials’ off-the-shelf product suite includes patented BioPaper technology that serves as a 3D nanofiber-based cell scaffold for 3D cell culture and bioprinting. BioPapers allow researchers to use electrospun scaffolds of biological-derived materials with controlled properties, custom formats and easy handling. Our unique process, which challenges the use of synthetic equivalents, provides a natural, 3D environment for exceptional cell growth and viability.  

DiPole Materials also offers a team of experts, each of whom have years of experience in nanofiber manufacturing, to custom manufacture nanofibers to meet industry needs.

Name: SYNERGIE4
Description:
SYNERGIE4 est votre interlocuteur privilégié pour ce qui concerne la microanalyse, la micro tomographie par rayons X, le dépôt de couches minces et également la microscopie électronique.

Toujours à la recherche de la satisfaction de nos clients, nous vous proposons des solutions complètes pour : 
-	des microscopes électroniques (MEB de table, MEB, MEB FIB et MET)
-	des systèmes EDS, WDS, µXRF et EBSD
-	des appareils de micro tomographie
-	des Pico et Nano-Indenteurs pour MEB et MET
-	des micromanipulateurs, micro-injecteurs et stations de tests électriques pour MEB et MET
-	des appareils de découpe et de polissage par faisceau d’ions
-	des appareils de dépôts de couches minces

Name: neoxid group
Description:
Die neoxid group ist der Verbund rechtlich eigenständiger Unternehmen rund um die Forschung und Entwicklung, sowie um die Produktion und den Vertrieb neuer Technologien, die es bisher noch nicht auf dem Markt gibt.

Mit der Gründung der ODB-Tec GmbH & Co. KG vor 15 Jahren als Spin-off der Universität Düsseldorf, begann die Forschung und Entwicklungen neuer Technologien, deren gemeinsamer Nenner die Anwendung nanotechnologischer Verfahren und Werkstoffe ist. Im Laufe der Jahre wurden mehr als 50 Patente aus 8 Patentfamilien angemeldet.

Mit Verlagerung der Forschungs- und Entwicklungstätigkeiten in die neoxid GmbH in 2014 richtete sich die ODB-Tec neu aus: Die Umfirmierung in die neopatents GmbH & Co.KG beschreibt den Fokus auf die Verwaltung und Verwertung der in den Jahren entwickelten Patente. Mit der neo hydrogen sensors GmbH gehen wir noch einen Schritt weiter und fokussieren uns hier auf die Produktion und den Verkauf der langjährig entwickelten Wasserstoffsensoren und -katalysatoren.

Denn neben der Kraft der Sonne steht Wasserstoff im Blickpunkt von Wissenschaft und unseren Entwicklern.  Neben Wasserstoffkatalysatoren, zur Verbrennung von Wasserstoffemissionen, können nach 10jähriger Forschung heute   kostengünstige Wasserstoff-Sensoren mit sehr schneller Reaktionszeit und hoher Empfindlichkeit über die neo hydrogen sensors GmbH erworben werden, die mit ihren Eigenschaften bisher noch nicht auf dem Markt verfügbar sind. 

Unser IT KnowHow zur Organsiation und Digitalisierung mittelständischer Unternehmen möchten wir auch gerne anderen Kleinunternehmen zur Verfügung stellen über die neoprocessing (i.G. ).

Lesen Sie mehr über diese und weitere Produkte und Innovationen auf www.neoxid-group.de

Name: WBG Pilot Line | future power electronics
Description: The WBG Pilot Line is one of the five pilot lines launched under the Chips for Europe Initiative, a key pillar of the European Chips Act. It focuses on Wide Bandgap semiconductors, particularly SiC and GaN, which are used in high-power and high-frequency applications. The project is coordinated by CNR and include 15 RTOs from 7 EU countries. The WBG Pilot Line is supported from the Chips JU and the National authorities of Italy, Sweden, Poland, France, Germany, Austria and Finland.

Name: IR Dynamics
Description: IR Dynamics (IRD), a New Mexico based nanotechnology company, has developed a new energy-saving glass that automatically adjusts to outside conditions to reduce heating and cooling costs for residential and commercial buildings.  Preliminary studies show that IRD’s Delta-T-Glass can reduce energy losses by as much as 32% over dual pane glass. In addition, this adaptive glass will increase occupant comfort, reduce sound penetration, and block 100% of destructive UV rays.

Name: Nanosensors and Nanoenergy Lab
Description:
"Revolutionizing the future of energy harvesting and self-powered devices"

Triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) are transforming everyday motion into electrical energy, enabling self-powered devices to act as a standalone system. By harnessing friction, vibrations, and movement, TENGs power:

Wearable sensors for health and fitness
Environmental sensors for pollution monitoring
Smart home devices for efficient automation
Medical implants for improved healthcare

Name: სახელმწიფო სერვისების განვითარების სააგენტო/Public Service Development Agency
Description:

სსგს-ს ძირითად ფუნქციებს წარმოადგენს სამოქალაქო აქტების რეგისტრაცია და რეგისტრაციის მოწმობების გაცემა,  პირადობის დამადასტურებელი დოკუმენტების გაცემა და მისამართზე რეგისტრაცია/რეგისტრაციიდან მოხსნა, ამომრჩეველთა ერთიანი სიის ფორმირების ხელშეწყობა. დოკუმენტების აპოსტილით დამოწმება და ლეგალიზაცია. სანდო და კვალიფიციური სანდო მომსახურების გაწევა და მასთან დაკავშირებული პროცესების შემდგომი განვითარება.
სსგს უზრუნველყოფს საქართველოს მოქალაქეობის საკითხებზე მოქალაქეთა განცხადებების კანონმდებლობით განსაზვრული პროცედურის წარმოებას, საქართველოს მოქალაქეობის მოპოვებისა და საქართველოს მოქალაქეობის შეწყვეტის საკითხებზე საბოლოო დასკვნის მომზადებასა და საქართველოს პრეზიდენტისთვის წარდგენას. აგრეთვე საქართველოში ემიგრაციის ნებართვის, საქართველოდან გასვლის ვალდებულების გადავადების და  ყველა ტიპის ბინადრობის ნებართვის გაცემის თაობაზე მოქალაქეთა განცხადებებზე კანონმდებლობით განსაზვრული პროცედურის წარმოებას და შესაბამისი გადაწყვეტილებების მიებას.
მიგრაციის სფეროში საქართველოს მთავრობის ერთიანი პოლიტიკის განსაზვრისა და მიგრაციული პროცესების მართვის გაუმჯობესების მიზნით, ფუნქციონირებს მიგრაციის საკითხთა სამთავრობო კომისია (მსსკ/კომისია), რომელსაც  ევროკავშირის ხელშეწყობით ანალიტიკურ და ადმინისტრაციულ მხარდაჭერას უწევს სახელმწიფო სერვისების განვითარების სააგენტოში სტრუქტურულ ერთეულად შექმნილი სამდივნო. სააგენტო ადმინისტრირებას უწევს მიგრაციის მონაცემთა ერთიანი ანალიტიკურ სისტემას.

Name: Societat Catalana de Nanociència i Nanotecnologia
Description:
La Societat Catalana de Nanociència i Nanotecnologia (SCN2) és una societat científica independent sense ànim de lucre reuneix estudiants i personal investigador del camp de la nanociència i la nanotecnologia, promou la recerca i innovació nanotecnològica i contribueix en la seva divulgació i debat. L'SCN2 té un àmbit d'actuació ampli i desenvolupa projectes de formació, educació, divulgació, promoció i connexió actuant a través d'una creixent xarxa de membres voluntaris, explorant la col·laboració amb altres entitats i dinamitzant el sector de la nanociència i la nanotecnologia a Catalunya. 

La transversalitat organitzativa, llibertat d'actuació i joventut donen un caràcter molt dinàmic a l'associació en què qualsevol persona pot proposar, participar o liderar nous projectes que es transformen del paper a la realitat. D'aquesta manera, l'associació ofereix grans oportunitats personals i professionals a estudiants de grau, màster i doctorat per aprendre i explotar noves habilitats comunicatives, socials, organitzatives, de gestió i lideratge.

AFSEM is an atomic force microscope (AFM) by GETec Microscopy, designed for integration in a SEM or Dualbeam (SEM/FIB) microscope. Its open access design allows to simultaneously operate SEM and AFM inside the SEM vacuum chamber. The complimentary image data of AFM and SEM enable unique characterization of your sample.

Name: Nano Optic Devices
Description:
Nano-Optic Devices llc develops innovative optical equipment.

The key technology of NOD is Digital Planar Hologram (DPH), which allows managing and guiding beams of light with any required predesigned characteristics (phase, polarization, direction) using CMOS process.  DPH inside the spectrometer consists of millions of grooves - lines ranging in width from several dozens to several hundred nanometers. Lines are fabricated at the surface of a planar waveguide by means of Electron-Beam or Nano Imprint Lithography. Light propagating in the hologram resonantly interacts with the grooves. Various positions of grooves program Digital Planar Hologram to perform various operations, like the zeros and ones encode a variety of computer programs. In NOD spectrometers-on-chip the DPH serves as a diffractive device performing a spectral decomposition of input light by focusing the different spectral components at the different places along the chip output where the linear detector array is installed.

The first device created by Nano-Optic Devices is Nano Spectrometer which demonstrates unique characteristics: unprecedented resolution, miniature size and robustness.

Name: Spectradyne
Description:
Spectradyne's particle analysis technology is rapidly being adopted as a powerful and significantly more accurate alternative to light scattering-based methods of particle analysis.  The technology is fast and easy to use, and accurately measures the concentration, size and fluorescence of particles from 50 nanometers to 10 microns in diameter in a liquid, using a disposable microfluidic cartridge. 

Spectradyne's patented technology uses Microfluidic Resistive Pulse Sensing (MRPS) to count and size particles one-by-one electrically—not optically.  MRPS is a modern implementation of a technique commonly referred to as Coulter counting that has long served as the gold standard for quantifying larger microparticles and cells.  Spectradyne’s new microfluidic implementation enables unparalleled strengths:  Complex biological samples such as plasma and cell culture media can be analyzed directly and total analysis of a sample requires only 3 microliters, enabling completely new science to be realized.  

Scientists around the world use MRPS in a broad range of life science application areas including extracellular vesicle (EV) research, gene therapy and nanomedicine, virus and phage quantification, formulation development & stability, vaccine development, liposomes, lipid nanoparticles.  MRPS is also used to quantify industrial materials such as polymeric nanoparticles, CMP slurry, paints, inks, foods, floor finish, magnetic beads and coatings, and other inorganic materials. 

Interested in learning how our technology can help your science?  Spectradyne demonstrates the power of its technology to researchers by measuring a few samples for free—visit us at www.nanoparticleanalyzer.com to learn more.

Name: Gesellschaft für Bioanalytik Münster e.V.
Description:
Bioanalytik-muenster is a regional network of
•universities 
•research centres 
•enterprises 
•transfer institutions 
•investors

which have joined forces in this non-profit-making organisation. 

Bioanalytik-muenster (founded in November 2000) provides a central communication and information platform for scientists, entrepreneurs, investors and members of the public who are interested in bioanalytics. 

Purpose

The purpose of the organisation is to promote science, research and education in the field of bioanalytics in the Münster region.

Aim

The aim of the organisation is to develop the Münster region into a leading bioanalytics location with the emphasis on nano-bioanalytics. From February 2004 to September 2008 bioanalytik-muenster coordinated together with the Atomic Energy Commisssion (CEA) in Grenoble the EC Network of Excellence Nano2Life to strengthen the European scientific and economic leadership in nanobiotechnology. By taking part in various EU projects the organisation expands its Network trans-regionally.

Name: Elementag
Description:
Elementag is the simplest, most revolutionary marker that integrates nanoparticles into any material or product. Elementag can seamlessly add a layer of information to a-n-y-t-h-i-n-g, to make it traceable, authentic and to fight against its counterfeiting.

It is invisible, because it’s deep inside the thing. 
It is permanent, because it’s not a removable tag or label.
It is simple, because in the end, it’s the thing itself.

Before Elementag, objects just used to have an owner. 
Now they have an identity too.

Name: 2DProtoPrint
Description: 2DProtoPrint is a dedicated printing and materials research node of Monash University. Leveraging a network of national and international collaborators, 2DProtoPrint. A powerhouse of materials innovation, ink formulation, and advanced printing technologies, bridging the gap between breakthrough research and real-world applications in flexible electronics, energy storage, sensors, and more. With a state-of-the-art facility and a world-class team, we are shaping the future of printable electronics. If you're passionate about disruptive printed technologies, we invite you to connect, collaborate, and innovate with us.

Name: DoMicro BV
Description:
DoMicro BV is a technology company providing innovative manufacturing technology, application solutions and micro assembly technology for flexible hybrid electronics (FHE) and micro devices. DoMicro excells in developing cutting edge inkjet printing processes, micro assembly and 3D packaging technology. 

The company delivers R&D services, small series production, system architecture and project management. Typically for customers exploring new technologies for circuitry on flexible substrates like transparent conductive films, OPV electrodes, OLED, Lab-on-chip, wearables, inmold electronics, IC and MEMS integrations.

•	Integrated processes, technology and materials for Flexible Hybrid Electronics

•	Application development and industrialization services

Name: Nano Physics and Spintronics Group
Description:
The nanophysics and spintronics group (NPSG) was established in 2014 at physics department of Shahid Beheshti University (SBU).
This young group has focused on concepts of science at the nano-scale to introduce them in applied physics. Important subjects could be two-dimensional materials including their fabrication and application in electronics, photonics, mechanics, etc. Other studies are spintronics, magnonics and some biological elements.
The group leader Assist. Prof. Mohseni and his team try to understand and connect different disciplines such as advanced devices and their future use in industry, life and health. These could be from fundamental aspects to engineering point of views. Our team includes postdoc reaserchers, graduate students and collaborates with other groups.

Name: Isolcoat: coatings nanotecnologici ad alta efficienza
Description:
Isolacoat è il partner italiano, tecnico e commerciale per ricoprimenti nanotecnologici. 
- Ricoprimenti isolanti termici, acustici, anticorrosivi, anticondensa liquidi, per ricoprire qualsiasi supporto. Progettati per essere duraturi nel tempo e ad alte prestazioni.
- Ricoprimenti ad alta emissività costante, per alte temperature

 I NOSTRI PRODOTTI
TEMP COAT 101 è un isolante termico basso spessore, fornito allo stato liquido, ideale per applicazioni industriali e navali e nel settore petrolchimico, nelle costruzioni e trasporti, come ad esempio, per la coibentazione tubi, coibentazioni pareti, coibentazioni serbatoi, container, quadri elettrici, ecc...
Un isolante ad alte prestazioni, leggero, flessibile, previene la condensa e la corrosione, resistente all'acqua salina, certificato per uso navale, di veloce applicazione.

SR1000  è un isolante acustico liquido, nanotecnologico, progettato specificatamente per eliminare rumori e vibrazioni, nelle
applicazioni navali, HVAC, elettrodomestici, industriale, trasporti.

Rivestimenti ceramici ad alta emissività, prodotti dall'azienda americana EMISSIHIELD, progettati per il risparmio energetico nei settori siderurgico, vetro, ceramica, petrolio, gas, combustione, generazione energia. Applicati direttamente sulle pareti dove avvengono i processi termici, contribuiscono a risparmi dal 5 al 15%, all'aumento della produttività, riduzione dei GAS SERRA, e una riduzione delle manutenzioni. Ricoprimento eccezionalmente performante per la protezione delle superfici sottoposte ad alte temperature.

SERVIZI
Siamo in grado di assistere, supportati dal know-how dei nostri partners, i nostri clienti a 360 gradi, dalla scelta del prodotto, alla vendita e applicazione tramite personale specializzato.

Name: Advanced Research Systems, Inc.
Description:
ARS is a sole-source manufacturer of cryogenic equipment tailored for scientific research such as quantum optics,  optical spectroscopy, microscopy, neutron scattering, and more. 

Our company specializes in Helium-Free Cryostats, Flow Cryostats, and Probe Stations with temperature ranges of 1.5K to 800K while achieving low vibrations as low as 3-5 nanometers.

We have a passion for understanding a researcher's key needs and delivering a tailored system that will empower them to innovate within their field.

Name: NANOTECH SOLUTIONS
Description: We design, develop, manufacture and commercialise advanced instruments for probing magnetic properties of magnetic nanomaterials, paying especial attention to their interest in energy, security, biomedical and biotechnological applications.

Name: RELIANCE HE Project
Description: RELIANCE is a Research and Innovation Action project funded by the EU that aims to design and develop smart response self-disinfectant antimicrobial nano-coatings based on a new range of smart antimicrobial nanoparticles. These nanoparticles will consist of mesoporous silica nanoparticles with metallic copper in their structure, modified with biobased bioactive compounds, such as Antimicrobial Peptides (AMP’s) based on protein containing waste streams, and Essential Oils (EOs) coming from non-edible plants. The antibacterial action of these additives will be adjusted to the specific application as far as dosages and durability requirements are concerned.

Name: Coxem Co., Ltd
Description: COXEM is a leading global manufacturer and supplier of Scanning Electron Microscopes (SEM), a powerful tool for nanometrology. As part of the nano-tech revolution that is powering development in the 21st century, COXEM provides high-quality SEMs and accessories backed by our factory-trained service engineers.

Name: Nano-Bay
Description:
Nano-Bay was born in 2019 as a spin-off of Oribay Group Automotive, a company with more than 30 years of experience with polymers for Automotive industry applications.

We are strongly committed to the research and development of conductive nanoparticles and inks for multiple industrial applications.

Constantly we collaborate with some of the main European research centres, taking part in multiple R&D projects in the area of nanoparticles.

Name: India Graphene Engineering and Innovation Centre
Description:
India Graphene Engineering & Innovation Centre (iGEIC), is a section 8, not-for-profit graphene translational research and manufacturing company, with registered office in Trivandrum, Kerala, and satellite office for Business Development headquartered in Bangalore, Karnataka, and other cities, as required.

The mission of the company is to nurture emerging graphene technology, revolutionize graphene research, and create a commercialization eco-system for adoption of graphene technologies at scale.

Founded in 2023, India Graphene Engineering & Innovation Centre (iGEIC) aims to develop graphene applications around programs of National significance launched by honourable Prime Minister of India. Applications including conveyance system for water & green energy, barrier coatings, edge devices, supporting government programs like, Jal Jeevan Mission, Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, Self-Reliance in Aerospace & Defence, and Net Zero & Energy Transmission program.

India Graphene Engineering & Innovation Centre (iGEIC), is the delivery partner for Program Graphene Aurora, with Digital University Kerala. Program Graphene Aurora is an approved program under Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology, Government of India (MeitY). The translational research facility is located at Digital University, Kerala.

In collaboration with industry, academia, SMEs, and start-ups, India Graphene Engineering & Innovation Centre will launch targeted translational graphene programs for commercialization. 

Contact us at (alliances@igeic.org)

Name: FunNanoBio
Description:
Research group at Universidade de Vigo, led by Professors Isabel Pastoriza-Santos and Jorge Pérez-Juste, focused on the design and fabrication of novel nanostructured materials for a wide range of applications, including environmental sensing, biosensing, nanomedicine, theranostics, controlled release, and catalysis.

Main research lines:

- Nanoparticle assembly and fabrication of nanostructured materials
- (Bio)sensors, imaging, and diagnostics based on SERS and SPR
- Development of bio-applications for nanomaterials

By utilizing structural mass of an application for energy storage, we can reduce weight, improve energy efficiency, and increase system performance.

Liquidity brings these products to the consumer market, via a B2B model, in conjunction with multinational consumer products OEMs.

Name: TSS Microscopy
Description: Since 2003 TSS Microscopy has been providing ReManufactured electron and ion microscopes across the globe. Our Analytical Lab Service is available for overflow or speciality imaging.   Please visit our website www.TSSMicroscopy.com to learn more.

Name: NANOTEC ELECTRONICA S.L.
Description: Nanotec Electrónica develops Scanning Probe Microscopes (SPM) with the best quality and the latest technology in the field, providing both scientific and industrial communities easy access to the nanometer scale. Nanotec Electrónica also designs and manufactures control systems for Scanning Probe Microscopes. In the software sector, we have developed the free WSxM software for data visualization and processing of SPM images, and distribute the SIESTA DFT software for first principle calculations.

Name: MicroVision Laboratories, Inc.
Description:
MicroVision Laboratories, Inc. has been providing businesses, consultants and other testing laboratories with expert microscopy services since 2003.  Our client base covers a broad spectrum of industries including: semi-conductors, photolithography, aerospace engineering, electronics and micro-electronics, mineralogy, biomedical technologies, ceramics, optics, thin film coatings, plasma etching, failure analysis, filter membrane technologies, industrial hygiene, pharmaceuticals, metallurgy, research and development (R&D), manufacturing, quality control (QC), advertising, environmental/environmental engineering, etc.

Our laboratory offers a highly-trained and experienced staff accompanied by a powerful set of analytical tools (SEM with light element EDS detector, Bruker X-Flash elemental mapping, Robinson Backscatter detector, Digital Imaging, Micro-XRF, PLM, FTIR spectroscopy,  semi-conductor grade Carl Zeiss light microscope, cross sectioning/precision polishing equipment, and wet chemistry).    MicroVision Laboratories is owned and operated by an career Microscopist, John Knowles, who understands both the needs of the client and the needs of the business. Setting us apart from other labs, is our emphasis on providing detailed answers for our clients as quickly as possible. We have the ability to work one on one with our clients in order to provide the highest quality experience available. We have the technology and the expertise to solve a vast array of problems in a manner which is second to none.

Name: SPEC Sensors
Description:
Low profile, high performance, low power, high volume, reliable gas sensors for the Internet of Things.  

SPEC-Sensors was founded to provide the sensors to make gas sensing a part of our everyday lives. Based in California near Silicon Valley, we combine our expertise in reliable and time tested sensing technologies with novel manufacturing techniques to produce sensors that are small enough to go anywhere while maintaining the high performance that is critical to delivering useful information to users. We take pride in both our long history and gas detection and our deep involvement in cutting edge technology.

Name: OkyayTechALD
Description: We develop Atomic Layer Deposition and Electrospinning tools for university researchers customized for your requirements.

Name: nano3Dprint
Description:
Expand Your Field of Innovation. 
3D Print wearables with embedded conductors, cell phone parts with tiny antennas or electronic devices with built-in sensors. nano3Dprint's direct-write technology can incorporate strain gauges, heaters, complex antenna geometries and optical components in fully functional 3D prints. Don't add wires - 3D print them. Our A2200, B3300, and MatDep Pro direct-write systems print a wide range of multi-materials side-by-side, so you can 3D print electronics housings as well as the internal circuitry. We always say, “Don’t add wires, print them!” Embedding the connections between components allows for more conformal features. Discover our direct-write systems today, and transform the way you engineer. 

nano3Dprint is a Carbon Design Innovations, Inc. brand.

Name: Graphene Square Inc.
Description: Graphene Square, Inc. is a pioneer in the commercialization of graphene material and graphene films for use as a transparent conductor and in other electronics applications. Established as a spin-off of the research of Prof. Byung Hee Hong at Seoul National University and with headquarters in Seoul, Korea. Graphene Square will continue to stay as the world leader of the graphene commercialization research, also will continue to provide the best quality graphene and equipment to all over the world for the continuous development of carbon and 2D materials for the bright future of the science & technology for mankind.

Name: Kleindiek Nanotechnik
Description:
Kleindiek Nanotechnik is a young, customer oriented high-tech company. With an innovative and powerful driving concept we are entering new space in micro- and nano-positioning.

The company founder Stephan Kleindiek invented the Nanomotor in 1991 during his PhD thesis at the University of Tuebingen. This motor and subsequent inventions of rotational motors based on the same principle are the foundation of Kleindiek Nanotechnik's products.

Due to miniaturisation in semiconductor technology, optics, micro-mechanics, medicine, gene- and bio-technology, highly precise positioning techniques are becoming increasingly important. Our products meet and exceed customer's requirements, offering them a new level of precision.

Our customer-driven approach is focused on providing complete and innovative solutions for each of our market segments: researchers, industrial customers and enterprises.

Our product development philosophy is the direct solution of the specific underlying problem. The simplicity, homogeneity and harmony of our designs guarantee maximum manoeuvrability and highest resolution while maintaining the smallest outer dimensions.

Name: nPoint, Inc.
Description:
We are a part of Motion Solutions. nPoint designs and manufactures piezo stages, nanopositioners, and nanopositioning systems for nano-scale research and manufacturing.

Our expertise in closed-loop rapid nanopositioners has made nPoint the leading provider to customers that depend on high-speed and high-resolution performance. nPoint's piezo-actuated flexure stages combined with our DSP-based controllers provide the highest level of nanomotion control.

In addition to our extensive product line of piezo stages, nPoint develops custom nanopositioner solutions to meet customers’ unique requirements. From the researcher who needs a single nanopositioner to OEMs that require hundreds of identical units, nPoint has the experience and knowledge to find the right solution. Our goal is to understand your nanopositioning requirements and provide the solution that best fits those needs.

Name: SPECS-TII Inc.
Description: SPECS-TII Inc. is the USA-based subsidiary of SPECS Surface Nano Analysis GmbH, a global market-leading supplier of UHV surface analysis instrumentation.  We are the sole distributor of all SPECSGROUP, UNISOKU (Tokyo Instruments Inc.) SPM, and Nanosurf AFM products in North America, offering state-of-the-art technology in low temperature UHV scanning probe microscopes, photoelectron spectroscopy systems, control systems, atomic force microscopes, and cutting-edge systems and components for surface analysis.  Our US-based personnel provide local sales and service for North American product inquiries, purchase processes, installations, and support.

Name: IQS Group
Description:
IQS Group companies supply the international market with innovative products and technologies in areas from counterfeit protection, nanostructured optics and 3D printing to medical applications.
At the heart of the IQS Group’s international success is a team of engineer scientists and managers with a deep knowledge of the design, implementation, and mass production of nano/micro structured products.

IQS Group benefits from its own intellectual property and know-how in lithography techniques, 3D nano-printing and industrial replication of nanostructured materials.

Name: NanoTherapies Lab
Description:
NanoTherapies Lab is a laboratory situated inside the IQS (Institut Químic de Sarrià) of Universitat Ramon Llull in Barcelona Spain. We conduct research in nanomedicine Engineering for Targeted Interaction with Physiological Barriers. Our main focus areas include development of polymeric nanoparticles, bioengineering extracellular Vesicle-Based Drug Delivery systems, designing mRNA Vaccines and Therapeutics, developing Combination Cancer Immunotherapies and other applications of Nano- and Microencapsulation.
Our group's coordinator is Dra. Cristina Fornaguera Puigvert and consists of more than 20 PhD and Post-Doc researchers.

Name: Soquelec Ltd.
Description: Soquelec is the exclusive Canadian representative of several world renowned Scientific Equipment Manufacturers. For the past 36 years, Soquelec has been supplying the Canadian research and industrial community with state of the art equipment for Materials Sciences, Life Sciences and Telecommunication.

Name: Ionic Industries Ltd.
Description: Ionic Industries Limited (being “Company” or “Ionic”) is Australia’s first company focused on the commercialisation of graphene technologies. After achieving a range of critical milestones over the past five years and a number of recent successes, we are planning for our next stage of growth and progress toward developing real applications for graphene based technologies in the fields of energy storage, nano-filtration and water treatment.

Name: Weber Technologies by Kyocera
Description:
Weber Technologies wurde im Juli 2018 in Potsdam bei Berlin gegründet. Ende März 2019 konnte das Diamant-Beschichtungszentrum in Betrieb genommen werden.

Weber Technologies hat sich mittlerweile auf dem Markt als starker und zuverlässiger Partner im Beschichtungsservice, insbesondere für Diamantbeschichtungen für Hartmetallwerkzeuge und Verschleißteile positioniert.

Wir bei Weber Technologies wollen, dass Ihr Projekt ein Erfolg wird.

Name: ChemReclaim
Description:
WHO ARE WE
ChemReclaim is a nanotechnology startup redefining how high-performance materials are engineered. We specialize in advanced nanopowders and nanocoatings made from precious metals and rare-earth metal oxides for cutting-edge industries.
Founded with a passion for scientific rigor and sustainable innovation, we operate at the intersection of materials science, performance, and circularity.

WHAT DO WE DO
We design and produce customizable nanopowders and nanocoatings with tightly controlled purity, particle size, and surface chemistry. Our materials are built for critical applications in hydrogen technologies, semiconductors, aerospace, catalysis, and advanced ceramics - where consistency, reliability, and functionality are essential.

Our solutions enable up to 5–40x reductions in critical metal usage, without compromising performance. 
This is how we help our partners achieve more - with less.

WHY DO WE DO IT
Strategic and high-tech industries face rising pressure from critical metal scarcity, supply chain volatility, and surging demand fueled by the green transition. Mining alone won’t close the gap and recycling requires time. 
We believe smarter, more efficient use of critical materials is key to building a resilient and sustainable future.

At ChemReclaim, we're committed to enabling innovation without environmental compromise.

Whether you're an advanced manufacturer, researcher, or policymaker, ChemReclaim offers smarter materials, sustainable sourcing, and reliable performance from lab to scale.

We’re not just making powders - we’re reshaping the future of materials.
Let’s connect, collaborate, and scale impact together.

Name: Nanoplexus
Description: Nanoplexus was founded in late 2019 with the aim of delivering scalable and cost-effective material platforms for a more sustainable future. We are driven by a diverse experience in advanced manufacturing techniques and material synthesis that allows us to apply materials into more demanding environments.

Name: CREAVAC PVD AG
Description:
Wir vereinen die Entwicklung und Fertigung von Beschichtungen im Hochvakuum mit dem Bau von Anlagen zur industriellen Beschichtung von Materialien wie Kunststoff, Glas, Wafer, Metallband oder flexiblen Substraten. Je nach Anwendung sind diese Schichten geringe Nanometer bis wenige Mikrometer dünn und verleihen den Materialien neue funktionale und dekorative Eigenschaften. So können unsere weltweiten Kunden hochwertige Produkte, wie Reflektoren für die Automobil- und Lichtindustrie, Kunststoffgehäuse mit EMV-Abschirmung oder Membrane zur sterilen Filtration von Wasser herstellen. Die Produkte unserer Kunden finden Sie fast überall im täglichen Leben. 

Die Creavac Creative Vakuumbeschichtung GmbH wurde 1998 in Dresden gegründet. Seither erweitern wir stetig das Portfolio. Wir entwickeln unsere technologischen Grundlagen und Kompetenzen konsequent weiter, die entscheidend für unseren Unternehmenserfolg sind. Mit unserer Abteilung für Forschung und Entwicklung sind wir wichtiger Bestandteil des Kompetenz- und Forschungsnetzwerks für Dünnschichten in Sachsen. Dieses Know-How fließt in alle unsere Produkte ein und garantiert unseren Kunden zuverlässige Beschichtungen und technologisch ausgereifte Anlagen für ihre Produktion.

Name: Newport Thin Film Laboratory
Description:
Newport Thin Film Laboratory (NTFL) has been proving quality coatings worldwide for 40+ years, helping customers overcome engineering hurdles by designing and manufacturing coatings for commercial, aerospace, military, UV curing, germicidal, semiconductor, medical technology and scientific applications. NTFL produce a wide variety of coatings from the UV through the IR spectrum, most of which are designed and manufactured to specific customer specifications.

Newport Thin Film Laboratory is headquartered in Chino, CA and has large chambers specialized for vacuum deposition, employing electron beam gun PVD and Ion Assisted Deposition. NTFL is a wholly owned and operated US company with all US employees, and works extensively with equipment manufacturers and defense contractors. We are proud to have been awarded various supplier awards over the years. Let our expertise and commitment to customer service contribute to your competitive edge.

For more information please contact us at (909) 591-0276 or sales@newportlab.com

Name: Grand Potentiel
Description:
Le Grand Potentiel est l'association des étudiants du département Génie Physique de l'INSA Toulouse, fondée pour enrichir les relations entre étudiants, enseignants, chercheurs, entreprises, etc. L'association organise des conférences et des visites d'entreprises afin de permettre aux étudiants de développer leur réseau professionnel et d'avoir une vision plus précise de leur future carrière.
Elle sert aussi de lien entre les étudiants et la direction du département afin d'améliorer le cadre d'études des étudiants.
Enfin, l'organisation de repas, d'événements et de soirées sur le thème de la physique permet de promouvoir le département et de rassembler les différentes promotions, anciennes ou nouvelles, autour de la physique.

Name: M3NIR
Description:
M3NIR develops very innovative (currently at TRL2) mid-IR sensing approaches to significantly boost the technology in terms of performance (low limit of detection, multiple-species detection), footprint (co-integrating of
lasers and components), reduction of energy consumption and cost. For the latest two, M3NIR implements detector-free
sensing by means of the self-mixing detection scheme. Moreover, combining mid-IR and near-IR components in photothermal sensing is yet another approach for implementing a miniaturized, energy-efficient, and low-cost advanced sensory system. To accomplish its goals, M3NIR co-integrates advanced electronics and data processing units
in the systems. M3NIR demonstrates its novel approaches at TRL5 for monitoring GHG and ships emission (a drone-mounted sensor to be demonstrated), detecting phosphates and nitrates in the water, and breath analysis for health and well-being-related applications.

Name: ChipUSM
Description:
ChipUSM es una asociación estudiantil de la UTFSM (Chile) dedicada a fomentar el aprendizaje de la microelectrónica y su aplicación práctica en diseño de circuitos integrados, valorando la participación estudiantil.

Nuestra actividad principal consiste en conectar al estudiantado con oportunidades de formación y de trabajo en el ámbito de los circuitos integrados. Además, la asociación genera instancias propias de aprendizaje y provee tanto asistencia técnica como recursos tecnológicos para habilitar la inserción de estudiantes al mundo de la microelectrónica aplicada.

Name: Demcon TSST
Description:
Demcon TSST in Enschede develops and produces deposition equipment for research in the field of materials science. The company specializes in Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) and develops customized solutions for university research centers throughout the world. Aside from PLD equipment, TSST also supplies supplementary modules and components, provides substrate treatment and thin film deposition services, and shares knowledge through means of courses, training and participation in conferences. It is part of the international Demcon group (1,000 employees) as an independent company. 
Demcon develops, produces and supplies technology and innovative products. The Demcon group has multiple locations in the Netherlands, Germany, Japan and Singapore. Whether it concerns medical systems, systems that monitor our security or sustainable innovations relating to themes such as water and energy, Demcon makes a contribution for current and future generations. In addition to developing technical solutions, Demcon also focuses its efforts on promoting entrepreneurship and investing in talent and education.

Name: nFluids Inc.
Description:
Based in Alberta, Canada, nFluids was incorporated in 2012 to solve oil and gas drilling fluid challenges using leading edge nanotechnology.

Today, nFluids Inc. is an innovative nanotechnology company that manufactures cost-effective nanoparticle additives.  The team’s focus forms the basis of four filed patent applications that defines nFluids’ technology platform.  Using similar processes and materials, nFluids plans to use the technology to solve related challenges in other industries.

By using controlled chemical reactions we are able to control the size, shape, functionality, and final concentration of the nanoparticles. This mechanism allows us to offer unique solutions to our clients for high-volume bulk processes or bespoke applications.

nForcer, the first commercial product, is a high-performing, multi-functional drilling fluid additive compatible with all mud types. nForcer is a true platform technology in that this product can be used in applications beyond oil and gas.

Name: XploSafe LLC.
Description: To create safe environments through chemical detection and neutralization. XploSafe is focused on conducting ground breaking research and commercializing pragmatic solutions. Xplosafe is a proud founder of AiroTect and Hydra Water Technologies.

Name: Kremenak NanoTech, Inc.
Description:
Ever since we established Kremenak NanoTech, Inc. in 2018, our deep tech startup has thrived due to our dedication and knowledge in material science. As an example, Shredder, instant zero-energy pasteurization, makes use of advanced material science to control microbes in commercial beverage production.

Our mission is to pioneer elegant innovations that solve complex human and environmental problems.

We envision a world where transformative innovations seamlessly integrate into daily life, converting challenges into opportunities, and enhancing the prosperity of both people and the planet.

At NanoViz, we are redefining innovation and sustainability as a global distributor of high-tech solutions. Specializing in nanotechnology, fusion resonance and advanced technologies, we design and deliver transformative products that drive progress across industries.

Our collaborations with Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) empower us to create cutting-edge solutions for consumer electronics, transportation, wellness, food supplements, personal care, home care and agriculture. Name: Prosper Technologies, LLC
Description:
Prosper Technologies is a broad platform technology company that owns an intellectual property portfolio of several Gas Infusion patents. Our gas infusion process is primarily used to infuse high concentrations of oxygen into liquids. Our technology is a unique Nano technology that can instantaneously infuse supersaturated levels of oxygen into liquids, while simultaneously removing a gas. The proprietary process creates a highly-oxygenated water or other liquids, with very stable and extended oxygen retention, which makes it ideal for use in water treatment, agriculture, healthcare, and aquaculture.

The long retention time of the oxygen delivers unprecedented results in the following industries; agriculture, health & medical, energy, consumer goods, and water treatment.

Prosper has developed several patented applications with the technology such as; the accelerated growth and increased yield of crops through oxygenated irrigation, a process that significantly increases the output of usable water from water treatment and water purification, and several topical oxygenation treatments for wounds that improve healing time.

Prosper’s strategy is to develop unique patentable applications to license to business sectors such as; consumer goods, healthcare & wellness, agriculture, aquaculture, energy, environmental, and water treatment. 

The value proposition of our technology is that it can increase revenue, while providing significant cost, time, and energy savings versus commonly used gas transfer technologies.

Name: AARD Technology
Description:
AARD Technology supplies equipment, materials, and components to manufacturers and R&D centers in North America.

Equipment includes plasma and ion beam equipment and associated materials handling equipment.

Industries served include Semiconductor, MEMS, Disk Drive, Aerospace, and Optics.SMD

Applications include thin film coating, surface structuring, wafer planarization, and localized trimming of optics.

Name: DCN Corp
Description:
𝗗𝗲𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 & 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝘂𝗳𝗮𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗘𝗾𝘂𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲 𝗡𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗧𝗲𝗰𝗵 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 

At DCN Corp we hope to transform Nano-tech into accessible, life-enhancing diagnostics.

Our proprietary platform technology is validated to TRL 3, and protected by a robust hybrid IP strategy.

- What We Do:
We’ve developed a useful Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) process technology, designed for next-gen diagnostics.

It enables:
- Real-time biological insights
- Quantitative severity analysis
- Compatibility with Raman & Infrared instruments

This innovation should evolve into a scalable handheld bio-kit for early detection and precision diagnostics.

- Our Mission:
To pioneer Nano-tech applications that positively impact humanity. Through rigorous science and inclusive design, we aim to democratise diagnostics for global health equity.

- Our Vision:
We all know someone affected by cancer, cardiovascular disease, or other life-altering conditions. These illnesses respond better to early, accurate detection. Therefore, our vision is to create a handheld diagnostic bio-kit that supports this goal - aligned with future at-home diagnostics requirements.

Supported by research - Crosby et al. (2022), Science Journal: “Early Detection of Cancer”.

- Market & Impact Potential:
- Global diagnostics market: £100B+
- SPR-based tools projected to grow 18% CAGR (2024–2030)
- Early detection improves survival rates by up to 70%

- What We’re Looking For:
To bring this vision to life, we’re actively seeking:
- R&D & Technical Collaborations
- Financial Partnerships
- Design & Engineering Support
- Next Milestone: Applying nano-tech to plastic polymer plates to increase bio-sensitivity.
- Fundraising Now: Seed round of £500K+ ($677K+)

 Let’s Shape the Future of Diagnostics Together

If you're an investor, scientist, or industrial partner, request our Executive Summary today.

 Book a 30-min Intro call:
 calendly.com/dcncorp-info/30min

Name: Flexegraph
Description:
FlexeGRAPH began in 2015 as a producer of graphene and 2D materials based on patented technology invented at the Australian National University. It has since grown through the in-house development of a range of fluids enhanced with nanotechnology for use in heat transfer applications. Core focus is coolants for the automotive sector, high powered computing and data centres.
Our nano-enhanced product range is continually evolving and we work with partners to achieve performance required for specific applications.

FlexeGRAPH Graphene produced via a scalable method is ideally suited for use in many applications. The facile technique leads to large defect free particles with very low thickness (typically 90% less than 2nm) with high electrical and thermal conductivity. Production capacity  is currently 2 tonnes per year however this process is easily scaled to larger volumes. The process itself is non-toxic and environmentally friendly.

In addition to graphene, we can apply our methods for the production of other 2D materials including minerals such as talc, boron nitride and transition metal dichalcogenides (like MoS2 and WS2).

Name: Indium Laboratory
Description: At Indium Lab, our unwavering mission is to spearhead groundbreaking scientific research that propels our understanding of the world, with a particular focus on two vital areas: biosensor and clean energy research. Name: LIG Technologies
Description:
LIG Technologies is a Manchester based nanotechnology R&D company founded in 2014, developing world leading product offerings in microscopy, microelectronics manufacturing, rapid diagnostics, disinfection and micro-3D printing. These technologies emerged from our unique fusion of some of the world’s brightest scientific minds in laser engineering, life science and materials science. 

We formed three subsidiary companies in order to successfully pursue the multi-faceted applications of our inventions:

LIG Nanowise applies microsphere technology to imaging and industrial manufacturing. We build the world’s first virtual imaging nanoscope, which can match the resolution of current super-resolution techniques whilst opening up new capabilities for at least a third of the price. This has major applications in drug discovery, semiconductor quality control and life/ materials science R&D more generally in industry and academia. We are launching this product at the end of June 2017 through our imaging brand NANOPSIS (www.nanopsis.com).
We are also developing a best in class particle lens array that can produce nanoscale patterns rapidly and repeatably with high precision over a wide area, with applications in microfluidics, semiconductors and bank note security.

LIG Biowise applies our expertise in nanoparticles to the problem of anti-microbial resistance. We produce an antibacterial biocide currently in the testing phase and are working on a novel assay for the rapid point of care diagnostics of upper respiratory tract infections. 

LIG 3D applies our microsphere technology to 3D printing. We are working on the fabrication of nanoscale components and 3D printed organs.

Name: Innovation Center for Nanoscience and Technology (ICNT) Laboratory
Description:
The Innovation Centre for Nanoscience and Technologies (ICNT), established in 2020 at the A. B. Nalbandyan Institute of Chemical Physics (NAS RA), is Armenia’s leading interdisciplinary laboratory for advanced research in nanostructured thin films and high-tech material systems.

Founded under a competitive national grant program, ICNT unites ten research institutions and is equipped with state-of-the-art facilities, including:

eLINE Plus Electron Beam Lithography (EBL) system from RAITH with nanomanipulators for advanced patterning and device prototyping at the nanoscale
Pioneer 180 PLD system with excimer laser, DC/RF magnetrons, high-pressure RHEED, and CVD systems
These integrated tools allow the lab to fabricate complex nanostructures, test theoretical predictions with high experimental precision, and pioneer applications in electronics, optoelectronics, and quantum technologies.

Our mission is to accelerate innovation in nanoscience by bridging experimental and theoretical research, fostering scientific diplomacy, and strengthening international collaboration. We serve as a national hub for cutting-edge materials research, education, and technology transfer.

Founded in 2014, Cambridge Xray Systems (CXS) focuses on the development of nanoengineered Xray sources. A spin-out company from the Department of Engineering, we are developing and manufacturing paradigm shifting Xray emitters tailored, through the use of novel one- and two-dimensional nanomaterials, to the ever increasing demands of continuous inspection systems for varied diagnostic applications.

Our Xray sources employ state-of-the art nanomaterials with integrated on-chip control to afford truly unprecedented functionality over the beam temporal and spatial characteristics for food security, border control, pharmaceutical inspection, electronics validation, and medical diagnostics and treatment.

CXS is steering the pragmatic integration of nanotechnologies for beyond-thermionic-source Xray technologies through the design, development and installation of high temporal response field emission nanoscale emitters, each some 1000 times smaller than a human hair. Our emitters integrate ballasting to ensure longer lasting emitters with the gold-standard in emission stability, perfectly suited to immediate integration into customers’ products which represent an optimised application specific solution.

Name: Nano-Clear ME
Description:
Nano-Clear is designed to dramatically enhance and extend the surface life of "Newly Painted" or "Oxidized" painted surfaces by 10+ years.  Organizations have invested billions of dollars to protect high-value assets from corrosion, scratching, chipping, chemical attack and weathering.  Many of these assets were painted using inferior paint materials not designed to withstand long-term weathering and use.  Organizations have various options when these painted assets degrade:
 
1.  Do nothing and allow the paint weathering cycle to continue (resulting in decreased corrosion, chemical and UV resistance).
2.  Replace the existing paint system using the same conventional systems (epoxy topcoat, polyurethane or latex).     
3.  Enhance and extend the surface life of "New" or "Oxidized" painted assets by 10+ years using Nano-Clear.
 
Conventional industrial paint systems including epoxies provide good corrosion resistance and initial surface hardness, but unfortunately, fall short in long-term UV resistance and weathering. Two-component polyurethanes provide good UV resistance and weathering, but typically cost much more than epoxy or polyester topcoats. Unfortunately, conventional paint systems like topcoats and epoxies begin the oxidization process or “chalking” from weathering within 6 months from application. This surface chalking phenomenon is actually degraded paint that resides on the surface from UV exposure and weathering. Nano-Clear Coatings penetrate deep into the smallest pores within newly painted or highly oxidized paint surfaces to form a highly cross-link dense/hard coat surface.  Nano-Clear Coatings provide dramatically longer surface protection over conventional automotive, marine, aerospace and industrial paints including epoxies and polyurethanes.

Aquaneers is a new venture that incorporated in January 2016 and was founded by Dr. Habib, Professor Ulman, and Mr. Day. Aquaneers was founded by people who are passionate about clean technology and realistic about successfully developing and deploying them. The company strives daily to improve the world through the application of nanotechnology to sustainability with the development of innovative energy conversion materials.

Aquaneers Inc. develops innovative nanotechnology enabled solutions to some of society’s most vexing energy challenges. Tremendous opportunity exists in applying novel materials for clean energy conversion to industrial applications that consume vast quantities of fossil fuels and contribute substantially to greenhouse gas emissions. Aquaneers Plasmonic Ribbon is a photo-thermal energy conversion material used to harvest solar energy through the direct vaporization of liquids, for example, converting sunlight directly into heated steam. Plasmonic Ribbon has commercial applications in heating, steam generation, desalination (water purification), chemical separations, and catalysis. The company is first applying its photothermal energy conversion technology to desalination and water purification and is developing a portable solar desalination system under support from a Department of Energy (DOE) Small Business Innovative Research (SBIR) grant.

Description:
Var olan ve var olmamış binlerce soru var: Madde neydi? Ne kadarını görebiliyorduk elementler nasıl oluşuyordu? Yapay zeka gerçekten bizi ele geçire bilir miydi? Ya da aslında ele geçirmiş miydi? Gökyüzünde gördüklerimiz sadece yıldızlardan mı ibaretti? Nasıl oluyordu da düşünebiliyorduk? Teknoloji daha ne kadar gelişebilirdi? Bilim, çözümü

Biz her gün yeni sorular soran ve bu soruların peşinden koşan bir grup öğrenciyiz.2019 Bahar
döneminde sizin de bu yolculuğa katılmanız için KTO Karatay Üniversitesinde Nanoteknoloji ve Bilim Topluluğu olarak kurulduk. Kurulduğumuz ilk günden beri hayalini kurduğumuz dergimizi nihayet hayata geçirebildik. Topluluğu kurma amaçlarımızdan olan sorularımızı bilimin başladığı noktayla birleştirdik.
Bilimin ilk başlangıç noktalarından biri simyaydı. Simyanın ise ateş, hava, su ve toprak. Ateş, hava, su ve toprağın tıpkı sorularımız gibi sürekli birbirine dönüşmesinden esinlendik ve ilk sayımız da tema olarak dört elemente yer verdik. Dergi; ateş ,hava, su ve toprak olmak üzere dört bölümden oluşuyor .Her bölümün zihninizde yeni dönüşümlere kapı açması dileğiyle…

Name: Vecoy
Description:
The company has created a totally new way of fighting viruses. Its nano-scale virus traps mimic human cells in order to lure viruses. The viruses are then trapped and destroyed.

The nano-scale “traps” differ from existing treatment for viral illness by targeting viruses before the viruses can affect human cells. The traps are non-toxic to the human host, avoiding the adverse side effects typical of current treatment.

Moreover, viruses cannot develop a resistance to the traps, as by doing so they would become resistance to human cells as well.

“Essentially, we are creating antibiotics for viruses,” said founder and CEO Erez Livneh, a graduate of the Weizmann Institute of Science. “The Vecoy technology will fundamentally change the treatment of viral infections.”

Name: SOFT RAIN Srl
Description:
In the field of the pretreatment of metals, in recent years the chemical industry has developed products with a high technological content.
Research has focused on chemical products that, in addition to low environmental impact, also
provide better quality coating of the supports,greater eff ectiveness in terms of improved
corrosion resistance of the painted substrate and a less expensive process, due to the reduction in
operating costs. Recently formulators have been concentrating on the development of so-called
“nanotechnological” products, namely products with innovative and improved functionalities
deriving from the handling of the material on a scale less than a micron.
Thanks to its close collaboration with Dollmar Spa, one of the leading companies in the production of
chemical products for the treatment of metallic surfaces, EuroPlast has been able to develop a
technology called Soft Rain that is used for mixing the pretreatment nanotechnological chemical
solution and its successive application, and which is capable of fully exploiting the qualitative and
environmental benefi ts of nanotechnological products, lowering product consumption and
eliminating the production of any sludge that needs to be disposed of. The equipment can easily be put
inside traditional pretreatment tunnels without requiring any major work on the finishing line.

Name: Nano-Product Engineering, LLC
Description:
Nano-Product Engineering, LLC is an R&D and transfer/commercialization company specializing in advanced surface engineering techniques to enhance material properties for a wide variety of applications. Founded in January, 2006, NPE is a small company in Lafayette, CO. NPE represents the culmination of work starting at Arcomac Plasma Processing Lab in Toronto, Canada and continuing at Arcomac Surface Engineering, LLC in Missoula, MT over a period of years from 1994 through 2010.

Our techniques include patented hybrid Physical Vapor Deposition and Plasma-Assisted Chemical Vapor Deposition (PVD and PACVD) processes, which result in high density, low defect thin film coatings with uniform large-area coverage at industrial-scale production. Coatings are specifically developed for applications ranging from wear and corrosion resistance to optics, semiconductors and fuel cell systems.

Proven applications include: superhard and corrosion resistant coatings for medical instruments; low-friction, wear resistance coatings for gears and bearing races; erosion resistant coatings for compressor and rotor blades; high temperature oxidation resistant and conductive coatings for fuel cells and diamond and related materials superhard coatings for tools, machine parts and powder synthesis.

NPE is pursuing the commercialization of patented and proprietary technologies in:

Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) - Patents encompassing architecture, processes and apparatuses.
Plasma Assisted Chemical Vapor Deposition (PACVD) - Patents encompassing architecture, processes and apparatuses.
Electric propulsion – New generation of arcjet thrusters and combined engines.
Plasma actuators for high altitude planes and missiles
Plasma generators of energetic particles - Proprietary processes covering varied applications from neutron generators to ion lasers.

L’offerta globale NanoTech Surface è un work in progress, una ricerca continua che affianca materiali e soluzioni diverse per garantire una nuova qualità dell’abitare e del vivere. Lontano da ogni eccesso massmediologico, l’approccio NanoTech Surface è un ambientalismo scientifico, pragmatico, dedicato alle applicazioni su larga scala. La semplicità è alla base di ogni proposta: le reazioni chimiche più elementari portano a risultati sorprendenti, tecnologie consolidate da tempo sul mercato scoprono nuovi ambiti di utilizzo.

La sicurezza è un altro versante della ricerca NanoTech Surface, con proposte che spaziano dalle soluzioni a difesa fuoco-calore-acqua a progetti assolutamente innovativi, che utilizzano nuovi materiali per ampliare le possibilità delle tecnologie di soccorso e di bonifica ambientale. Una realtà ampia, capace di guardare il mondo contemporaneo nella propria interezza, oltre ogni settorialità, e proporre nuove idee per migliorarlo.

Name: UCI INRF
Description:
The Integrated Nanosystems Research Facility at The University of California, Irvine (INRF UCI) in the Henry Samueli School of Engineering is dedicated to developing and promoting micro and nano technology through research, education and outreach. The INRF advances multidisciplinary research through fostering communication between traditionally distant fields such as bioscience and engineering and by enlisting researchers from diverse backgrounds. The facility is a 9,600 square foot class 100/1,000/10,000 clean room equipped with all the major devices for micro and nano fabrication. Capabilities include such diverse disciplines such as MEMS, biology, chemistry, optics and physics equipped with all the major devices for micro and nano fabrication.
Our facilities are available to our distinguished faculty and students along with other academic institutions and industry. We welcome you to visit us, take a tour and join us in performing high quality research at our world class facility to engineer the microworld.

Name: CardTech / Card Technologies
Description:
HISTORY

CARD TECHNOLOGIES  was originally founded in 2004 by DR. SERIGNE DIOUM, who remains its Chief Science Officer and acts as its Chief Executive Officer. The company specializes in Nanotechnology Research and Development in environmental and biological areas. At present, Card Technologies' facilities are located in Olivet, France (just south of Orleans). In 2013, Mr. Ralph Ledee, a US investor, provided angel capital to convert the R&D-oriented company in a commercial operation and bring the technology to the Americas: projects "Card Technologies LATAM" (started 2014) and project "Card Technologies North America" (started 2016).

￼THE FOUNDER

Serigne Dioum is a Doctor in Physical Science and holds a PhD from the Université d'Orléans on Energetics (Plasmas & Laser), Photonics, and on the Mechanic of Fluids, Thermodynamic Systems and Nanotechnology. While a senior researcher with GREMI (a division of the prestigious French CNRS), Serigne participated in the elaboration of NANOCAR+. He was also a senior researcher with CRMD (Research Center on Divided Matter) at CNRS.
Note: CNRS, the National Center for Scientific Research (Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique, www.cnrs.fr) is the largest governmental research organization in France and the largest fundamental science agency in Europe. It employs 26,000 permanent employees (research engineers and support staff).

￼￼THE RESEARCH

In the course of his research activities, Dr. Dioum made the invention that would be the basis of NANOCAR+: an innovative Filtration Method for purification and treatment of water and other fluids. In the following years, he further developed the process (underlying science and manufacturing technology) and the product (the NANOCAR+ Filtration Media). The invention was patented in France in September 2011 (At present the patent is registered in 17 countries, including but not limited to the USA, China, India, Canada, and Venezuela).

Name: Pharmaceutical Innovation and Translational Research Lab (PITRL)
Description:
At the Pharmaceutical Innovation and Translational Research Laboratory (PITRL), Department of Pharmaceutics, NIPER Hyderabad, we are committed to advancing next-generation therapeutic strategies for the prevention, management, and treatment of complex diseases. Our multidisciplinary research focuses on a diverse range of health challenges and unmet clinical needs, including cancer (breast, prostate, oral, lung, brain, and skin cancers), diabetes and its ocular complications, diabetic foot ulcers (DFU), neurodegenerative disorders, and dermal conditions such as atopic dermatitis, psoriasis, and melanoma.
To address these challenges, we ideate, design, and establish innovative nanocarrier-based drug delivery systems, including lipid nanoparticles, polymeric nanoparticles, metallic and inorganic nanoparticles, and electrospun nanofibers, that enhance therapeutic efficacy, target specificity, and patient compliance.
Beyond nanoparticle platforms, PITRL also pioneers the development of advanced biomaterials, including stimuli-responsive gels and polymeric films, engineered for controlled release, regenerative applications, and personalized treatment approaches.
Our overarching goal is to translate laboratory innovations into clinically viable solutions, bridging the gap between pharmaceutical research and real-world therapy to improve global healthcare outcomes.

Principal Investigator (PI): Prof. (Dr.) Saurabh Srivastava, Ph.D. FTASc.
