Name: Phantaslube LLC
Description:
Phantaslube LLC is the maker of Phantaslube, a patented (non-toxic) revolutionary molecular nanopolishing additive for any sealed mechanical gear system, engine, turbine or bearing.

Phantaslube works by forming spherical nanopolishing particles (in situ) from the heat generated by friction in your engine or transmission. Once formed, these “Phantasmene” nanopolishing balls cut through the rough surface imperfections on the metal parts and polish them to atomic-level perfection (Ra = <4 nm), trapping metal wear particles in the center of each new nano-sized sphere they create.

The formed Phantasmene spheres then remain permanently stuck to the surfaces of the parts, preventing future metal-to-metal contact. These nano-ball bearings are made of the hardest substance known to mankind (Graphene Oxide) and are harder than diamond.

Phantaslube technology is a revolution in lubrication that REMOVES the source of all friction in engines – the asperities on the surfaces of machined metal parts. Additives that coat surfaces will never work. To eliminate friction, you must eliminate its source . . . asperities on the surface of metal parts. The only lubricant technology In the world that can do that is Phantaslube.

Phantaslube additive is completely non-toxic and compatible with all base lubricants and existing additive packages.

The revolution in lubrication has begun, and its name is Phantaslube.

Name: 4D-NMR project
Description:
Nanotechnology is emerging as a key area to address global challenges in health, energy, environment and information technologies. However, we are still investigating most nanomaterials with bulk techniques, averaging over large samples, instead of looking at one
single nanostructure with true nanoscale sensors. Particularly, Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) as our workhorse for bio/chemical synthesis and medical imaging is inherently limited to bulk samples. The most fundamental challenge, to turn NMR from an ensemblemeasurement technique (Commercial NMRs typically have a sensitivity of billions of molecules) into a nanocale technique remains unsolved. In this project we will overcome this challenge by reaching single molecule sensitivity, thus converting NMR into an imaging
technique thanks to the exploitation of the unparalleled atomic resolution of the scanning probe microscopy (SPM) technology. This breakthrough will be based on resonant, high frequency, electro-magnetic excitation and readout including important advances in GHz
technology. We will use the capabilities of the novel technology to demonstrate detection of single spin NMR and to test the limits of our understanding of nuclear-electron interactions, probing the physics of molecular nanoobjects, 1D carbon nanoribbons with
delocalized coherent states, and 2D atomically-thin magnetic materials. This novel technology will not only open up new fundamental scientific insights but should also have a strong impact in the markets of NMR and SPM. In this context, the project will be a keystone, demonstrating the novel platform conceived as a versatile upgrade for commercially-available SPMs, that can routinely operate in various environments (vacuum, ambient, liquid) with a variety of molecules and materials.

This EU Pathfinder open project was funded by the EIC under GA101099676.

Description:
Microshield360 UK is focussed on providing a range of solutions to make your environment permanently safer for your people. Our cornerstone product, Microshield, is a unique anti-microbial coating developed in conjunction with Ohio State University which has been rigorously tested and evaluated to the highest international standards. It is electrostatically sprayed onto high touch point surfaces and kills a range of 90+ membrane encapsulated microbes, including a clinically validated SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) surrogate, on contact. It also kills flu, SARS, the common cold and all other similar infections in a size range from 5 to 400 nanometres. 

Acting like a suit of armour and protecting your environment and people from the spread of infectious diseases, 24/7, for a minimum of 12 months, Microshield360 means we can go back to work, back to school, get back together and feel more comfortable living in the new normal.

Our nano technology is the only one in the world to be approved by both the US Food & Drug Administration Service ("FDA") and Environmental Protection Agency ("EPA") for use on over 1,500 different surfaces, and its efficacy is supported by more than 300 pages of scientific studies. 

We can help you to differentiate and gain real market advantage by promoting the safety of people in your environment by making them feel safe, secure and reassured that they truly matter. 

Applying Microshield360 will have a longer term benefit by lowering infections from common winter bugs and reducing the costs of absenteeism. As an employer, it helps demonstrate your commitment to employee wellbeing, as well as mitigating employee and public liability risks. 

Your people want reassurance that their work environment is a safe place and expect their employer to do all they can to mitigate their risk of infection and transmission - they expect their employers to utilise the optimal protection solution. Microshield360 is that solution!

Our goal is to provide industry professionals with the skills and knowledge they need to successfully manage OEHS activities within their day-to-day operations.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Horse chestnut trees affected by bleeding canker, palms affected by red palm weevils; bacteria, fungi and pests pose a serious global problem to nature and food producers.

TreeHold is a agro-/biotech company that specializes in the removal of bacteria, fungi and pests from trees, plants, seeds and harvested fruit & vegetables.

Name: Polymer-free DCN filtration membrane
Description:
Polymer-free fouling-resistant DCN filtration membrane

Polymer membrane fouling remains a challenge, especially in monoclonal antibody production, vaccine production and gene therapy. Membrane fouling mechanism is still poorly understood. 

4th Phase Technologies (the Company) focuses on commercializing flexible, robust and durable, ultrathin and lightweight polymer-free densified carbon nanotube (DCN) sheets for filtration applications. Being superior in resisting surface fouling, organic solvents, corrosion and extreme temperatures, polymer-free DCN sheets are especially suitable for applications in bioprocessing, where membrane fouling/clogging has been a significant challenge. 

Polymer-free DCN sheet membrane resists surface fouling. One type of DCN sheet membrane has water flux rate of >100 L/(m2.h) at 0.1 MPa, with cut-off rate of 85% for 1% BSA solution (Fig. 2). Water flux rate is fully recovered upon rinse. A second test with 1% BSA solution shows identical flux rate and BSA cut-off rate, demonstrating membrane stability and superior fouling resistance. 

Current state-of-the-art polyether sulfone (PES) or modified PES membrane for bioprocessing is expensive and prone to clog. Polymer-free DCN membrane is especially advantageous for monoclonal antibody and vaccine filtration.

Name: Sensing Labs, Inc.
Description: Our plasmonic based chemical sensing platform can detect analytes in particles per billion (ppb) range. With the assist of our Artificial intelligence (AI) alogrithm, we can discriminate and quantify the target analytes in complex backgrounds such as human breath. The initial goal of Sensing Labs is to improve, expand, and benefit the breath-based detecting devices in health-related applications. So far, many substances in human breath have been studied and proven to be related to certain diseases (e.g., acetone in breath vs. blood glucose level). Outcomes of those studies, however, have not been fully utilized for disease detection due to the lack of capability in detecting those tiny amounts of substances from human breath - a complex mixture of various gas-phase analytes. Hence, we not only provide a promising technical solution to this application but also fill a broad range of pressing demands in various health-related fields.

Name: IOTA NanoSolutions Limited
Description:
IOTA NanoSolutions Limited is a nanomaterials engineering company specialising in the delivery and formulation of poorly soluble ingredients. In so doing IOTA NanoSolutions’ technologies can help overcome many of the factors that prevent the use and full value-realisation of many poorly soluble actives. IOTA NanoSolutions has actively developed a number of novel generic approaches to enable the formation of dry physical blends of insoluble and soluble materials. The blends are produced without a traditional particle formation step such as milling and are equally applicable with amorphous, crystalline or waxy materials. When the blends are added to a liquid the soluble material dissolves consequently releasing the insoluble material as a nanodispersion. 

Materials processed by IOTA NanoSolutions can be used to form nanodispersions in almost any liquid ranging from water to natural fluids, hydrophilic/hydrophobic solvents, monomers or oils. The nanodispersions are often transparent although nanoparticles of the insoluble material can be present at concentrations unobtainable through traditional techniques.

Description:
Extremely precise and reliable timekeeping is crucial for current and near future technologies used in data centers, aviation, navigation, computing and many other fields. TIMEKEEPER is set to redefine the future of timekeeping by making it all-optical, leveraging epsilon-near-zero (ENZ) metamaterials to achieve femtosecond precision.

The laser-based solution is designed to ensure ultra-precise and stable synchronization, minimize the use of expensive electronic components, and significantly reduce drift.

Our origin is in the robust research of the aQUARiUM project (2019-2024) at Tampere University, funded by the European Research Council (ERC) and a Proof-of-Concept grant from ERC (2023). We are supported by Business Finland’s Research to Business (R2B) program since 2025.

Name: Cool-X LLC
Description:
Cool-X LLC is a start-up with offices in MA and MI that has developed new, proprietary nanotechnology that improves oil performance. Cool-X is a semifinalist in the 2012 Cleantech Open, a national competition and accelerator for leading cleantech technology start-ups. 

Cool-X sells nanofluid oil additives that reduce heat and friction and improve fuel economy and energy efficiency in motors, transmissions, gearboxes, and hydraulic systems. Cool-X oil additives add carbon nanoparticles to oil in a stable colloidal suspension, and these nanoparticles improve the heat transfer and thermal conductivity of oils, by increasing the speed that heat moves through the oil and into metal surfaces through convection. Cool-X has been shown to reduce oil temperatures in engines and gearboxes by 10-30F, which prevents breakdowns, reduces wear, and prolongs oil life. Oil with Cool-X in it typically lasts twice as long with significantly better performance, as the additive packages are protected from excess heat.

Cool-X additives also burnish at a nano scale. Cool-X nanoparticles smooth surfaces, improving surface finish, preventing the build up of oxidation byproducts, and reducing friction over time. Cool-X has been shown to improve fuel economy in Class 8 trucks by 5% in a fleet test of 26 vehicles driven over 2 million miles. 

Cool-X technology is being tested by major OEMs, gearbox manufacturers, racing teams, large truck fleets, and car enthusiasts everywhere. www.cool-x.com

Name: 4th Nanomedicine and Nanosafety Conference (NMNS 2023)
Description:
About conference


 

On behalf of the scientific and executive committees, it is our great pleasure to cordially invite you to attend the 4th Nanomedicine and Nanosafety Conference (NMNS 2023) which will be held at Shiraz University of Medical Sciences on 10-11 May 2023.

NMNS 2023 is jointly organized by Iranian Society of Nanomedicine (ISNM), Shiraz University of Medical Sciences (SUMS), Iran Nanosafety Network (INSN) of Iran Nanotechnology Initiative Council (INIC)  and supported by Iran Nanohealth Committee of Food & Drug Administration and Iranian Environmental Mutagen Society (IrEMS).

 

The Conference aims to cover all medical and safety aspects of nanomaterials in human, animals and environment. 


The scientific program will consist of keynote/distinguished lectures, symposia, discussion panels and poster presentations. This conference aims to provide attendees proper opportunities to meet scientists from all over the world to exchange the ideas and to launch national and international collaborations in different aspects of Nanomedicine and Nanosafety.

 

The organizing committee is also planning a variety of unique social programs to provide the chance for participants to enjoy from fascinating Iranian culture and warm spirit of friendship.
We look forward to welcoming you and your active participation in the NMNS 2023 in Shiraz, I.R. Iran.

Name: GrapheneX
Description: Our GraphenX products are comprised of single layers of graphene oxide. It is a product of oxidation of graphite through a modified Hummers’ method. In contrast to commercially available graphene oxide sheets which possess lateral size generally less than 5μm, GraphenX are monolayers of oxidized graphene with outstandingly high lateral size (up to 0.1mm), rendering these products as an excellent candidate for diverse applications such as electronics, composite materials, energy and etc. GraphenX is a polar and functionalized material bearing several types of oxygen groups and is available in polar solvents (Water, NMP, DMF and Ethanol).

Name: MSc in Nanomedicine NKUA
Description:
This postgraduate program, accredited by the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens, is designed to lay the scientific and technological foundation regarding the fast-growing field of Nanomedicine. In particular, this program provides an opportunity for students to better understand concepts that deal with nanotechnology applications in drug delivery, disease diagnosis, therapy, imaging and new applications in medicine as well as in numerous techniques for the physicochemical characterization of nanoparticles, in vitro and in vivo. Knowledge acquired during the course of this MSc Program, constitutes an important qualification for graduates who aim to be involved in:
•	Divisions of Production relevant to Nanotechnology 
•	Quality Control of Assembly Lines 
•	Medical Imaging 
•	Regenerative Medicine
•	Research and Development of Nano-Pharmaceuticals
•	Doctoral Degree in Nanomedicine
The teaching program ensures that graduates will gain the necessary theoretical knowledge in order to meet the increased demands both in the field of medicine and pharmacy, and thus to work independently and effectively in the field of Nanomedicine. To accomplish that, theoretical teaching courses are complemented by:
•	hands-on projects, such as active participation of students in laboratory exercises, workshops and presentations of their scientific projects in meetings and conferences.
•	future perspectives projects, where students investigate new frontiers opening in this field
Programs' duration is two (2) Academic Semesters (1 year), including the time for writing the dissertation thesis, which is equivalent to 60 ECTS credit points. The “Nanomedicine” program accepts graduates in the subjects of Medicine, Pharmacology, Biology, Physics, Chemistry, Engineering and other related subjects from Greek Universities or foreign institutions (recognized by the Hellenic National Academic Recognition and Information Centre).

Name: Internet of Bio-Nano-Things Research Project
Description: Research and development on sensor networks and body area networks has gained increased maturity in recent years. Yet, in-body nanonetworks built from nanodevices represent a new and fascinating direction of research, extending upon and going well beyond sensor networks. The vision of these networks is that nanodevices (nanoscale devices), for example, patrol the body, take measurements wherever necessary, and send collected data to the outside. Even better, these machines may immediately work on problems they detect within the body, such as cancer cells, arteriosclerosis, or HI viruses. In this project, we are looking at biomedical applications with a focus on the necessary integration of in-body nano communication with out-of-body IT infrastructures, resulting in the so-called Internet of Nano Things (IoNT). We research topics like nanosensors for biomarkers detection, simulators for the human circulatory system, nanonetworks for monitoring the human body, communication interfaces in the thera hertz band to enable information exchange between nanosensors in the human body and external monitoring devices.

Name: SNAPTECH S.R.L.
Description:
SNAPTECH, spin-off dell’Università degli Studi dell’Aquila, è una startup che si propone come obiettivo primario l’impiego di processi di sintesi sostenibili per la produzione di una vasta gamma di nanoparticelle di composti inorganici, (ossidi/idrossidi di metalli alcalino-terrosi, ossidi di ferro, o di zinco), che possono trovare impiego in svariati settori applicativi.
In particolare, il core business di SNAPTECH è rivolto alla produzione e commercializzazione di nanoparticelle di ossidi/idrossidi metallici (di cui detiene la licenza ad uso esclusivo di un brevetto europeo e due brevetti italiani).
Inoltre, in qualità di Spin-Off dell’Università degli Studi dell’Aquila, SNAPTECH è in grado di fornire prestazioni dinamiche correlando l’attività di produzione con la ricerca, sviluppando soluzioni pensate su misura per i Clienti che si affidano a SNAPTECH.

Grazie a processi a basso impatto ambientale, che operano in acqua, a temperatura ambiente e pressione atmosferica, offriamo soluzioni efficienti, sicure e personalizzabili.

I nanomateriali, grazie alle loro proprietà peculiari, sono fondamentali per numerose applicazioni e rappresentano un settore economico in forte crescita. Tra questi, le nanoparticelle si distinguono per la loro natura nanometrica, la versatilità morfologica e la possibilità di personalizzazione.
In particolare, le nanoparticelle di ossidi e idrossidi metallici stanno suscitando un interesse crescente sia in ambito scientifico che tecnologico. Il loro utilizzo si estende a diversi settori, tra cui batterie, celle solari, catalisi, edilizia, conservazione dei beni culturali, medicina e risanamento ambientale, grazie alla varietà di composizioni, strutture e proprietà che possiedono.

La nostra missione è quella di produrre nanoparticelle mediante metodi di sintesi che garantiscano un basso impatto ambientale, (sia in termini di costi energetici che di impiego o smaltimento di sostanze inquinanti).

Name: sgraphen
Description:
About the s graphen as private owned company.
Graphene is a remarkable material that has the potential to revolutionize a wide range of industries. However, producing graphene that is pure and free from defects can be a significant challenge. That's why it's so exciting to be part of world race for better future. s graphen made it posible to develope a uniqe method folowing several step procedures for producing high-quality graphene.

S graphen has developed a process for producing pure, oxide-free graphene that is both scalable and cost-effective. This is a major accomplishment that has the potential to transform the graphene industry and ease the stragle of world labs and researcher to hunt for better way to get as pure as posible this magical material.

S graphen production method involves using a carefully annealing and curing process to remove impurities and defects from the graphene layer. This process results in a graphene sheet that is free from oxidation and other defects, making it ideal for a wide range of applications until now it is was not posible, because of impurity and defects.

One of the key advantages of s graphen production method is that it is scalable. This means that we can produce large quantities of pure graphene to meet the needs of researchers, laboratories, and industrial customers around the world. Whether customers need just a few grams of graphene for research purposes or many tons of graphene for industrial applications, s graphen is able to meet their needs.
Another advantage of s graphen production method is that it is cost-effective.
 
S graphen insures high-quality graphene more accessible to a wider range of customers. By offering competitive pricing, your company is helping to drive the adoption of graphene across a wide range of industries. Get in touch for placing order for your production line or developing a brand new product

Name: NT-MDT B.V.
Description:
Nanotechnology is exploding. The world of small sizes has never been as vital for so many directions in science and industry. With this new direction comes a greater need for measurement on the atomic scale and new demands for reliable, precise, and multi-purpose instrumentation. NT-MDT enjoys a 30-year history in instrumentation created specifically for nanotechnology research, leading the field in originality, quality, and high tech development.
We strive for next-generation SPM technology, whether it be in pure modularity that allows a university or industrial lab to start with a cost-effective core product and build to a grand, multi-user research center or the ultimate amalgamation of SPM with related technologies that has resulted in ultramicrotomy for nanotomography and spectroscopy-based instruments that meld the world of imaging with the world of chemical analysis. We believe passionately in pushing the envelope for rapid innovation while still delivering superb customer service.

NT-MDT offers expert service and applications development through more than 20 representative offices and distributor centers around the globe. In the past years, our installed base has grown to over 1000 instruments, promoting growth of both lab and research programs world-wide.

Whether you are engaged every day in nano research or just contemplating it, coupling your specific scientific knowledge and expertise with our competence in instrument creation will produce the highest quality research results currently available.

Mission
Our mission is to enable researchers, engineers and developers to conduct nanoscale research by creating ever more perfect nanotechnology instrumentation. Along the way, we maintain a global perspective, always taking into consideration the needs of student in the classroom, the researcher at the cutting edge in the laboratory, and the practicalities of industrial R&D.

Name: LAB ICS
Description:
The LAB-ICS is a mixed Unit that involves Researchers from the Department of Physics and Geology of the University of Perugia (Italy) and from the Istituto Officina dei Materiali (IOM) of the Italian National Research Council (CNR).


Our research group explores the intricate interactions between light and matter to advance the understanding of biological mechanics and phononic properties for advanced materials. By leveraging cutting-edge optical techniques, we investigate how light can be used to shield light on fundamental biophysical mechanisms at the molecular and cellular levels, and to study phononic properties to engineer novel metamaterials with tailored acoustic and thermal behaviors. 

 

In parallel, through collaboration and innovation, our research aims to develop new experimental methods, theoretical models, and applications that span from biomedical diagnostics to next-generation materials with engineered wave propagation properties.

 

This interdisciplinary approach bridges physics, materials science, and bioengineering, fostering innovations in mechanobiology, optomechanics, and phononic engineering.

Name: 21st International Conference on Advanced Materials Science & Nano Technology

Description:
Low Dimensional Materials Research Centre (LDMRC) was founded in 2010 and formerly known as Solid State Physics Laboratory (SSPL). This Centre conducts various research works related to low-dimensional systems including dimensional structures less than three dimensions. These materials have a lattice structure that approximates sheets (two-dimensional) or chains (one-dimensional) or which exists as a thin film or surface layer, needles, or clusters (zero-dimensional) with the size of the atomic scale dimensions (ie nm). Current research is focused on silicon and carbon-based nanomaterials, organic electronics, metal oxides, III-nitrides and bio-materials. The fundamental and potential applications of these materials are actively studied in this research center. The research center is equipped with the necessary facilities needed to fabricate, characterize, and analyze the low-dimensional materials.

OUR OBJECTIVE
To carry out high-quality research and produce scientific knowledge and new technologies for low-dimensional materials

OUR MISSION
To compete in the global rankings through the discovery of a new phenomena and potential applications of low-dimensional materials to improve modern world technology

OUR VISION
To become a leading research center at the international level in research, innovation and scientific publication of low-dimensional materials

Name: INTELLECT-PrINTable mEmristive solar ceLls for seLf-powered internet of things edge CompuTing
Description:
INTELLECT develops and demonstrates a radically new, printable, light-operated and -powered memristive technology. The computation building block is a hybrid memristor with an electrochromic feature that exhibits a unique Photo-Volta-Chromic Processing-in-Memory, enabling simultaneous IoT energy-efficient computing and solar energy harvesting. A prototype of up to a 3×3-cell memristor crossbar array will be realized by a low-cost, clean-room-less fabrication employing industrially compatible printing techniques based on environmentally friendly materials and processes. This photovoltaic memristor technology is anticipated to overcome cell selection and sneak-path current issues at the minimum footprint, improve energy-delay product and computation power consumption, while significantly reducing the production cost compared to today’s architectures.

This research project is implemented under H.F.R.I.’s action “Basic Research Financing (Horizontal Support for all Sciences)" of the National Recovery and Resilience Plan “Greece 2.0” with financing from the European Union –NextGenerationEU (H.F.R.I. Project No.:14728).

Name: NxNANO AB
Description:
In short, our solution is about utilizing liquid flows in all heating and cooling systems and with a simple injection of HTF Compact during ongoing operation, increasing heat or cooling capacity which radically reduces electricity consumption and other loads on existing systems.

HTF Compact fits well in refrigeration and heating systems and reduces energy consumption by up to 40%

Nanofluids have been considered to be useful as advanced heat transfer fluids for almost two decades, as they have better suspension stability compared to micron solids, can flow smoothly without clogging the system and provide improved thermal and physical properties.




HTF Compact reduces the flow temperature in heat exchangers, whether they are water-to-water, water-to-air or refrigerant-to-water. Through a more efficient heat transfer, energy consumers (eg boiler, heat pump or cooling unit) run more efficiently and need to walk less to reach the desired temperature settings.




Our nanofluid has proven to be stable when circulated in closed systems and works well in black water found in our radiator systems or in various glycol mixtures in cooling systems and requires no extra effort to be installed and it can be installed without shutting down the system.




The nanoparticles in HTF Compact do not cause electrolysis, as they are oxides.

HTF Compact does not cause erosion or corrosion in pipe systems or equipment.

The main active component of HTF Compact is inert and non-carcinogenic.



HTF Compact concentration of 5% in the final system requires no change in installation, provides high flexibility to reach desired setpoints and is easy to install during operation. Through a simple water test and our questionnaire, you get a quick answer to what an installation of HTF Compact can provide for energy savings and what it costs.

With the help of our partners, we can also offer energy mapping, cleaning of existing systems, energy optimization of systems after installation of HTF .

Fotocatálisis:
Una revolución para su salud, su entorno y el medio ambiente.

¿Que es?
La fotocatálisis es un proceso natural en el que una sustancia es activada por la energía de la luz (luz natural o artificial) y utiliza esta energía para destruir contaminantes y compuestos dañinos.

Una vez activas, todas las superficies usan la luz recibida de la misma manera que una célula solar (panel fotovoltaico) y eliminarán a través de un proceso de red-ox cualquier sustancia orgánica o inorgánica, suciedad y contaminantes que entren en contacto con la superficie fotocatalítica.

La fotocatálisis es eficaz contra muchos productos químicos nocivos, incluidos:

Microbios y Bacterias
Monóxido de Carbono (CO)
Compuestos Orgánicos e Inorgánicos
Xileno
Óxidos de nitrógeno (NOx)
Etanol
Compuestos aromáticos policondensados
Metanol
Óxidos de azufre (SOx)
Partículas finas (PM 2,5, PM5, PM10)
Acetaldehídos

Graphene is the Future :
 
Graphene is the first in a growing family of 2 dimensional advanced nanomaterials that hold unique properties that can be used to address some of the world’s most challenging problems, including sustainability, decarbonization, achieving Net-Zero and building resilient infrastructure. Name: CHOOSE NanoTech UK
Description:
CHOOSE NanoTech specialises in manufacturing high-performance coating solutions for industrial applications. CHOOSE NanoTech UK is also excited to offer our range of coatings for professional detailers in the automotive sector, KubeBond.

Since 2010, CHOOSE NanoTech has been developing high performance coatings for the consumer market. Based on experience from the high-tech industry of photo-electron semiconductor, CHOOSE NanoTech develops many coatings to achieve the best protection with multi-functional properties for different applications.

CHOOSE NanoTech has a series of Ceramic Molecule Coatings for different substrates which dramatically reduces the surface energy of substrate, so that when liquids come into contact with it, they form beads & simply roll off. This enables our customers to dramatically improve the performance of their products by protecting them from the effects of water/dust & all other liquids, without affecting the look or feel. The Ceramic Molecule Coating can be used on a wide range of substrates which includes paint surface, glass, leather, textile, wood, plastic, rubber, marble ......etc.

The latest product from CHOOSE NanoTech is the 9H glass coating which dramatically improves the scratch resistance & also reduces the surface energy so that when liquids come into contact with the coated surface, they form beads & simply roll off. Another name for the 9H glass coating is “Liquid Diamond” which means it is a transparent liquid when in the container & a transparent 9H pencil hardness glass film when it is cured on the surface. The film comes with multi-properties, such as easy-to-clean, super hydrophobic, scratch resistance, anti-graffiti, corrosion resistance, UV resistance, high temperature resistance. This enables our customers to dramatically improve their products by protecting from water, dust & all other liquids, without affecting it's look or feel. Many applications; vehicles, building materials, electrical devices,...etc.

Name: Journal of Nanostructures
Description:
Journal of Nanostructures (Abbreviation: J Nanostruct) is a quarterly, academic publication by the Nanoscience and Nanotechnology Research Center of the University of Kashan. Journal of Nanostructures is a medium for global academics to exchange and disseminate their knowledge as well as the latest discoveries and advances in the science and engineering of nanostructured materials.  Journal of Nanostructures follows a libre open access policy under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International public license. We do not charge authors for any submission or processing fees. Submitted manuscripts are peer-reviewed by the Editorial Board and reviewers of Journal of Nanostructures before acceptance for publication.
To submit a new manuscript, please start by reading the journal’s instructions for authors.
*Publication process of manuscripts submitted to Journal of Nanostructures is free of charge.

Name: Resolve Nanotherapeutics Corp.
Description: Resolve Nanotherapeutics (RNTx) is developing completely novel drugs for severe infections such as pneumonia, abdominal infections and others. These types of infections kill millions of people every year and our current frontline treatments – antibiotics – are rapidly losing their effectiveness due to the rise in antibiotic resistance. RNTx is taking a host-directed approach to address this problem and using cutting-edge lipid nanoparticle (LNP) RNA technology to activate the body’s natural toxin clearance systems, ultimately overcoming the devastating clinical complications and vital organ dysfunction that accompany these types of severe infections. RNTx is the product of a collaboration between the UBC labs of Dr. Pieter Cullis (LNP expert) and Dr. James Russell (critical care physician and researcher) developing de-risked technology packages in pre-clinical models of severe infection. Studies to date have demonstrated remarkable efficacy in an in vivo model of pneumonia caused by antibiotic resistant bacteria. The key strengths of the technology are: (1) that the approach is organism and antibiotic resistance agnostic, (2) it does not create a selective pressure for new resistance development, ensuring a sustained market once approved and, (3) it uses clinically validated components (LNPs and RNA) that can readily be scaled for manufacturing and widespread distribution.

Name: Ram Nanotech
Description: Ram Nanotech (RNT) leverages the unique properties of silicon to overcome the limitations of technologies in various industries such as life sciences, energy storage, and environmental remediation.  We offer a variety of porous silicon products and we work with you to select the appropriate performance metrics based on the final product’s requirements. Etching can be performed either to leave a solid core or such that the particles are completely porosified. Powder particles can have a mean size between 1 and 75 µm and still be etched completely through the core. Larger particles can also be etched but will generally have a solid core. The mean pore size can be varied over the range of roughly 3 nm to 20 nm. Hierarchical pore size distributions can also be produced, for example, combinations of 3–5 nm pores with larger mesopores in the 10–20 nm range or with macropores larger than 50 nm. Porosity can be varied up to approximately 50% and specific surface areas of 50–450 cm3/g are available.

Name: International Freik Conference on Physics and Technology of Thin Films and Nanosystems
Description:
The ICPTTFN Conference covers a wide range of topics related to thin-film and nanostructural materials science, including thin film technologies, nanotechnologies, nanomaterials, and quantum-size structures. It also focuses on the physical and chemical properties of thin films and nanostructures, approaches to physical and mathematical modeling of nanoscale objects (both analytical and computational), and applied aspects of these issues – thin-film components of electronic devices, particularly devices for photoelectronics, spintronics, nanoelectronics, and nanophotonics.
The history of conferences on Physics and Technology of Thin Films began in 1975.
In 2017, by the initiative of the Ukrainian Physical Society and the Organizing Committee, the Conference was named after its founder, a renowned Ukrainian scientist and Professor at Vasyl Stefanyk Carpathian National University, Dmytro Freik.

At Loginns, innovation means efficiency, precision, and measurable impact. With over a decade of dedicated research, we have developed a unique patented (pending) process that converts graphite into Few-Layer Graphene (FLG) with unmatched simplicity—requiring no chemicals, heat, ultrasound, or generating any waste or by-products.

Our cutting-edge technology produces high-purity FLG at industrial scale, achieving an output of 120 kg per hour within a compact 20 m² footprint. Fully automated and user-friendly, our system allows straightforward integration into existing production lines, enabling businesses to scale their graphene production smoothly as demand grows.

Loginns offers this advanced technology through a convenient licensing model, providing companies flexible access without the need for significant upfront investments. By transforming graphite into FLG, our process significantly improves material efficiency, providing equivalent or superior performance while substantially reducing raw material usage. Industries utilizing coatings, composites, or energy-related applications can significantly enhance their products’ performance and competitiveness while benefiting from an environmentally responsible approach.

Loginns is committed to empowering businesses with advanced graphene solutions driving innovation forward and enhancing product quality while maintaining sustainability.

Visit our website to view our detailed video and discover how Loginns’ graphene technology can help you achieve superior product performance and sustainability.

Name: 2D-Enhanced Project
Description:
The 2D–ENHANCED project brings together the advanced potential of nanomaterials, particularly graphene and its derivatives, with cutting-edge manufacturing technologies of the Industry 4.0 era. This initiative is driven by a global collaboration between academic and industrial partners, including key players from the UAE, to explore and apply 2D materials in real-world applications. The project focuses on demonstrating the practical use of 2D materials, such as graphene, combined with modern production techniques like additive manufacturing. The goal is to develop high-tech prototypes and solutions that reach high technology readiness levels (TRL) and can have a significant market impact. Building on recent scientific breakthroughs and substantial global investments, 2D–ENHANCED aims to push the boundaries of graphene applications in industrial plastics across various sectors, including construction, healthcare (for pathogen protection), and aerospace (for electromagnetic radiation shielding). The project seeks to transition these innovative materials into ready-for-market products, fostering a deeper connection between academia, industry, and the UAE’s economy. The outcomes of this project include the development of new products, advanced manufacturing processes, and personnel training programs. It will create valuable industry/academic collaborations, promote sustainable manufacturing practices, and enhance the scientific and technical capacity of all partners involved.

Funded by RIC2D. Any opinions, findings and conclusions or recommendations expressed in this material are those of the author(s) and do not necessarily reflect the views of Khalifa University Research and Innovation Center for Graphene and 2D Materials (RIC2D), Abu Dhabi, UAE, as sponsor of the research.

Name: mechonics ag
Description:
Ultra-high precision spatial positioning of objects is of prime importance in the emerging field of nanotechnology. mechOnics patented new type of precision-positioning technology is based on an innovative concept that meets those market demands. The ultra-compact translation stages allow operation under ambient and extreme environmental conditions such as cryogenic temperatures (4 K) and ultra-high vacuum environments (10-9 mbar). These features present a revolutionary advancement for the positioning market leading to new research in numerous areas. 

Applications of these outstanding nanopositioning modules, well-known in many labs around the world, include open and closed loop nanopositioning stages with resolution up to 1 nm, ultra compact 3D-positioner with up to 10 mm in travel, or monomode coupler for fibres, to name just a few. Furthermore, they are suitable for general beam manipulation applications involving optical fibers and solid state waveguides.

The product line of mechOnics AG ranges from these stand-alone simple positioning components for laboratory applications to complete automated and integrated solutions for low temperature-positioning. The product range includes different sizes and travel of the stages for ambient temperature up to low temperature operation.
 
The product range is completed by innovative and highly flexible control systems for multiple axes operation.

As a market leader for micro-/nanopositioning devices we continuously work on supporting our customers to achieve reliable scientific results efficiently. Thus, our aim is to open up new possibilities ranging from scientific research to industrial applications.
 
Linear positioners are available with travel ranges up to 50 mm for ambient temperatures and UHV applications, for cryogenic application linear stages up to 20 mm can be delivered. Together with the open or closed loop controllers you can reach a repeatability up to 1 nm over the total travel range.

He called upon long-time colleague Ron, with whom he had developed a prototype disc centrifuge in the 1970's, to help develop a state-of-the-art disc centrifuge for a consulting client. Name: Nanovation Partners, LLC
Description: Starting in the mid 1990’s, the founders of Nanovation Partners LLC sponsored research at the University of California at San Diego (UCSD) in the lab of Sungho Jin, PhD. This research showed enhanced osseointegration and reduced implant surface infection by forming thousands of rows of tiny metal oxide nanotubes per square millimeter on the medical implant-to-tissue surface. These nanotubes were formed into the metal oxide surface already present on metal implants. No new materials were formed and no synthetic hydroxyapatite or other coating was needed. The group noticed that nearby stem cells treated the nanotube surfaces more like the outer membrane of other cells and they immediately clung to and branched out on this surface. These cells rapidly spread and differentiated into bone cells. Mineralization and mechanical bonding occurred more quickly and four times more prevalently with nanotube surfaces than non-treated surfaces. Ultimately in vivo studies showed that this caused a nine times stronger bond between implant and bone tissue after just four weeks.

Name: NT-MDT America Inc.
Description: For a quarter of century commercial SPM microscopes have attracted scientists by the unique capabilities of materials’ characterization at small scales. This is an ongoing journey to the nanoscale, and our team consolidates long- term experience, strong motivation and hard-work ethics for continuous improvements of the instrumentation and applications. Our goal is to provide researchers with the most sophisticated instruments for quantitative measurements of structure and properties of materials. In achieving this mission we are advancing the local studies with contact and oscillatory (resonant - Amplitude and Frequency Modulation modes; and non-resonant – HybriD mode) techniques using their complimentary nature for quantitative nanoscale measurements of mechanical and electric properties. In a further extension of AFM we have combined it with spectroscopic Raman studies where the spatial resolution approaches a few nanometers with the tip-enhanced technique. We are rigorously proving the quality of our microscopes and validity of various measurements with applications on diﬀerent samples thus providing our customers with a database for successful studies of materials of their interest. Starting the end of 2014 NT-MDT activities in the USA are guided from a new oﬃce in Tempe AZ where the sales and technical support groups are working side-by-side with an experienced research and development team. This arrangement further strengthens our support of our US customers and enhances their awareness about novel research projects and advanced applications of our microscopes.

Name: Stamper Technology, Inc
Description:
Stamper Technology works everyday with executives of archival foundations to archive knowledge of Earth permanently for future generations. We have archived a 30 million page library on the Moon as well as ancient texts for the Buddhists and the Argentina government.

Today most of the world’s knowledge and individual legacies are stored on a digital device that, over a short period of time, will disappear if they are not regenerated on new form of media. Digital encryption has gotten so sophisticated that if a chunk of the bitstream is damaged, information is lost forever (assuming a reader is available to retrieve the information in a few decades). Even the cloud is relegated to just bits on spinning discs that will eventually stop functioning.

At Stamper Technology we’re not only scientists, but we’re also artists. We preserve legacies forever in a nano-format that can easily be stored, carried, or even worn. Everyone has a story and collectively those stories make history and comprise a legacy for humanity. We share those stories and find comfort in them. We all have stories of our faith, our country, our heroes, our knowledge of math and science, our arts and music, our family, and our passion.

We connect humanity’s incredible stories to each individual’s own heart. We want to touch everyone in some way, which is why we created our enabling technology to preserve their stories, their passion, and their memorials. We preserve the history and legacies of yesterday, powered by the technology of tomorrow. Humans have many fascinating stories to tell. Let us at Stamper Technology tell those stories forever.

Granted four US patents, the high-speed 300,000 dpi printing process into metal has also been licensed to be used in all Microsoft discs since 2007 for high security authentication and has not been copied since.

Simprint Nanotechnologies provides software tools and simulation services to users of nanoimprint lithography (NIL). We offer an extremely fast way of simulating the nano-scale transformation of material involved in NIL — a sorely-needed capability that will, for the first time, allow semiconductor, photonics, and data-storage manufacturers to use nanoimprint reliably and with greatly reduced development costs. Our company will help accelerate the adoption of NIL and will enhance the benefits of using this elegant, fast and inexpensive patterning technique. In December 2010 we launched our first product, Simprint Core, which simulates thermal nanoimprint lithography.

Simprint Nanotechnologies is a privately held UK limited company.

Name: F2N2Lab UGR
Description:
El grupo de Materia Blanda Magnética (Magnetic Soft Matter Group) es un grupo joven y de marcado carácter multidisciplinar constituido por físicos, químicos, biólogos, biotecnólogos, ingenieros químicos, industriales y de telecomunicaciones.

Realiza investigación puntera en el Laboratorio Singular de Tecnologías Avanzadas F2N2Lab de la Universidad de Granada e investiga a nivel fundamental con nanomateriales magnéticos mediante experimentos de reomicroscopía en laboratorio y simulaciones numéricas a nivel de partícula por dinámica molecular y por elementos finitos. En la actualidad, se trabaja en aplicaciones del ámbito de la Nanomedicina, y en particular en el tratamiento del cáncer y la regeneración tisular mediante el uso de campos magnéticos triaxiales no estacionarios con equipamiento único en el mundo diseñado “ad hoc”.

El grupo mantiene colaboraciones estables con investigadores de primer nivel de alcance internacional y empresas multinacionales de renombre.

---

The Magnetic Soft Matter Group is a multidisciplinary group of young researchers from diverse backgrounds in physics, chemistry, biology, biotechnology, chemical engineering, industrial and telecommunication engineering.

In the Singular Laboratory in Advanced Technologies F2N2Lab at the University of Granada, the group carries out cutting-edge research on magnetic nanomaterials through rheomicroscopy experiments, particle-level simulations (molecular dynamics), and finite element method simulations. Currently, we are developing several Nanomedicine applications, in particular cancer treatment and tissue regeneration methods, through the use of non-stationary triaxial magnetic fields using our specially designed equipment. 

The group collaborates with leading international researchers and research institutes as well as renowned multinational companies.

Name: Waviks, Inc.
Description:
Waviks was founded in 2013 by Dr. Tom Moore in Dallas, TX, to develop and produce innovative accessories for the SEM, FIB, HIM, and TEM to facilitate nanoanalysis and nanofabrication. Waviks core team has enjoyed more than 50 years of experience in designing and producing nanomanipulation and nanofabrication accessories for Omniprobe, Inc., now an Oxford Instruments company.  Waviks carries on the tradition of high value accessories and a focus on customer satisfaction that can best be provided by a small and agile corporation.  

Waviks cornerstone product is the Vesta laser sample heating system for the TEM, SEM. FIB and TEM.  The Vesta system relies on Waviks expertise in fully programmable nanomanipulators and the physics of nanofabrication.  The Vesta system produces a very small (10 um) heated area on the sample, so fast temperature ramping is easy and multiple sites on the same sample can be examined.  There is no thermal damage to detectors or the column, and there is minimal thermal-mechanical drift in the sample, so long duration studies and movies are easy.  The laser can be pulsed to look at transient events.  On the analytical side, EEGS (Electron Energy Gain Spectroscopy) with laser stimulation is possible.

Our Patented Multifunctional Nano Coatings address the problems with the functions of Antisoiling, Anticorrosion, Anti-abrasion, Antireflection (ARC), Antiglare, Sodium migration barrier (Anti PID), Self Cleaning and anti dripping. 
The glass is used as a protection and media of transfer of light in different applications in Industry, buildings, Household, Machines, Vehicles, Electronic Displays, Ships, aeroplanes, PV modules, and Greenhouse. The philosophy and science behind the protection could not recognize that who should "Protect the Protector"!  We were able to foresight and vision for the "Protection of the Protector"! This enables us to innovate responsibly by developing different protection technologies to protect the glass surface against corrosion, abrasion, reflection, soiling, cementing, osmosis, scaling, and drippings. We have Patented Nano-Technologies with multiple functionalities for application in different areas. These technologies enhance the life, performance, protectability, safety, and quality of glass applications. 
The coated PV modules have shown higher performance in the range of 8 to 20% as compared to ARC coated or non coated PV modules available in the market. Our technologies like HP+ has shown more than 8% power gain when this applied to existing old PV fields. The coatings have reduced the water requirement by 70% for wet cleaning. The cleaning cycles required per year were also reduced by  70%. Accumulated revenue due to bundles of benefits leads to 10 to 20% higher in coated projects with our coatings. Robotic cleaning and dry cleaning becomes efficient and effective due to nano-coating with 20 years of life. Our coatings are plasma technology-based. They create 90 to 120nm thick very hard uniform layers which protect the surface against chemical and physical forces.  Our coating can be used for the production of solar glass and installed old PV fields. Our clients have seen consistent high performance around the world.

The coating formulas are molecularly designed for surfaces such as paint, vinyl, polymers, metals, stones, glass, teak, gelcoats, ceramics, marble, stainless steel, woods, glass, leather, fabrics and much more.
Our protective coatings bond to surfaces at a molecular level, filling in any nano-pores and creating a hydrophobic surface that is impervious to contamination.

It is completely safe and non-toxic and is designed to purify surfaces from bad smells, disease causing pathogens, mold & other issues caused by microbes, bacteria & viruses. Carbon Design Innovations has a novel process for producing carbon nanotubes that are predictably straight and we can also form them into any desired shape.

Our first application is for Atomic Force Microscope (AFM) Probes.  Although this has been attempted for years without success, the company has patented processes that allows us to make CNTs that are straight, strong and durable. This means when used as an AFM probe the angle remains normal to the surface in the compression regime and do not bend so that there are no trailing artifacts. OUR CHALLENGE
1. Eliminate the use of hexavalent chromium, maintaining the same features in the final product

3. Preserve the environment by eliminating one of the most dangerous environmental contaminants which, due to its high solubility in water, can spread over large areas.

Description:
MERIDIAN SPECIALITY TECHNOLOGIES has the pan-India exclusive marketing rights of fuel and lube additives manufactured by H2OIL Corporation, a US based research & development company. H2OIL Corporation is the world leader in nanotechnology fuel additives and have been manufacturing and supplying these additives worldwide since the last 20 years.
F2-21 eeFuel additive is a highly concentrated, non-toxic, non-hazardous & non-inflammable water based fuel additive that works well in all liquid fuels such as petrol, diesel, bio-diesel, ethanol and heavy fuel oil. When added to fuel, this multi-functional additive uses “dynamic liquid nanotechnology” to transform the fuel into a high performance nanotechnology enhanced fuel providing multiple benefits such as improved efficiency, increased engine power, reduction in harmful emissions & a cleaner combustion chamber. F2-21 eeFuel is easily miscible with fuel & can be added into the fuel tank of the equipment or in the bulk storage tanks. The additive does not alter the physical and chemical properties of fuel. The additives do not have any metal particles. F2-21 eeFuel is available in two variants: 
(1) F2-21 eeFuel (Diesel Additive) When added to fuel, the additive breaks down large fuel droplets into smaller & more readily vaporized sizes and increases turbulence thereby improving localized mixing of air-fuel vapor. This process results in the reduction of fuel consumption by 5-12%. It also cleans the combustion chamber giving cooler combustion, lower emission, cleaner lubrication oil and reduced engine octane requirement. 
(2) F2-21 eeFuel (Heavy Fuel Oil Additive) F2-21 ee Fuel enhanced HFO increases the performance of boilers thereby reducing fuel consumption by 5-11%. Adding the additive also reduces corrosion on boiler surface and cleans the surface of boilers, storage tanks, filters and pipe lines. F2-21 eeFuel enhanced HFO also improves the quality of the fuel – reduces viscosity and enhances shelf life

Name: Vesiculate
Description:
Reliable nanoparticle analysis starts with the right reference material.

For years, a fundamental disconnect has compromised data integrity: instruments are calibrated using perfectly spherical solid "beads" with inert surfaces, yet researchers measure soft, often hollow, therapeutic nanoparticles with complex embedded cargo and surface properties.

This "apples-to-oranges" mismatch creates a critical gap, leading to unreliable data, flawed conclusions, and ultimately, costly delays in therapeutic development.

Vesiculate is the pioneering manufacturer of Syncles, synthetic vesicle standards designed to bridge this gap.

We provide the "ground truth" for analyzing all vesicular nanoparticles, including extracellular vesicles (EVs), lipid nanoparticles (LNPs), virus-like particles (VLPs), liposomes, polymersomes, and similar. Our monodisperse standards enable researchers to validate instrument performance with confidence and standardize analytical workflows, ensuring meaningful data comparison across labs.

We are a fully vertically integrated nanoparticle provider. We design, synthesize, and validate every vesicle in-house to ensure absolute data integrity.

Standardize your workflow with the new gold standard.

Learn more: www.vesiculate.com

Name: Applied Multilayers LLC
Description:
Applied Multilayers offers next generation optical coating sputtering systems for high quality optical coatings. We offer a wide range of system platforms together with turn-key processes. Our patented technology is simple, scaleable and significantly less expensive.  Discover the unique near-bulk coating properties and applications which our patented CFM technology offers.You will also discover numerous applications such as durable IR coatings, durable visible AR coatings, high laser damage coatings, ophthalmics, electrochromics and solar adsorbers. 

Our patented Closed Field Magnetron (CFM) sputtering process technology is the most advanced coating technology yet devised. It takes optical coating into a new era of precision coatings with near-bulk film densification and allows productivity from pilot to full production scalability. CFM is of “cold” deposition standards. It now allows multilayered optical film coatings onto sensitive polymer substrates or plastics which other deposition technologies would consider prohibitive due the elevated plasma temperatures.The technology achieves much higher reactive deposition rates by operating near the “metallic-mode”. Unlike other reactive coating systems, the reaction occurs all the way around the vertical rotating drum without the need for expensive and high maintenance ion sources or plasma sources. Exceptional coatings for oxides, nitrides, carbides or pure metals.

Applied Multilayers is a SDVOSB.

Name: Damorphe Hydrogen (Green Revolution Hydrogen)
Description:
REVOLUTIONARY HYDROGEN TECHNOLOGY
Green Revolution Hydrogen has nano-engineered and patented an environmentally friendly, bio-compatible degradable rare earth oxide alloy that can be dissolved in any kind of water to make 100% pure hydrogen at a cost of less than $1/kilo hydrogen (H2).

It’s a revolution …

1. It is the greenest hydrogen in the world
2. It is the cheapest hydrogen in the world
3. It can use saltwater or sewage water, rather than drinking water
4. It doesn’t require electricity
5. It can be built to any scale, onsite or hub and spoke
6. Secure supply based on a diversified North American supply chain

… in short, it can provide green energy security for the entire world.

Electrolysis and steam methane reformation will both be made largely redundant by this technology.

SCIENCE
We can produce hydrogen electrochemically using nano-engineered alloys as reagents. This works by reacting de-passivated alloy and water, to yield hydrogen. It’s extremely cost-effective due to the cheap cost of the nano-engineered alloy reagent, we can achieve a price point of <$1/kg hydrogen. The process is also insensitive to the salinity of the water, and does not passivate.

The founders Carmine, Ting and Indranil as graduates of Oxford, MIT and the University of California, respectively, along with the rest of their team have extensive experience in energy and nano-materials at companies such as Shell and Schlumberger where they have collectively filed over 150 career patents and memos. They have each launched commercially successful start-ups across hydrogen, energy and nano-materials.

Green Revolution Hydrogen is a subsidiary of Damorphe.

Name: NexGen Futures
Description:
NexGen Futures is a startup company embracing new disruptive technologies. Our vision is to provide a portfolio of solutions which contribute to a lower carbon footprint, address fuel poverty, and create a range of innovative products designed to disrupt market sectors and challenge the status quo.

The first product in our portfolio integrates NexGen nanotechnology to develop the most advanced waterproof heating films available. Our heating films are suitable for use in both internal and external projects and product integration.

NexGen multi-award winning, low resistance, waterproof, heating films have been developed by a team with over 30 years’ experience working within the fields of engineering technology and conductive and resistive inks.

The graphite and graphene base nanomaterial deployed as an innovative heating solution for multiple applications across industry sectors including Construction, Automotive, Marine, Stadiums, Festivals, Crisis Relief and Consumer Goods.

Utilizing our core nanotechnology, we work with clients to develop and implement bespoke integrated solutions which give a competitive advantage and help to differentiate from their competitors.

Ongoing research and development by our pioneering team is driving our nanotechnology forward to meet future challenges. Our company strives to be ahead of the curve and deliver cutting edge solutions to solve tomorrows problems today.

Name: Wireless Power Link Technologies Ltd
Description:
Wireless Power Link Technologies Ltd. (dba “Wi-WATT”) is a semiconductor, solar and mechanical energy technology development company, that has operated in “stealth mode” for many years, but can now publicly and proudly boast of some of its major technological breakthroughs, including substantially solving the age-old challenges of wireless power transfer, i.e. safety, distance, efficiency and cost. Our multiple technologies cohesively bring a fresh perspective to safely delivering sufficient energy to any device, at any location, at any time. Through our Wi-WATT patent-pending slate of technologies, we are also developing, amongst other things, an ecosystem that charge multiple devices without any user intervention, enabling an efficient and truly wire-free power. Our vision of “Wireless Power, Anywhere, Anytime!” drives every aspect of our technologies.

Our technologies have also solved the issue of low efficiency with organic solar cells and theoretical efficiency limits of silicon based solar cells. We are super excited that our group's collective slate of current and developing renewable energy technologies have the potential to eliminate the majority of carbon emissions within a decade!

We are proud members of SolarPower Europe and AirFuel Alliance and intend to partner will top solar panel manufacturers and smartphone manufacturers to commercialize some of our innovative, disruptive technologies.

Name: Gongali Model Co. ltd
Description:
As a social enterprise, we are passionate about change. Nanofilter has won numerous international awards, including those from the WHO & the Royal Academy of Engineering. 

Currently installed in 127 public kiosks, more than 1000 households & around 100 private institutions (schools, universities etc.), Nanofilter directly benefits 250,000 people each day in Tanzania, Kenya & Zambia. Name: Aquashield Colombia
Description:
Aquashield OnGuard es el futuro de la tecnología antimicrobiana. Todos los días estamos en riesgo de contraer enfermedades que afectan nuestra salud, es por eso que Aquashield ha creado una solución de protección 24/7.
Nuestros desarrollos de última tecnología garantizan protección continua hasta por 90 días, su fácil aplicación y ausencia de toxicidad lo hace un producto amigable con el medio ambiente.
La formula de Aquashield OnGuard funciona creando una barrera bacterioestatica que atrae microorganismos acelerando el proceso de eliminación de los mismos. Estos entran en la barrera gracias a las fuerzas magnéticas creadas por el átomo de nitrógeno. Los átomos de nitrógeno actúan junto con la barrera para electrocutar los microbios que se acercan, eliminándolos de manera instantánea sin que estos tengan alguna posibilidad de resistir o mutar en sepas más agresivas.
Nuestra tecnología de punta certificada por la EPA ataca patógenos peligrosos que causan enfermedades y los elimina con una efectividad del 99.99 %. Hospitales, oficinas, escuelas, restaurantes, gimnasios, industrias, medios de transporte, entre otros ,se benefician de nuestra tecnología altamente eficiente y duradera en el tiempo.
Somos una empresa comprometida con el bienestar de las personas, por lo que nuestras soluciones garantizan entornos mas seguros y un estilo de vida saludable. Que estas esperando para protegerte a ti y a las personas que están a tu alrededor, contáctanos y siéntete mas seguro con Aquashield Onguard.

 Ciência, inovação e impacto!
A Radíon nasceu da pesquisa no Instituto de Engenharia Nuclear (IEN) e já conquistou espaço no setor de Radioquímica e Medicina Nuclear, sendo selecionada em dois editais do programa Catalisa ICT do SEBRAE pelo alto potencial de inovação.

 Nossa tecnologia permite:
 Maior segurança e precisão no uso de radiofármacos
 Desenvolvimento de biopolímeros e filmes poliméricos para dosimetria
 Novas soluções para embolização vascular e aplicações médicas

 Além de inovar, valorizamos a diversidade! Nossa equipe é composta por profissionais diversos e comprometidos com o avanço da ciência, tecnologia e sustentabilidade.

Venha conhecer nossas soluções tecnológicas em Radioquímica! Juntos, podemos transformar o futuro da Medicina Nuclear no Brasil e no mundo!

#Inovação #Ciência #Radioquímica #MedicinaNuclear #Tecnologia #Startups #MulheresNaCiência

Name: Vista Therapeutics, Inc.
Description:
Vista Therapeutics has been developing, collaborating, and fine tuning the NanoBioSensorTM Platform based on research developed in the Charles M. Lieber Laboratory at Harvard University.  

The software, instrument, and functionalized NanoCards for target immobilization are based on silicon nanowires that act as Field Effect Transistors (FETs) to quantitatively measure and provide kinetic analysis of real time changes in conductance with high sensitivity based on detector:target sample interactions without requiring secondary detection molecules.  

Applications include:

-Multiplex biomarker measurement in body fluids and cell culture matrices
-Bioprocess monitoring
-Toxicology/Pharmacokinetics
-Portable option for field based diagnostics
-Pathogen detection 
-Environmental monitoring
-Historical exposure/vaccine analyis (IgM or IgG formation)
-PCR/rtPCR coming soon...

Name: Pranan Technologies
Description:
Pranan Technologies es una empresa tecnológica de investigación y desarrollo destinada a la mejora del bienestar, que nace con la intención de dar respuesta a la inquietud social que rodea la contaminación electromagnética, después de que la OMS alertase de la naturaleza dañina de la radiación emitida por los móviles y de que un reciente dictamen de la Unión Europa que emplazase a los países miembros a tomar medidas preventivas en este ámbito.

La tecnología Pranan consiste en desarrollar métodos basados en identificar y decodificar frecuencias electromagnéticas y aplicarlos a unos dispositivos denominados "Convertidores Pasivos", construidos a partir de nanopartículas de minerales y metales. La principal innovación es que estos convertidores son eficientes energéticamente y, por lo tanto, respetuosos con el medioambiente y el consumo de materias primas: no consumen electricidad, tienen una duración ilimitada, no requieren mantenimiento y no contaminan.

Esta tecnología ha sido testada científicamente mediante diferentes estudios de universidades de prestigio como la Universidad de Granada (Estudio bioquímico del Centro de Investigación Biomédico - Grupo de Investigación CTS-101: Comunicación Intercelular) y la Universidad Complutense de Madrid (Estudio bioléctrico de la Facultad de Medicina - Departamento de Psiquiatría y Psicología Médica) y comprobada por el doctor K. Korotkov a través de bioelectrografía GDV-EPC , desarrollada en la Universidad Estatal de San Petersburgo.

Description:
P2i 创立于 2004 年，对首席技术官 Stephen Coulson 和英国国防部研发的斥水技术进行了商业化运作。仅在过去十年间，我们的团队就已在多个市场领域取得非凡业绩，我们的专利组合也已超过 65 个系列。作为一个真正的跨国公司，我们的主要办公地点位于英国和中国，而我们的技术已在世界各地的工厂实施应用。

P2i 是一家技术解决方案供应商，十多年来，致力于开发防水纳米涂层。我们专注于消费电子产品行业，与原始设备制造商直接开展合作，为电子产品提供防水性能，提升它们的可靠性，并使其免于因日常意外接触液体而受损。

过去十年，我们的研发团队始终致力于完善 P2i 的独家技术。我们的专利脉冲等离子体工艺流程可生产超薄斥水性纳米涂层，打造一流的防水效果。我们是该领域公认的唯一实现批量生产的公司，并具备将我们技术与生产线彻底融合的多年经验，同时提供世界一流的服务与支持。我们的商业模式和在多个市场批量生产所取得的公认成就是前所未有的。

Name: Astra Optics Limited
Description:
Astra Optics Limited, founded by a team of experts in optical engineering, material science, and nanomanufacturing, is a spin-off company from the Chinese University of Hong Kong. 
Our ground-breaking 3D nanofabrication technology, i.e., femtosecond projection laser patterning system and hydrogel-based multi-material fabrication platform, has proven records to revolutionize the micro-additive manufacturing industry by overcoming the critical bottlenecks in current state-of-the-art solutions, namely, low-throughput (~0.1 mm3/hr) and high fabrication cost ($20/mm3). The FP NanoPrinter is the only truly scalable nano-printing solution that simultaneously demonstrates record resolution (20 nm), throughput (100s mm3/hour), and substantially reduced cost ($1.5/mm3), which is three orders of magnitude faster than existing commercial methods. 
Our records have been internationally recognized by our 2019 and 2022 publications in the Science Magazine (Saha et al., Science, 366(6461): 105-109, 2019 & Han et al., Science, 378(6626): 1325-1331, 2022) with multiple U.S. patents. 
By substantially lowering the cost of micro-additive manufacturing, our product enables industrial scale application of TPL and multi-material 3D nanofabrication at 20-nm resolution for the first time. Our product will generate impact in different industries such as nanotechnology, biotechnology, healthcare, renewable energy, and electronics.

Name: Nanouptake COST Action
Description:
Los nanofluidos (NF) se definen como fluidos de transferencia de calor o de almacenamiento de energía térmica con propiedades mejoradas mediante la adición de nanopartículas. A pesar del enorme esfuerzo realizado en la investigación y el desarrollo de las FN en la última década, todavía existen barreras significativas para su aceptación en el mercado por la implementación comercial en aplicaciones industriales.

Algunas de estas barreras no están coordinadas. Los esfuerzos de investigación europeos, el considerable aumento de la pérdida de presión y los problemas de estabilidad de las FN, las FN no desarrolladas para aplicaciones industriales específicas, el alto costo y los métodos de fabricación no desarrollados, los problemas incompletos de la Evaluación del ciclo de vida de las FN y el difícil acceso a las llamadas H2020.

Name: GreenGlass Italia
Description:
GreenGlass è un processo chimico brevettato, che riempie le creste microscopiche del vetro. Il risultato è una barriera idrorepellente, ultra sottile e trasparente che blocca sin dall’inizio la corrosione e le macchie grazie all'innovativo trattamento nanotecnologico. 

Vantaggi:
- Pulizia. Sporcizia, polvere e detriti scivolano via. Pulire il vetro è più facile, non servono detergenti chimici aggressivi e i tempi si riducono del 90%.
- Resistenza. Protegge le superfici da macchie e opacità, riducendo i costi di sostituzione e manutenzione.
- Efficienza. Incrementa trasparenza e brillantezza, garantendo massima efficienza energetica ai pannelli fotovoltaici. Vetri sempre nuovi, ad un costo minimo.

Applicazioni:
- Grandi facciate, 
- Vetrate esterne e interne, 
- Box doccia, specchi
- Finestre
- Pannelli solari 
- Parabrezza auto. 

La tecnologia:
Il trattamento GreenGlass consiste nella creazione di un sottile e invisibile rivestimento reticolato e ramificato sulla superficie del vetro. Una pellicola capace di riempire ogni angolo della vetrata, respingendo totalmente tutti gli agenti esterni contaminanti. Un risultato possibile grazie alla formazione, tra il rivestimento e il vetro, di un legame covalente.
Si tratta di un legame chimico più forte e difficile da “rompere” rispetto al legame ionico che è  presente nella maggior parte di altri trattamenti idrofobici.

I vetri trattati con il metodo GreenGlass:
- Sono trasparenti al 100%
- Sono resistenti e duraturi
- Non ingialliscono e non si macchiano

Una barriera protettiva che ripara dai danni ambientali, come polvere, acqua o graffi, garantendo un notevole risparmio. 
Il vetro si mantiene sempre trasparente brillante, come se fosse nuovo.

Name: SLD Photonics
Description:
SLD Photonics is a start-up company seeking to further develop research that was originally performed in the Physics department at the University of Wyoming.  The innovation is a single broadband detector that can span from 390-2200nm with a featureless quantum efficiency.

There are two key advantages for the application of this new technology: 1) simplicity of using a single detector instead of multiple detectors, e.g., cross-calibration, and 2) a featureless responsivity over the spectral range eliminating the for post-processing to account for wavelength and intensity dependent responsivity.

Currently, there is not a commercially available camera that can capture visible
light and near infrared light. This innovation advances a camera capable of spanning this light range and fulfilling this unmet need. This research started in a state-sponsored university physics lab and requires further work to overcome technical barriers to become a commercially viable product. As a commercial product, this detector can be used in multiple commercial sectors including unmanned vehicles, the medical field, and for the defense and security of the nation.  For example, in the medical field the ideal light for tissue penetration is between visible and near infrared. The camera developed in this project would permit optical scans of human bodies to diagnose diseases without expensive MRI technology or radiological exposure from high energy sources, i.e., x-rays. Additionally, this would help healthcare services in rural areas to have access to cheaper diagnosing services. This device is currently under active research with an NSF SBIR Phase I grant.

Name: NanoFab Research Project
Description:
The NanoFab Research Project aims to demonstrate a new sustainable and relatively inexpensive manufacturing process for nanofabrication. 

Semiconductors or “chips” are the core component of all electronic devices. Over a trillion semiconductors are manufactured globally each year. The most advanced are incredibly technically complex: chips the size of a postage stamp can have 60 billion transistors. Once designed, chips need to be manufactured, or ‘fabricated’. This involves the etching and depositing of a design onto a semiconductor ‘wafer’. Often defined by ‘node size’; relating to the distance at which each tiny transistor can be etched between each other onto a chip. Fabrication is reliant on specialist materials and equipment including high purity wafers and powerful short wavelength lithography lasers, as well as vacuum sealed clean rooms.

If we could directly write structures at the nanoscale, we would use less precursor, produce less waste and reduce energy consumption and potentially make the process sustainable as well as accessible for smaller manufacturers.

The research team is based at The University of Manchester and includes:

PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR
- Professor David Lewis, Head of the Department of Materials

CO-INVESTIGATORS
- Professor Richard Winpenny, Professor of Inorganic Chemistry, Department of Chemistry
- Dr Jessica Boland, Senior Lecturer of Functional Materials and Devices, Department of Electronic and Electrical Engineering

POSTDOCTORAL RESEARCHERS
- Dr Grigore Timco, Department of Materials
- Dr Milad Nonahal, Department of Chemistry

DOCTORAL STUDENT
 - Jacob Young, Department of Materials

PROJECT MANAGER
- Gaynor Spink, School of Natural Sciences, Faculty of Science and Engineering

The University of Manchester has received a UKRI, Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council grant to implement the three-year project. It started on 1 May 2023.

Name: NextGenerationEU-Project BG-RRP-2.011-0001-C0 "t-ShM Filament"
Description:
Project BG-RRP-2.011-0001-C0*: „Smart filament of biodegradable polymers with graphene for 4D-printing with thermo-stimulated shape memory activated by resistive heating”   (t-ShM Filament)
Coordinator:  Prof. DSc. Rumiana Kotsilkova -  kotsilkova@imbm.bas.bg
Funded by EC under NextGenerationEU.
Host: Institute of Mechanics -BAS (OLEM)
Overview:
The project aims to develop electroactive nanocomposite filament of biodegradable polymers with graphene for 4D-printing (4DP) with “thermo-stimulated shape memory” (t-ShM) activated by Joule heating. The new 4DP technology is the next generation of additive manufacturing, which adds unique features to 3D printed architectures under the influence of external stimuli. The 4DP concept requires a shape memory material with specific properties; sensitivity to external stimuli; and structural design that are necessary to unlock the potential of functionality. The project will develop a multicomponent polymer-polymer nanocomposite with graphene, that demonstrate high elasticity, electroactivity and thermal properties. An innovative t-ShM filament and 4D-printing technology will be developed, with a representative model for testing in a simulated Joule heating environment. We will optimize the temperature transitions for t-ShM depending on the electroactivity and the nanocomposite properties. The expected outcome of the project is: Biodegradable, multifunctional t-ShM filament for 4D-printing (FDM), demonstrated with a Basic Prototype that unlocks reversible thermo-stimulated effects such as self-heating, self-actuation and self-healing from defects. The innovative t-ShM filament will have great potential for printing objects with complex shapes and reversible thermo-stimulated effects, that is suitable for many revolutionary applications. This material is biodegradable, biocompatible, recyclable and will reduce the carbon footprint of the rapidly developing additive manufacturing.
